128.description: Numeric constant specifying double-double extended precision (128-bit) floating-point arithmetic in the Math section. Provides approximately 32 decimal digits of precision.

256.description: Numeric constant specifying quad-double extended precision (256-bit) floating-point arithmetic in the Math section. Provides approximately 63 decimal digits of precision.

1D.description: Plot quantity qualifier indicating one-dimensional (line) data. Used in the File section for NonLocalPlot output and in various sections to denote 1D datasets or 1D Schrodinger equation solutions.

AC.description: Prefix/suffix related to AC (alternating current) small-signal analysis. In the File section, the AC analysis output file uses the extension _ac_des.plt. In Math and Solve sections, AC-related keywords control the AC solver method.

contexts.File: File naming for AC analysis output (extension _ac_des.plt)
contexts.Math: AC solver configuration (ACMethod, ACSubMethod)
contexts.Solve: AC analysis execution (ACCoupled, ACCompute, ACPlot)

AC analysis.description: Small-signal AC analysis performed within the Solve section. Computes the frequency-dependent admittance matrix between circuit nodes by extending a Coupled solve with AC-specific parameters.

contexts.File: ACExtract specifies the output file prefix for AC analysis results
contexts.Math: ACMethod and ACSubMethod control the linear solver used for AC analysis
contexts.Solve: ACCoupled performs the actual AC analysis computation

ACCompute.description: Controls AC or noise analysis performance within a Quasistationary or Transient ramp. Specifies at which bias points AC analysis is performed, reducing computational cost by avoiding unnecessary AC computations.

parameters.name: Time
parameters.desc: Optional. Time points at which to perform AC analysis during transient.

ACCoupled.description: Performs small-signal AC analysis. Extends a Coupled section with parameters for computing the frequency-dependent admittance matrix between circuit nodes. Typically used in mixed-mode simulations. The admittance matrix Y = A + i*2*pi*f*C is decomposed into conductance (A) and capacitance (C) matrices.

parameters.name: StartFrequency
parameters.desc: Start frequency for AC analysis sweep [Hz]

parameters.name: EndFrequency
parameters.desc: End frequency for AC analysis sweep [Hz]

parameters.name: NumberOfPoints
parameters.desc: Number of frequency points in the sweep

parameters.name: Decade
parameters.desc: Use decade (logarithmic) frequency spacing

parameters.name: Node
parameters.desc: List of AC circuit nodes for admittance matrix computation

parameters.name: Exclude
parameters.desc: List of circuit instances to exclude from AC analysis

parameters.name: Linear
parameters.desc: Use linear frequency spacing

parameters.name: ACExtract
parameters.desc: Prefix for the AC analysis output file name (overrides File section)

parameters.name: ACMethod
parameters.desc: Linear solver method for AC analysis. "Blocked" (block decomposition solver)

parameters.name: ACSubMethod
parameters.desc: Inner linear solver for Blocked method. Accepts solver identifiers from Table 223

parameters.name: ACPlot
parameters.desc: Prefix for AC response plot files

parameters.name: Optical
parameters.desc: Perform optical AC analysis

parameters.name: ACCompute
parameters.desc: Restrict AC or noise analysis to selected points in a Quasistationary command

parameters.name: CircuitNoise
parameters.desc: Compute noise from SPICE circuit elements

parameters.name: Extraction
parameters.desc: List of frequency-dependent extraction curves

parameters.name: NoisePlot
parameters.desc: Prefix for noise analysis output file

parameters.name: ObservationNode
parameters.desc: Nodes for noise analysis; subset of those in Node

parameters.name: VoltageGreenFunctions
parameters.desc: Compute voltage responses for frequency zero

ACExtract.description: Specifies the base name for the small-signal and noise analysis output file (extension _ac_des.plt). Used in the global File section or as a parameter inside ACCoupled() in the Solve section to override the File section setting.

parameters.name: ACExtract
parameters.desc: Base name for AC analysis output file

contexts.File: Global specification of AC output file name
contexts.Solve: Local override inside ACCoupled() to specify per-solve AC output file

ACMethod.description: Specifies the linear solver method for ACCoupled analysis in the Math section. Currently only "Blocked" (block decomposition solver) is supported as the default.

parameters.name: ACMethod
parameters.desc: Solver method. "Blocked" (block decomposition solver, default)

ACPlot.description: Specifies which quantities to plot in the AC analysis plot file within the System section. If specified, the given quantities (solution variables, current densities) are plotted alongside AC response data for each AC node voltage excitation and frequency. Without this statement, only node voltages are plotted.

ACSubMethod.description: Specifies the inner linear solver used with the Blocked method for ACCoupled analysis in the Math section. Accepts solver identifiers from Table 223 (Linear Solvers). Default is Super for 1D/2D and ILS for 3D.

parameters.name: ACSubMethod
parameters.desc: Inner solver identifier. "Super" (default for 1D/2D), "ILS" (default for 3D), or other Table 223 solvers

ASE.description: Amplified Spontaneous Emission. A plot variable available in LED simulations for visualizing ASE-related quantities in the current plot file.

Abort.description: Option for ParallelLicense in the Math section. Terminates the simulation immediately if insufficient parallel licenses are available.

AbsVal.description: Parameter in BreakCriteria for Current, DevicePower, and OuterDevicePower. Specifies an upper limit for the absolute value of the monitored quantity. The simulation stops when the absolute value exceeds this limit.

parameters.name: absval
parameters.desc: Upper limit for the absolute value. For Current: A/um/d. For DevicePower: W/um^d.

AbsorbedPhotonDensity.description: A scalar plot quantity representing the absorbed photon density (number of absorbed photons per unit volume) at each vertex. Available as a built-in dataset name in OpticalGeneration ReadFromFile and as a plot variable.

AbsorbedPhotonDensityCoherent.description: A scalar plot quantity representing the coherent component of absorbed photon density. Part of the spectral decomposition of absorbed photon density into coherent and incoherent contributions.

AbsorbedPhotonDensityFromSpectrum.description: A scalar plot quantity representing the absorbed photon density computed from the illumination spectrum. Part of the spectral decomposition of absorbed photon density.

AbsorbedPhotonDensityIncoherent.description: A scalar plot quantity representing the incoherent component of absorbed photon density. Part of the spectral decomposition of absorbed photon density into coherent and incoherent contributions.

Absorption.description: In Physics{Optics{}}, activates optical absorption computation in nonsemiconductor regions or materials. When specified inside RayTracing(), enables absorption calculation for optical generation in insulator regions.

Absval.description: Alias for AbsVal. Parameter in BreakCriteria for Current, DevicePower, and OuterDevicePower specifying an upper limit for the absolute value of the monitored quantity.

parameters.name: absval
parameters.desc: Upper limit for the absolute value

AccepMinusConcentration.description: A scalar plot quantity representing the ionized acceptor concentration (NA-), available when the incomplete ionization model is activated. Can be visualized in the Plot section of a device.

AcceptNewtonParameter.description: Specifies relaxed Newton convergence parameters in the Math section. Allows looser convergence criteria to be applied when standard convergence fails. Also available in Continuation and Quasistationary sections to apply relaxed Newton at specific step sizes.

parameters.name: Digits
parameters.desc: Number of relative error digits for relaxed convergence

parameters.name: Error
parameters.desc: Absolute error parameters per equation: Electron, Hole, Poisson, eQuantumPotential, hQuantumPotential, eTemperature, hTemperature, Temperature

parameters.name: ErrRef
parameters.desc: Reference error parameters per equation in their native units

parameters.name: InvokeAtDivergence
parameters.desc: Allow relaxed convergence to be considered for diverged solutions

parameters.name: RelErrControl
parameters.desc: Use relative error control

parameters.name: RhsAndUpdateConvergence
parameters.desc: Require both RHS and update error convergence

parameters.name: RhsMin
parameters.desc: Minimum of RHS norm

parameters.name: UpdateScale
parameters.desc: Additional factor for the update error

contexts.Math: Global relaxed Newton parameters applied to all solves
contexts.Solve: Inside Quasistationary() with ReferenceStep to apply at specific step sizes

Acceptor.description: Specifies acceptor-type dopant species in the Physics section. Used in doping profile definitions, PMI (Physical Model Interface) C++ classes, and incomplete ionization model specifications. In trap specifications, identifies an acceptor-like trap level.

AcceptorConcentration.description: Acceptor doping concentration (NA) at each vertex, specified through the doping profile in the device structure. Used in physics models for computing carrier statistics, mobility, and recombination rates.

Accuracy.description: Specifies a tolerance value for various numerical computations. In Cyclic(), sets the cyclic convergence tolerance. In optical solver contexts, controls the accuracy of the optical generation computation.

parameters.name: Accuracy
parameters.desc: Tolerance value. In Cyclic: tolerance epsilon_cyc.

contexts.Solve: In Cyclic() as cyclic convergence tolerance
contexts.Physics: In optical generation and transfer matrix method as solver accuracy

ActEnergy.description: Activation energy for hydrogen depassivation on Si-H bonds in the trap Degradation model. Specified in eV. Used within Physics{Traps(...Degradation...)} to model bias temperature instability (BTI) and hot-carrier degradation.

parameters.name: ActEnergy
parameters.desc: Activation energy in eV for hydrogen bond depassivation

Active.description: Activates quantum well models for use in conjunction with QWLocal (localized) or Schroedinger (nonlocal) models. The Type parameter specifies which quantum well model to activate.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.desc: Type of quantum well model to activate. "QuantumWell" activates the localized or nonlocal QW model depending on whether QWLocal or Schroedinger is also specified.

ActiveDopingConcentration.description: A scalar plot quantity representing the substitutional (active) net doping concentration, computed as DonorConcentration minus AcceptorConcentration. Available when the incomplete ionization model is activated.

ActiveVertex.description: Used inside Print() in the RayTrace section to restrict ray path output to rays emitted from a specific active vertex only. The integer argument specifies the global index of the active vertex.

Adaptive.description: Related to adaptive algorithm control in Continuation simulations. In the Continuation section, Iadapt specifies a lower current limit for activating the adaptive load resistance algorithm. Below this current threshold, the simulation uses a fixed resistor instead of the adaptive one.

parameters.name: Iadapt
parameters.desc: Lower current limit (A) for adaptive algorithm activation. Default is 0.0.

Add2TotalDoping.description: In Traps(), includes the trap concentration in the acceptor, donor, and total doping concentrations used to compute mobility, lifetimes, and other quantities. With ChargedTraps option, only includes charged trap concentrations.

Add_F.description: Recognized extraction curve in the Solve Extraction option for small-signal AC analysis. Adds the derivative of the gate capacitance with respect to frequency to the output.

AdjustImpurityScattering.description: Adjusts impurity scattering parameters in mobility models to account for screening effects at high carrier concentrations. Referenced in the mobility model context for various scattering mechanisms.

Ads_Vg.description: Recognized extraction curve in the Solve Extraction option for quasistationary analysis. Computes the adsorption capacitance (gate charge vs. gate voltage).

Affinity.description: Uses a PMI (Physical Model Interface) model for electron affinity. Allows user-defined or parameter-file-based specification of the electron affinity for a region or material.

Agg_Vg.description: Recognized extraction curve in the Solve Extraction option for quasistationary analysis. Computes the aggregation capacitance (gate charge vs. gate voltage).

Algorithm.description: In Extrapolate(), selects the extrapolation algorithm used to obtain an initial guess for the next solution. Algorithm 1 and Algorithm 2 provide different extrapolation schemes.

contexts.Math: In Extrapolate(), selects the extrapolation algorithm for initial guess computation.
contexts.Physics: In Optics sections, selects the algorithm for optical solver computations such as raytracing or BPM.
contexts.Solve: In Solve sections, may reference algorithm selection for solver methods.

AllLNS.description: In NoisePlot{}, plots all used local noise sources. Outputs the complete set of local noise source data to the noise plot file.

AllLNVSD.description: In NoisePlot{}, plots all used local noise voltage spectral densities. Outputs the complete set of local noise voltage spectral density data to the noise plot file.

AllLNVXVSD.description: In NoisePlot{}, plots all used local noise voltage cross-correlation spectral densities. Outputs the complete set of local noise voltage cross-correlation spectral density data to the noise plot file.

AllowDuplicates.description: In Select() within ComputeFromSpectrum(), controls whether duplicate entries of the spectrum are ignored (off) or allowed (on). Default is off.

AllowLayerThickness.description: Specifies which material types account for LayerThickness extraction in the Math section. Default is Semiconductor.

parameters.name: Everywhere
parameters.desc: Account for LayerThickness in all materials.

parameters.name: Insulator
parameters.desc: Account for LayerThickness in insulators only.

parameters.name: Metal
parameters.desc: Account for LayerThickness in metals only.

parameters.name: Semiconductor
parameters.desc: Account for LayerThickness in semiconductors only (default).

Alpha.description: Ionization coefficient (alpha) for electrons or holes in avalanche generation models. Represents the number of electron-hole pairs generated per unit distance by a carrier traveling through a high electric field. The PMI allows user-defined alpha calculations.

contexts.Physics: Ionization coefficient in avalanche generation, used in Recombination(Avalanche()) or PMI context.
contexts.Plot: Scalar plot dataset: eAlphaAvalanche (electron) or hAlphaAvalanche (hole), in units of cm^-1.

AlphaCharge.description: Scalar plot dataset representing the time-integrated alpha particle charge density, i.e., the total charge deposited by alpha particles integrated over all time. Units: cm^-3.

AlphaChargeDensity.description: Scalar plot dataset representing the charge density generated by alpha particles at each vertex. Units: cm^-3.

AlphaGeneration.description: Scalar plot dataset representing the instant generation rate G(t) due to alpha particles at each vertex. Units: cm^-3 s^-1.

AlphaParticle.signature: AlphaParticle( Direction=<vector> Energy=<float> Location=<vector> StartPoint=<vector> Time=<float> )
AlphaParticle.description: Activates the alpha particle carrier generation model. Specifies particle energy, entry point, direction of motion, and timing. Only usable in transient simulations.

parameters.name: Direction
parameters.desc: Direction of motion of the alpha particle.

parameters.name: Energy
parameters.desc: Energy of the alpha particle in eV.

parameters.name: Location
parameters.desc: Point where the alpha particle enters the device (bidirectional track) in um.

parameters.name: StartPoint
parameters.desc: Point where the alpha particle enters the device (one-directional track) in um.

parameters.name: Time
parameters.desc: Time at which the charge generation peaks in s.

Amplitude.description: Amplitude parameter used in various RandomizedVariation() sub-models to specify the magnitude of random fluctuations. The meaning and units depend on the specific sub-model context.

parameters.name: Amplitude
parameters.desc: Amplitude value; units vary by sub-model (eV for workfunction/band edge, um for geometry, dimensionless for dielectric constant).

contexts.Physics: Amplitude parameter in various RandomizedVariation() sub-sections (Workfunction, BandEdge, Epsilon, Geometric, Doping) specifying the magnitude of random fluctuations.
contexts.Math: In OpticalTurningPoints(), MinAmplitude specifies the signal amplitude threshold for defining time points.

Amplitude_Abs.description: In DopingVariation() within DeterministicVariation(), specifies the vectorial amplitude for absolute gradient specification of doping variation.

parameters.name: Amplitude_Abs
parameters.desc: Vectorial amplitude for absolute gradient specification in um.

Amplitude_Chi.description: In BandEdge() within RandomizedVariation(), specifies the amplitude of electron affinity (chi) variation for exponential and Gaussian correlation. Units: eV.

parameters.name: Amplitude_Chi
parameters.desc: Amplitude of affinity variation in eV.

Amplitude_Eg.description: In BandEdge() within RandomizedVariation(), specifies the amplitude of bandgap variation for exponential and Gaussian correlation. Units: eV.

parameters.name: Amplitude_Eg
parameters.desc: Amplitude of bandgap variation in eV.

Amplitude_Iso.description: Isotropic amplitude for displacement in geometric and doping variation models within RandomizedVariation() or DeterministicVariation(). Units: um.

parameters.name: Amplitude_Iso
parameters.desc: Isotropic amplitude for displacement in um.

Analytic.description: In Math section Extrapolate context, selects analytic expression for certain computations. In Physics, may refer to analytic models or approximations for thermoelectric power (AnalyticTEP) or carrier distribution functions.

contexts.Math: Flag for analytic computation methods in various Math subsections.
contexts.Physics: Used in TEPower(Analytic) to select analytic thermoelectric power model.

AnalyticLTFit.description: Enables analytic fit for strained effective masses and density-of-states calculations in the context of strained layer simulations.

AnalyticTEP.description: Uses an analytic expression for the thermoelectric power (TEP) calculation instead of the default numerical approach. Available as a Physics section keyword and within TEPower().

Angular.description: In Optics sections, specifies angular discretization parameters for optical solvers. For example, in TMM(Scattering(Angular Discretization=<int>)), sets the angular discretization of the interval used in the scattering solver.

parameters.name: Phi
parameters.desc: Range of phi angle for the angular sensor.

parameters.name: Theta
parameters.desc: Range of theta angle for the angular sensor.

AngularDiscretization.description: In TMM() Scattering(), specifies the angular discretization of the interval [-pi/2, pi/2] used in the iterative scattering solver. Default is 91.

parameters.name: AngularDiscretization
parameters.desc: Number of angular discretization points. Default 91.

Aniso.description: Activates anisotropic physical models. Supports anisotropic mobility (electrons/holes), avalanche generation, quantum potential (density gradient), metal resistivity, permittivity, and thermal conductivity. The anisotropy direction can be specified explicitly or by crystal/system coordinates.

AnisoAxes.description: In eQuantumPotential() and hQuantumPotential(), specifies two directions of anisotropy for the density gradient quantum correction model. Can use crystal/system coordinates or explicit vector definitions.

AnisoSG.description: In the Math section, activates the anisotropic Scharfetter-Gummel (AnisoSG) discretization scheme for anisotropic models. Provides improved numerical stability when simulating anisotropic mobility, thermal conductivity, or permittivity. Related parameters include AnisoSG_DerivativeMinDen and AnisoSG_MinDen.

AnisoSG_DerivativeMinDen.description: Lower limit of carrier densities for the anisotropic Scharfetter-Gummel (AnisoSG) approximation. Corresponding derivatives are switched off if the node carrier density is below this value. Used to mitigate convergence problems caused by unstable computation of the Bernoulli function argument at very small carrier concentrations.

AnisoSG_MinDen.description: Stabilization parameter for the computation of x* (the Bernoulli function argument) in the anisotropic Scharfetter-Gummel (AnisoSG) approximation. Helps stabilize convergence when carrier concentrations are very small.

Anisotropic.description: Keyword used within Aniso() in the Physics section to specify anisotropic material properties. Selects which physical models use anisotropic formulations, including mobility, avalanche generation, permittivity, thermal conductivity, metal resistivity, and quantum potential.

AntimonyActiveConcentration.description: Scalar dataset representing the electrically active antimony (Sb) dopant concentration at each vertex. Related to the incomplete ionization model where not all dopants are ionized.

AntimonyConcentration.description: Scalar dataset representing the total antimony (Sb) dopant concentration at each vertex, including both ionized and non-ionized fractions.

AntimonyPlusConcentration.description: Scalar dataset representing the ionized antimony (Sb+) donor concentration at each vertex. Relevant when using the incomplete ionization model (Chapter 13).

ApplyToMobilityComponents.description: Option within the piezoresistance isotropic Factor mobility model that allows the mobility enhancement factor to be applied to individual mobility components (such as doping-dependent, high-field saturation, and surface mobility components) rather than only to the total mobility. This provides more physically accurate stress-dependent mobility modeling.

Area.description: Specifies the area of a raytracing window, excitation source, or sensor in optical simulations. Used in RayDistribution, UserWindow, FarField, and Sensor sections within Physics.

contexts.Physics/Optics/RayTrace/RayDistribution: Area of the ray distribution window for optical excitation.
contexts.Physics/Optics/RayTrace/FarField: Area of the far-field observation sphere.

AreaFactor.description: Multiplier for electrode current. Used to scale the computed current to account for device geometry factors such as width or repetition in a layout. Default is 1.

contexts.Electrode: Multiplier for electrode current to account for device width or layout repetition.
contexts.Physics: Multiplier for current and heat flux densities at electrodes and thermodes.
contexts.IFM: Used in statistical impedance field method variations.

Arora.description: Arora doping-dependent mobility model. An analytical model that computes bulk mobility degradation as a function of total doping concentration. The default doping-dependent model for GaAs and some other III-V materials. For silicon, the default is Masetti.

Arsenic.description: Selects arsenic-specific parameters for the Philips unified mobility (PhuMob) model. When specified, the model uses parameters calibrated for arsenic-doped material rather than the default phosphorus parameters.

ArsenicActiveConcentration.description: Scalar dataset representing the electrically active arsenic (As) dopant concentration at each vertex.

ArsenicConcentration.description: Scalar dataset representing the total arsenic (As) dopant concentration at each vertex, including both ionized and non-ionized fractions.

ArsenicPlusConcentration.description: Scalar dataset representing the ionized arsenic (As+) donor concentration at each vertex. Relevant when using the incomplete ionization model.

AsIs.description: Keeps the coordinate system of the input TDR grid file as-is at runtime without any transformation. When AsIs is specified, the output TDR file uses the same coordinate system as the input.

Ascii.description: Selects ASCII format for LightTools ray data file output. Used in LED raytracing simulations when interfacing with LightTools for external optical simulation.

AtInsulatorInterface.description: Restricts metal workfunction randomization to metal-insulator interface vertices only. When specified within MetalWorkfunction(Randomize()), the random workfunction values are applied only at vertices where the metal contact touches an insulator, leaving metal-semiconductor interface vertices unaffected.

Auger.description: Enables Auger recombination model within Recombination(). Auger recombination is a three-carrier process dominant at high carrier concentrations. The optional (WithGeneration) qualifier also includes the Auger generation counterpart.

AugerRecombination.description: Scalar dataset representing the Auger recombination rate (RA) at each vertex. This is the total Auger recombination rate combining electron and hole contributions.

Auto.description: Automatic mode keyword used in various sections to let Sentaurus Device determine an appropriate value automatically. Common usages include GainPlot Range (energy range for gain plot) and other parameter selections.

contexts.GainPlot: Automatically find the energy range for the gain plot.
contexts.Math: Used as option in various Math section parameters.

AutoCNPMinStepFactor.description: Multiplier of MinStep for the automatic activation of CNormPrint. When the step size during a Quasistationary or Transient solve is less than AutoCNPMinStepFactor times MinStep, CNormPrint is automatically activated to monitor convergence.

AutoDetect.description: Automatically detects the width of quantum wells in LED simulations. The quantum-well region must be specified by the keyword Active. When used, Sentaurus Device determines the QW extension without manual width specification.

AutoNPMinStepFactor.description: Multiplier of MinStep for the automatic activation of NewtonPlot. When the step size during a Quasistationary or Transient solve is less than AutoNPMinStepFactor times MinStep, NewtonPlot is automatically activated to write convergence data.

AutoOrientation.description: Specifies a list of Miller indices for surface orientations supported by the auto-orientation framework. Models that support AutoOrientation (such as HighFieldSaturation, IALMob, Lombardi, piezoresistance Factor models) will switch between parameter sets based on the surface orientation of the nearest interface.

contexts.Math: Global declaration of supported surface orientations for auto-orientation.
contexts.Physics/Mobility/HighFieldSaturation: Use parameter set based on nearest interface orientation.
contexts.Physics/Mobility/IALMob: Use IALMob parameter set based on nearest interface orientation.
contexts.Physics/Mobility/Lombardi: Use Lombardi parameter set based on nearest interface orientation.
contexts.Physics/Piezo/Mobility/Factor: Use piezoresistance Factor parameter set based on nearest interface orientation.

AutoOrientationSmoothing.description: Scalar dataset that shows the vertices where auto-orientation smoothing is active and the dominant orientation at those vertices. Plotted as a dimensionless quantity. Available when AutoOrientationSmoothingDistance is nonzero in the Math section.

AutoOrientationSmoothingDistance.description: Sets the auto-orientation smoothing distance in micrometers. When nonzero, enables smooth transitions between different parameter sets in the auto-orientation framework instead of abrupt switching. The smoothing distance is an approximate measure of the transition zone width. Specifying a negative value uses the average interface vertex spacing.

AutoPeak.description: Automatically determines the LED wavelength at the peak of the spontaneous emission rate spectrum using a robust multisection search algorithm. The peak energy is then translated into the corresponding wavelength.

AutoPeakPower.description: Automatically determines the LED wavelength at the peak of the spontaneous emission power spectrum (rate times energy versus energy curve). Uses the same multisection search algorithm as AutoPeak but applied to the power spectrum instead of the rate spectrum.

AutoPopulate.description: Automatically populates rays within the excitation shape for optical raytracing. The number of rays is determined by the NumberOfRays parameter. This mode distributes rays uniformly within the defined excitation window.

Autodetect.description: Case-insensitive alias for AutoDetect. Automatically detects the width of quantum wells in LED simulations when the quantum-well region is specified by the Active keyword.

AutomaticCircuitContact.description: In the Math section, enables the Poisson equation to cover the circuit in mixed-mode simulations. When enabled, the Poisson solver includes circuit node equations. Use NoAutomaticCircuitContact to disable.

AutomaticUpdate.description: In the OpticalGeneration section, controls whether optical generation is automatically recomputed when quantities on which it depends are not up-to-date. Enabled by default; use -AutomaticUpdate to switch off.

AvaHomotopy.description: In the Math section, activates the avalanche homotopy continuation method for improving Newton convergence in breakdown simulations. Gradually introduces avalanche generation terms to help the solver converge at high bias near breakdown conditions.

AvalDensGradQF.description: In the Math section, activates an alternative driving force for avalanche generation that uses the density-gradient quasi-Fermi approximation (J = -qn*mu*n*grad(Phi_n)) instead of the default Scharfetter-Gummel discretization. Improves convergence and stability for power-device applications.

AvalDerivatives.description: In the Math section, enables computation of analytic derivatives of avalanche generation with respect to solution variables. Enabled by default; use -AvalDerivatives to disable.

AvalFlatElementExclusion.description: In the Math section, excludes nearly flat mesh elements from contributing to avalanche generation calculations. Elements with all angles below the specified value (0-90 degrees) or above 180-minus-value are excluded. Useful for 3D breakdown simulations where flat elements cause numerical singularity and premature breakdown.

parameters.name: AvalFlatElementExclusion
parameters.desc: Angle threshold in degrees (0-90). Default: 0.0. Must not exceed 1-2 degrees.

AvalFlatElementMax.description: In the Plot section, outputs the maximum volume angle for each element. Used to diagnose and locate nearly flat elements that may cause problems in avalanche generation calculations.

AvalFlatElementMin.description: In the Plot section, outputs the minimum volume angle for each element. Used to diagnose and locate nearly flat elements that may cause problems in avalanche generation calculations.

AvalPostProcessing.description: In the Math section, prevents impact ionization-generated carriers from being included self-consistently in the device equations. Ionization integrals can still be calculated when solving for carriers, but retains the benefits of approximate breakdown analysis (faster simulations, fewer convergence issues).

Avalanche.description: In the Physics Recombination section, enables impact ionization (avalanche generation). Multiple models are available: vanOverstraeten (default), Okuto, Lackner, UniBo, UniBo2, Hatakeyama, or a PMI model. Driving force options include GradQuasiFermi (default), ElectricField, Eparallel, and CarrierTempDrive.

parameters.name: BandgapDependence
parameters.desc: Include bandgap dependency in avalanche generation coefficient b.

parameters.name: CarrierTempDrive
parameters.desc: Use carrier temperature as driving force (default for hydrodynamic).

parameters.name: ElectricField
parameters.desc: Use plain electric field driving force.

parameters.name: Eparallel
parameters.desc: Use current-parallel electric field driving force.

parameters.name: GradQuasiFermi
parameters.desc: Use gradient of quasi-Fermi potential driving force (default).

parameters.name: Hatakeyama
parameters.desc: Use Hatakeyama avalanche model.

parameters.name: Lackner
parameters.desc: Use Lackner avalanche model.

parameters.name: Okuto
parameters.desc: Use Okuto-Crowell avalanche model.

parameters.name: UniBo
parameters.desc: Use University of Bologna impact ionization model.

parameters.name: UniBo2
parameters.desc: Use new University of Bologna impact ionization model.

parameters.name: vanOverstraeten
parameters.desc: Use van Overstraeten-de Man model (default).

parameters.name: <ident>
parameters.desc: Use PMI model <ident> for avalanche generation.

AvalancheGeneration.description: In the Plot section, outputs the total avalanche generation rate (sum of electron and hole contributions). Related plot quantities include eAvalanche, hAvalanche, eAlphaAvalanche, and hAlphaAvalanche.

AveDistanceFactor.description: In the Math section within MVMLDAcontrols, specifies a factor that controls the computation of an averaged distance from a vertex to the interface for the multivalley MLDA model. Used to improve numerical stability of the multivalley quantum correction.

parameters.name: AveDistanceFactor
parameters.desc: Averaging factor for MLDA distance computation. Default: 0.05.

Average.description: In the Math section, activates averaging algorithms for anisotropic model computations. Related options include AverageAniso (average-aniso approximation) and AverageBoxMethod (element-oriented box method for integration).

AverageBoxMethod.description: In the Math section, enables the element-oriented element intersection box method algorithm for computing control volumes and integration coefficients. When disabled, the quadrilateral box method algorithm is used instead. Enabled by default.

AverageGrainSize.description: Specifies the approximate average grain size in micrometers. Used in multiple contexts: (1) In GrainFieldGenerator() for creating grain fields for statistical variation simulations. (2) In MetalWorkfunction Randomize() for metal grain size in workfunction randomization. (3) In RandomField() as correlation length vector.

parameters.name: AverageGrainSize
parameters.desc: Average grain size in um.

parameters.name: AverageGrainSizeVector
parameters.desc: Vector (ax, ay, az) allowing different grain sizes along different directions.

contexts.GrainFieldGenerator: Average grain size for Voronoi grain field generation
contexts.MetalWorkfunction/Randomize: Average metal grain size for workfunction randomization
contexts.RandomField: Average grain size along main axes for spatial correlations

AxisAligned.description: In the Optics Farfield Sensor section, specifies that only opposing corners of a 3D rectangle sensor are required. Sentaurus Device automatically computes the other two corners from the axis-aligned constraint.

AxisAligned2d.description: Specifies axis-aligned geometry constraint for 2D mesh operations. Used in mesh generation and geometry processing to align mesh elements with the coordinate axes, simplifying mesh structure for planar device geometries.

AxisAligned3d.description: Specifies axis-aligned geometry constraint for 3D mesh operations. Used in mesh generation and geometry processing to align mesh elements with the coordinate axes, simplifying mesh structure for rectangular device geometries.

AxisAlignedNormals.description: In the Physics Piezo Model Mobility Factor section, selects an assignment method for the EffectiveStressModel where S22 is chosen based on the diagonal stress component corresponding to the direction of the nearest interface normal (largest component, excluding channel direction). The interface normal is treated as aligned along an axis.

BDEnergyIntervals.description: In the Physics Traps section for eHCSDegradation or hHCSDegradation models, specifies the number of energy intervals to use in the bond dispersion integral. Used by the HCS (hot-carrier-stress) degradation model for interface trap generation.

parameters.name: BDEnergyIntervals
parameters.desc: Number of energy intervals for bond dispersion integral. Default: 20.

BE.description: Backward Euler time integration method for transient simulations. A first-order implicit scheme that can be specified either in the Math section (changing the global default) or in the Transient statement options within the Solve section. The default transient method is TRBDF.

BGN.description: In the Physics section, enables bandgap narrowing (BGN) models. Also available as a PMI interface (PMI_BandGapNarrowing) for custom implementations. Standard models include Slotboom, Bennett-Wilson, and Schenk.

BM_AngleElements.description: In the Plot section, outputs the angle statistics for mesh elements. Part of the box method (BM) diagnostic data for non-Delaunay elements.

BM_AngleVertex.description: In the Plot section, outputs the angle statistics for mesh vertices. Part of the box method (BM) diagnostic data for non-Delaunay elements.

BM_CoeffIntersectionNonDelaunayElements.description: In the Plot section, outputs the box method coefficient for intersection of non-Delaunay elements. Used for mesh quality diagnostics.

BM_EdgesPerVertex.description: In the Plot section, outputs the number of edges per mesh vertex. Part of box method (BM) diagnostic data.

BM_ElementVolume.description: In the Plot section, outputs the volume of each mesh element. Part of box method (BM) diagnostic data for mesh quality analysis.

BM_ElementsPerVertex.description: In the Plot section, outputs the number of elements per mesh vertex. Part of box method (BM) diagnostic data.

BM_ElementsWithCommonObtuseFace.description: In the Plot section, outputs the count of elements that share a common obtuse face. Part of box method (BM) diagnostic data for non-Delaunay mesh analysis.

BM_ElementsWithObtuseFaceOnBoundaryDevic.description: In the Plot section, outputs the count of elements with obtuse faces on the device boundary. Part of box method (BM) diagnostic data for non-Delaunay mesh analysis.

BM_ExtendedPrecision.description: Uses long double precision to compute box method coefficients and control volumes, improving accuracy for mesh geometry calculations.

BM_IntersectionNonDelaunayElements.description: Scalar plot dataset on elements. Length of intersection edges of non-Delaunay elements, in micrometers. Used for mesh quality diagnostics.

BM_VolumeIntersectionNonDelaunayElements.description: Scalar plot dataset on elements. Volume of intersection of non-Delaunay elements, in um^3. Used for mesh quality diagnostics.

BM_wCoeffIntersectionNonDelaunayElements.description: Scalar plot dataset on elements. Weighted coefficient for intersection of non-Delaunay elements (dimensionless). Used for mesh quality diagnostics.

BM_wElementsWithCommonObtuseFace.description: Scalar plot dataset on elements. Weighted count of elements sharing a common obtuse face (dimensionless). Used for mesh quality diagnostics.

BM_wElementsWithObtuseFaceOnBoundaryDevi.description: Scalar plot dataset on elements. Weighted count of elements with obtuse face on device boundary (dimensionless). Used for mesh quality diagnostics.

BM_wIntersectionNonDelaunayElements.description: Scalar plot dataset on elements. Weighted length of intersection edges of non-Delaunay elements, in micrometers. Used for mesh quality diagnostics.

BM_wVolumeIntersectionNonDelaunayElement.description: Scalar plot dataset on elements. Weighted volume of intersection of non-Delaunay elements, in um^3. Used for mesh quality diagnostics.

BPM.description: Beam propagation method for optical simulation of waveguide structures. Solves the paraxial wave equation along the propagation direction to compute the optical field distribution. Supports bidirectional propagation, PML boundary conditions, Gaussian and plane-wave excitation, and configurable reference refractive index.

BPMScalar.description: Scalar beam propagation method (BPM) optical solver. A simplified BPM variant used within Physics{Optics{OpticalSolver{...}}}. Uses the same Table 240 parameters as the full BPM but operates in scalar mode.

BTE.description: External Boltzmann transport equation solver. Interfaces with the Subband-BTE solver of Sentaurus Device QTX for quantum-corrected transport simulations. Supports extraction of effective mobility and current-voltage characteristics for specified contacts.

parameters.name: Carriers
parameters.desc: Carrier type: Electron or Hole.

parameters.name: ContactName
parameters.desc: Name of contact for current output.

parameters.name: DampingLength
parameters.desc: Distance (um) from volume enclosed by slices beyond which quantum correction is deactivated.

parameters.name: MaxMismatch
parameters.desc: Tolerance (um) in matching of slice meshes and device mesh.

parameters.name: Mobility
parameters.desc: Activate extraction of effective mobility by specifying carrier type (Electron|Hole).

parameters.name: NumberOfSlices
parameters.desc: Number of slices (minimum 2).

parameters.name: SBTECommandFile
parameters.desc: Command file to start the Subband-BTE solver automatically.

parameters.name: Volume
parameters.desc: Named volume in which to restrict quantum corrections and effective mobility.

BalMob.description: Ballistic mobility model for carrier transport. Accounts for ballistic transport effects in short-channel devices. Options include Fermi-Dirac correction, Frensley final mobility rule, kinetic energy formulation, explicit channel length, and temperature dependence.

parameters.name: Fermi
parameters.desc: Use Fermi-Dirac correction in the ballistic mobility.

parameters.name: Frensley
parameters.desc: Use Frensley rule for final mobility.

parameters.name: KineticEnergy
parameters.desc: Use kinetic ballistic mobility model.

parameters.name: Lch
parameters.desc: Explicit channel length in nm for simple ballistic mobility.

parameters.name: TempDep
parameters.desc: Use temperature dependency in simple ballistic mobility.

Band2Band.description: Band-to-band tunneling model, specified within Recombination() or eBarrierTunneling()/hBarrierTunneling(). Options control the parallel momentum integral treatment: None disables tunneling, Full uses the consistent integral, Simple uses a simplified form, and UpsideDown uses a nonphysical variant.

Band2BandGeneration.description: Scalar plot dataset at vertices. Band-to-band tunneling generation rate (Gbb) from the dynamic nonlocal path band-to-band tunneling model. Unit: cm^-3 s^-1.

BandEdge.description: In RandomizedVariation(), activates band edge variations for statistical impedance field method (sIFM) simulations. Randomizes both electron affinity and bandgap with configurable amplitude, correlation, and grain structure. Supports exponential, Gaussian, and grain-type correlation models.

parameters.name: Amplitude_Chi
parameters.desc: eV amplitude of affinity variation for exponential and Gaussian correlation.

parameters.name: Amplitude_Eg
parameters.desc: eV amplitude of bandgap variation for exponential and Gaussian correlation.

parameters.name: Chi2Eg
parameters.desc: Correlation between affinity and band gap (default 0).

parameters.name: ChiComponent
parameters.desc: Named random field component for affinity randomization (0-1).

parameters.name: EgComponent
parameters.desc: Named random field component for bandgap randomization (0-1, default 1).

parameters.name: GrainChi
parameters.desc: eV grain affinity values.

parameters.name: GrainEg
parameters.desc: eV grain bandgap values.

parameters.name: GrainProbability
parameters.desc: Probability for grain types.

parameters.name: Volume
parameters.desc: Named volume in which to activate the variation.

BandEdgeFluctuations.description: In Noise(), activates band edge fluctuations for noise analysis. Uses the same parameters as BandEdge() in RandomizedVariation() with spatial restriction options (SpaceMid, SpaceSig, SpatialShape). Models random variations in band edge energies as a noise source.

BandEdgeShift.description: Computes band-edge shift due to various physical effects. The electron variant eBandEdgeShift and hole variant hBandEdgeShift use PMI models to compute conduction and valence band-edge shifts respectively.

BandGap.description: Intrinsic band gap energy (Eg) at each vertex, computed by the bandgap model. Available as a plot dataset (unit: eV). Configured through EffectiveIntrinsicDensity() which controls the bandgap and bandgap narrowing models such as Slotboom, OldSlotboom, and Bennett-Wilson.

BandStructure.description: In the File section, specifies the base name for plotting local band structure data in LED simulations. Output files use this base name appended with vertex coordinates and file extensions (kpbandstruc, kpeigenfunc). NOTE: This is a case variant of Bandstructure found in audit; both map to the same File section keyword.

parameters.name: Bandstructure
parameters.desc: Base name for band structure output files.

Bandgap.description: Alias for BandGap. Scalar plot dataset at vertices showing the intrinsic band gap energy Eg (unit: eV). Plot name is mapped to BandGap in the scalar data table.

BandgapDependence.description: In Avalanche(), includes a dependency on the energy bandgap in the avalanche generation models. The ionization coefficients become dependent on the local bandgap value, which affects breakdown voltage predictions.

BandgapNarrowing.description: Selects the bandgap narrowing (BGN) model used in EffectiveIntrinsicDensity. Supported models include Slotboom, OldSlotboom, BennettWilson (default), delAlamo, JainRoulston, and TableBGN. Use NoBandGapNarrowing to disable.

contexts.Plot: Scalar plot dataset at vertices showing BGN value Ebgn (eV)

Bandstructure.description: Specifies the crystal structure type for band structure calculations in LED simulations. For example, CrystalType=Wurtzite activates the simplified band structure model for wurtzite crystals, computing band-edge energies and effective masses for heavy hole, light hole, and crystal-field split-off hole bands. Used within the LED section.

BandstructurePlot.description: Plots local band structure data in an LED simulation. Output files use the base name specified in File { Bandstructure=... } and are appended with vertex coordinates and file extensions.

Barrier.description: Configures barrier-related physics models at interfaces. Used in eBarrierTunneling/hBarrierTunneling for nonlocal tunneling with options like BarrierLowering, Schroedinger-based tunneling probabilities, TwoBand dispersion, and Transmission coefficients. Also used in SingletExciton for specifying barrier type (CondBand/ValBand) at heterointerfaces.

BarrierLowering.description: In eBarrierTunneling()/hBarrierTunneling(), applies a position-dependent barrier lowering for the tunneling barrier. Used to model image-force-induced barrier reduction at Schottky contacts and heterointerfaces.

BarrierTunneling.description: Specifies parameters for nonlocal tunneling at interfaces, contacts, and junctions. Defines tunneling effective mass (mt) and other fit parameters. Can be applied per material or to named nonlocal meshes.

BarrierTunnelingEnhan.description: In Traps() for the Degradation model, specifies barrier tunneling enhancement parameters. Four float values define the enhancement factors for the depassivation constant: deltaBTe, rhoBTe, deltaBTh, and rhoBTh.

parameters.name: BarrierTunnelingEnhan
parameters.desc: Four enhancement parameters: (deltaBTe, rhoBTe, deltaBTh, rhoBTh). Default (1 1 1 1).

BarrierType.description: In SingletExciton() at heterointerfaces, selects the barrier type for singlet exciton flux injection. Options: Bandgap (default, uses bandgap difference), CondBand (conduction band-edge difference), ValBand (valence band-edge difference).

BehavSrcNumericDeriv.description: In the Math section, computes the numeric Jacobian for behavioral sources. The float parameter controls the step size for numeric differentiation.

parameters.name: BehavSrcNumericDeriv
parameters.desc: Step size for numeric Jacobian computation of behavioral sources.

BennettWilson.description: Bennett-Wilson bandgap narrowing model for effective intrinsic carrier density calculation. Uses a logarithmic dependence on total doping concentration with reference energy E_ref and reference doping N_ref parameters. This is the default BGN model in Sentaurus Device. Specified within EffectiveIntrinsicDensity() in the Physics section.

Bias.description: General concept referring to applied voltage or electric field conditions in device simulation. Appears in multiple contexts including ferroelectrics/ferromagnetics (exchange bias), degradation models (bias-dependent reaction enhancement), and thermal simulations. Not a standalone command keyword.

contexts.Ferroelectrics: Exchange bias parameter I_bias for ferromagnetic/antiferromagnetic interface PMI models
contexts.Degradation: Bias-dependent depassivation rate in trap degradation model (PowerEnhan)

Bidirectional.description: In Optics(OpticalSolver(BPMScalar(Bidirectional()))), activates the bidirectional beam propagation method (BPM). Performs both forward and backward passes iteratively until convergence. Controls the maximum number of forward/backward iterations and the relative error break criterion.

Bimolecular.description: Activates bimolecular recombination in organic semiconductor simulations. Within SingletExciton, switches on bimolecular recombination in both the continuity and singlet exciton equations, acting as a singlet exciton generation term through electron-hole recombination. The bimolecular recombination rate R_bimolec = B * n * p serves as an exciton source.

contexts.SingletExciton: Recombination sub-keyword for singlet exciton equation in OLED simulations

Binary.description: Binary output format option for LightTools ray data files in LED simulations. Used within OutputLightToolsRays() and OutputLightToolsFarfieldRays() in the LED(Optics(RayTrace())) section. Specifies that ray data should be written in binary format rather than ASCII.

Blocked.description: Uses the Blocked solver for small-signal AC analysis. The Blocked method employs block decomposition to solve the AC-coupled system, which can be more efficient for certain device configurations.

Boltzmann.description: Boltzmann carrier statistics model, the default statistics mode in Sentaurus Device. Assumes non-degenerate carrier concentrations where Maxwell-Boltzmann distribution applies. Used in contrast to Fermi-Dirac statistics. Also appears as a conceptual term in thermoelectric power calculations, mobility models, and density-of-states references.

BondConc.description: Specifies the total silicon dangling bond concentration (N) in cm^-d, representing the maximum concentration of interface traps that can be generated by the degradation model. Used within Physics{Traps(Degradation)} at a MaterialInterface. The degradation process increases the initial trap concentration up to BondConc. Can be combined with BondConcSFactor for spatial dependency.

parameters.name: BondConc
parameters.desc: Total silicon dangling bond concentration N in cm^-d (d=2 for interface, d=3 for bulk)

BondConcSFactor.description: Specifies a spatially dependent bond concentration for the trap degradation model. Can reference a dataset name (string) for spatial distribution, or a PMI space factor model (ident) to compute the distribution. If specified without BondConc, values are used directly. If both are specified, BondConcSFactor values are normalized by the maximum and multiplied by BondConc.

parameters.name: BondConcSFactor
parameters.desc: Dataset name for spatial distribution of bond concentration, or PMI model identifier

BondDispersion.description: Activates bond dispersion in the hot-carrier stress (HCS) degradation model for electrons or holes. Accounts for energy dispersion of the Si-H bond breaking energy in the bond dispersion integral. Enabled by default; use -BondDispersion to disable. The number of energy intervals for the integral is controlled by BDEnergyIntervals (default: 20).

parameters.name: BDEnergyIntervals
parameters.desc: Number of energy intervals in the bond dispersion integral (default: 20)

BoronActiveConcentration.description: Boron active (electrically active, substitutional) doping concentration dataset available as a plot quantity in cm^-3. Read from the Grid file if present; otherwise, the chemical BoronConcentration is used. Used in incomplete ionization model specification via IncompleteIonization(Dopants="BoronActiveConcentration").

BoronConcentration.description: Boron chemical doping concentration dataset available as a plot quantity in cm^-3 (B). Represents the total boron concentration from process simulation. Linked to BoronActiveConcentration through the active field in datexcodes.txt. If BoronActiveConcentration is not in the Grid file, BoronConcentration is used as the active concentration.

BoronMinusConcentration.description: Ionized (negatively charged) boron acceptor concentration dataset available as a plot quantity in cm^-3 (B-). Relevant when incomplete ionization is activated, showing the concentration of boron atoms that have accepted an electron. Linked to BoronActiveConcentration through the ionized field in datexcodes.txt.

Both.description: Qualifier keyword in various PMI (Physical Model Interface) contexts, indicating that a model, function, or evaluation applies to both electrons and holes. For example, in Jacobian matrix contributions, thread-local storage scope, or nonlocal interface support. Not a standalone command.

contexts.PMI: Qualifier for models applying to both carrier types in Jacobian, runtime support, and interface evaluations

Bottom.description: Specifies the bottom position of a medium relative to the extracted layer structure in optical simulations (TMM), or specifies a bottom thermal boundary condition. In Medium(), sets the medium location with respect to the extracted optical layer stack. In thermal boundary conditions, identifies the bottom surface for heat flow specification.

contexts.Medium: Location qualifier for TMM layer stack positioning

Boundary.description: Configures boundary conditions in various physics contexts. In BPM, specifies PML (perfectly matched layer) absorbing boundary conditions with grid nodes, stretching parameters, and order. In optics, sets reflection/transmission properties. In raytracing, defines special contact boundary conditions for ray behavior.

BoundaryCondition.description: Specifies the boundary condition for the density gradient quantum potential model at semiconductor-insulator interfaces. Possible values are Dirichlet (homogeneous), Neumann (homogeneous), and HomogeneousNeumann (at region interfaces only). Overrides the default nonhomogeneous boundary condition. Used in electrode-specific or metal-interface-specific Physics sections.

parameters.name: BoundaryCondition
parameters.desc: Boundary condition type: Dirichlet, Neumann, or HomogeneousNeumann

Box.signature: Box { MinX MaxX MinY MaxY MinZ MaxZ }
Box.description: Defines a rectangular box region used for spatial restrictions in various math and physics settings, such as limiting quantum potential corrections or density gradient models to specific areas.

BoxCoefficientsFromFile.description: In the global Math section, disables extended-precision box method calculations and instead loads precomputed box method coefficients from a file. When specified, extended-precision calculation (BM_ExtendedPrecision) is switched off. Must be used together with BoxMeasureFromFile for consistency. Related to saving and restoring box method coefficients for reproducibility.

BoxMeasureFromFile.description: In the global Math section, loads precomputed box method control volumes (measures) from a file instead of computing them during simulation. Used together with BoxCoefficientsFromFile for saving and restoring the complete box method state. When specified, extended-precision box method calculations are disabled.

BoxMethodFromFile.description: In the global Math section, indicates that box method parameters should be loaded from a previously saved file rather than computed. Shorthand reference in the Math section table (Table 211). Related to the box method discretization scheme used for finite volume discretization of semiconductor equations.

BreakAtIonIntegral.description: Terminates the quasistationary simulation when the electron and hole ionization integrals per path reach a threshold. Without arguments, simulation stops when the largest integrals are >= 1 (Muller's breakdown criterion). With arguments, stops when <number> of integrals are >= <value>. Used for breakdown simulation stopping criteria.

parameters.name: <number>
parameters.desc: Number of ionization integrals that must exceed the threshold (optional)

parameters.name: <value>
parameters.desc: Threshold value for ionization integrals (optional, default 1.0)

BreakCriteria.description: Specifies conditions for prematurely terminating a simulation when monitored values exceed given limits. Useful for stopping breakdown simulations when current exceeds a threshold, or nonisothermal simulations when lattice temperature approaches the melting point. Can be specified globally in the Math section or as a sweep-specific option inside Quasistationary, Transient, or Continuation.

parameters.name: Voltage
parameters.desc: Contact voltage break criterion: Voltage(Contact="<name>" AbsVal=<float>)

parameters.name: Current
parameters.desc: Contact current break criterion: Current(Contact="<name>" MinVal=<float> MaxVal=<float>)

parameters.name: LatticeTemperature
parameters.desc: Lattice temperature criterion: LatticeTemperature(maxval=<float>)

parameters.name: CurrentDensity
parameters.desc: Current density criterion: CurrentDensity(maxval=<float>)

parameters.name: ElectricField
parameters.desc: Electric field criterion: ElectricField(maxval=<float>)

parameters.name: DevicePower
parameters.desc: Device power criterion: DevicePower(AbsVal=<float>)

BreakOnFailure.description: In the Math section, controls simulation behavior when Newton iterations fail to converge. When specified, the simulation terminates upon Newton convergence failure instead of attempting recovery strategies such as step reduction or damping. Used for debugging or when continuation is not desired.

BreakRequest.description: In the Math section, specifies that when the INT signal is sent to the Sentaurus Device process, the simulation should save a snapshot and then terminate the current solve statement. Used for interactive simulation control via POSIX signals. Alternative is Interrupt=PlotRequest which continues the simulation after saving the snapshot.

Breakdown.description: General concept referring to electrical breakdown in semiconductor devices. Appears in multiple contexts: breakdown simulation analysis (ComputeIonizationIntegrals), time-dependent dielectric breakdown (TDDB in KMC-MIM), breakdown path visualization, and avalanche generation models. Not a standalone command keyword but a physics concept referenced by several models.

contexts.Physics: Concept referenced in avalanche generation and breakdown probability models
contexts.Math: Breakdown simulation control via ComputeIonizationIntegrals and BreakAtIonIntegral
contexts.Plot: Breakdown path visualization datasets (eIonIntegral, hIonIntegral, MeanIonIntegral)

BreakdownProbability.description: Controls the computation of avalanche breakdown probability for quasistationary simulations. Computes the probability that carriers initiate impact ionization along their paths through the multiplication region. The multiplication region is defined by a threshold electric field (controlled by MinElectricField, default 1e5 V/cm). Requires AvalPostprocessing in Math section to exclude avalanche-generated carriers from device equations.

parameters.name: InterpolatedDiscretization
parameters.desc: Use interpolated discretization for better carrier path approximation

parameters.name: MinElectricField
parameters.desc: Threshold electric field for multiplication region definition in V/cm (default: 1e5)

Broadening.description: Activates gain-broadening models or nonlinear gain saturation for quantum well simulations. Supported models include Lorentzian, Landsberg, and Hyperbolic-Cosine broadening.

BroadeningIntegration.description: In the Math section, specifies the numeric integration method for gain broadening calculations in LED/laser simulations. Uses Gaussian quadrature to accelerate gain table computations including broadening effects.

BrooksHerring.description: Selects the Brooks-Herring carrier-carrier scattering model for mobility calculation. An alternative to the default Conwell-Weisskopf model. The model computes carrier-carrier scattering-limited mobility based on a screened Coulomb potential approach using Fermi integrals. Parameters c1 and c2 are material-dependent and accessible in the parameter file.

BuiltinPotential.description: Built-in potential (phi_0) at each vertex, computed from the doping profile at thermal equilibrium. Used to plot the contact potential difference across p-n junctions. This is a scalar data quantity available in the Plot and NonLocalPlot sections.

Bulk.description: Refers to the bulk (interior) semiconductor material properties in the Physical Model Interface (PMI) framework. Used in PMI model implementations to access bulk material parameters such as mobility, recombination rates, and band structure properties. In command files, typically appears within PMI-related Physics section specifications.

CB.description: Conduction band particle reservoir identifier used in multistate configuration (MSC) transition definitions. When a transition between states involves a change in charge, the conduction band acts as an electron reservoir. Specified as a Reservoirs argument in Transition statements within MSConfig sections.

CBBarrierLowering.description: Conduction band barrier lowering value (in eV) computed by the BarrierLowering tunneling model. Available for visualization in the NonLocalPlot section to show the image-force-induced reduction of the conduction band barrier at interfaces. For accurate results, should be plotted via NonLocalPlot rather than Plot due to nonlocal mesh resolution requirements.

CBEnergyWithLowering.description: Conduction band energy modified by the barrier lowering effect (in eV). Shows the effective conduction band edge after image-force barrier lowering is applied. Available in NonLocalPlot section for visualization of tunneling barrier profiles at interfaces.

CBRate.description: Specifies a PMI model for computing conduction band-related capture and emission rates for traps or multistate configuration transitions. Used as an option within Traps statements to define how electrons are captured and emitted at trap sites. The optional integer argument is passed to the PMI model constructor for model variant selection.

parameters.name: model_name
parameters.desc: Name of the PMI model for conduction band capture/emission rate computation

parameters.name: model_index
parameters.desc: Optional integer parameter passed to the PMI model constructor (default: 0)

CCVS_pset.description: Defines a current-controlled voltage source (CCVS) compact model instance in the System section. The output voltage is proportional to the current flowing through a specified sensing element. The CCVS follows the relation V = gain * I_sense.

parameters.name: gain
parameters.desc: Transimpedance gain [Ohm], voltage per unit sensing current

parameters.name: tc1
parameters.desc: First-order temperature coefficient for gain

parameters.name: tc2
parameters.desc: Second-order temperature coefficient for gain

parameters.name: ic
parameters.desc: Initial condition (voltage) for transient analysis

parameters.name: temp
parameters.desc: Instance operating temperature [C]

CDL.description: Enables the Coupled Defect Level (CDL) recombination model, which generalizes the single-level SRH recombination to two coupled defect levels. An important feature is the possibly increased field effect that may lead to large excess currents. Optionally accepts a PMI model name for lifetime computation. The recombination rate depends on two sets of SRH-like parameters (tau_n1, tau_p1, tau_n2, tau_p2) and a coupling parameter (TrapTrapRate).

parameters.name: pmi_model_name
parameters.desc: Optional PMI model name for CDL lifetime computation. If specified, overrides all other CDL keywords.

CDL1.description: Plots the first component R1 of the CDL (Coupled Defect Level) recombination rate, corresponding to the SRH-like recombination rate of the first defect level. Unit: cm^-3 s^-1.

CDL1Recombination.description: Scalar data name for the first defect level recombination rate R1 in the CDL model. Equivalent to the CDL1 plot keyword. Unit: cm^-3 s^-1.

CDL2.description: Plots the second component R2 of the CDL (Coupled Defect Level) recombination rate, corresponding to the SRH-like recombination rate of the second defect level. Unit: cm^-3 s^-1.

CDL2Recombination.description: Scalar data name for the second defect level recombination rate R2 in the CDL model. Equivalent to the CDL2 plot keyword. Unit: cm^-3 s^-1.

CDL3.description: Plots the net coupling term R - R1 - R2 of the CDL recombination model, which represents the coupling contribution between the two defect levels beyond the individual SRH-like rates. This term arises from the correlation between the two defect levels.

CDLRecombination.description: Scalar data name for the total CDL (Coupled Defect Level) recombination rate R, including both individual defect level contributions and the coupling term. Unit: cm^-3 s^-1.

CDLcRecombination.description: Scalar data name for the CDL coupling recombination rate R - R1 - R2, representing only the coupling contribution between the two defect levels. Unit: cm^-3 s^-1.

CDensityMin.description: Sets the minimum current density threshold (in A/cm^2) below which the parallel electric field driving force (Eparallel) is set to zero to avoid numerical noise. Default is 0. Used to prevent numerical instabilities in high-field mobility and avalanche driving force calculations when current density is very small.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Minimum current density threshold in A/cm^2 (default: 0.0)

CEModel.description: Specifies a physical model interface (PMI) model for computing multistate configuration transition rates. Used within Transition statements in MSConfig sections for degradation simulations.

parameters.name: model_name
parameters.desc: Name of the PMI model for transition rate computation

parameters.name: model_index
parameters.desc: Optional integer parameter passed to the PMI model constructor (default: 0)

CMIPath.description: Specifies the search path for compact model interface (CMI) files with extension .ccf and their corresponding shared object files (.so.arch). Files found are parsed and added to the System section of the command file. If the environment variable STROOT_Arch_OS_LIB is defined, the directory $STROOT_Arch_OS_LIB/sdevice is automatically added to the search path.

parameters.name: <string>
parameters.desc: Colon-separated directory search path for compact model files

CNTMob.description: Enables the Carbon Nanotube (CNT) mobility model for carrier transport. Uses a mean free path (MFP) approach with acoustic phonon and optical phonon scattering mechanisms for both intraband and interband scattering. Requires CNT bands to be defined in the parameter file with dosmodel=CNT. When combined with other low-field mobility models, Matthiessen's rule is applied.

parameters.name: Lch
parameters.desc: Explicit channel length in nm for CNT mobility computation

CNormPrint.description: Enables convergence monitoring by printing diagnostic information after each Newton step. For each equation solved, outputs the largest error, the vertex where it occurs, the vertex coordinates, and the current solution variable value. Has negligible performance impact. Can also be specified within HBCoupled for harmonic balance error printing. Automatically activated when step size decreases below a threshold (controlled by AutoCNPMinStepFactor).

contexts.HBCoupled: Print instance equation errors per Newton step in MDFT and SDFT harmonic balance modes

CRIModel.description: Specifies a user-defined complex refractive index (CRI) PMI model name for computing wavelength-, temperature-, and carrier-dependent optical properties. The named model is loaded from shared libraries and provides custom implementations of the complex refractive index computation. Used within the ComplexRefractiveIndex section of the Optics/Physics command.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.desc: Name of the user-defined CRI PMI model

Cap.description: Capacitance variable available in mixed-mode circuit simulations and LED radiation pattern calculations. In mixed-mode, represents the capacitance value from compact model evaluation. In LED optics, used for capacitance-related calculations in staggered 3D grid radiation pattern analysis.

Capacitor_pset.description: Declares a SPICE capacitor instance in the System section for mixed-mode simulations. Connects between two circuit nodes with a specified capacitance. The default parameter set Capacitor_pset provides standard SPICE capacitor parameters. Used for coupling capacitors, bypass capacitors, and charge storage elements in mixed-mode circuit simulations.

parameters.name: capacitance
parameters.desc: Capacitance value in farads (default: 1)

parameters.name: ic
parameters.desc: Initial voltage across the capacitor

parameters.name: tc1
parameters.desc: First-order temperature coefficient

parameters.name: tc2
parameters.desc: Second-order temperature coefficient

CarrierCarrierScattering.description: Enables carrier-carrier (electron-hole) scattering mobility degradation. Two models are available: Conwell-Weisskopf (default) and Brooks-Herring. The resulting mobility is combined with other mobility components via Matthiessen's rule: 1/mu = 1/mu_other + 1/mu_eh.

parameters.name: BrooksHerring
parameters.desc: Use the Brooks-Herring screening model for carrier-carrier scattering

parameters.name: ConwellWeisskopf
parameters.desc: Use the Conwell-Weisskopf model (default when no option specified)

CarrierDensityInAltermatt.description: Replaces carrier densities (n, p) with doping concentrations (N_D,0, N_A,0) in the Altermatt incomplete ionization model to improve numerical stability. The original Altermatt formulation is not recommended in Sentaurus Device, but remains available with this option. When specified without the minus sign prefix, activates the replacement.

CarrierDep.description: Activates carrier-density-dependent terms in the complex refractive index model. When specified with 'Imag', enables free carrier absorption (FCA) which modifies the extinction coefficient based on electron and hole densities. This affects quantum yield calculations by reducing the effective quantum yield according to the ratio of FCA to total absorption.

parameters.name: Imag
parameters.desc: Enable carrier-dependent imaginary part (extinction coefficient) for free carrier absorption

parameters.name: real
parameters.desc: Enable carrier-dependent real part of refractive index

CarrierPath.description: Specifies the base file name for saving carrier paths and spatially interpolated plot variables as 1D datasets in a separate TDR output file. Carrier paths are computed by the ComputeCarrierPath model and triggered when TDR files are saved. The output file uses the .tdr extension.

parameters.name: <string>
parameters.desc: Base file name for carrier path output (extension .tdr appended automatically)

CarrierTempDrive.description: Selects the carrier temperature as the driving force for high-field saturation mobility models. This is the default driving force for hydrodynamic simulations. Uses the carrier temperature gradient rather than electric field or quasi-Fermi potential gradient to determine the mobility reduction at high fields.

CarrierTempDriveBasic.description: Selects the basic temperature driving force model for high-field saturation mobility. This model uses a simplified carrier temperature dependence for the mobility driving force. Requires hydrodynamic simulations. When selected, the driving force is part of the actual mobility model and cannot be chosen independently.

CarrierTempDriveME.description: Selects the Meinerzhagen-Engl temperature driving force model for high-field saturation mobility. An alternative carrier-temperature-based driving force model. Requires hydrodynamic simulations. When selected, the driving force is part of the actual mobility model and cannot be chosen independently.

CarrierTempDrivePolynomial.description: Energy-dependent mobility model using the ratio of two irrational polynomials as a function of normalized carrier temperature (w = Tc/Tl). The high-field mobility is computed as mu_hf = mu * factor * (Numerator^Gn) / (Denominator^Gd), where the polynomial coefficients are specified in the HydroHighFieldDependence parameter file section. Used within HighFieldSaturation() for hydrodynamic simulations.

CarrierTempDriveSpline.description: Energy-dependent mobility model using spline interpolation as a function of normalized carrier temperature. The mobility is computed as mu(w) = mu_low * spline(w), where the spline function is defined by value pairs in the parameter file. An alternative to CarrierTempDrivePolynomial for hydrodynamic simulations.

Carriers.description: Specifies which carrier types (electrons and/or holes) to include in a quantum correction model. Used within ExternalBoltzmannSolver() and ExternalSchroedinger() to restrict quantum corrections to specific carrier types.

contexts.Physics: within ExternalBoltzmannSolver() or ExternalSchroedinger()

Cartesian.description: Specifies Cartesian coordinate system for raytracing output in LED simulations. This is the default coordinate system for the RayTrace() optical solver. The alternative is Cylindrical.

contexts.Physics: within LED(Optics(RayTrace()))

CaugheyThomas.description: Uses the Canali-Hänsch model (extended Caughey-Thomas) for high-field saturation mobility. A widely used empirical model for silicon carrier velocity saturation at high electric fields.

Cavity.description: Defines a cavity structure for LED optical simulations within the raytracing framework. Used in modeling resonant cavity LEDs (RCLEDs) where the cavity design enhances emission through resonance characteristics.

Center.description: Specifies the center of a spatial distribution. Used in Math section as SpaceMid parameter for trap spatial distributions, and in Physics section as Origin for illumination windows, far-field sensors, and geometric shapes (Cuboid, Sphere, Cylinder).

contexts.Math: as SpaceMid in spatial restriction (Table 327)
contexts.Physics: as Origin in Window/Farfield/Cuboid/Sphere/Cylinder

CenterGauss.description: Specifies the center position of a Gaussian excitation beam in BPM (Beam Propagation Method) optical simulations. The vector size is d-1 (one less than the spatial dimension). Used within Excitation() of the BPM optical solver.

parameters.name: <float>*2
parameters.desc: Center coordinates of the Gaussian beam in micrometers. Vector size is d-1 for the spatial dimensions transverse to the propagation direction.

Cgd_F.description: Gate-drain capacitance (Cgd) extracted as a function of frequency in AC-coupled small-signal analysis. Specified in the Extraction option of the ACCoupled statement within the Solve section.

Cgd_Vg.description: Gate-drain capacitance (Cgd) extracted as a function of gate voltage in quasistationary analysis. Specified in the Extraction option of the Quasistationary statement within the Solve section.

ChannelDirection.description: Specifies the channel direction for isotropic stress mobility factor models. Values 1, 2, or 3 correspond to x, y, and z axes respectively. Used within Factor(), eFactor(), or hFactor() in stress-dependent mobility models.

parameters.desc: Channel direction index: 1=x, 2=y, 3=z.

Charge.description: In the Math section, refers to charge-based error control or charge computation options. Used as a qualifier for quantities such as ErrRef(Charge) to set reference values for charge-related convergence criteria in Newton iterations.

ChargedTraps.description: Option within Traps() that includes charged trap concentration in the acceptor or donor doping concentrations used to compute mobility. Only traps with nonzero charge state affect the doping. Distinguished from Add2TotalDoping without arguments which includes all trap concentrations.

CheckRhsAfterUpdate.description: Forces Sentaurus Device to perform an additional check on the RHS (right-hand side) norm after the update error criteria is satisfied during Newton iterations. If the RHS norm can be made smaller, additional iterations are performed. Usually adds one or two extra iterations but can improve solution quality.

CheckTransientError.description: Activates time-step error control for transient simulations. When specified, Sentaurus Device controls the time step to limit discretization errors in addition to Newton convergence. Uses TransientDigits for relative error and TransientError/TransientErrRef for absolute error specifications.

CheckUndefinedModels.description: Disables the automatic checking procedure for undefined physical model parameters. By default, Sentaurus Device checks that required mobility models (ConstantMobility, DopingDependence) and DOS masses (eDOSmass, hDOSmass) are defined for all materials and insulators.

Chi2Eg.description: Correlation parameter between electron affinity (chi) and band gap (Eg) variations in random band edge fluctuation models. Dimensionless parameter with default 0. Specified in the BandEdgeFluctuations parameter set for both sIFM noise analysis and RandomizedVariation.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Correlation coefficient between affinity and band gap variations (dimensionless, default 0).

ChiComponent.description: Specifies which named random field component to use for electron affinity randomization in band edge variations. Default is 0. Used within BandEdge() in RandomizedVariation() to independently control affinity and band gap randomization using different random field components.

parameters.desc: Index of the random field component for affinity randomization (default 0).

ChordWeight.description: Specifies the weight of chord length for thickness extraction in thin-layer mobility models and width extraction in LED simulations. Used within ThinLayer() in Mobility() and WidthExtraction() in LED(). Controls the geometric distance computation at interfaces.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Weight factor for chord length (default 0).
