Circle.description: Defines a circular geometry for optical illumination windows or sensor configurations in raytracing simulations. Specifies the radius of the circular region.

Circuit.description: Circuit equation solved in mixed-mode simulations. When specified in Load, loads the circuit state from a previous simulation. In the equations table (Table 342), represents the compact circuit equations with a reference value of 0.0258 V and relative error tolerance of 1e-3.

CircuitNoise.description: Option for ACCoupled small-signal analysis that includes noise generated by SPICE circuit elements in the noise computation. SPICE circuit elements contribute only to autocorrelation noise; for cross-correlation noise they are treated as noiseless.

ClusterActive.description: Activates the clustering option for LED raytracing, which groups active vertices into clusters to reduce the total number of rays emitted. Improves raytracing performance for devices with many emission centers.

ClusterQuantity.description: Specifies the quantity used to determine the number of active vertex clusters in LED raytracing. Nodes clusters by vertex count, PlaneArea by projected area, and OpticalGridElement by optical grid elements. Used with NumberOfClusters to control clustering granularity.

ClusteringEverywhere.description: Uses the Philips unified mobility clustering formulas for all occurrences of acceptor (NA) and donor (ND) concentrations in the IALMob model, not just at interface points. This extends the impurity scattering model to the bulk region as well.

Collected.description: Specifies the name of the collected data file in the File section. The collected file (.coll) contains optical generation data collected during TMM or raytracing optical simulations.

CompactMemoryOption.description: Activates the compact memory model for LED raytracing, which reduces memory consumption at the cost of some functionality. Not compatible with the full photon-recycling model. Used within RayTrace() or RayTracing() in the Optics section.

CompatibleFEPolarization.description: Activates the multidimensional FEPolarization solver that simultaneously solves for all Cartesian components of the polarization vector field. Uses the previous formulation of the Ginzburg-Landau-Khalatnikov equation. Supports the FEPolarizationIP parameter for divergence-free polarization fields.

Complex.description: Complex refractive index model interface keyword. Used in PMI (Physical Model Interface) programming for defining user-defined complex refractive index models. Provides access to complex number utilities and refractive index data within PMI C++ runtime.

contexts.Physics: within PMI complex refractive index model interface

ComplexRefractiveIndex.description: Activates the complex refractive index (CRI) model for optical simulations. Controls carrier, gain, temperature, and wavelength dependencies of the refractive index (n) and extinction coefficient (k). Supports user-defined PMI models via CRIModel.

ComplexRefractiveIndexThreshold.description: In the OptBeam/LayerStackExtraction section, specifies a threshold value for merging grid elements into layers when using ElementWise extraction mode. Elements whose relative difference in refractive index or extinction coefficient (compared with the previous layer) is below this threshold are merged. Default: 0.

Composite.description: Refers to the Composite Method for mixed-mode optical-electrical co-simulation. An OpticalDevice section defines an optical device that is coupled with the electrical device simulation. The Composite Method enables self-consistent optical and electrical simulations, particularly for VCSEL, LED, and solar cell applications.

contexts.Physics: Specifies the optical solver type (Composite Method)
contexts.Solve: OpticalDevice declaration for composite method

ComputeAll.description: In ComputeIonizationIntegrals(), specifies that all semiconductor elements are taken into the initial element list for path search. Should be combined with another spatial restriction strategy (e.g., Window) to avoid excessive computation times.

ComputeAtMaxElectricField.description: In ComputeIonizationIntegrals(), specifies that the path search starts from the element(s) with the maximal electric field. Without delta, only one element is used. With delta=<float> (V/cm), all elements where the electric field exceeds (Emax - delta) are included. This is the default strategy.

parameters.name: delta
parameters.desc: Electric field tolerance in V/cm. All elements with E > Emax - delta are included. Default: 1e4 V/cm.

ComputeDopingConcentration.description: In the global Math section, forces recomputation of net doping and total doping from individual species concentrations, even when these datasets are already present in the Grid file. Useful when doping has been modified by physics models (e.g., traps with Add2TotalDoping).

ComputeFromMonochromaticSource.description: In the OpticalGeneration section, activates optical generation computation using a single wavelength specified in the Excitation section or as a ramping variable. Supports scaling factor for quasistationary simulations and time-dependency specification for transient simulations.

parameters.name: Scaling
parameters.desc: Scaling factor for optical generation. Default: 1. Applies to quasistationary simulations only.

parameters.name: TimeDependence
parameters.desc: Time-dependency specification for transient simulations (WaveTime, FromFile, etc.).

ComputeFromSpectrum.description: In the OpticalGeneration section, activates optical generation computation from an input illumination spectrum of wavelengths. Supports scaling, spectral data retention, forced recomputation, multidimensional spectrum file selection, and time-dependency specification.

parameters.name: Scaling
parameters.desc: Scaling factor for optical generation. Default: 1.

parameters.name: KeepSpectralData
parameters.desc: Keep spectral data for plotting or to avoid recomputation.

parameters.name: RefreshEveryTime
parameters.desc: Force recomputation of optical generation at every occasion.

parameters.name: Select
parameters.desc: Active parameters of multidimensional spectrum file.

parameters.name: TimeDependence
parameters.desc: Time-dependency specification for transient simulations.

ComputeGradQuasiFermiAtContacts.description: In the Math section, requests that the gradient of quasi-Fermi potential (Equation 365) is used as the driving force at all contact elements for avalanche breakdown analysis. This replaces the default parallel electric field driving force at contacts.

ComputeIonizationIntegrals.description: In the Math section, enables approximate breakdown analysis by computing electron and hole ionization integrals along geometric paths through the device. Controls initial element selection (ComputeAtMaxElectricField, ComputeAll, MinElectricField), path search options (Direction, PathReflection, PeriodicBC), and output options (SaveEIonIntegralPaths, SaveHIonIntegralPaths, WriteAll).

parameters.name: ComputeAll
parameters.desc: Include all semiconductor elements in initial element list.

parameters.name: ComputeAtMaxElectricField
parameters.desc: Start from element(s) with maximal electric field. Optional delta parameter.

parameters.name: MinElectricField
parameters.desc: Minimum electric field threshold (V/cm) for initial element selection.

parameters.name: Direction
parameters.desc: Electric field direction used for geometric path search.

parameters.name: PathReflection
parameters.desc: Path reflection options at device boundaries.

parameters.name: SaveEIonIntegralPaths
parameters.desc: Number of electron ionization integral paths to store.

parameters.name: SaveHIonIntegralPaths
parameters.desc: Number of hole ionization integral paths to store.

parameters.name: Window
parameters.desc: Geometric shape restriction (Cuboid, Sphere, Cylinder) for initial element list.

Conc.description: Normalization concentration parameter (unit: cm^-3) used in DopingVariation() within both DeterministicVariation() and RandomizedVariation() sections. Defines the reference doping concentration for computing relative doping variations.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Normalization concentration in cm^-3.

CondBand.description: In SingletExciton() > BarrierType(), uses the conduction band-edge difference as the barrier height at organic heterointerfaces. Alternative to Bandgap (default) and ValBand.

CondInsulator.description: In the Physics section, turns an insulator into a conductive insulator by solving the Fermi potential equation. Enables current flow through dielectric materials using a conductivity model. Requires adding CondInsulator to the Coupled equation list in the Solve section. The resistivity is specified in the Resistivity parameter set in the parameter file.

contexts.Solve: CondInsulator as equation name in Coupled equation list

Condition.description: In BreakCriteria{}, defines a Boolean Tcl expression as a break condition for stopping the simulation. In Select() for spectrum files, defines a Boolean expression to select a subset of a multidimensional spectrum.

ConductionBandEnergy.description: Scalar plot dataset available at vertex and element-vertex locations. Represents the conduction band edge energy EC (eV). Related PMI datasets include CBEnergywithLowering (barrier-lowered) and CBBarrierLowering (barrier lowering amount).

ConductionCurrentDensity.description: Vector plot dataset available at vertex locations. Represents the total conduction current density |Jn+Jp| in semiconductors (A/cm^2) or |JM| in metals. Does not include displacement current.

Conductivity.description: Refers to thermal conductivity (kappa) in various physics contexts. In the Physics section, specified via ThermalConductivity() with temperature-dependent, thin-layer, and composition-dependent models. In PMI, accessible through the C++ interface. In parameter files, defined as Resistivity parameters for metals.

contexts.IFM: Conductivity() controls metal conductivity variation in RandomizedVariation

ConductivityFluctuations.description: Activates random metal conductivity fluctuations as a noise source in the global Physics section. The string identifies a named volume specification where the fluctuations are active. Parameters (Amplitude, Lambda) are specified in the ConductivityFluctuations parameter set in the parameter file. Assumes Gaussian spatial correlations.

Conservative.description: In ReadFromFile() and FromFile() sections, selects conservative interpolation algorithm when loading optical generation profiles from a different grid. Guarantees that the integral of the interpolation quantity on the source grid equals that on the destination grid. Alternative: Simple (linear interpolation, default).

ConstRefPot.description: In the Math section, explicitly specifies the reference potential Phi_ref used in quasi-Fermi potential and energy band calculations. If not specified, Sentaurus Device computes Phi_ref from the vacuum level, preferring intrinsic Fermi levels of Si, GaAs, or the material with the smallest band gap.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Reference potential value in V.

Constant.description: Constant (temperature-independent) lattice heat capacity model within HeatCapacity(). When selected, the heat capacity is treated as a constant value rather than using the default temperature-dependent model.

ConstantCarrierGeneration.description: Defines a constant carrier generation rate for debugging or testing purposes. The value is specified in cm^-3 s^-1. Can be used as a Goal model parameter in quasistationary ramps.

ConstantMobility.description: Uses constant mobility values within Mobility() if neither PhuMob (Philips unified) nor DopingDependence is specified. Provides a simple constant mobility model as a fallback, typically used for initial debugging or when doping-dependent effects are negligible.

Contact.description: In the Solve section, Contact is an equation name that can be added to the Coupled equation list. It represents the contact equation solved at metal-semiconductor or conductive insulator-semiconductor interfaces. Default error reference is 0.0258 V with relative error tolerance of 1e-3. Also refers to contact-related parameters across multiple sections (Electrode, Physics, IFM).

contexts.Physics: Contact-related physics models (Schottky, eRecVelocity, etc.)

ContactName.description: In ExternalBoltzmannSolver(), specifies the name of the contact for which Sentaurus Device sends the current value to the Subband-BTE solver for outputting current-voltage characteristics.

parameters.name: name
parameters.desc: Name of the contact.

ContactSurfaceNormal.description: Vector plot dataset available at vertex locations. Represents the contact surface normal vector (dimensionless). Used in CurrentPlot Tcl formulas to compute current components normal to a contact surface.

Continuation.description: Enables automated tracing of arbitrarily shaped I-V curves using a dynamic load-line technique. Particularly useful for tracing multivalued I-V curves associated with breakdown, snapback, or latchup phenomena. The method adapts boundary conditions along the I-V curve by adjusting a variable load resistor attached to the continuation electrode, ensuring the load line remains orthogonal to the local tangent of the I-V curve.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.desc: Electrode name for continuation (prefix with <device>. for mixed mode)

parameters.name: InitialVstep
parameters.desc: Initial voltage-controlled step size (V)

parameters.name: MinVoltage
parameters.desc: Minimum voltage limit of the continuation window (V)

parameters.name: MaxVoltage
parameters.desc: Maximum voltage limit of the continuation window (V)

parameters.name: MinCurrent
parameters.desc: Minimum current limit of the continuation window (A)

parameters.name: MaxCurrent
parameters.desc: Maximum current limit of the continuation window (A)

Convergence.description: Refers to convergence control parameters in the Math section. Controls Newton solver convergence through error criteria (ErrRef, Error), number of iterations (Iterations), damping parameters (NotDamped, LineSearchDamping), and relative error control (RelErrControl). Affects all Coupled, Plugin, Quasistationary, and Transient solve commands.

ConversePiezoelectricField.description: Tensor plot dataset representing the converse piezoelectric field, computed from the tensor product of piezoelectric coefficients and electric field components. A dimensionless tensor available at vertex locations. Individual components (XX, XY, XZ, YY, YZ, ZZ) are also available as scalar datasets.

ConversePiezoelectricFieldXX.description: Scalar plot dataset representing the XX component of the converse piezoelectric field tensor at vertex locations (dimensionless). Computed from piezoelectric coefficients and electric field components.

ConversePiezoelectricFieldXY.description: Scalar plot dataset representing the XY component of the converse piezoelectric field tensor at vertex locations (dimensionless).

ConversePiezoelectricFieldXZ.description: Scalar plot data: XZ component of the converse piezoelectric field tensor. Available for output in the Plot section when piezoelectric models are active. The converse piezoelectric field arises from the inverse piezoelectric effect where applied stress generates an electric field.

ConversePiezoelectricFieldYY.description: Scalar plot data: YY component of the converse piezoelectric field tensor. Available for output in the Plot section when piezoelectric models are active.

ConversePiezoelectricFieldYZ.description: Scalar plot data: YZ component of the converse piezoelectric field tensor. Available for output in the Plot section when piezoelectric models are active.

ConversePiezoelectricFieldZZ.description: Scalar plot data: ZZ component of the converse piezoelectric field tensor. Available for output in the Plot section when piezoelectric models are active.

ConwellWeisskopf.description: Activates the Conwell-Weisskopf carrier-carrier scattering model for mobility computation. This is an alternative to the Brooks-Herring model. Both models account for the screening effect of free carriers on ionized impurity scattering, using different screening length approximations.

CoordinateSystem.description: Specifies the coordinate system used for explicit coordinates in the Sentaurus Device command file. Affects current plot statements, lattice parameters, and other features that use explicit coordinates. If the coordinate system of the device is incompatible, Sentaurus Device automatically transforms the device into the required coordinate system.

parameters.name: AsIs
parameters.desc: (Default) No transformation; device coordinates are compatible with explicit coordinates

parameters.name: DFISE
parameters.desc: In 2D: x across surface, y into device. In 3D: x,y span surface, z points up

parameters.name: UCS
parameters.desc: Unified coordinate system: x into device, y across surface (2D), z right-handed (3D)

Coordinates.description: Specifies the coordinates of left and right periodic boundary planes in the PeriodicBC section of Math. Also used as a general parameter name in various contexts (RayTrace, CurrentPlot, etc.) to specify coordinate values.

parameters.name: Coordinates
parameters.desc: Pair of float values (left, right) in um specifying the periodic boundary plane positions along the Direction axis

contexts.Math: PeriodicBC sub-option for periodic boundary condition coordinates

Corner1.description: Defines the first corner point of a geometric shape (rectangle, cuboid, sensor, or illumination window). Used in multiple contexts: Window/Cuboid, Sensor/Rectangle, Sensor/Line, and Window/Illumination to specify spatial boundaries.

parameters.name: Corner1
parameters.desc: First corner point coordinates (um)

contexts.Math: Window/Rectangle and Window/Cuboid for spatial restriction
contexts.Plot: Sensor/Rectangle and Sensor/Line for raytracing far-field collection

Corner2.description: Defines the second corner point (diagonally opposite to Corner1) of a geometric shape (rectangle, cuboid, sensor, or illumination window). Used in multiple contexts for specifying spatial boundaries.

parameters.name: Corner2
parameters.desc: Second corner point coordinates (um), diagonally opposite to Corner1

contexts.Math: Window/Rectangle and Window/Cuboid for spatial restriction
contexts.Plot: Sensor/Rectangle and Sensor/Line for raytracing far-field collection

Corner3.description: Defines the third corner point of a rectangle sensor in the unified raytracing far field. Together with Corner1 and Corner2, defines the full rectangle plane for sensor collection.

parameters.name: Corner3
parameters.desc: Third corner point coordinates (um) to define the rectangle plane orientation

Corner3.example:
Sensor (
  Name="my_sensor"
  Rectangle ( Corner1=(0 0 0) Corner2=(1 0 0) Corner3=(0 1 0) )
)

contexts.Plot: Sensor/Rectangle in unified raytracing far field

CorrelationFunction.signature: Math { RandomizedVariation <name> ( RandomField ( CorrelationFunction = Exponential | Gaussian | Grain ) ) }
CorrelationFunction.description: Specifies the spatial correlation function type for random fields in the statistical impedance field method. Determines how spatial correlations are modeled for randomized variations such as workfunction, geometric, band edge, doping, dielectric constant, and metal conductivity variations.

contexts.Math: Sub-option of RandomField within RandomizedVariation

CosHyper.signature: Physics { LED ( Broadening ( Type = CosHyper Gamma = <float> ) ) }
CosHyper.description: Specifies hyperbolic-cosine broadening type for the gain broadening model in LED simulations. Controls the spectral broadening of the gain and spontaneous emission coefficients using a cosh function.

parameters.name: Gamma
parameters.desc: Broadening coefficient in eV

Coulomb2D.description: Activates the two-dimensional ionized impurity mobility degradation component at semiconductor-insulator interfaces. Accounts for mobility degradation due to ionized impurities near the interface, combined with other Coulomb scattering components (NegInterfaceCharge, PosInterfaceCharge) via Matthiessen's rule.

contexts.Physics: Sub-option of Enormal within Mobility section

Coupled.description: Activates a Newton-like solver over a set of equations such as Poisson, Electron, Hole, Circuit, Contact, and thermal equations. This is the primary solve command in Sentaurus Device. Parameters control Newton iteration count, convergence precision, and linear solver selection. The Coupled statement can also appear as a standalone equation keyword without braces.

parameters.name: Iterations
parameters.desc: Maximum number of Newton iterations

parameters.name: Method
parameters.desc: Linear solver method: Blocked, ILS, ParDiSo, Direct, Super

parameters.name: SubMethod
parameters.desc: Inner method for block-decomposition solvers

parameters.name: Digits
parameters.desc: Number of significant digits for relative error control

parameters.name: RelErrControl
parameters.desc: Use relative error control (default)

parameters.name: IncompleteNewton
parameters.desc: Enable incomplete Newton (Newton-Richardson) algorithm

CritConc.description: Specifies the critical concentration N_crit for the trap degradation model. When the trap concentration reaches CritConc, the degradation rate changes. Default is 0.1 times the total silicon-bond concentration (BondConc).

parameters.name: CritConc
parameters.desc: Critical concentration in cm^-d (d=3 for bulk, d=2 for interface traps). Default: 0.1*N

Criterion.description: Convergence criterion keyword used in the Math section for controlling Newton iteration convergence. The Newton iterations stop when both the norm of the right-hand side and the relative error of the solution variables fall below specified tolerances (Digits).

CrystalFieldSplitHole.description: Specifies the crystal-field split-hole band type in an explicit ladder specification for wurtzite crystal structures. Used within the SchroedingerParameters section to define effective mass, degeneracy, and band offset for crystal-field split-off holes in GaN-based semiconductors.

CrystalSystem.description: Specifies that the anisotropic direction is defined in the crystal coordinate system, which depends on the LatticeParameters section. Used to define anisotropic directions for mobility, thermal conductivity, and other anisotropic properties. The direction vector is transformed from crystal to simulation coordinates using the lattice vectors.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.desc: Anisotropic direction vector (cx, cy, cz) in crystal coordinate system

contexts.Math: Aniso direction specification within Physics or Math section

CrystalType.signature: Physics { LED ( Bandstructure ( CrystalType = Zincblende | Wurtzite ) ) }
CrystalType.description: Specifies the crystal structure type of the active region in LED simulations. Determines which band structure model is used: Zincblende (default, for GaAs-based materials) or Wurtzite (for GaN-based materials). Affects the computation of band-edge energies, effective masses, and strain effects.

Csd_F.description: Specifies extraction of the drain-source capacitance (Csd) as a function of frequency during an AC-coupled quasistationary simulation. The result is written to the output file specified in the File section.

Cup.description: False positive from LaTeX rendering of \cup in mathematical formulas. This is not an actual Sentaurus Device keyword but appears in the audit due to the markdown representation of union symbols in trap capture cross-section equations.

CurECImACGreenFunction.description: Scalar plot data: imaginary part of the electron current Green's function for AC small-signal analysis. Part of the device noise data output. Related to the sensitivity of electron current to electron quasi-Fermi potential perturbations.

CurECReACGreenFunction.description: Scalar plot data: real part of the electron current Green's function for AC small-signal analysis. Part of the device noise data output.

CurETImACGreenFunction.description: Scalar plot data: imaginary part of the electron temperature current Green's function for AC small-signal analysis. Part of the device noise data output. Related to the sensitivity of current to electron temperature perturbations.

CurETReACGreenFunction.description: Scalar plot data: real part of the electron temperature current Green's function for AC small-signal analysis. Part of the device noise data output.

CurGeoGreenFunction.description: Scalar plot data: real part of the Green's function for geometric variations in AC small-signal analysis. Part of the device noise data output. Used for statistical impedance field method (SIFM) simulations.

CurHCImACGreenFunction.description: Scalar plot data: imaginary part of the hole current Green's function for AC small-signal analysis. Part of the device noise data output. Related to the sensitivity of hole current to hole quasi-Fermi potential perturbations.

CurHCReACGreenFunction.description: Scalar plot data: real part of the hole current Green's function for AC small-signal analysis. Part of the device noise data output.

CurHTImACGreenFunction.description: Scalar plot data: imaginary part of the hole temperature current Green's function for AC small-signal analysis. Part of the device noise data output. Related to the sensitivity of current to hole temperature perturbations.

CurHTReACGreenFunction.description: Scalar plot data: real part of the hole temperature current Green's function for AC small-signal analysis. Part of the device noise data output.

CurPotImACGreenFunction.description: Scalar plot dataset representing the imaginary part of the current-potential AC Green's function. Units: s^-1. Part of the noise analysis Green's function datasets computed during small-signal AC analysis. Used in IFM (Impedance Field Method) noise simulations.

CurPotReACGreenFunction.description: Scalar plot dataset representing the real part of the current-potential AC Green's function. Units: s^-1. Part of the noise analysis Green's function datasets computed during small-signal AC analysis. Used in IFM (Impedance Field Method) noise simulations.

Current.description: Specifies a target current value for a contact boundary condition in mixed-mode or quasistationary simulations. The contact voltage is adjusted to achieve the specified current.

parameters.name: contact
parameters.desc: Name of contact with break criterion (in BreakCriteria).

parameters.name: absval
parameters.desc: Upper limit for absolute current value (A/um/d) in BreakCriteria.

parameters.name: maxval
parameters.desc: Upper limit for current (A/um/d) in BreakCriteria.

parameters.name: minval
parameters.desc: Lower limit for current (A/um/d) in BreakCriteria.

parameters.name: Node
parameters.desc: Circuit node name for mixed-mode current break criterion.

parameters.name: DevName
parameters.desc: Circuit device name identifier (mixed mode only).

contexts.Electrode: Current boundary condition in A/um/d^3. Voltage is used as initial guess only.
contexts.Solve: Target current for Goal/Continuation, or trigger condition for Load/Plot/Save.

CurrentDensity.description: In BreakCriteria{}, defines a break criterion based on current density (A/cm^2). The simulation stops when the current density exceeds the specified maximum value. DevName identifies the device in mixed-mode simulations.

CurrentEnhan.description: Within Traps(Degradation), specifies parameters of the tunneling and hot carrier-dependent terms of the depassivation constant. The four parameters are: delta_Tun (tunneling enhancement prefactor), rho_Tun (tunneling enhancement field exponent), delta_HC (hot carrier enhancement prefactor), and rho_HC (hot carrier enhancement field exponent). Default: (0 1 0 1). Used in the NBTI/HCS degradation model to model trap generation enhanced by tunneling and hot carrier injection.

parameters.name: delta_Tun
parameters.desc: Tunneling enhancement prefactor. Default: 0.

parameters.name: rho_Tun
parameters.desc: Tunneling enhancement field exponent. Default: 1.

parameters.name: delta_HC
parameters.desc: Hot carrier enhancement prefactor. Default: 0.

parameters.name: rho_HC
parameters.desc: Hot carrier enhancement field exponent. Default: 1.

CurrentPlot.description: Specifies which current-related quantities to write to the current plot (.plt) file. Controls output of terminal currents, contact currents, and integrated quantities during simulation.

parameters.name: Digits
parameters.desc: Number of digits in the names of quantities in the current plot file. Default: 6.

parameters.name: IntegrationUnit
parameters.desc: Length unit for current plot integration (e.g., 'um').

contexts.Math: Global CurrentPlot options (Digits, IntegrationUnit)
contexts.Solve: Specifies scalar/vector data to output at each solve step

CurrentPotential.description: Scalar plot dataset representing the current potential W, used in the context of the current continuity equation formulation. The current potential is a scalar quantity (unit: A/cm) related to the electric current flow. Referenced in Chapter 8 (Carrier Transport Equations in Semiconductors).

CurrentWeighting.description: Global Math section switch that enables an optimal weighting scheme for computing contact currents. When enabled, uses a numerically optimized method to improve current computation accuracy at contacts. Default: off. This is part of the numeric approaches for contact current computation, alongside DirectCurrent.

Cutoff.description: Within Traps(), specifies how the trap energy distribution is truncated relative to the band edges. BandGap truncates to the plain band gap at 300 K. EffectiveBandgap truncates to the effective band gap at 300 K (default). None disables truncation. Simple uses a legacy truncation method. This parameter controls the energy range over which traps can exist, preventing unphysical trap energies outside the band gap.

Cyclic.description: Within Transient(), activates large-signal cyclic (steady-state periodic) analysis. The device is driven by a periodic signal, and the solver extrapolates the solution from cycle to cycle until convergence to the periodic steady state is achieved. Parameters include Period (cycle period), Accuracy (tolerance), MaximumCycles, and Extrapolate options. Can also appear in HBCoupled() as a cyclic harmonic balance analysis mode. In Electrode{}, CyclicNorm specifies output file for cyclic extrapolation norm.

parameters.name: Period
parameters.desc: Period of the cycle in seconds.

parameters.name: Accuracy
parameters.desc: Tolerance for cyclic convergence (epsilon_cyc).

parameters.name: MaximumCycles
parameters.desc: Maximum number of cyclic cycles before switching to transient. Default: 100000.

parameters.name: Extrapolate
parameters.desc: Extrapolation options: Average, Forward, Factor, MaxVal, MinVal, etc.

parameters.name: StartingPeriod
parameters.desc: Period from which extrapolation starts. Default: 2.

parameters.name: RelFactor
parameters.desc: Relaxation factor for cyclic convergence.

contexts.Electrode: CyclicNorm specifies cyclic extrapolation norm output file

CyclicNorm.description: Within Electrode{}, specifies a filename for output of cyclic extrapolation norm information. The file stores information about the cyclic norm versus the number of cyclic cycles and time. Used for monitoring convergence of large-signal cyclic analysis.

Cylindrical.description: Global Math section keyword that enables simulation of a 3D cylindrical device using a 2D mesh. The device is assumed to be rotationally symmetric around a specified axis. The argument specifies the coordinate of the rotation axis in micrometers, which must be less than or equal to the smallest coordinate in the perpendicular direction. xAxis specifies vertical axis symmetry (default), yAxis specifies horizontal axis symmetry (required for UCS coordinates). Used for simulating cylindrical devices such as vias, pillar transistors, and nanowires.

parameters.name: xAxis
parameters.desc: Rotation axis at x=<float> (vertical axis). Device is symmetric around this vertical line.

parameters.name: yAxis
parameters.desc: Rotation axis at y=<float> (horizontal axis). Required for UCS coordinate system.

contexts.Optics: Cylindrical raytracing coordinates for optical simulations

DC.description: Refers to DC (direct current / steady-state) analysis. In Solve, a simple Coupled { Poisson Electron Hole } statement performs a DC solve. In HBCoupled, Initialize=DCMode specifies that the 0-th harmonic is initialized from a DC solution. In Transient, DC refers to the initial steady-state solution before transient simulation begins. Also used in the context of AC simulation where the AC response is computed as a small perturbation around the DC operating point.

DCMode.description: In HBCoupled(), specifies the initialization mode for harmonic balance analysis. When DCMode is selected, the 0-th (DC) harmonic is taken from the preceding DC solution, and all other harmonics are initialized to zero. This is the default initialization method. Alternatives are HBMode (all harmonics from previous HB solution) and MixedMode (DC from DC solution, others from previous HB).

DFB.description: Configures distributed feedback (DFB) laser simulation physics. DFB lasers use a built-in grating structure for optical feedback, requiring coupled-wave analysis of the optical field. Used in conjunction with laser/LED physics models for simulating spectral properties and threshold behavior of DFB laser diodes.

DFISE.description: Specifies the DF-ISE coordinate system convention, where the bottom-to-top growth orientation aligns with the +z axis. This is the default coordinate system in Sentaurus Device. The DFISE option can be used in the Math section's CoordinateSystem{} to override the coordinate system of the input TDR grid file. Also controls the coordinate system used for optical excitation and window parameters.

contexts.Physics: Optical excitation and window parameters default to DF-ISE coordinate system

DOE_prefix.description: Within SimStats in the Math section, specifies a string prefix to be prepended to all DOE (Design-of-Experiments) variable names written to the output log file. For example, with DOE_prefix="test_", the variable Restarts is written as test_Restarts. Requires WriteDOE to be specified.

parameters.name: prefix_string
parameters.desc: String to prepend to all DOE variable names.

DOS.description: Density of states. Used in quantum-confined mobility models (eSubband/hSubband) and SHE method to specify how the density of states is computed, affecting scattering rates and transport properties.

Damping.description: In the Math section, LineSearchDamping specifies the minimal coefficient for line search damping in Newton iterations. A value of 1 disables damping (default). In Traps() within Physics, Damping specifies a damping factor (default: 10) for traps in the nonlinear Poisson equation, with 0 disabling damping. In the Coupled() solve statement, LineSearchDamping controls Newton damping per solve. In ferromagnetic dynamics, damping refers to the Gilbert damping parameter alpha in the LLG equation.

contexts.Math: Global Newton damping control (LineSearchDamping)
contexts.Solve: Per-Coupled solve damping override
contexts.Physics: Trap damping parameter in Traps

DampingLength.description: In the Various physics section, specifies a damping length parameter (in micrometers) used in quantization models. This parameter controls the spatial decay of quantum corrections, determining how far quantum effects extend from interfaces into the bulk. Used in conjunction with external Schrodinger solver configurations and quantization models.

DataName.description: In the Solve section's Math-specific contexts, specifies a data name for solution variables or formula options. Also used in the RayTrace section as part of the Ray Data Export File configuration to name exported ray data. Controls how computed or loaded datasets are identified in the output.

Dataset.description: Refers to named datasets in various contexts: current plot file structure, optical generation loading/saving, formula options, and simulation results. Datasets contain scalar or vector data defined on the mesh (vertex or element). Used for loading precomputed optical generation rates, specifying space factors for trap distributions, and referencing PMI model data.

DatasetName.description: In Physics, specifies the name of a dataset for loading optical generation data from files or saving optical generation results. Used in OpticalGenerationInput, OpticalGenerationOutput, and optical solver configurations. The dataset name references data stored in TDR format files containing spatially resolved optical generation rates.

Datasetname.description: Variant spelling of DatasetName (lowercase 'n'). In Physics, specifies the name of a dataset for loading or saving optical generation data. Functionally identical to DatasetName. Used in OpticalGenerationInput, OpticalGenerationOutput, and optical solver configurations.

DatasetsFromGrid.description: Within Plot{}, copies datasets from the TDR grid file to the plot output file. When used without arguments, copies all available datasets. When used with a list of identifiers, copies only the specified datasets. A dataset is only copied if it is not computed by Sentaurus Device (computed datasets take precedence). Cannot copy partial datasets (e.g., individual components of tensor or vector datasets).

parameters.name: ident
parameters.desc: Name of dataset to copy from grid file. Multiple datasets can be listed.

DePasCoeff.description: Within Traps(Degradation), specifies the depassivation coefficient v0 (in s^-1) at the passivation equilibrium conditions (temperature T0). This is the rate constant for Si-H bond breaking in the NBTI/HCS degradation model. Together with ActEnergy (activation energy), it controls the rate of trap generation due to hot-carrier and bias-temperature stress.

parameters.name: v0
parameters.desc: Depassivation coefficient in s^-1.

Debug.description: Command-line option and Math section keyword for debugging purposes. When specified, enables additional debug output. Used in the context of saving and restoring box method coefficients (BoxCoefficientsFromFile with Debug), PMI subroutine debugging (compile with -g option and use gdb), and 2D case diagnostics. Primarily a developer-level feature for troubleshooting simulation issues.

DebugLEDRadiation.description: Debugging feature for LED raytracing within the RayTrace section. Traces output rays within a specified angular range (in degrees) and above a minimum intensity threshold back to their source active vertices. Results are written to the specified file. In 2D, the angle uses regular polar coordinates; in 3D, the angle is measured from the z-axis (spherical theta).

Decade.description: In time conditions (Table 351) within Solve{} Load/Plot/Save/SpectralPlot, specifies logarithmic (decade) subdivision of a time range. When Decade is specified, the range is subdivided on a logarithmic scale. Default is Linear. Also appears in AC analysis contexts for specifying frequency sweep ranges on a logarithmic scale.

contexts.Solve: Logarithmic time range division in Load/Plot/Save
contexts.AC: Logarithmic frequency range division in AC analysis

Decrement.description: In Quasistationary(), Continuation(), and Transient(), specifies the divisor applied to the step size when the last step failed to converge. The step size is reduced by this factor. Default: 2.0 for Quasistationary and Transient, 1.5 for Continuation (2.0 with NewArc). Larger values give more aggressive step reduction on failure.

parameters.name: factor
parameters.desc: Step size reduction factor. Default: 2.0 (1.5 with NewArc in Continuation).

DeepLevels.description: Scalar dataset name representing deep-level (trap) concentration distribution. Can be read from doping files or used as spatial distribution dataset for SFactor in Traps definitions. Unit: cm^-3.

DefaultParametersFromFile.description: Flag in the global Physics section controlling whether to read material parameter files from default locations. When enabled, Sentaurus Device reads default parameter files for each material, which can add or override built-in parameter values. Affects material-level and region-level parameter inheritance.

Deformation.description: Activates the deformation potential model for stress-induced band structure changes. Computes strain-dependent shifts in conduction and valence band edges based on deformation potential theory.

DeformationPotential.description: Option in the LatticeParameters subsection of Physics to specify the deformation potential model. Controls how stress/strain-induced band structure changes are calculated. ekp/hkp use k.p models for electron/hole band deformation potential respectively; minimum uses the simplest model; multivalley propagates multi-valley band structure changes to other models (e.g., recombination models). Default recommendation: ekp and hkp options.

parameters.name: ekp
parameters.desc: Use k.p model for electron band deformation potentials.

parameters.name: hkp
parameters.desc: Use k.p model for hole band deformation potentials.

parameters.name: minimum
parameters.desc: Use minimum deformation potential model (not recommended without ekp/hkp).

parameters.name: multivalley
parameters.desc: Propagate multivalley bandgap and affinity changes to other models.

Degradation.description: Hot-carrier stress degradation model for MOS-based devices. Includes trap degradation kinetics, reaction enhancement factors, hydrogen diffusion in oxide, and activated barrier double well thermionic model for NBTI/PBTI. Invoked within Traps specification to model interface trap generation and threshold voltage shift over device lifetime.

DelVorWeight.description: Scalar dataset name representing the squared radius of vertices in a weighted Voronoi diagram. Computed by Sentaurus Process and written to TDR files; can be read by Sentaurus Device using the WeightedVoronoiBox keyword in the Math section. Unit: um^2.

Delaunizer2d.description: Selects the 2D Delaunay triangulation algorithm for mesh generation or refinement in sdevice. Used as a math option for controlling the meshing method applied to 2D device structures.

Delaunizer3d.description: Selects the 3D Delaunay triangulation algorithm for mesh processing. Used to enforce Delaunay constraints on the computational grid, improving the quality of box method coefficients and numerical accuracy.

DeltaVolumeLimit.description: Sets the limit value for DeltaVolume, used for handling non-Delaunay mesh elements. When the volume change of an element exceeds this limit, related processing is triggered. Default value: 1e-4.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Delta volume limit value. Default: 1e-4.

Denominator.description: Interface method in the Physics Model Interface (PMI) for defining denominator functions. Used in physical models requiring custom denominator calculations, such as energy-dependent relaxation times, mobility, Peltier coefficients, and the enhanced Lombardi model.

DensLowLimit.description: Sets the lower limit for carrier concentration (cm^-3). During Newton iterations, if the concentration erroneously becomes negative, Sentaurus Device uses a damping procedure to clamp it to a positive value; the final concentration will not fall below this limit. Default value: 1e-100 cm^-3.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Lower limit for carrier densities in cm^-3. Default: 1e-100.

Density.description: Interface method in the Physics Model Interface (PMI) for defining carrier density calculations. Used to obtain electron or hole density values; one of the core functions in both the standard and simplified PMI interfaces.

DensityCorrection.description: Controls local density correction in SRH or Band2Band recombination models. When set to Local, enables the local density correction of the Schenk/Hurkx tunneling model; when set to None (default), it is disabled. Used to improve accuracy of trap-assisted tunneling and band-to-band tunneling in high-field regions.

parameters.name: Local
parameters.desc: Enable local density correction.

parameters.name: None
parameters.desc: Disable density correction (default).

DensityGradient.description: Activates the density-gradient quantum correction model. This model approximates quantum confinement effects by introducing a quantum potential, improving carrier distribution calculations in inversion layers. Complementary to the MLDA model.

DensityIntegral.description: Activates numeric Gauss-Laguerre integration for the Fermi-Dirac integral F_1/2 in multivalley band structure calculations. Used as an option within (e|h)Multivalley for testing and comparison with the analytic approximation. Specified in the Physics section.

parameters.name: <int>
parameters.desc: Number of Gauss-Laguerre quadrature integration points. Default: 30.

DensityTail.description: Band-tail density parameter used in 1D Schrodinger equation solving. Controls the contribution of band-tail states to carrier density calculations.

DephasingRates.description: Specifies the directory path for saving dephasing rates in the File section. Dephasing rates are computed using the second Born approximation.

parameters.name: <string>
parameters.desc: Directory path for saving dephasing rates.

DepthLimit.description: Sets the maximum number of material interfaces a ray can pass through in ray tracing (i.e., maximum reflections/refractions). Ray tracing terminates when this limit is exceeded. Default value: 1000.

parameters.name: <int>
parameters.desc: Maximum number of material boundaries a ray can pass through. Default: 1000.

Derivative.description: Interface method in the Physics Model Interface (PMI) for defining derivative calculations. Used in reaction-diffusion species interface (Compute Scope) derivative computations, supporting custom implementations of physical quantity derivatives in PMI models.

Derivatives.description: Global flag in the Math section enabling analytical derivative computation for mobility and avalanche generation rates. When enabled (default '+'), the Jacobian matrix uses analytical derivatives, improving Newton convergence. When disabled, numerical derivatives are used.

DestinationBoxCorner1.description: Specifies the first corner coordinate (x, y, z) of the target domain in the ImportDomain option of OpticalGeneration. Together with DestinationBoxCorner2, defines a rectangular region for interpolating optical generation rates from the source mesh to the target mesh.

parameters.name: <vector>
parameters.desc: First corner (x y z) of the destination box in um.

DestinationBoxCorner2.description: Specifies the second corner coordinate (x, y, z) of the target domain in the ImportDomain option of OpticalGeneration. Together with DestinationBoxCorner1, defines a rectangular region for interpolating optical generation rates from the source mesh to the target mesh.

parameters.name: <vector>
parameters.desc: Second corner (x y z) of the destination box in um.

DestinationRegions.description: Specifies the target region name list in the ImportDomain option of OpticalGeneration. Optical generation rate interpolation mapping is performed only within these regions.

parameters.name: <string_list>
parameters.desc: List of destination region names.

DeterministicProblem.description: Specifies a deterministic (non-statistical) variation problem in the IFM (Impedance Field Method) section. Used for systematic parameter sweeps such as deterministic doping variations, geometric variations, or parameter variations, as opposed to randomized statistical variations.

DeterministicVariation.description: Specifies deterministic variations for sensitivity analysis. Includes doping variations, geometric variations (surface displacement), and parameter variations to compute cumulative impact on device characteristics.

DevFields.description: Specifies the file path (input .tdr format) containing spatial distributions of trapped carrier charge density in the File section. This file must match the mesh file. Spatial distribution datasets for SFactor in Traps definitions (e.g., eTrappedCharge, hTrappedCharge, TotalTrapConcentration) are read from this file.

parameters.name: <string>
parameters.desc: Path to the TDR file containing trapped carrier charge density distribution.

DevName.description: Specifies the circuit device name identifier in the BreakCriteria Current and DevicePower options for mixed-mode simulations. Associates the breakdown condition with a specific device.

parameters.name: <ident>
parameters.desc: Identifier of the circuit device name (mixed mode only).

Device.description: Defines a device for single-device or mixed-mode simulation. Contains all device-specific sections including electrodes, physics models, plots, math settings, and file I/O.

parameters.name: <string>
parameters.desc: Device name used in mixed-mode System section.

parameters.name: File
parameters.desc: Input and output file specifications (Table 207).

parameters.name: Electrode
parameters.desc: Electrical boundary conditions (Table 206).

parameters.name: Physics
parameters.desc: Physical models and material parameters (Table 236).

parameters.name: Plot
parameters.desc: Output plot datasets (Table 334).

parameters.name: Math
parameters.desc: Numerical solver settings (Table 211).

DevicePath.description: Specifies the directory path for searching .device files in the File section. Path format: dir1:dir2:dir3. Sentaurus Device loads found device definitions, which can then be used in the System section. Devices loaded via DevicePath are not overwritten by same-name definitions in the command file.

parameters.name: <string>
parameters.desc: Colon-separated list of directory paths to search for .device files.

DevicePower.description: Defines a break criterion based on the total power dissipation in the device. The simulation stops when the absolute value of device power exceeds the specified threshold. Synonym of OuterDevicePower.

parameters.name: AbsVal
parameters.desc: Upper limit for absolute power value in W/um^d.

parameters.name: maxval
parameters.desc: Upper limit for power value in W/um^d.

parameters.name: minval
parameters.desc: Lower limit for power value in W/um^d.

parameters.name: DevName
parameters.desc: Identifier of circuit device name (mixed mode only).

DielectricConstant.description: PMI interface keyword that activates a user-defined dielectric constant model in the Physics section. <string> is the PMI model name. The model can depend on lattice temperature and must compute the relative permittivity epsilon and its temperature derivative depsilondt. Supports anisotropic dielectric constants.

parameters.name: <string>
parameters.desc: PMI model name for dielectric permittivity.

DielectricConstantAniso.description: Scalar dataset name representing the anisotropic dielectric constant. Available at vertex and element locations when the anisotropic dielectric model is activated. Dimensionless unit.

Difference.description: Parameter used in the Current statement of the Solve section for Continuation simulations, controlling file write conditions: files are written only when the contact current value differs from the last written value by more than the specified Difference. Can also be used in Voltage statements.

parameters.name: Difference
parameters.desc: Current difference threshold [A]. Only for Continuation simulations.

DiffusionEnhan.description: Parameter used in the Degradation model of Traps statements, specifying hydrogen diffusion enhancement factors. The six floats are: xP (hydrogen diffusion hop distance [cm]), D0 (diffusion pre-factor [cm^2/s]), epsilon_H (hydrogen activation energy [eV]), kP (recombination rate coefficient [cm^3/s]), NH0 (initial hydrogen concentration [cm^-3]), Nox (hydrogen density in oxide [cm^-3]).

parameters.name: xP
parameters.desc: Hydrogen diffusion hop distance [cm].

parameters.name: D0
parameters.desc: Diffusion prefactor [cm^2/s].

parameters.name: epsilon_H
parameters.desc: Hydrogen activation energy [eV].

parameters.name: kP
parameters.desc: Passivation rate coefficient [cm^3/s].

parameters.name: NH0
parameters.desc: Initial hydrogen density [cm^-3].

parameters.name: Nox
parameters.desc: Hydrogen density in oxide [cm^-3].

DiffusionNoise.description: Diffusion noise model in Physics{Noise(DiffusionNoise(...))}. Computes small-signal noise spectral density due to carrier diffusion processes. The eTemperature and hTemperature options use carrier temperatures instead of lattice temperature in the noise computation.

Diffusivity.description: PMI interface keyword specifying a custom hydrogen diffusivity model in HydrogenDiffusion of the Physics section. <string> is the PMI model name. Diffusivity takes the form D_i*exp(-E_di/(kT)). The model can depend on electric field magnitude, hydrogen species density, and lattice temperature.

parameters.name: <string>
parameters.desc: PMI model name for hydrogen diffusivity.

Digits.description: Parameter used in Coupled/ACCoupled/HBCoupled/Plugin statements of the Solve section, specifying the relative error convergence target for Newton iterations. Controls solution accuracy; smaller values mean stricter convergence criteria.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Relative error target for Newton convergence.

contexts.Solve: Convergence precision parameter in Coupled/ACCoupled/HBCoupled/Plugin statements

Diode_pset.description: Defines a diode compact model instance in the System section for mixed-mode circuit simulations. The diode follows the SPICE diode model with parameters for saturation current, series resistance, junction capacitance, transit time, and breakdown characteristics.

parameters.name: is
parameters.desc: Saturation current [A]

parameters.name: rs
parameters.desc: Ohmic series resistance [Ohm]

parameters.name: n
parameters.desc: Emission coefficient (ideality factor)

parameters.name: cjo
parameters.desc: Zero-bias junction capacitance [F]

parameters.name: tt
parameters.desc: Transit time [s]

parameters.name: bv
parameters.desc: Reverse breakdown voltage [V]

Dipole.description: At insulator-insulator interfaces, activates the dipole layer model for immobile charges causing a potential jump across the interface. Reference specifies the reference side of the interface (region or material name). Parameters include sigma_D and epsilon.

contexts.Physics: Interface dipole layer model
contexts.KMCDefects: Dipole factor parameter in KMC events

Direct.description: Used as a flag in the Math section indicating a direct linear solver method. Can also indicate direct computation in PMI contexts, or direct band-to-band transitions in optical models.

DirectCurrent.description: Flag in the Current statement of the Solve section specifying direct contact current computation. Controls current write conditions in Continuation simulations. Can also indicate DC current analysis in Math contexts.

DirectQuantumCorrection.description: Global flag in the Math section that changes the approximation method of the density-gradient model. By default, the density-gradient model uses exponential approximation (carrier concentration multiplied by a quantum mechanical factor). When enabled, the quantum potential is directly applied to the electrostatic potential as quantum mechanical band edges.

DirectTunneling.description: Used in multiple contexts in the Physics section: (1) selects the Schenk direct tunneling model in GateCurrent; (2) selects the direct tunneling process in KMC_MIM_Transport; (3) serves as the DirectTunnelingEnhan enhancement factor parameter in the Traps Degradation model.

contexts.GateCurrent: Schenk direct tunneling gate current model

DirectTunnelingEnhan.description: Parameter used in the Degradation model of Traps statements, specifying direct tunneling enhancement factors. The four floats are: delta_DTe (electron direct tunneling enhancement coefficient), rho_DTe (electron direct tunneling power exponent), delta_DTh (hole direct tunneling enhancement coefficient), rho_DTh (hole direct tunneling power exponent). Default value: (1 1 1 1).

parameters.name: delta_DTe
parameters.desc: Electron direct tunneling enhancement coefficient delta_DTe.

parameters.name: rho_DTe
parameters.desc: Electron direct tunneling enhancement exponent rho_DTe.

parameters.name: delta_DTh
parameters.desc: Hole direct tunneling enhancement coefficient delta_DTh.

parameters.name: rho_DTh
parameters.desc: Hole direct tunneling enhancement exponent rho_DTh.

Direction.description: Specifies anisotropy direction in UCS (Unified Coordinate System) of the Math section. Can be used for anisotropy direction definitions of eQuantumPotential/hQuantumPotential and ThermalConductivity. Can also be specified as System_Coord to use the crystal coordinate system.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.desc: Direction vector (x y z), or xAxis/yAxis/zAxis shorthand, or System_Coord.

Dirichlet.description: Used in multiple contexts in SDEVICE: (1) Poisson=Dirichlet in Electrode specifies Dirichlet boundary conditions for the Poisson equation; (2) BoundaryCondition=Dirichlet in eQuantumPotential/hQuantumPotential specifies homogeneous Dirichlet boundary conditions for the quantum potential equation. Indicates boundary conditions with fixed function values.

contexts.Electrode: Dirichlet boundary condition for Poisson equation
contexts.eQuantumPotential: Homogeneous Dirichlet boundary condition for quantum potential
contexts.hQuantumPotential: Homogeneous Dirichlet boundary condition for quantum potential

Disable.description: Uses the '-' prefix in the Solve section to disable specific features. For example, -CheckRhsAfterUpdate disables RHS checking in Coupled statements. In the Physics section, -Discretization can disable specific discretization methods. Can also be used in Quasistationary/Transient to disable default behaviors.

DisableInternalMobilityModels.description: Used in ExternalBoltzmannSolver of the Physics section to enable Sentaurus Device built-in mobility models, allowing effective mobilities extracted by the external Subband-BTE solver to complement certain mobility models specified in the command file.

Discontinuity.description: Used in multiple interface-related contexts in the Physics section: (1) controls continuity handling at interfaces in the optical solver; (2) handles interface polarization discontinuity in the piezoelectric polarization model; (3) handles boundary condition discontinuity in the Robin PBC method. Represents jumps or discontinuous changes of physical quantities at interfaces.

DiscreteTrapT2T.description: Specifies trap-to-trap tunneling entries in Traps statements. Used to define tunneling coupling between discrete traps, and can be combined with Location, NumberOfRandomTraps, T2TconcScale, T2TfixedConc and other parameters to control the spatial distribution and concentration of tunneling traps. Supports elastic and inelastic (phonon/multi-phonon) tunneling mechanisms.

parameters.name: Location
parameters.desc: Locations of traps coupled by tunneling, in um. Use (mesh) for all vertices.

parameters.name: NumberOfRandomTraps
parameters.desc: Number of randomly distributed traps for T2T tunneling.

parameters.name: T2TconcScale
parameters.desc: Scaling factor for DiscreteTrapT2T concentration (default 1).

parameters.name: T2TfixedConc
parameters.desc: Fixed concentration at every T2T vertex in the region (default 0).

parameters.name: Transition
parameters.desc: Tunneling transition type: InelasticPhonon or ModeOscillator.

Discretization.description: Controls the discretization method for physical equations in the Math section. Affects numerical solving of the Poisson equation, continuity equations, quantum potential equations, etc. In the Physics section, -Discretization can disable specific discretization schemes.

DisplacementCurrent.description: Displacement current density (JD) at each vertex, available as a plot dataset (unit: A/cm^2). Represents the time-varying electric field contribution to total current, significant in transient and AC simulations. Related AC response datasets include ImDisplacementCurrentResponse and ReDisplacementCurrentResponse.

DisplacementCurrentDensity.description: Vector dataset name representing the displacement current density vector JD at each vertex. Unit: A/cm^2. Can be specified for output in the Plot section or used in formula calculations in CurrentPlot. Has a corresponding response dataset ImDisplacementCurrentResponse in AC analysis.

Dissociation.description: Used in SingletExciton Recombination of the Physics section to activate the exciton dissociation model. For organic semiconductor and OLED simulations, describing the exciton dissociation process at interfaces. The net exciton recombination rate may include volume terms converted by the exciton interface dissociation model.

DistResist.description: Specifies distributed resistance in the Electrode section. DistResist=<float> directly specifies the resistance value [Ohm*cm^2]; DistResist=SchottkyResist uses the Schottky contact resistance model. Used to model resistive contacts and resistive interfaces.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Distributed resistance value [Ohm*cm^2].

parameters.name: SchottkyResist
parameters.desc: Use Schottky contact resistance model instead of fixed value.

Distribution.description: Used in multiple contexts in the Physics section, primarily representing the type of trap energy distribution or spatial distribution. In the PMI interface it represents data distribution. In parameter randomization it represents statistical distribution type. A highly ambiguous keyword.

DoZero.description: Flag used in the Quasistationary statement of the Solve section. When enabled, the equations are solved once before the ramp starts (t=0). Enabled by default. Use -DoZero to disable this behavior.

Donor.description: Specifies donor-type traps in the Traps statement of the Physics section. Donor traps are positively charged when unoccupied and neutral when occupied. Also represents donor doping-related physical quantities in the PMI interface.

DonorConcentration.description: Donor doping concentration (ND) at each vertex, specified through the doping profile in the device structure. Used in physics models for computing carrier statistics, mobility, and recombination rates.

DonorPlusConcentration.description: Scalar dataset name representing ionized donor concentration (ND+) at each vertex. Unit: cm^-3. In incomplete ionization models, this differs from DonorConcentration, reflecting the actual ionized donor concentration.

Dopants.description: Scalar dataset name representing the concentration distribution of each dopant species in the device (e.g., Antimony, Arsenic, Boron, Phosphorus). Can be read from doping files or used in incomplete ionization models. Unit: cm^-3. Available in Plot and CurrentPlot sections.

Doping.description: Enables random dopant fluctuation models for statistical impedance field method (sIFM) noise analysis. Specifies spatial correlation properties of doping variations.

DopingConcentration.description: Scalar dataset name representing net doping concentration (donors minus acceptors), defined at vertices. Unit: cm^-3. Can be used directly in Plot and CurrentPlot sections. Can also be recomputed based on individual dopants via the ComputeDopingConcentration option in the Math section.

DopingDependence.description: Doping-dependent mobility model within Mobility(). The default Arora model computes mobility degradation due to ionized impurity scattering. Can also accept a PMI model identifier for custom doping-dependent mobility. Controls the low-field mobility as a function of doping concentration.

DopingThreshold.description: Specifies the doping concentration threshold Nth in Physics section Piezo(Model(Mobility(eMinorityFactor | hMinorityFactor))). When DopingThreshold is specified, the minority carrier factor beta is applied when below the majority carrier doping threshold, and the doping dependence of the stress model is disabled. Positive values are for electrons, negative values for holes. DopingThreshold=0 corresponds to the conventional minority/majority carrier definition.

parameters.name: DopingThreshold
parameters.desc: Net doping threshold Nth in cm^-3. Positive for electrons, negative for holes. 0 corresponds to standard minority/majority definition.

DopingVariation.description: In RandomizedVariation(), activates doping profile variations for statistical impedance field method (sIFM) simulations. Generates random spatial variations of the doping concentration.

DopingWell.description: Specifies a doping well by the coordinates of a point inside it. Used in the Goal and Set statements of a Quasistationary command to ramp electron or hole quasi-Fermi potentials in the specified well. More general than WellContactName because it works for buried wells without contacts. In mixed-mode simulations, the device instance name must be prefixed.

parameters.name: x
parameters.desc: X coordinate of a point inside the doping well [cm]

parameters.name: y
parameters.desc: Y coordinate of a point inside the doping well [cm]

parameters.name: z
parameters.desc: Z coordinate of a point inside the doping well [cm] (3D only)

DopingWells.signature: DopingWells([Region=<string> | Material=<string> | Semiconductor] [(x1 y1 z1) (x2 y2 z2)])
DopingWells.description: Multiple-well ramping feature in the Solve section. Allows simultaneous ramping of quasi-Fermi potentials for all doping wells within a specified region, material, or all semiconductor wells. Used inside Set() and Goal() to set or ramp electron/hole quasi-Fermi levels across multiple quantum wells during quasistationary simulation.

Dose.description: Specifies the total gamma radiation dose in Radiation() of the Physics section. Unit: rad. When using Dose, DoseTime must also be specified. Dose represents the total radiation exposure over the DoseTime interval; Sentaurus Device automatically calculates DoseRate as Dose/DoseTime.

parameters.name: Dose
parameters.desc: Total radiation dose over exposure time [rad].

DoseRate.description: Specifies the gamma radiation dose rate in Radiation() of the Physics section. Unit: rad/s. DoseRate represents the D parameter in the equations. When used with DoseTime, the radiation exposure time window can be constrained; when used with DoseTSigma, Gaussian rise/fall edges can be added.

parameters.name: DoseRate
parameters.desc: Gamma radiation dose rate [rad/s].

DoseTSigma.description: Specifies the standard deviation time for Gaussian rise and fall edges of the radiation dose rate in Radiation() of the Physics section. Unit: s. Used with DoseTime to simulate gradual radiation exposure on/off transitions.

parameters.name: DoseTSigma
parameters.desc: Standard deviation of Gaussian rise and fall of dose rate over time [s].

DoseTime.description: Specifies the radiation exposure time interval in Radiation() of the Physics section. Parameter is a two-element float vector (t_start, t_end) in seconds. Defines the active time range of constant DoseRate. Must be specified when using Dose.

parameters.name: DoseTime
parameters.desc: Exposure time interval as (t_start, t_end) [s].

Dt.description: Uniformly sets the time step limit for optical turning points in OpticalGeneration(TimeDependence(OpticalTurningPoints())) of the Physics section. For FromFile type time dependence, Dt limits the time step in the [t0,t3] range (default: minimum time interval in the OptGenTransientScaling file). For other types, Dt simultaneously sets DtRise, DtPlateau, and DtFall. If these sub-parameters are set individually, they override Dt.

parameters.name: Dt
parameters.desc: Limiting time step for all optical turning point ranges [s].

DtFall.description: Sets the limited time step for the optical signal falling edge [t2,t3] in OpticalTurningPoints(). Unit: s. Default: 0. Not applicable to FromFile type time dependence.

parameters.name: DtFall
parameters.desc: Limiting time step for fall range [t2,t3] [s]. Default: 0.

DtFallEnd.description: Sets the limited time step at time t3 in OpticalTurningPoints(). Unit: s. Default: (t1-t0)/10. Controls time step accuracy when the optical signal is fully turned off.

parameters.name: DtFallEnd
parameters.desc: Limiting time step at t3 [s]. Default: (t1-t0)/10.

DtFallStart.description: Sets the limited time step at time t2 in OpticalTurningPoints(). Unit: s. Default: (t1-t0)/10. Not applicable to FromFile type time dependence. Controls time step accuracy when the optical signal begins to fall.

parameters.name: DtFallStart
parameters.desc: Limiting time step at t2 [s]. Default: (t1-t0)/10.

DtPlateau.description: Sets the limited time step for the optical signal plateau [t1,t2] in OpticalTurningPoints(). Unit: s. Default: 0. Not applicable to FromFile type time dependence.

parameters.name: DtPlateau
parameters.desc: Limiting time step for plateau range [t1,t2] [s]. Default: 0.

DtRise.description: Sets the limited time step for the optical signal rising edge [t0,t1] in OpticalTurningPoints(). Unit: s. Default: 0. Not applicable to FromFile type time dependence.

parameters.name: DtRise
parameters.desc: Limiting time step for rise range [t0,t1] [s]. Default: 0.

DtRiseEnd.description: Sets the limited time step at time t1 in OpticalTurningPoints(). Unit: s. Default: (t1-t0)/10. Not applicable to FromFile type time dependence. Controls time step accuracy when the optical signal reaches its maximum.

parameters.name: DtRiseEnd
parameters.desc: Limiting time step at t1 [s]. Default: (t1-t0)/10.

DtRiseStart.description: Sets the limited time step at time t0 in OpticalTurningPoints(). Unit: s. Default: (t1-t0)/10. Not applicable to FromFile type time dependence. Controls time step accuracy at the start of the optical signal.

parameters.name: DtRiseStart
parameters.desc: Limiting time step at t0 [s]. Default: (t1-t0)/10.

DualGridInterpolation.description: In the Math section, controls interpolation between dual grids (for example, between the optical grid and the device mesh). Method=Simple uses simple interpolation algorithm for mapping data between different grid systems.

Dx.description: Specifies the x-direction discretization size for ray distribution when Mode=Equidistant in RayDistribution(). Unit: um. Controls the x-direction spacing of equidistantly distributed rays in the excitation region.

parameters.name: Dx
parameters.desc: Discretized x-size for Mode=Equidistant [um].

Dy.description: Specifies the y-direction discretization size for ray distribution when Mode=Equidistant in RayDistribution(). Unit: um. Controls the y-direction spacing of equidistantly distributed rays in the excitation region.

parameters.name: Dy
parameters.desc: Discretized y-size for Mode=Equidistant [um].

E1.description: Selects the simple band-to-band tunneling model with power exponent P=1 in Recombination(Band2Band(Model=E1)) of the Physics section. The E1 model is the simplest Hurkx band-to-band tunneling model variant, suitable for quick estimation. Can also be used as a reference electric field parameter name in the Avalanche model.

contexts.Avalanche: Avalanche breakdown reference electric field parameter

E1_5.description: Selects the simple band-to-band tunneling model with power exponent P=1.5 in Recombination(Band2Band(Model=E1_5)) of the Physics section. E1_5 is the intermediate accuracy option in the Hurkx band-to-band tunneling model series, with P value between E1 and E2.

E2.description: Selects the simple band-to-band tunneling model with power exponent P=2 in Recombination(Band2Band(Model=E2)) of the Physics section. The E2 model uses the highest power exponent in the Hurkx series, providing the strongest field dependence. Can also be used as a reference electric field in DirectTunneling parameters.

contexts.DirectTunneling: Direct tunneling reference electric field parameter

East.description: Specifies the starting direction of the layer stack extraction line as East (rightward) in the WindowPosition parameter of LayerStackExtraction(). Used with OptBeam() LayerStackExtraction to determine the direction of the layer stack relative to the device in TMM calculations.

Ed.description: Used as an electric field parameter in multiple physical models. In the Avalanche model, Ed is the reference parameter for the electric field driving force (unit: V/cm). In the hydrogen transport model it serves as a diffusion driving force parameter. Also used as a reference electric field in DirectTunneling parameters. Specific meaning depends on context.

parameters.name: Ed
parameters.desc: Reference electric field parameter [V/cm]. Specific meaning depends on context.

contexts.Avalanche: Avalanche breakdown reference electric field parameter
contexts.DirectTunneling: Direct tunneling reference electric field parameter

Effective.description: Appears as a modifier prefix in multiple physical models in the Physics section, indicating an effective value. Common combinations include EffectiveMass (effective mass model), EffectiveBandGap (effective bandgap), EffectiveIntrinsicDensity (effective intrinsic carrier concentration), EffectiveAbsorption (effective absorption), etc. Effective itself is not a standalone section keyword.

EffectiveAbsorption.description: Enables the effective absorption quantum yield model in Optics(OpticalGeneration(QuantumYield(EffectiveAbsorption))) of the Physics section. Sets the quantum yield prefactor to 1, indicating quantum yield considering effective absorption. If StepFunction is also specified, StepFunction takes precedence. More complex quantum yield models can also be implemented via the PMI interface.

EffectiveBandGap.description: Scalar dataset name representing the effective bandgap Eg-Ebgn (bandgap minus bandgap narrowing). Can be used as a cutoff energy option in StepFunction of QuantumYield and OpticalAbsorptionHeat. When photon energy exceeds this cutoff, interband absorption occurs; when below, intraband absorption. Unit: eV. Note: the case variant EffectiveBandgap also exists in the command syntax.

contexts.Plot: Scalar dataset name for outputting effective bandgap distribution
contexts.Physics: StepFunction cutoff energy option

EffectiveBandgap.description: Specifies using Eg-Ebgn (quantum yield model) or Eg-Ebgn+2*(3/2)kT (optical absorption heat model) as the interband absorption cutoff energy in StepFunction of QuantumYield or OpticalAbsorptionHeat. Interband absorption occurs when photon energy exceeds the cutoff value.

contexts.Physics: StepFunction cutoff energy option
contexts.Plot: Scalar dataset name (EffectiveBandGap, uppercase G)

EffectiveIndex.description: In optics and waveguide simulations, computes the effective refractive index of optical modes. Used in BPM (Beam Propagation Method) and VCSEL simulations for modal analysis.

EffectiveIntrinsicDensity.description: Selects the effective intrinsic carrier density model. Controls how bandgap narrowing affects the intrinsic carrier concentration ni. Options include BandGapNarrowing(Slotboom), BandGapNarrowing(BennettWilson), NoBandGapNarrowing, or a PMI model name.

EffectiveMass.description: Specifies the effective mass PMI model for computing electron and hole effective masses (expressed as multiples of the vacuum electron mass). Effective mass can depend on lattice temperature t[K] and bandgap bg[eV]. The PMI model must compute the relative effective mass m and its derivatives with respect to temperature and bandgap. Used for gamma factor calculation in the Density Gradient model, also in multi-valley electron/hole mobility models and organic heterojunction Gaussian transport.

parameters.name: <pmi_model_name>
parameters.desc: Name of the PMI model for effective mass computation.

EffectiveStressModel.description: Isotropic factor model option for computing the stress enhancement factor in piezoresistive mobility models. Maps the 3D stress tensor to an effective stress scalar (based on principal stress components S11, S22, S33), then computes the mobility enhancement factor gamma. The AxisAlignedNormals option can be specified to change how S22 is assigned. Model parameters (alpha, beta, mu0, a, s, t, F0, etc.) can be defined in the EffectiveStressModel parameter set of the parameter file.

parameters.name: AxisAlignedNormals
parameters.desc: Use alternative S22 assignment based on nearest interface normal direction.

EgComponent.description: Specifies which component of the bandgap random field to use in the RandomField declaration of BandEdgeVariations. Range: 0 to 1, default: 1. When ChiComponent and EgComponent are used together, they separately control random variations of electron affinity and bandgap.

parameters.desc: Component index of the RandomField to use for bandgap variations, default 1.

EigenEnergy.description: Specifies output of Schrodinger equation eigenenergies in NonLocalPlot. Electron and Hole options can be used to limit output to electron or hole eigenenergies respectively; the Number sub-option limits the number of lowest energy states to output. Unit: eV.

parameters.name: Electron
parameters.desc: Restrict output to electron eigensolutions.

parameters.name: Hole
parameters.desc: Restrict output to hole eigensolutions.

parameters.name: Number
parameters.desc: Restrict output to the specified number of lowest-energy states.

ElasticStrain.description: Tensor dataset name representing the elastic strain tensor read from a TDR file. Requires specifying the Piezo file in the File section. Dimensionless. When Strain=LoadFromFile() is selected, Sentaurus Device reads this strain field from the TDR file.

contexts.Plot: Tensor dataset name for outputting elastic strain field

ElectricField.description: Used as an option to Avalanche in the Recombination section to select the electric-field-dependent impact ionization model. Also available as a plot dataset (ElectricField/Vector for vector field visualization) and as an option to HighFieldSaturation for PMI driving force selection (EparallelToInterface vs ElectricField).

ElectricFieldDep.description: Used in QWLocal (local quantum well model) to disable electric field dependence. By default, the local quantum well model has electric field dependence enabled. Use -ElectricFieldDep to disable this, making quantum well calculations independent of the electric field.

Electrode.description: Specifies electrical boundary conditions for a device contact, including voltage, current, or charge boundary conditions, as well as Schottky and resistive contact models.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.desc: Name of the electrode, or Regexp(<pattern>) for regular expression matching.

parameters.name: Voltage
parameters.desc: Contact voltage in V. Can also use (val1 At val2, ...) for transient piecewise-linear voltage.

parameters.name: Current
parameters.desc: Current boundary condition in A/um/d. Voltage is used as initial guess only.

parameters.name: Charge
parameters.desc: Charge for floating electrode in C. Voltage must not be specified.

parameters.name: Barrier
parameters.desc: Barrier voltage in V.

parameters.name: Material
