parameters.desc: Electrode material for contacts on insulators. Can include doping spec (N=<val> or P=<val>).

Electron.description: Electron continuity equation solved in Sentaurus Device. In the Solve section, specifies that the electron continuity equation is to be solved. In equation error control (Table 342), the reference value is 1e10 cm^-3 with relative error tolerance of 1e-5.

ElectronAffinity.description: Scalar dataset name representing the electron affinity chi. Can be read from TDR files or computed by bandgap models. Unit: eV. Defined in Bandgap and Electron-Affinity Models.

ElectrostaticPotential.description: Scalar dataset name representing the electrostatic potential phi (Poisson equation solution variable). At equilibrium with zero bias, can also be output as EquilibriumPotential. Unit: V. A key field in the current plot file structure.

Element.description: Basic unit of the finite element mesh. In the PMI C++ interface, Element is a core object of the physics model interface, providing access to mesh element geometric information (vertices, edges, volume, etc.) and physical quantities (electric field, current density, etc.). In ElementWise contexts, refers to the per-element operation mode.

contexts.Math: Mesh element-related parameters

ElementEdge.description: Mobility averaging method option. When specified as ElementEdge via eMobilityAveraging and hMobilityAveraging in the Math section, mobility values are computed for each edge of every semiconductor element. Compared to the default Element averaging method, ElementEdge averaging has smaller discretization error, especially advantageous when using a_perp != 0 in the enhanced Lombardi model. Computation time is nearly the same, but memory consumption is higher.

ElementEdgeCurrent.description: Option in the Math section to enable per-element-edge current density computation. When activated, current density is computed independently on each element edge, providing a more accurate current distribution.

ElementLimit.description: Sets the maximum number of finite elements for mesh generation or refinement operations in sdevice simulations. Used in the Math section to control mesh density and simulation resource limits.

ElementSize.description: Controls mesh element size refinement parameters. Used in adaptive meshing and nonlocal mesh construction to specify discretization criteria for numerical accuracy.

ElementVolumeAvalanche.description: Option enabled in the Math section that uses truncated element-vertex control volumes for avalanche generation calculations. Normally, the AverageBoxMethod algorithm may compute total vertex control volumes for obtuse elements that are several times larger than the actual geometric volume, causing avalanche-generated carrier counts to be exaggerated and potentially premature breakdown. When enabled, the sum of truncated vertex volumes for each element exactly equals the geometric volume.

ElementWise.description: Layer stack extraction mode option used in LayerStackExtraction of TMM or OptBeam. Specifies per-element creation of the layer stack structure (as opposed to RegionWise per-region mode). Also used in Volume Dataset save operations to specify per-element search datasets.

Elimination.description: Option used in MSConfig for applying different solving algorithms to state occupancy calculations of multi-state configurations. When prefixed with a minus sign, the elimination algorithm is enabled, commonly used to improve numerical robustness, especially when forward and reverse rates of all transitions differ by several orders of magnitude at certain operating points.

EmissionTable.description: Specifies the filename prefix for output radiation data tables in the File section. Sentaurus Device writes radiation-related data to this file.

parameters.name: <string>
parameters.desc: Base name for the tabulated emission data output file.

EmissionType.description: Specifies the type of emission model used in radiative recombination calculations for optoelectronic device simulations.

Empty.description: Command in the Solve section used to create an empty solve step. No equations are solved in this step, commonly used to insert placeholder steps in quasistationary or transient simulation sequences for subsequent Set/Goal initial condition setup.

EndFrequency.description: Specifies the upper frequency limit (unit: Hz) for small-signal AC analysis in the ACCoupled section. Used with StartFrequency and NumberOfPoints to select the AC analysis frequency range. Linear or Decade can be used to specify the frequency interval method.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Upper frequency limit in Hz. Must be non-negative.

EndPoint.description: Option used for Barrier specifications in NonLocal definitions. Specifies which regions or materials are considered non-conductive, thus not serving as endpoints of non-local lines. By default, semiconductor regions, metal regions, and electrodes are considered conductive. -EndPoint accepts Region and Material specification lists with the same syntax as Barrier.

parameters.name: Region
parameters.desc: Region name to treat as nonconductive endpoint.

parameters.name: Material
parameters.desc: Material name to treat as nonconductive endpoint.

Endpoint.description: Same as EndPoint; option used for Barrier specifications in NonLocal definitions. Specifies which regions or materials are considered non-conductive endpoints. A case variant of EndPoint.

parameters.name: Region
parameters.desc: Region name to treat as nonconductive endpoint.

parameters.name: Material
parameters.desc: Material name to treat as nonconductive endpoint.

Energy.description: General-purpose keyword representing energy values in multiple contexts. In StepFunction of QuantumYield/OpticalAbsorptionHeat, specifies the cutoff energy in eV. In the PMI interface, serves as a core variable for band-related calculations. Related to bandgap and electron affinity computations in bandgap models.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Energy value in eV (context-dependent).

contexts.Physics: StepFunction energy option, PMI energy variable
contexts.Plot: PMI energy-related data

EnergyFlux.description: Parameter file section specifying the coefficients r and fnf of the energy flux transport equation in the hydrodynamic model. These coefficients modify the default energy flux relationship. Used in the thermodynamic (hydrodynamic) transport model to define energy transport behavior.

parameters.name: r
parameters.desc: Energy flux coefficient vector (3 components).

parameters.name: fnf
parameters.desc: Nonlinear energy flux coefficient vector (3 components).

EnergyInterval.description: In Schroedinger(), specifies the highest energy (in eV) above the lowest interior potential point to which eigensolutions are computed. When set to 0 (default), only bound states are computed. When nonzero, unbound states up to the specified energy are also included. Supports carrier-specific values via Electron or Hole arguments. Used to avoid hard transitions between classical and quantum solutions near flatband conditions.

parameters.name: Electron
parameters.desc: Restricts the value to electrons only.

parameters.name: Hole
parameters.desc: Restricts the value to holes only.

EnergyMid.description: In Traps(), specifies the central energy E0 (in eV) of the trap energy distribution (Gaussian, Exponential, Uniform). Combined with EnergySig (Es) to define the distribution shape per Equation 521. The actual trap energy center is determined by E0 plus optional EnergyShift, referenced from the conduction band (fromCondBand), intrinsic energy (fromMidBandGap, default), or valence band (fromValBand).

EnergyRelaxationTime.description: Parameter file section specifying energy-dependent energy relaxation times (tau_w_ele for electrons, tau_w_hol for holes) in picoseconds. Supports constant values, piecewise polynomial approximation (Formula=2), and spline interpolation (Formula=3). Used in thermodynamic transport models to define carrier energy relaxation dynamics.

EnergyResolution.description: In Nonlocal(), specifies the minimum energy resolution (in eV) for integrals in the computation of nonlocal tunneling currents. Default is 0.005 eV. Higher values give faster but less accurate tunneling current calculations.

EnergyShift.description: In Traps(), specifies a PMI (Physical Model Interface) model to compute a position-dependent energy shift for trap energies. The PMI depends on the electric field and lattice temperature at the trap location (or at a ReferencePoint). Used for trap energy shift modeling in degradation simulations.

parameters.name: ReferencePoint
parameters.desc: Coordinate (vector) where electric field and temperature are taken for the PMI, instead of at the trap location.

EnergySig.description: In Traps(), specifies the width Es (in eV) of the trap energy distribution (Gaussian, Exponential, Uniform). Combined with EnergyMid (E0) to define the distribution shape per Equation 521. Must be non-negative. A narrow width (small Es) produces a more localized trap distribution.

Enormal.description: In Mobility(), specifies a PMI model for computing mobility degradation due to the electric field component normal to the interface (Enormal). Used in piezoresistance mobility models and interface mobility degradation. Also available as a plot dataset (eEnormal/hEnormal) showing the perpendicular electric field at each vertex.

contexts.Mobility: PMI model for normal electric field mobility degradation
contexts.Plot: Plot dataset name (eEnormal, hEnormal) for perpendicular electric field

EnormalInterface.description: In Physics{} at interfaces, activates the electric field normal to the interface for physics model evaluation. Used for interface mobility degradation and other interface-dependent models where the normal electric field component is needed.

Envelope.description: In the context of optical transitions for wurtzite crystals, refers to the envelope function approximation used in computing the optical transition matrix element. Also appears in optical generation and LED simulation discussions.

Eparallel.description: Driving force option for high-field saturation mobility models. Selects the electric field parallel to the current (grad quasi-Fermi) as the driving force for high-field mobility degradation. Also available as a plot dataset (eEparallel/hEparallel). Alternative driving forces include EparallelToInterface and ElectricField.

contexts.HighFieldSaturation: Driving force option for mobility model
contexts.Plot: Plot dataset (eEparallel, hEparallel) for parallel electric field

EparallelToInterface.description: In the Math section, controls the computation of the electric field parallel to the interface for use as a driving force in mobility and avalanche models. Specifies the current direction vector and an optional spatial restriction box. Also available as a driving force option for HighFieldSaturation mobility models.

parameters.name: Direction
parameters.desc: Direction vector of the current flow.

parameters.name: Box
parameters.desc: Restrict the Direction vector to vertices contained in the specified box.

contexts.Math: Global option to control parallel-to-interface field computation
contexts.HighFieldSaturation: Driving force option for mobility model

Epsilon.description: Refers to the dielectric constant (relative permittivity) of materials. Used in RandomizedVariation for dielectric constant fluctuations, in the PMI interface for computing epsilon-dependent properties, and as a parameter in various physical models. Can be temperature-dependent and anisotropic.

contexts.Physics: Dielectric constant in physical model computations

EpsilonFluctuations.description: In Noise(), activates random dielectric constant fluctuations in the device. The string argument identifies a named volume specification determining where fluctuations are active. Assumes Gaussian spatial correlations. Parameters (Amplitude and Lambda) are specified in a named global EpsilonFluctuations parameter set in the parameter file.

parameters.name: Amplitude
parameters.desc: Amplitude of dielectric constant fluctuation (dimensionless).

parameters.name: Lambda
parameters.desc: Correlation length in um.

EqOhmic.description: In Electrode{}, activates a modified Ohmic contact boundary condition. The contact behaves as Ohmic but with an alternative mathematical formulation that can improve convergence in certain situations, particularly for contacts on highly doped regions.

Equidistant.description: In RayDistribution(), selects an equidistant distribution of rays within the excitation shape. The spacing is controlled by Dx and Dy parameters. Also used in other contexts for equidistant spacing of data points, rays, or grid elements.

parameters.name: Dx
parameters.desc: Discretized x-size for equidistant ray distribution.

parameters.name: Dy
parameters.desc: Discretized y-size for equidistant ray distribution.

contexts.RayDistribution: Mode option for equidistant ray distribution

EquilibriumElectricField.description: Plot dataset for the electric field vector at zero applied voltages (zero currents), corresponding to the equilibrium state of the device. Available as vector data (Table 196/Vector).

EquilibriumPotential.description: Plot dataset for the electrostatic potential at zero applied voltages (zero currents), corresponding to the equilibrium state of the device. Available as scalar data at vertex locations.

EquilibriumSolution.description: Controls the initial equilibrium solution computation before bias is applied. Specifies Newton iteration parameters, error control, and line search damping for the equilibrium solve step.

ErrRef.description: Reference error value for convergence control in Newton iterations. Sets the scaling factor for relative error computation of a specific quantity (e.g., ErrRef(Electron), ErrRef(Hole), ErrRef(Charge)). The solver converges when the error drops below this reference.

Error.description: Sets the precision target (error tolerance) for nonlinear solver convergence. An optional carrier argument (Electron/Hole) allows carrier-specific error control. Default is 1e-5 eV.

Everywhere.description: Qualifier keyword used in various Physics contexts to indicate that a model or parameter applies everywhere in the device (all regions and materials), rather than being restricted to a specific region, material, or interface. Used with models such as ApparentBandEdgeShift, CarrierHeating, and others.

Excitation.description: In Physics{Optics{}}, defines the optical excitation configuration including illumination windows (circle, rectangle, polygon, line) and their spatial intensity distributions. Specifies the optical source geometry for raytracing and TMM solvers.

Exclude.description: Used in KMC (Kinetic Monte Carlo) defect definitions to specify a list of excluded particle types. Applied within Particle and Filament definitions in the KMCDefects section to prevent certain particles from participating in filament formation or particle interactions.

ExcludeHorizontalSource.description: Within the RayTrace section, excludes source rays emitting within a specified angular range (in degrees) from the horizontal plane (the plane of quantum wells). The power from excluded rays is redistributed equally among remaining rays, producing an approximate anisotropic emission pattern. Used to mitigate unrealistic spikes in LED radiation patterns caused by rays traveling along the quantum well plane.

ExitOnFailure.description: In the global Math section, terminates the simulation as soon as a Solve command fails. Default is off. When enabled, the simulation stops immediately rather than attempting to continue with subsequent solve steps.

ExitOnUnknownParameterRegion.description: In the global Math section, controls behavior when an unknown region, region interface, or electrode appears in the parameter file. When enabled (default), Sentaurus Device exits with an error. When disabled (- prefix), it prints a warning message and continues.

ExpTempDependence.description: In SRH(), activates an exponential temperature dependence model for SRH recombination. The temperature factor follows f(T) = exp(C*(T/300K - 1)), where C is the Tcoeff parameter from the Scharfetter parameter set (default 2.55). Used to model the temperature dependence of SRH carrier lifetimes.

Explicit.description: Used in multiple contexts: (1) In Physics{Mobility()}, selects the explicit (non-self-consistent) evaluation of mobility models. (2) In Physics{}, activates explicit treatment of certain physics models. (3) In Solve{}, specifies explicit time integration method. (4) In the PMI interface, indicates explicit model evaluation.

contexts.Solve: Explicit time integration or solve method

Exponential.description: In Traps(), selects an exponential energetic distribution for trap energy levels. Combined with EnergyMid and EnergySig to define the distribution per Equation 521. Also used in other contexts: as a correlation function type in RandomizedVariation, as a spatial shape option, and in radiation/heavy-ion generation models.

ExtendedPrecision.description: Enables extended precision floating-point arithmetic in the global Math section. Supports long double (80-bit), double-double (128-bit), quad-double (256-bit), and arbitrary precision modes. Particularly useful for wide-bandgap semiconductor simulations where small currents and low carrier concentrations must be resolved accurately.

parameters.name: Digits
parameters.desc: Number of decimal digits for arbitrary precision mode. Storage requirements increase linearly, runtime quadratically.

ExternalBoltzmannSolver.description: Coupling interface to the external Subband-BTE solver of Sentaurus Device QTX. Enables quantum corrections and effective mobility extraction from the subband Boltzmann transport equation solver. Configures slices, volume restriction, damping length, and mobility options.

parameters.name: MaxMismatch
parameters.desc: Tolerance (um) for matching slice meshes to device mesh. Default: 1e-4.

ExternalBoundary.description: Deactivates the ParallelToInterfaceInBoundaryLayer feature on all external boundaries. Used as a sub-option of ParallelToInterfaceInBoundaryLayer in the Math section to control where interface-parallel driving force corrections are applied.

ExternalMaterialCRIFile.description: Specifies an external file containing the complex refractive index (CRI) data for the external medium in raytracing optical simulations. The file should contain three columns: wavelength (um), n, and k. Lines starting with # are treated as comments. If not specified, the external medium defaults to air (refractive index 1.0).

parameters.name: ExternalMaterialCRIFile
parameters.desc: Path to the external material CRI file with wavelength, n, k columns.

ExternalSchroedinger.description: Defines the connection to an external 2D Schrodinger solver (Sentaurus Band Structure). Enables quantum corrections from 2D slices of a 3D mesh to be interpolated into the full device volume. The quantum correction is damped beyond a specified DampingLength from the enclosed volume.

parameters.name: NumberOfSlices
parameters.desc: Number of 2D slices (cross sections) of the 3D mesh. Required, at least 2.

parameters.name: Carriers
parameters.desc: Carrier types for quantum correction: (Electron), (Hole), or (Electron Hole). Required.

parameters.name: Volume
parameters.desc: Named volume to restrict quantum corrections to.

parameters.name: SBandCommandFile
parameters.desc: Command file for Sentaurus Band Structure; Sentaurus Device will start it automatically.

parameters.name: DampingLength
parameters.desc: Distance (um) from enclosed volume beyond which quantum correction is damped to zero. Default: 0.005.

parameters.name: MaxMismatch
parameters.desc: Tolerance (um) for matching slice meshes to device mesh. Default: 1e-4.

ExternalXPlane.description: Deactivates the ParallelToInterfaceInBoundaryLayer feature on external boundaries perpendicular to the x-axis. Used as a sub-option of ParallelToInterfaceInBoundaryLayer in the Math section.

ExternalYPlane.description: Deactivates the ParallelToInterfaceInBoundaryLayer feature on external boundaries perpendicular to the y-axis. Used as a sub-option of ParallelToInterfaceInBoundaryLayer in the Math section.

ExternalZPlane.description: Deactivates the ParallelToInterfaceInBoundaryLayer feature on external boundaries perpendicular to the z-axis. Used as a sub-option of ParallelToInterfaceInBoundaryLayer in the Math section.

ExtinctionCoefficient.description: Specifies the extinction coefficient (k) of a medium surrounding the extracted layer structure in the TMM (Transfer Matrix Method) optical solver. Used within the Medium section of LayerStackExtraction to define the optical properties of the top or bottom surrounding medium. Alternatively, a Material name can be specified instead.

parameters.name: ExtinctionCoefficient
parameters.desc: Extinction coefficient (imaginary part of complex refractive index). Default: 1.

Extraction.description: Specifies voltage-dependent extraction curves to be generated during a Quasistationary ramp. Multiple curves can be listed. No extraction output is produced if the Extraction option is missing from the Quasistationary statement. For AC and noise simulations, frequency-dependent curves are specified within an ACCoupled statement instead.

parameters.name: IdVg
parameters.desc: Drain current vs. gate voltage extraction curve

parameters.name: IsVg
parameters.desc: Source current vs. gate voltage extraction curve

parameters.name: IgVg
parameters.desc: Gate current vs. gate voltage extraction curve

parameters.name: IdVd
parameters.desc: Drain current vs. drain voltage extraction curve

parameters.name: IbVd
parameters.desc: Bulk current vs. drain voltage extraction curve

parameters.name: Ads_Vg
parameters.desc: AC conductance Ads vs. gate voltage (AC analysis)

parameters.name: IsubVd
parameters.desc: Substrate current vs drain voltage extraction curve.

Extrapolate.description: Controls solution extrapolation for accelerating Quasistationary and Transient simulations. Uses previous solution as initial guess for the next step. Configurable extrapolation order, algorithm selection, minimum step size, and density limit below which extrapolation is skipped.

parameters.name: Algorithm
parameters.desc: Extrapolation algorithm selection: 1 (default) or 2.

parameters.name: Order
parameters.desc: Order of extrapolation. Default: 1.

parameters.name: Exclude
parameters.desc: Equations to exclude from extrapolation (from Table 342).

parameters.name: MinStep
parameters.desc: Minimum step size for extrapolation to be applied.

parameters.name: LowDensityLimit
parameters.desc: Lower limit (cm-3) for carrier density starting from which extrapolation is applied. Default: 1e-100.

parameters.name: NumberOfFailures
parameters.desc: Number of failed extrapolations allowed. Default: 0.

contexts.Math: Global default extrapolation settings
contexts.Cyclic: Cyclic extrapolation with additional options (Average, Forward, MaxVal, MinVal, Factor, Print, QFtraps, IgnoreCircuit, IgnoreContact, etc.)

ExtrudeTo3D.description: For correlated variations in the statistical impedance field method (sIFM), internally extrudes 2D structures to three dimensions. Used as an option within RandomizedVariation in the Math section.

FDTD.description: Specifies the finite-difference time-domain (FDTD) optical solver. Controls tensor mesh generation by Sentaurus Mesh, including options to generate mesh once or at each wavelength change.

parameters.name: GenerateMesh
parameters.desc: Control tensor mesh generation: Once (generate once), ForEachWavelength (regenerate on wavelength change), Wavelength=(list) (specify wavelength breakpoints).

FEPolarization.description: Activates the ferroelectric polarization model using the Ginzburg-Landau-Khalatnikov (GLK) equation. Must be specified in regionwise or materialwise Physics sections for insulator materials, and also added to the Solve section. Transient simulations are recommended to emulate the physical process of polarization evolution. The model solves for the polarization vector field as an additional equation coupled with Poisson.

parameters.name: Direction
parameters.desc: Direction of the ferroelectric polarization component being solved (default depends on dimensionality).

parameters.name: PMIModel
parameters.desc: Use Landau expansion PMI model.

parameters.name: SFactor
parameters.desc: Dataset name for initial polarization spatial distribution.

parameters.name: Tensor
parameters.desc: Enable anisotropic formulation of the model.

contexts.Physics: Activate ferroelectric model in region/material
contexts.Solve: Add FEPolarization to Coupled equation set for solving
contexts.Plot: FEPolarization/Vector or FEPolarization/Element/Vector for visualization

FEScalar.description: Finite element scalar option in the Math section. Controls scalar finite element discretization settings for specific equation types or solution variables.

FEVectorial.description: In the Math section, selects the vectorial finite element formulation for solving the electromagnetic wave equation. Used in optical simulations (BPM, FDTD) where the full vector nature of the electromagnetic field must be resolved.

FGCap.description: Specifies an additional capacitance between a floating contact and a control contact in the Electrode section. Used for 2D EEPROM simulations to account for additional capacitance from the real 3D layout. The capacitance modifies the floating-contact boundary condition equation. Units depend on dimensionality: F/um^2 (1D), F/um (2D), F (3D).

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Capacitance value between floating contact and control contact.

parameters.name: name
parameters.desc: Name of the control electrode (reference contact).

FGcap.description: Alias for FGCap. Specifies an additional capacitance between a floating contact and a control contact. See FGCap for full documentation.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Capacitance value between floating contact and control contact.

parameters.name: name
parameters.desc: Name of the control electrode.

Factor.description: Coefficient for averaged extrapolation factor estimation in Cyclic analysis. Used within Extrapolate in the Cyclic section of Solve. Controls the relaxation of the averaged factor used for cyclic extrapolation.

parameters.name: Factor
parameters.desc: Coefficient f for averaged factor estimation. Default: 1.

contexts.Cyclic: Within Extrapolate sub-option of Cyclic analysis

FarField.signature: Farfield( Origin | Discretization | ObservationRadius | Sensor | SensorSweep )
FarField.description: Activates the far-field and sensor feature for raytracing optical simulations. Controls the observation hemisphere discretization and angular sensor definitions for light extraction analysis.

parameters.name: Origin
parameters.desc: Far-field origin: Auto (center of device, default) or a vector (x,y,z) in um.

parameters.name: Discretization
parameters.desc: Angular discretization. Default: 360 for 2D, 36 for 3D.

parameters.name: ObservationRadius
parameters.desc: Observation radius in um. Default: 1e6 (1 meter).

parameters.name: Sensor
parameters.desc: Define a sensor detector with Rectangle (3D), Line (2D), or Angular specification.

parameters.name: SensorSweep
parameters.desc: Define a sensor sweep with Ndivisions, VaryPhi, or VaryTheta.

FarFieldPlot.description: Generates far-field radiation pattern plots for optical simulations, typically used in LED or VCSEL device simulations to visualize angular emission characteristics.

Farfield.description: Alias for FarField. Activates the far-field and sensor feature for raytracing optical simulations. See FarField for full documentation.

parameters.name: Origin
parameters.desc: Far-field origin: Auto (default) or a vector (x,y,z) in um.

parameters.name: Discretization
parameters.desc: Angular discretization. Default: 360 for 2D, 36 for 3D.

parameters.name: ObservationRadius
parameters.desc: Observation radius in um. Default: 1e6 (1 meter).

Fermi.description: Activates Fermi-Dirac statistics instead of default Boltzmann statistics for carrier concentration computations. Used as an option within Mobility models (e.g., BalMob, eSubband, hSubband, hSixband) to account for carrier degeneracy effects on mobility at high doping or high carrier concentrations.

FieldEnhan.description: Parameters of the electric field-dependent terms of the Si-H bond energy and activation energy in the trap degradation model. Four values specify delta_parallel, rho_parallel, delta_perpendicular, and rho_perpendicular. Used within Traps in the Physics section.

parameters.name: FieldEnhan
parameters.desc: Four floats: (delta_par, rho_par, delta_perp, rho_perp) for field-dependent Si-H bond energy and activation energy. Default: (0 1 0 1). Units: eV, cm/V, eV, cm/V.

FieldFromInsulator.description: Uses the insulator electric field instead of the semiconductor electric field for multistate configurations (MSCs) defined at the semiconductor-insulator interface. Used within MSConfigs in the Physics section.

FieldFromMaterial.description: Specifies the material from which the electric field is obtained for hydrogen reaction rate calculations. Used within HydrogenReaction in the Physics section.

parameters.name: FieldFromMaterial
parameters.desc: Material name where the electric field is obtained.

FieldFromRegion.description: Specifies the region from which the electric field is obtained for hydrogen reaction rate calculations. Used within HydrogenReaction in the Physics section.

parameters.name: FieldFromRegion
parameters.desc: Region name where the electric field is obtained.

Fieldweighted.description: Uses field-weighted refractive index in the propagation plane as the reference refractive index for the beam propagation method (BPM) optical solver. One of several options for choosing the reference refractive index.

Filament.description: Defines conductive particle types in KMC-ReRAM simulations. Particles defined as Filament1 to Filament3 are conductive and considered in the conductance equation. Filaments can have exclusion relationships (cannot share location) or shared capacity relationships with other particles.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.desc: Name of the filament particle.

parameters.name: Exclude
parameters.desc: List of excluded particle types that cannot share the same location.

parameters.name: ShareCapacity
parameters.desc: List of shared capacity values with other particle types.

FilamentGrowth.description: Defines filament growth events in KMC-ReRAM simulations. Specifies the growth of Filament{i} from Particle{j} with Arrhenius-type rate parameters (activation energy, frequency factor) and optional dipole moment.

parameters.name: Ea
parameters.desc: Activation energy for the growth event.

parameters.name: Frequency
parameters.desc: Maximum rate (frequency factor) of the growth event.

parameters.name: Dipole
parameters.desc: Dipole moment for the growth event.

parameters.name: Particle
parameters.desc: Source particle from which the filament grows.

FilamentRecession.description: KMC (Kinetic Monte Carlo) event keyword defining the recession process of filamentary conductors. Specifies an event where Filament{i} degrades into Particle{j}, belonging to physical event definitions in the KMCDefects section. Event rate is computed using the Arrhenius formula, involving activation energy Ea, molecular dipole moment Dipole, and maximum rate Frequency.

parameters.name: Dipole
parameters.desc: Molecular dipole moment parameter for electric field-enhanced rate calculation.

parameters.name: Ea
parameters.desc: Activation energy (eV) determining the energy barrier for the thermally activated process.

parameters.name: Frequency
parameters.desc: Pre-exponential factor of the maximum rate (diffusion rate).

parameters.name: Particle{j}
parameters.desc: Particle type generated after recession.

File.description: Specifies input and output file names. Global File sections use (g) keywords; device-specific File sections use (d) keywords. Supports grid files, parameter files, plot files, and many specialized output types.

parameters.name: Grid
parameters.desc: (d) Device geometry and mesh file (input .tdr, or @tdr@).

parameters.name: Plot
parameters.desc: Spatially distributed simulation results (output .tdr, @tdrdat@).

parameters.name: Current
parameters.desc: Device currents, voltages, charges, temperatures (output .plt, @plot@).

parameters.name: Output
parameters.desc: (g) Runtime log file (output .log, @log@). Default is 'output'.

parameters.name: Parameters
parameters.desc: (d) Device parameter file (input .par, @parameter@).

parameters.name: Save
parameters.desc: Simulation results for retrieval with Load (output .sav).

File.example:
File {
  Grid="nmos.tdr"
  Parameter="nmos.par"
  Plot="nmos_des"
  Current="nmos_plt"
  Output="nmos_log"
}

FilePrefix.description: Specifies the output filename prefix in Plot(), Save(), SpectralPlot(), and CurrentPlot() statements of the Solve section. The full filename consists of the prefix, instance name, optional local index, and extension. Default prefix: save<globalsaveindex> (Save) and plot<globalplotindex> (Plot).

parameters.name: FilePrefix
parameters.desc: Output filename prefix string.

Filename.description: PMI (Physics Model Interface) base class member function returning the parameter filename associated with the current PMI model. Provided as a read-only property in the PMI C++ interface. Can also be used in optical simulations to specify the filename for loading/saving optical generation rates.

contexts.Physics: PMI C++ interface member function

FinalTime.description: Specifies the end time (unit: s) for transient simulation in the Transient statement. Together with InitialTime, InitialStep, MaxStep, MinStep and other parameters, controls the time range and step size of transient simulations.

parameters.name: FinalTime
parameters.desc: Transient simulation end time (s).

Finish.description: Specifies Tcl code to execute last at each plotting time point in CurrentPlot Tcl formulas. For post-processing purposes; can access Tcl list results containing final current plot values and coordinates. When the operation is Minimum, Maximum, Average, or Integrate, the coordinates are the corresponding extremum or centroid coordinates.

parameters.name: Finish
parameters.desc: Tcl code string to execute last at each plotting time point.

FirstOrder.description: Selects the first-order model in the Tensor option of the Piezoresistance mobility model. Default value. Uses first-order piezoresistive coefficients to calculate the effect of stress on mobility; coefficients can be modified in the Piezoresistance section of the parameter file. Corresponds to the SecondOrder second-order model.

Flatten.description: Global parameter inheritance mode in the Math section. When set to Flatten (default), region-level parameters inherit materialwise parameter settings, enabling hierarchical parameter merging. When set to None, region parameter settings override materialwise settings.

FlickerGRNoise.description: Enables the bulk flicker noise (1/f noise) model in the Noise section. Based on the Hooge formula with a 1/f noise spectral density. This model does not use the GR and trap models actually activated in the simulation, so it is not a physical flicker noise model. Parameters e_alpha_H/h_alpha_H (fitting coefficients), e_tau0/h_tau0 and e_tau1/h_tau1 (time constants) can be modified in the parameter file.

parameters.name: e_alpha_H
parameters.desc: Electron flicker noise Hooge coefficient, default 2e-3.

parameters.name: h_alpha_H
parameters.desc: Hole flicker noise Hooge coefficient, default 2e-3.

parameters.name: e_tau0
parameters.desc: Electron short time constant (s), default 1e-6.

parameters.name: h_tau0
parameters.desc: Hole short time constant (s), default 1e-6.

parameters.name: e_tau1
parameters.desc: Electron long time constant (s), default 3e-4.

parameters.name: h_tau1
parameters.desc: Hole long time constant (s), default 3e-4.

Fluctuation.description: Random fluctuation analysis concepts. Sentaurus Device handles noise analysis, statistical fluctuation analysis, and sensitivity analysis uniformly through the impedance field method. Supports random doping fluctuation (Doping), random geometric fluctuation (GeometricFluctuations), random trap concentration fluctuation (TrapConcentration), random workfunction fluctuation (WorkfunctionFluctuations), and deterministic doping variation (DeterministicVariation).

FluxBC.description: Specifies zero-flux boundary conditions for the SingletExciton equation in the Physics RegionInterface section. By default, the singlet exciton equation uses natural boundary conditions at interfaces where it is solved on only one side. Specifying FluxBC switches to zero-flux boundary condition nabla_n_se . n_hat = 0, preventing exciton flow across the interface.

ForEachWavelength.description: Enables wavelength-adaptive mesh generation in the FDTD (Finite-Difference Time-Domain) optical solver's GenerateMesh section. Automatically regenerates the tensor mesh whenever the excitation wavelength changes from the previous FDTD solve. A strictly monotonically increasing wavelength list can be specified so that the mesh is only updated when the wavelength enters a new interval.

parameters.name: Wavelength
parameters.desc: Wavelength list (unit: um), strictly monotonically increasing, used to constrain mesh update intervals.

Formula.description: Specifies Tcl code in a CurrentPlot Tcl formula that is executed at each plot time point to compute current plot values. Can contain arbitrary Tcl expressions and command calls, using functions like tcl_lp_ReadScalar to read scalar data. Used in conjunction with Init, Finish, and other options.

parameters.name: Formula
parameters.desc: Tcl code string for computing current plot values.

contexts.Solve: CurrentPlot Tcl formula option
contexts.Physics: PMI formula temperature dependence and gamma factor
contexts.Math: Formula parameter specification

Forward.description: In Cyclic() large-signal periodic analysis, specifies that periodic extrapolation is not used and the simulation proceeds in standard transient mode. One of the Extrapolate options, corresponding to extrapolation methods such as Average.

ForwardReactionCoef.description: Specifies the forward reaction coefficient (depassivation coefficient v0) in the hydrogen transport degradation model. This coefficient describes the Si-H bond breaking rate under passivation conditions (passivation temperature T0), in units of s^-1.

parameters.name: ForwardReactionCoef
parameters.desc: Forward reaction coefficient (s^-1), pre-exponential factor for Si-H bond depassivation rate.

ForwardReactionEnergy.description: Specifies the activation energy (eV) of the forward reaction in the hydrogen transport degradation model. Together with ForwardReactionCoef, determines the depassivation reaction rate.

parameters.name: ForwardReactionEnergy
parameters.desc: Forward reaction activation energy (eV).

ForwardReactionFieldCoef.description: Specifies the electric field dependence coefficient of the forward reaction in the hydrogen transport degradation model. This parameter controls the enhancement effect of the electric field on the depassivation reaction rate.

parameters.name: ForwardReactionFieldCoef
parameters.desc: Forward reaction electric field coefficient.

Fowler.description: Enables the Fowler-Nordheim (FN) tunneling model in the Physics MaterialInterface section. FN tunneling is a special case of direct tunneling and nonlocal tunneling models where electrons tunnel into the oxide conduction band. The model is simple and efficient, suitable for describing EEPROM erase operations. Optional EVB keyword enables FN tunneling of valence band electrons.

FowlerNordheim.description: Fowler-Nordheim tunneling current density (jFN) at each vertex, available as a plot dataset (unit: A/cm^2). Represents the field-emission tunneling current through thin insulating barriers. The tunneling model is activated within GateCurrent() using the Fowler keyword.

FowlerNordheimEnhan.description: Specifies Fowler-Nordheim tunneling enhancement parameters in the Traps degradation model. Four floating-point values: delta_FN and rho_FN, used to modify the depassivation constant of the degradation model, accounting for the enhancement effect of FN tunneling on the Si-H bond breaking rate. Default value is (1 1).

parameters.name: delta_FN
parameters.desc: FN tunneling enhancement parameter delta.

parameters.name: rho_FN
parameters.desc: FN tunneling enhancement parameter rho.

Fraction.description: Scalar data name representing trap occupancy fraction (TTOMFraction) or other ratio-type data. In the PMI interface, Fraction is also used as a method parameter name. In optical simulation, Fraction represents absorption ratio. Meaning is highly context-dependent.

contexts.Plot: Scalar data name (trap occupancy fraction or similar ratio data)
contexts.Physics: PMI interface method parameter

FranzDispersion.description: Enables the Franz dispersion relation in the Band2Band (band-to-band tunneling) NonlocalPath model. Disabled by default. When enabled, the Franz dispersion relation (rather than the Kane dispersion relation) is used to calculate the imaginary wave vector during direct tunneling. Indirect tunneling processes are unaffected by this option.

Frensley.description: Enables the Frensley rule in BalMob (ballistic mobility model) to compute the final mobility. The Frensley rule corrects the ballistic mobility based on Fermi-Dirac statistics, replacing the default Maxwell-Boltzmann treatment. Requires the Fermi option.

Frequency.description: Specifies the analysis frequency in the Solve section's HBCoupled (harmonic balance) and ACCoupled (small-signal AC analysis). Also used as the maximum rate parameter for diffusion events in KMCDefects. In noise analysis, it is the frequency domain variable.

parameters.name: Frequency
parameters.desc: Frequency value (Hz) or frequency specification.

contexts.Solve: HBCoupled/ACCoupled frequency specification
contexts.Physics: KMCDefects diffusion rate parameter

Fresnel.description: Enables Fresnel boundary conditions in the Physics RayTraceBC section. At material interfaces, reflectance and transmittance are automatically calculated using Fresnel equations without manual specification. Applicable to optical raytracing simulations at material interfaces.

From.description: Specifies the start time of transient simulation in the Solve section's Transient statement. Together with FinalTime, defines the time range. Also used for parameter file reading in the Math section and specifying data sources in the PMI interface.

parameters.name: From
parameters.desc: Transient simulation start time (s).

contexts.Solve: Transient time range parameter
contexts.Math: PMI interface data source

FromElementGrid.description: Specifies that data should be read from or interpolated on the element grid rather than the vertex grid. Used for certain numerical operations requiring element-centered quantities.

FromFile.description: Optical solver that loads absorbed photon density or optical generation profiles from file. More flexible than OpticalGeneration(ReadFromFile), supporting illumination spectra and parameter ramping. Requires ComputeFromMonochromaticSource or ComputeFromSpectrum in the OpticalGeneration section. Supports DatasetName selection, spectral interpolation, and user-defined parameters. Works with TMM, OptBeam, and WeightedAPDintegration features.

Frozen.description: Freezes the SHE (Spherical Harmonics Expansion) carrier energy distribution function in the Solve section's Set statement. Once frozen, the distribution function remains unchanged during simulation and is not updated by post-processing. Use -Frozen to unfreeze. Can also freeze polytope configuration states in the Math section's MSConfigs.

contexts.Solve: Freeze/unfreeze SHE distribution in Set statement
contexts.Math: Freeze polytope configuration states in MSConfigs

Full.description: Specifies the full wireframe integration mode for weighted absorbed photon density (APD) integration. Contrasts with Auto (hybrid wireframe and 1D integration). Full mode uses wireframe integration for all layers to compute absorbed photon density. Can also be used as a modifier for various model options (e.g., FullLayer), indicating full/comprehensive mode.

FullBand.description: In eSHEDistribution() or hSHEDistribution(), enables the use of full band-structure data from empirical pseudopotential method for the Spherical Harmonics Expansion (SHE) distribution model. Optionally accepts a custom band-structure file path. Default files (e.g., eSHEBandSilicon.dat) are provided in $STROOT/tcad/$STRELEASE/lib/sdevice/MaterialDB/she. For SiGe regions, mole-fraction-dependent files are automatically selected with linear interpolation.

parameters.name: = <string>
parameters.desc: Optional user-defined band-structure file path. If omitted, default band file is used.

FullLayer.description: In Math section, specifies that the parallel-to-interface field computation extends to all elements touching an interface by a face, edge, or vertex. The default is PartialLayer which only applies to elements connected by an edge (2D) or face (3D). Part of the ParallelToInterfaceInBoundaryLayer switch for field correction near interfaces.

FullPhuMob.description: In IALMob(), enables the full PhuMob mobility expressions including the 'other' carrier contribution in the Coulomb scattering model. Enabled by default. Specify -FullPhuMob to ignore the other carrier contribution (c_other = 0) and the standard PhuMob modification of the G(P) function.

Function.description: Not a standalone SDevice command keyword. Appears in PMI (Physical Model Interface) C++ programming context as a general term for PMI model functions (e.g., PMI_HeatGenerationFunction, PMI_SpatialDistributionFunction). Also appears in floating gate charge computation and quasi-Fermi potential contexts. Not a user-facing command file keyword.

contexts.PMI: C++ PMI interface function typedef and extern declarations

GMRES.description: Generalized Minimal Residual linear solver. Requires Method=ILS in Coupled/HBCoupled. Uses an iterative Krylov subspace method to solve the linear system within Newton iterations. Parameters control the maximum iterations, subspace restart size, and convergence tolerance.

contexts.Solve: Used in Coupled{Method=ILS} or HBCoupled{Method=ILS}

Gain.description: In File section, specifies the base name for modal stimulated and spontaneous emission spectra output file (_gain_des.plt). Used in LED simulations. Also appears as a Plot data name (e.g., eDifferentialGain, hDifferentialGain) and in Physics{LED(Broadening(Gamma))} context.

parameters.name: Gain
parameters.desc: Base name for gain spectra output file (device-specific).

contexts.File: Output file keyword for gain spectra
contexts.Plot: Data name prefix for gain-related scalar/vector quantities

GainDep.description: In ComplexRefractiveIndex(), enables gain dependency of the complex refractive index. Two modes: linear (real) or logarithmic (real(log)) dependence. Used in LED and laser simulations where the refractive index changes with optical gain. Part of the optical model in the Physics section.

parameters.name: real
parameters.desc: Use linear gain dependency of refractive index.

parameters.name: real(log)
parameters.desc: Use logarithmic gain dependency of refractive index.

GainPlot.description: Configures parameters for gain spectrum plots in LED simulations, including the energy range and number of data points for each gain curve.

parameters.name: Intervals
parameters.desc: Number of points to plot for each gain curve.

parameters.name: Range
parameters.desc: Energy range in eV as (Emin, Emax), or 'Auto' for automatic range detection.

GainShift.description: In Physics{LED()}, shifts the effective emission wavelength (and therefore the stimulated and spontaneous emission spectra) by an energy amount specified in eV. Used to fine-tune emission spectra to match experimental data.

parameters.name: GainShift
parameters.desc: Energy shift amount in eV.

Gamma.description: In eQuantumPotential() and hQuantumPotential(), specifies the gamma factor for the density gradient quantization model. Gamma=EffectiveMass sets the quantization mass to the density-of-states mass. A PMI model can be used for spatially varying gamma.

contexts.Physics: Density gradient gamma factor

GateCurrent.description: Configures gate current models at material interfaces (e.g., Silicon/Oxide). Supports Fowler-Nordheim tunneling, direct tunneling (Schenk), lucky electron model (eLucky/hLucky), Fiegna model, SHE distribution model, and PMI-based hot-carrier injection models.

contexts.Physics: Gate current model specification at interfaces

GateName.description: In GateCurrent statement, specifies the name of the gate contact for monitoring tunneling current. Used for simple one-gate devices to restrict gate current computation to a specific electrode. The tunneling current is reported for this contact in the current plot file.

parameters.name: GateName
parameters.desc: Name of the gate electrode contact.

Gaussian.description: In HeavyIon(), uses a Gaussian spatial distribution for the charge generation track. In Traps(), specifies a Gaussian energetic distribution for trap energy levels. In SpatialShape options, uses a Gaussian shape function for spatial restriction.

GaussianDOS_full.description: Activates the full Gaussian density-of-states model for organic semiconductors. Uses Gaussian distributions for both conduction band (LUMO) and valence band (HOMO) states instead of standard parabolic band approximations. Parameters (Nt, sigmaDOS, E0) are specified in the GaussianDOS_full section of the parameter file. Requires Fermi statistics to be enabled globally.

GaussianDos_Full.description: Alias for GaussianDOS_full. Activates the full Gaussian density-of-states model for organic semiconductors. See GaussianDOS_full for details.

GaussianQuadrature.description: In Math{BroadeningIntegration(GaussianQuadrature(Order))}, uses Gaussian quadrature to integrate the broadening spectrum in LED simulations. Specifies the quadrature order for numerical integration of gain/spontaneous emission spectra.

GenerateMesh.description: In FDTD() optical solver specification, controls tensor mesh generation using Sentaurus Mesh. ForEachWavelength regenerates mesh when wavelength changes; Once generates mesh only at the beginning; Wavelength=(...) generates mesh only at specified wavelength transitions.

parameters.name: ForEachWavelength
parameters.desc: Generate tensor mesh whenever the wavelength changes.

parameters.name: Once
parameters.desc: Generate tensor mesh once at the beginning.

parameters.name: Wavelength
parameters.desc: List of strictly monotonically increasing wavelengths between which the tensor mesh is generated only once.

Geometric.description: Appears in multiple contexts: (1) In Math section as GeometricDistances switch for enhanced distance computation; (2) In RandomizedVariation/IFM as Geometric() for random geometric surface variations; (3) In Math section as GeometricDistances for MLDA mobility model; (4) General term in noise/fluctuation/sensitivity contexts. Not a single unified keyword but a prefix appearing in several distinct keywords.

contexts.Math: GeometricDistances switch

GeometricDistances.description: In Math section, activates enhanced distance and normal-to-interface computation for mobility models and MLDA (Modified Local-Density Approximation). Improves accuracy of interface distance calculations used by mobility degradation models and MLDA quantum correction.

GeometricFluctuations.description: In Noise() section, defines random geometric fluctuations on a named surface for noise analysis. Surface positions are correlated according to a Gaussian function with correlation length lambda. Options controls whether variation applies to both sides (0) or semiconductor side only (1) of the interface.

parameters.name: "<surface_name>"
parameters.desc: Name of the surface on which geometric fluctuations are defined.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.desc: 0 (default) applies to both sides; 1 applies to semiconductor side only.

parameters.name: WeightQuantumPotential
parameters.desc: 0.5 Interpolation coefficient for density-gradient quantum correction term.

parameters.name: WeightDielectric
parameters.desc: 0 Interpolation coefficient for dielectric term.

GeometricVariation.description: Specifies deterministic geometric (surface position) variations for sensitivity analysis. Defines amplitude and direction of surface displacement with interpolation options for dielectric and quantum potential terms.

Goal.description: Defines the target values for ramping in a Quasistationary command. Goals can specify contact voltages, currents, charges, quasi-Fermi potentials in doping wells, circuit node voltages, or physical model parameters. Multiple Goal statements can be used to ramp several quantities simultaneously.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.desc: Contact name to ramp (or Regexp pattern for matching multiple contacts)

parameters.name: Voltage
parameters.desc: Target voltage value [V] for the contact

parameters.name: Current
parameters.desc: Target current value [A] for the contact

parameters.name: Node
parameters.desc: Circuit node name to ramp (mixed mode)

parameters.name: Parameter
parameters.desc: Circuit model parameter to ramp, e.g. r1."resistance"

parameters.name: Value
parameters.desc: Target value for parameter or node ramping

parameters.name: Charge
parameters.desc: Target charge in C.

GrDistance.signature: Grading ( GrDistance=<float> RegionInterface=(<string>*2) xFraction=<float> yFraction=<float> ) | GrDistance = <float>
GrDistance.description: In MoleFraction() section, specifies the distance in um in the direction normal to the boundaries of the specified regions where linear interpolation of mole fractions from the specified constant value to zero occurs. Used for graded heterojunction modeling. Also appears in the Grading() sub-statement for interface-specific grading control.

parameters.name: GrDistance
parameters.desc: Distance in um for mole fraction grading transition.

parameters.name: RegionInterface
parameters.desc: Restrict grading to the given interface (applied to all by default).

parameters.name: xFraction
parameters.desc: Boundary x-mole fraction value.

parameters.name: yFraction
parameters.desc: Boundary y-mole fraction value.

Grad2PoECACGreenFunction.description: Scalar plot data name. The imaginary part of the second derivative of the electron-conduction Poisson Green function with respect to AC frequency. Used for small-signal AC noise analysis. Unit: V^2*s^2*C^-2*cm^-2.

Grad2PoHCACGreenFunction.description: Scalar plot data name. The imaginary part of the second derivative of the hole-conduction Poisson Green function with respect to AC frequency. Used for small-signal AC noise analysis. Unit: V^2*s^2*C^-2*cm^-2.

GradPoECImACGreenFunction.description: Vector plot data name. The imaginary part of the gradient of the electron-conduction Poisson Green function with respect to AC frequency. Used for small-signal AC noise analysis. Unit: V*s*C^-1*cm^-1.

GradPoECReACGreenFunction.description: Vector plot data name. The real part of the gradient of the electron-conduction Poisson Green function with respect to AC frequency. Used for small-signal AC noise analysis. Unit: V*s*C^-1*cm^-1.

GradPoETImACGreenFunction.description: Vector plot data name. The imaginary part of the gradient of the electron-transport Poisson Green function with respect to AC frequency. Used for small-signal AC noise analysis. Unit: A^-1*cm^-1.

GradPoETReACGreenFunction.description: Vector plot data name. The real part of the gradient of the electron-transport Poisson Green function with respect to AC frequency. Used for small-signal AC noise analysis. Unit: A^-1*cm^-1.

GradPoHCImACGreenFunction.description: Vector plot data name. The imaginary part of the gradient of the hole-conduction Poisson Green function with respect to AC frequency. Used for small-signal AC noise analysis. Unit: V*s*C^-1*cm^-1.

GradPoHCReACGreenFunction.description: Vector plot dataset: gradient of the Green's function for hole current with respect to contact HC, real part, AC Green's function. Used in NoisePlot for noise analysis.

GradPoHTImACGreenFunction.description: Vector plot dataset: gradient of the Green's function for hole temperature with respect to contact HT, imaginary part, AC Green's function. Used in NoisePlot for noise analysis.

GradPoHTReACGreenFunction.description: Vector plot dataset: gradient of the Green's function for hole temperature with respect to contact HT, real part, AC Green's function. Used in NoisePlot for noise analysis.

GradQuasiFermi.description: Gradient of quasi-Fermi potential driving force for carrier transport. Used in Avalanche() as the default driving force for impact ionization, in high-field saturation mobility models, and as a vector plot dataset (eGradQuasiFermi, hGradQuasiFermi). Can be interpolated to zero field for numerical stability via RefDens parameters in the Math section.

contexts.Physics: driving force option in Avalanche() and mobility models
contexts.Math: interpolation parameters (RefDens_*) for GradQuasiFermi driving force
contexts.Plot: vector plot datasets eGradQuasiFermi, hGradQuasiFermi

Grading.description: In Physics{} for thermoelectric or material property specification, defines graded material composition or properties that vary spatially across the device structure. Used for thermoelectric materials and compositionally graded alloys.

parameters.name: GrDistance
parameters.desc: Distance in um normal to the specified interface for linear interpolation of mole fractions.

parameters.name: RegionInterface
parameters.desc: Two region names defining the interface to which grading is restricted.

parameters.name: xFraction
parameters.desc: Boundary xMoleFraction value (0-1).

parameters.name: yFraction
parameters.desc: Boundary yMoleFraction value (0-1).

Grain.description: In the Math section, triggers grain field generation for statistical impedance field method (sIFM) simulations. Used in RandomizedVariation to model metal grain structures for workfunction, band edge, conductivity, and dielectric constant randomization. Grain-related parameters (GrainProbability, GrainWorkfunction, GrainChi, GrainEg, GrainConductivity, GrainEpsilon) define per-grain property values and their occurrence probabilities.

contexts.Math: GrainFieldGenerator for grain field generation
contexts.Physics: RandomizedVariation sub-models (Workfunction, BandEdge, Conductivity, Epsilon)

GrainChi.description: In BandEdge() of RandomizedVariation(), specifies grain electron affinity (chi) values in eV. Each value corresponds to a grain type defined by GrainProbability. Used for Grain correlation function type.

GrainConductivity.description: In Conductivity() of RandomizedVariation(), specifies grain metal conductivity shift values in A/cmV. Each value corresponds to a grain type defined by GrainProbability. Values are adjusted to have zero average. Used for Grain correlation function type.

GrainEg.description: In BandEdge() of RandomizedVariation(), specifies grain bandgap values in eV. Each value corresponds to a grain type defined by GrainProbability. Default is 0. List size can be smaller than GrainProbability. Used for Grain correlation function type.

GrainEpsilon.description: In Epsilon() of RandomizedVariation(), specifies grain relative dielectric constant values. Each value corresponds to a grain type defined by GrainProbability. Used for Grain correlation function type.

GrainProbability.description: In RandomizedVariation() sub-models (Workfunction, BandEdge, Conductivity, Epsilon), specifies the probability for each grain type. Used for Grain correlation function type. The list defines how often each grain type occurs relative to others. All Grain* value lists must have matching or compatible sizes.

GrainWorkfunction.description: In Workfunction() of RandomizedVariation(), specifies grain metal workfunction values in eV. Each value corresponds to a grain type defined by GrainProbability. Values are adjusted to match the nominal workfunction average. Used for Grain correlation function type.

GreenFunctions.description: In NoisePlot{}, activates output of all used Green's functions and their gradients for noise analysis. Green's functions relate contact perturbations to potential and current responses throughout the device.

Grid.description: Refers to the computational mesh/grid used in device simulation. Grid files are loaded via the File section. Grid-related parameters appear in various contexts including mesh adaptation, optical solver discretization (BPM GridNodes), and plot output on grid data.

contexts.Math: GridAdaptation for mesh refinement
contexts.Physics: BPM GridNodes for optical solver discretization
contexts.Plot: grid-based dataset output

GridAdaptation.description: Controls grid adaptation/mesh refinement during simulation. Specifies criteria for automatic mesh refinement based on solution gradients or error indicators.

GridNodes.description: In BPM() optical solver, specifies the number of discretization grid nodes in each spatial dimension for the beam propagation method computation.

HB.description: Specifies global harmonic balance (HB) analysis options in the Math section. HB is a frequency-domain method for solving periodic or quasi-periodic time-dependent problems. Controls the analysis mode (MDFT or SDFT), convergence parameters, logarithmic variable transformations, and solve spectrum truncation. MDFT mode supports multitone analysis and requires CMI-compatible compact models.

parameters.name: MDFT
parameters.desc: Enable multidimensional Fourier transform mode for multitone analysis

parameters.name: LogDensity
parameters.desc: Use logarithmic density variables in HB Newton algorithm

parameters.name: LogTemperature
parameters.desc: Use logarithmic temperature variables in HB Newton algorithm

parameters.name: CNormPrint
parameters.desc: Print residuum, update error, and correction count per Newton step

parameters.name: SolveSpectrum
parameters.desc: Define a named solve spectrum for truncation: SolveSpectrum(Name="<name>") { (<h1> <h2>)... }

contexts.Math: global HB configuration
contexts.Solve: HBCoupled statement uses HB settings

HBCoupled.description: Performs harmonic balance (HB) analysis, a frequency-domain method for solving periodic or quasi-periodic time-dependent problems. Supports single-tone (SDFT mode) and multi-tone (MDFT mode) analysis. The Tone option is mandatory. More computationally efficient than transient analysis for problems with widely different time constants.

parameters.name: Tone
parameters.desc: Base frequency and number of harmonics, e.g. Tone(Frequency=1e9 NumberOfHarmonics=5)

parameters.name: Initialize
parameters.desc: Initialization method: DCMode (use DC solution as initial guess)

parameters.name: Method
parameters.desc: Linear solver: ILS or Direct

parameters.name: GMRES
parameters.desc: GMRES iterative solver options: Tolerance, MaxIterations, Restart

parameters.name: SolveSpectrum
parameters.desc: Reference to a predefined solve spectrum for spectrum truncation (MDFT mode)

parameters.name: Derivative
parameters.desc: Overwrite Derivative flag of Math section for HB Newton process

parameters.name: CNormPrint
parameters.desc: Print instance equation errors per Newton step

parameters.name: Name
parameters.desc: Name component of plot files

parameters.name: RhsScale
parameters.desc: Scaling of RHS in Newton

parameters.name: UpdateScale
parameters.desc: Scaling of update in Newton

parameters.name: ValueMin
parameters.desc: Lower bound for quantity in time domain (MDFT mode only)

parameters.name: ValueVariation
parameters.desc: Allowed variation of quantity in time domain (MDFT mode only)

contexts.Solve: HBCoupled solve statement
contexts.Math: HB global options referenced by HBCoupled

HBMode.description: Initialization option for HBCoupled: all harmonics are taken from the previous harmonic balance solution. Used as Initialize=HBMode in the HBCoupled statement.

HBPlot.description: Specifies quantities to plot from harmonic balance (HB) simulations. Controls output of circuit currents and voltages in both frequency domain (HB_F) and time domain (HB_T). Supports filtering by harmonic order and dataset names. Used in the Plot section and System section for circuit quantities.

contexts.Plot: HB output in device Plot section
contexts.System: HB output in System section for circuit quantities

HBPlotFilePerNewton.description: In the Math section, activates output of harmonic balance .plt files per converged Newton solve. Default is off (only final output). Used for debugging HB convergence behavior.

HB_F.description: Output file prefix for harmonic balance frequency-domain circuit quantities. Generated automatically by HBPlot in both SDFT and MDFT modes. Contains voltage, current, and other circuit quantities at each frequency point.

HB_T.description: Output file prefix for harmonic balance time-domain circuit quantities. Generated automatically by HBPlot in both SDFT and MDFT modes. Contains voltage, current, and other circuit quantities at each time point.

HCI.description: Hot-carrier injection option for thermionic emission models. When specified in Physics{} at a semiconductor-insulator interface, injects hot carriers locally at the interface rather than nonlocally across the insulator. Used with eThermionic(HCI) and hThermionic(HCI) for electrons and holes respectively. Also available as Thermionic(HCI) for both carrier types.

HCS.description: Hot-carrier stress degradation model for MOS-based devices. Models interface trap generation due to bond breakage from single-particle, multiple-particle, and field-enhanced thermal processes. Includes electron (eHCSDegradation) and hole (hHCSDegradation) versions with options for SHE carrier distribution and bond dispersion integral.

parameters.name: SHE
parameters.desc: Use carrier distribution function from SHE model

parameters.name: BondDispersion
parameters.desc: Use bond dispersion model for energy distribution of bond breaking

parameters.name: BDEnergyIntervals
parameters.desc: Number of energy intervals for bond dispersion integral (default: 20)

Harmonics.description: Refers to harmonic components in frequency-domain analysis (AC, HB, noise). Harmonics define the spectral content of periodic signals. In harmonic balance, the number of harmonics is specified by NumberOfHarmonics in the Tone parameter. In AC analysis, harmonics relate to frequency multipliers. Also used in scalar/vector plot data for harmonic-dependent quantities.

contexts.Solve: HBCoupled Tone(NumberOfHarmonics=...)
contexts.Math: HB SolveSpectrum harmonic definitions
contexts.Plot: harmonic-dependent plot datasets

Heat.description: Refers to heat-related physics in Sentaurus Device, including heat capacity, heat generation, heat flux, and thermal conduction. Part of the thermodynamic and hydrodynamic transport models. Key sub-topics include HeatCapacity (lattice heat capacity model), HeatPreFactor (scaling of lattice heat generation), HeatSource (PMI heat source), and RecGenHeat (generation-recombination heat sources). Also used in PMI Physical Model Interface for user-defined heat generation rate models.

contexts.Physics: heat-related physics models
contexts.Math: heat equation solver parameters

HeatCapacity.description: Specifies the lattice heat capacity model for thermodynamic simulations. Two options: constant (default) uses constant heat capacity, or a PMI model name for user-defined or multistate-configuration-dependent heat capacity. The MSC-dependent variant allows heat capacity to depend on multistate configuration occupation probabilities.

parameters.name: constant
parameters.desc: Use constant heat capacity (default)

parameters.name: <pmi_model_name>
parameters.desc: PMI model name for user-defined heat capacity

HeatFlux.description: Parameter set in the hydrodynamic model that specifies the heat flux correction factors for electrons (hf_n) and holes (hf_p). Used in the energy-dependent Peltier coefficient model to modify the heat flux factor Q as a function of carrier energy. Coefficients can also be defined as rational polynomial functions of normalized carrier energy.

HeatPreFactor.description: Scaling factor for lattice heat generation in the thermodynamic model. Default is 1.0. Setting to 0 disables the heat term, reducing coupling between the lattice heat equation and carrier continuity equations for easier convergence. Can be ramped from 0 to 1 using Quasistationary Goal ModelParameter ramping to gradually introduce self-heating.

parameters.name: HeatPreFactor
parameters.desc: Scaling factor (0-1), default 1.0. 0 disables heat generation.

HeatSource.description: Specifies a PMI (Physical Model Interface) model for custom heat source definitions in thermodynamic simulations. The PMI model computes the local heat generation rate, which is added to the lattice heat equation. Used in the Physics section of the command file.

HeavyHole.description: Specifies the heavy-hole valence band in wurtzite crystal structures for LED simulations. Used within hLadder() in the SchroedingerParameters section to classify the specific hole type (HH, LH, or CH) for labeling subband energies and overlapping integrals during visualization.

HeavyIon.description: Activates carrier generation by heavy ion impact for single-event upset (SEU) simulation. Defines the ion track geometry (direction, location, length), temporal profile (time, Gaussian width), linear energy transfer (LET_f), and spatial distribution (exponential or Gaussian). Supports user-defined ions with custom names. Typically used in transient simulations. The spatial distribution can be defined by a PMI model via SpatialShape.

parameters.name: Direction
parameters.desc: Direction vector of the ion track, e.g., (0,1) for 2D or (0,0,1) for 3D.

parameters.name: Location
parameters.desc: Entry point coordinates where the ion penetrates the device.

parameters.name: StartPoint
parameters.desc: Defines a one-directional track (generation only in positive direction from incidence point).

parameters.name: Time
parameters.desc: Moment of heavy ion penetration (s).

parameters.name: Length
parameters.desc: Track length (cm, or um if PicoCoulomb is specified). Can be an array for position-dependent values.

parameters.name: Wt_hi
parameters.desc: Characteristic radius of the spatial distribution (cm, or um if PicoCoulomb). Can be an array.

parameters.name: LET_f
parameters.desc: Linear energy transfer values (pairs/cm^3 or pC/um with PicoCoulomb). Can be an array for position-dependent values.

parameters.name: Exponential
parameters.desc: Use exponential spatial distribution (default).

parameters.name: Gaussian
parameters.desc: Use Gaussian spatial distribution.

parameters.name: PicoCoulomb
parameters.desc: Switch LET_f units to pC/um and Length/Wt_hi units to um.

parameters.name: SpatialShape
parameters.desc: Name of PMI model for user-defined spatial distribution function R(w,l,E).

HeavyIon.example:
Physics {
    HeavyIon (
        Direction=(0,1)
        Location=(1.5,0)
        Time=1.0e-13
        Length=1
        Wt_hi=3
        LET_f=0.2
        Gaussian
        PicoCoulomb
    )
}

HeavyIonChargeDensity.description: Plot dataset for the total charge density generated by heavy ion impact at each vertex. Unit: cm^-3. Used in the Plot section to visualize the spatial distribution of ion-induced charge. Related to HeavyIonGeneration which shows the instantaneous generation rate.

HeavyIonGeneration.description: Plot dataset for the instantaneous carrier generation rate by heavy ion impact at each vertex. Unit: cm^-3 s^-1. Defined by G_HeavyIon(l,w,t) = G_LET(l) * R(w,l) * T(t). Used in the Plot section to visualize the generation rate profile.

HeteroInterface.description: Activates double-point discretization at region interfaces for abrupt and graded heterojunction simulations. Each vertex at a heterointerface belongs exclusively to one of the two adjacent regions (region1 or region2), enabling discontinuous solutions across the interface. Essential for organic device simulations with thermionic emission models. Not compatible with IFM (impedance field method) or Thermionic option in noise analysis.

contexts.Physics: Interface-specific physics option for double-point discretization at heterojunctions.
contexts.OpticalDevice: Used in dual-grid raytracing setups to define optical device interfaces.

HighFieldSaturation.description: High-field saturation mobility model within Mobility() or eMobility()/hMobility(). Models the velocity saturation effect at high electric fields using Caughey-Thomas or TransferredElectronEffect formulations. The hole variant is hHighFieldSaturation.

Hole.description: Hole continuity equation solved in Sentaurus Device. In the Solve section, specifies that the hole continuity equation is to be solved. In equation error control (Table 342), the reference value is 1e10 cm^-3 with relative error tolerance of 1e-5.

Hooke.description: Option in the Piezo section to compute the strain tensor from the stress tensor using the generalized Hooke's law. The elastic compliance tensor relates strain to stress through contracted tensor notation. This is the default method for strain computation. Alternatives are specifying a constant strain tensor or loading from file.

HotElectronInj.description: Plot dataset for hot-electron injection current density at region-interface vertices. Unit: A/cm^2. Represents the thermionic emission current density j_he at interfaces, computed from the thermionic emission model. Available at rivertex location only.

HotHoleInj.description: Plot dataset for hot-hole injection current density at region-interface vertices. Unit: A/cm^2. Represents the thermionic emission current density j_hh at interfaces, computed from the thermionic emission model. Available at rivertex location only.

HuangRhys.description: Huang-Rhys coupling factor parameter used in trap-assisted tunneling models within the Traps section. Specifies the electron-phonon coupling strength for inelastic tunneling processes, affecting trap-to-trap and electrode-to-trap tunneling rates. Also used in KMC-MIM transport simulations for inelastic phonon trap-to-trap tunneling.

parameters.name: HuangRhys
parameters.desc: Huang-Rhys coupling factor (dimensionless), characterizing electron-phonon coupling strength.

contexts.Traps: Within Traps() for SRH trap-assisted tunneling with inelastic tunneling.
contexts.KMC_MIM_Transport: Within KMC_MIM_Transport() for inelastic phonon trap-to-trap tunneling.

Hurkx.signature: Lifetime = Hurkx | Model = Hurkx | Model = modifiedHurkx
Hurkx.description: Hurkx trap-assisted tunneling model for SRH field enhancement. Used in three contexts: (1) as Lifetime=Hurkx in SRH() ElectricField() for field-enhanced SRH lifetime; (2) as Model=Hurkx in Band2Band() for band-to-band tunneling; (3) as Lifetime=Hurkx in NonlocalPath() for dynamic nonlocal path trap-assisted tunneling. The modified Hurkx variant provides an improved version. Augments the standard SRH recombination with tunneling-enhanced generation-recombination at high electric fields.

contexts.SRH: Lifetime=Hurkx in SRH(ElectricField()) for field-enhanced SRH recombination.
contexts.Band2Band: Model=Hurkx in Band2Band() for Hurkx band-to-band tunneling model.
contexts.NonlocalPath: Lifetime=Hurkx in NonlocalPath() for dynamic nonlocal path TAT.

Hydrodynamic.description: Activates the hydrodynamic transport model for electrons and holes. Solves energy transport equations in addition to continuity equations, enabling carrier heating/cooling effects, energy-dependent mobility, and thermoelectric phenomena. Options (eTemperature) and (hTemperature) restrict the model to electrons or holes only. Default (no option) enables hydrodynamic for both carriers.

contexts.Physics: Global or region-specific physics option to activate hydrodynamic transport.
contexts.Solve: Requires corresponding equation selection in Coupled/Plugin statements.

Hydrogen.description: Deprecated or generic plot keyword for hydrogen species. In the scalar data table, hydrogen-related plot datasets include HydrogenAtom, HydrogenMolecule, HydrogenIon, HydrogenSpeciesA/B/C. The generic 'Hydrogen' keyword may refer to the collective hydrogen transport framework.

HydrogenAtom.description: Species keyword for hydrogen atom transport in the Solve section's Coupled statement. Activates the drift-diffusion equation for hydrogen atoms. Parameters (diffusivity, activation energy, etc.) are set in the HydrogenDiffusion section of the parameter file. The hydrogen atom density can be plotted and its concentration set via Set(). Supports PMI for diffusivity and alpha parameters.

HydrogenBoundary.description: Specifies boundary conditions for hydrogen species transport in degradation models. Each species can have a fixed boundary concentration or a reflective (homogeneous Neumann) condition.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.desc: Name of the hydrogen contact.

parameters.name: HydrogenAtom
parameters.desc: Boundary concentration in cm^-3, or 'reflective' for Neumann BC.

parameters.name: HydrogenIon
parameters.desc: Boundary concentration in cm^-3, or 'reflective' for Neumann BC.

parameters.name: HydrogenMolecule
parameters.desc: Boundary concentration in cm^-3, or 'reflective' for Neumann BC.

parameters.name: HydrogenSpeciesA
parameters.desc: Boundary concentration in cm^-3, or 'reflective' for Neumann BC.

parameters.name: HydrogenSpeciesB
parameters.desc: Boundary concentration in cm^-3, or 'reflective' for Neumann BC.

parameters.name: HydrogenSpeciesC
parameters.desc: Boundary concentration in cm^-3, or 'reflective' for Neumann BC.

HydrogenDiffusion.description: Activates hydrogen transport model for degradation simulations. Enables drift-diffusion transport equations for hydrogen atoms, molecules, and ions. Can optionally include HydrogenReaction arguments for chemical reactions between mobile hydrogen species and charge carriers. Transport parameters (diffusivity D_i, activation energy E_di, thermal diffusion prefactor, initial density, recombination rate) are specified per species in the parameter file. Interface-specific specification adds surface recombination terms.

HydrogenIon.description: Specifies hydrogen ion (H+) transport properties in the hydrogen diffusion model. Supports PMI models for the thermal diffusion prefactor (alpha) and Arrhenius diffusivity parameters.

contexts.Plot: Scalar dataset for hydrogen ion density (cm^-3) at vertices.
contexts.Solve: Transport equation keyword in Coupled statement.

HydrogenMolecule.description: Hydrogen molecule species (H2) for hydrogen diffusion simulations. Used in Physics{HydrogenDiffusion()} to configure transport of molecular hydrogen. Must be coupled in the Solve section via Coupled{...HydrogenMolecule}. Plotted as HydrogenMolecule in the Plot section.
