contexts.Plot: Scalar dataset for hydrogen molecule density (cm^-3) at vertices.
contexts.Solve: Transport equation keyword in Coupled statement.

HydrogenReaction.description: Defines chemical reactions between mobile hydrogen species (atoms, ions, molecules, species A/B/C) and charge carriers (electrons, holes) in hydrogen transport simulations. Specifies forward/reverse reaction coefficients, activation energies, field coefficients, and particle conservation.

HydrogenSpeciesA.description: Generic hydrogen species A in the MSC-Hydrogen Transport Degradation Model. Used as a plot dataset (unit: cm^-3) at each vertex, as a transport equation keyword in Solve Coupled statement, and as a boundary condition species in HydrogenBoundary. Species A/B/C are user-defined additional hydrogen species for complex degradation models involving more than the standard three species (atom, molecule, ion). Charge and hydrogen composition can be customized in the parameter file.

HydrogenSpeciesB.description: Generic hydrogen species B in the MSC-Hydrogen Transport Degradation Model. Used as a plot dataset (unit: cm^-3) at each vertex, as a transport equation keyword in Solve Coupled statement, and as a boundary condition species in HydrogenBoundary. Species B is a user-defined additional hydrogen species for complex degradation models.

HydrogenSpeciesC.description: Generic hydrogen species C in the MSC-Hydrogen Transport Degradation Model. Used as a plot dataset (unit: cm^-3) at each vertex, as a transport equation keyword in Solve Coupled statement, and as a boundary condition species in HydrogenBoundary. Species C is a user-defined additional hydrogen species for complex degradation models.

IALMob.description: Inversion and Accumulation Layer Mobility model. Computes mobility degradation at semiconductor-insulator interfaces due to surface roughness and Coulomb scattering. Options include auto-orientation for parameter selection, clustering, full PhuMob expressions, and phonon combination modes.

IFM.description: IFM (Impedance Field Method) section for noise analysis, fluctuation analysis, and sensitivity analysis. Configures noise sources (Noise), randomized variations for statistical IFM (RandomizedVariation), and deterministic variations (DeterministicVariation). Used in conjunction with ACCoupled in the Solve section and ObservationNode to specify analysis targets.

ILS.description: Iterative Linear Solver used for solving linear systems in device simulation. The default solver for 3D structures (with set(1)), while SUPER is default for 1D/2D. Supports preconditioning, ordering, and iterative options configurable via ILSrc in the Math section. Supports MultipleRHS for AC analysis. May require extended precision or specialized preconditioners for MPBC simulations.

ILSrc.description: Configuration string for the ILS (Iterative Linear Solver) in the Math section. Defines the solver set with parameters including iterative method (GMRES), tolerance, preconditioning (ILUT), ordering (symmetric ND, nonsymmetric MPSILST), and solver options (compact, linscale, fit, refinebasis, refineresidual, verbose). Multiple solver sets can be defined and referenced by number.

Iadapt.description: Minimum current threshold (in A) that must be reached before the adaptive algorithm is switched on during I-V curve continuation. Used in the Quasistationary continuation method to prevent backtracking at low biases where current is small and noisy. From MinCurrent to Iadapt, the simulation proceeds as a simple voltage ramping through a fixed-value resistor.

parameters.name: Iadapt
parameters.desc: Minimum current (A) before adaptive algorithm activates.

IbVd.description: Extraction variable for drain current (I_b * V_d) in mixed-mode simulations. Used within the Extraction option of Quasistationary statements to extract the product of base current and drain voltage during DC sweeps. The value is computed as part of the extraction file output.

IdVd.description: Drain current extraction curve for voltage-dependent extraction in Quasistationary ramp. Records drain current versus drain voltage during the ramp for output characteristic analysis.

IdVg.description: Drain current extraction curve versus gate voltage for voltage-dependent extraction in Quasistationary ramp. Records drain current versus gate voltage during the ramp for transfer characteristic analysis.

IdentifyingParameter.description: Specifies the parameter names or parameter paths used to uniquely identify optical generation profiles loaded from files. Required when multiple profiles with different parameter sets are loaded. Supports simple parameter names (e.g., Wavelength) or full paths (e.g., Optics/Excitation/Wavelength). User-defined parameters not native to Sentaurus Device can also be introduced.

parameters.name: <string>...
parameters.desc: Names or full paths of identifying parameters. Must be unique across all loaded profiles.

Ignore.description: General-purpose keyword used in various contexts to exclude or disregard specific entities, models, or computations. Its exact meaning depends on the section in which it is used.

contexts.Math: Math section context

IgnoreTdrUnits.description: When enabled, ignores TDR (Tecplot Data Record) units when loading data from .tdr files. By default, TDR units are taken into account and converted to Sentaurus Device units. This keyword applies to the Grid file and all other loaded files (e.g., Save/Load statements).

Illumination.description: Enables optical generation computation in the device simulation. Specified within the Optics section to activate illumination-related models including monochromatic sources, spectral illumination, ray tracing, and transfer matrix method solvers.

IlluminationSpectrum.description: Specifies the file name of the illumination spectrum for spectral (white light) optical generation computation. The file uses a two-column format (wavelength in um and intensity in W/cm^2) or a multidimensional format with additional parameters. Used together with ComputeFromSpectrum in the OpticalGeneration section.

parameters.name: <string>
parameters.desc: Path to the illumination spectrum file. Comment lines start with # or *.

ImConductionCurrentResponse.description: Plots the imaginary part of the small-signal AC conduction current density response Im(Jn+Jp). Used in AC analysis to visualize the frequency-dependent conduction current response.

ImDisplacementCurrentResponse.description: Plots the imaginary part of the small-signal AC displacement current density response Im(JD). Used in AC analysis to visualize the frequency-dependent displacement current response.

ImElectrostaticPotentialResponse.description: Plots the imaginary part of the small-signal AC electrostatic potential response Im(phi). Used in AC analysis to visualize the frequency-dependent potential response.

ImLNISD.description: Plots the imaginary part of the local noise current spectral density (Im(LNISD)). Related to electron-electron scattering noise contribution.

ImLNVXVSD.description: Plots the imaginary part of the local noise voltage spectral density (Im(LNVXVSD)). Related to noise voltage fluctuations.

ImLatticeTemperatureResponse.description: Plots the imaginary part of the small-signal AC lattice temperature response Im(T). Used in AC analysis with thermal models to visualize frequency-dependent lattice temperature variation.

ImTotalCurrentResponse.description: Plots the imaginary part of the small-signal AC total current density response Im(Jn+Jp+JD). Used in AC analysis to visualize the combined conduction and displacement current response.

ImTrapLNISD.description: Plots the imaginary part of the local noise current spectral density from traps (Im(TrapLNISD)). Related to trap-assisted noise contributions.

ImTrapLNVSD.description: Plots the imaginary part of the local noise voltage spectral density from traps (Im(TrapLNVSD)). Related to trap-assisted noise voltage fluctuations.

ImeCurrentResponse.description: Plots the imaginary part of the small-signal AC electron current density response Im(Jn). Used in AC analysis to visualize the frequency-dependent electron current response.

ImeDensityResponse.description: Plots the imaginary part of the small-signal AC electron density response Im(n). Used in AC analysis to visualize the frequency-dependent electron carrier density variation.

ImeEnFluxResponse.description: Plots the imaginary part of the small-signal AC electron energy flux response Im(Sn). Used in AC analysis with energy transport models to visualize the frequency-dependent electron energy flux variation.

ImeTemperatureResponse.description: Plots the imaginary part of the small-signal AC electron temperature response Im(Tn). Used in AC analysis with hydrodynamic models to visualize the frequency-dependent electron temperature variation.

ImeeDiffusionLNVXVSD.description: Plots the imaginary part of the electron-electron diffusion local noise voltage spectral density (Im(eeDiffusionLNVXVSD)). Related to electron-electron scattering noise with diffusion mechanism.

ImeeFlickerGRLNVXVSD.description: Plots the imaginary part of the electron-electron flicker generation-recombination local noise voltage spectral density (Im(eeFlickerGRLNVXVSD)). Related to flicker noise with electron-electron scattering mechanism.

ImeeLNVXVSD.description: Plots the imaginary part of the electron-electron local noise voltage spectral density (Im(eeLNVXVSD)). Related to electron-electron scattering noise contributions.

ImeeMonopolarGRLNVXVSD.description: Plots the imaginary part of the electron-electron monopolar generation-recombination local noise voltage spectral density (Im(eeMonopolarGRLNVXVSD)). Related to monopolar GR noise with electron-electron scattering.

ImhCurrentResponse.description: Plots the imaginary part of the small-signal AC hole current density response Im(Jp). Used in AC analysis to visualize the frequency-dependent hole current response.

ImhDensityResponse.description: Plots the imaginary part of the small-signal AC hole density response Im(p). Used in AC analysis to visualize the frequency-dependent hole carrier density variation.

ImhEnFluxResponse.description: Plots the imaginary part of the small-signal AC hole energy flux response Im(Sp). Used in AC analysis with energy transport models to visualize the frequency-dependent hole energy flux variation.

ImhTemperatureResponse.description: Plots the imaginary part of the small-signal AC hole temperature response Im(Tp). Used in AC analysis with hydrodynamic models to visualize the frequency-dependent hole temperature variation.

ImhhDiffusionLNVXVSD.description: Plots the imaginary part of the hole-hole diffusion local noise voltage spectral density (Im(hhDiffusionLNVXVSD)). Related to hole-hole scattering noise with diffusion mechanism.

ImhhFlickerGRLNVXVSD.description: Plots the imaginary part of the hole-hole flicker generation-recombination local noise voltage spectral density (Im(hhFlickerGRLNVXVSD)). Related to flicker noise with hole-hole scattering mechanism.

ImhhLNVXVSD.description: Imaginary part of the hole-hole local noise voltage spectral density (LNVXVSD). A scalar plot quantity available for noise analysis output, representing the noise spectral density contribution from hole-hole interactions.

ImhhMonopolarGRLNVXVSD.description: Imaginary part of the hole-hole monopolar generation-recombination noise voltage spectral density (LNVXVSD). A scalar plot quantity for noise analysis, representing the monopolar GR noise contribution from hole-hole interactions.

ImlEnFluxResponse.description: Imaginary part of the lattice heat flux AC response (Im(SL)). A vector plot quantity available for AC analysis output, representing the imaginary component of the lattice thermal flux response to an AC signal.

ImplicitACSystem.description: Constructs an implicit AC system for single-device AC analysis without requiring an explicit System section. For each voltage-controlled electrode, an electrical circuit node is automatically created and connected to the device contact. The implicit AC system is built internally before the Solve section is executed.

ImportDomain.description: In OpticalGeneration{ReadFromFile()}, controls interpolation when loading optical generation profiles. Specifies source and destination regions, domain truncation boxes, and grid mapping between source and destination grids.

InBuilt.description: Use the internal geodesic distribution of starting rays from each active emission vertex cluster. A keyword value for IsotropyType in OutputLightToolsRays, which controls how LED starting rays are distributed when interfacing with LightTools.

IncompleteIonization.description: Activates the incomplete ionization model for dopants, accounting for the temperature-dependent ionization of donor and acceptor impurities. When enabled, not all dopants contribute free carriers, which affects carrier concentrations, mobility, and bandgap narrowing. Can be specified globally or per-region.

contexts.Mobility: IncompleteIonization in Mobility section enables incomplete-ionization-dependent mobility models.

IncompleteNewton.description: Enables the incomplete Newton method where the Jacobian matrix is reused across iterations instead of being recomputed each time. The Jacobian is reused if both RhsFactor and UpdateFactor conditions are satisfied. Can be specified globally in Math or locally as an option in Coupled statements.

parameters.name: RhsFactor
parameters.desc: Maximum allowed change in RHS (default 10) to reuse old Jacobian.

parameters.name: UpdateFactor
parameters.desc: Maximum allowed change in update (default 0.1) to reuse old Jacobian.

contexts.Coupled: Can also be specified inside Coupled{} solve statements.

Increment.description: Multiplier for the step size when the last step was successful in Quasistationary or Continuation commands. Default is 2.0 (1.5 with NewArc in Continuation). Controls how aggressively the step size increases after a converged solution.

parameters.name: Increment
parameters.desc: Step size multiplier on successful solve (default 2.0).

Index.description: Species index parameter used in Physical Model Interface (PMI) for multicomponent or multispecies models. Specifies the component index for reactions, species interfaces, and vertex/edge computations in PMI models.

IndiumActiveConcentration.description: Active (ionized) indium dopant concentration at each vertex. A scalar plot quantity representing the electrically active portion of indium doping, relevant when incomplete ionization is enabled.

IndiumConcentration.description: Total indium dopant concentration at each vertex. A scalar plot quantity representing the full (not just ionized) indium doping concentration in the semiconductor.

IndiumMinusConcentration.description: Negatively charged indium acceptor concentration at each vertex. A scalar plot quantity representing indium atoms that have accepted an electron (In-), relevant for incomplete ionization modeling.

InelasticPhonon.description: Selects the inelastic phonon transition mode for trap-to-trap tunneling (DiscreteTrapT2T). When specified in the Traps entry, tunneling between traps occurs via phonon-assisted inelastic transitions rather than elastic tunneling.

Init.description: Section in the command file for defining initialization variables and parameters. Used for setting initial conditions, domain selection, and parameter definitions that are referenced throughout the Sentaurus Device command file.

InitialStep.description: Initial step size for Quasistationary ramp parameter t. Default is 0.1. Controls the starting step size before adaptive stepping takes over based on convergence success or failure.

parameters.name: InitialStep
parameters.desc: Initial step size (default 0.1).

InitialTime.description: Starting time for transient simulation. Specifies the initial time point from which the transient analysis begins. Default depends on the context of the simulation.

parameters.name: InitialTime
parameters.desc: Initial time in seconds.

InitialVstep.description: Initial voltage step for Continuation simulation. Specifies the initial voltage increment applied to the continuation contact at the start of the continuation ramp.

parameters.name: InitialVstep
parameters.desc: Initial voltage step in V.

Initialize.description: Set circuit node voltage until the first transient solve. Similar to Hint but persists through transient analysis. Used in the System section to initialize circuit node values.

parameters.name: <ident>
parameters.desc: Circuit node name.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Initial voltage value.

contexts.Physics: Also appears in Physics section for PMI boundary condition initialization.

InjectionRegion.description: Specifies the destination region(s) to which hot carriers are injected. Used in the GateCurrent section of Physics to define where thermionic or hot-carrier injection current is delivered, enabling local injection into wide-bandgap semiconductor regions.

parameters.name: InjectionRegion
parameters.desc: Name of the region where hot carriers are injected.

InnerDevicePower.description: In BreakCriteria{}, defines a break criterion based on inner device power dissipation (W/um^d). The simulation stops when the inner device power exceeds the specified limits. Syntax mirrors DevicePower but computes only the inner power.

parameters.name: absval
parameters.desc: Upper limit for absolute power value (W/um^d-3).

Insert.description: Inserting files command for including external parameter files or command file snippets into the Sentaurus Device command file. Allows modular command file organization by inserting contents from referenced files.

Insulator.description: Built-in keyword indicating insulator regions in the device. Used as a location specifier in Physics and Plot sections to apply models or extract data specifically from insulator materials. Also refers to insulator-related plot data such as electric field on insulator regions.

contexts.Plot: Used as location qualifier for plot data extraction.

InsulatorElectricField.description: Electric field on insulator regions. Available as both a scalar plot quantity (vertex) and a vector plot quantity, representing the electric field magnitude and direction within insulator materials of the device.

Integrate.description: Computes the spatial integral of a specified quantity over a domain in the CurrentPlot section. The domain can be a region, material, semiconductor, or everywhere. Outputs the integrated value to the current plot file. Also used in Math section for tracking additional data.

contexts.CurrentPlot: Also available as a CurrentPlot operation for integration during simulation.

Integration.description: Refers to numerical integration operations throughout Sentaurus Device, including transient time integration schemes (Backward Euler, TRBDF), optical generation integration methods, and spatial integration of physical quantities. Context-dependent meaning based on the section in which it appears.

IntegrationUnit.description: Specifies the length unit used during current plot integration. Options are 'cm' (centimeters) and 'um' (micrometers). The default is 'um'. Affects the units of integrated quantities in the current plot file, particularly important for density integrations.

parameters.name: IntegrationUnit
parameters.desc: Length unit: 'cm' or 'um' (default: 'um').

Intensity.description: Optical excitation intensity in W/cm^2. Specifies the intensity of the light source for optical generation calculations. Used in the Excitation section of Optics to define the illumination strength.

parameters.name: Intensity
parameters.desc: Optical intensity in W/cm^2.

contexts.Plot: Also available as a scalar/vector plot quantity (OpticalIntensity).

IntensityDistribution.signature: IntensityDistribution( Gaussian( Sigma=<float> Length=<float> PeakPosition=<float> PeakWidth=<float> Scaling=<float> ) | Rectangle( dx=<float> dy=<float> ) )
IntensityDistribution.description: Specifies the spatial intensity profile within an illumination window. Supports Gaussian and rectangular distributions for optical excitation.

parameters.name: Sigma
parameters.desc: Standard deviation of Gaussian decay in um. Cannot specify together with Length.

parameters.name: Length
parameters.desc: Length of Gaussian decay in um. Cannot specify together with Sigma.

parameters.name: PeakPosition
parameters.desc: Peak position of Gaussian profile in local window coordinates (um).

parameters.name: PeakWidth
parameters.desc: Width of plateau in modified Gaussian profile (um).

parameters.name: Scaling
parameters.desc: Scaling factor for the Gaussian profile.

IntensityPattern.description: Selects the type of intensity pattern computed by the Transfer Matrix Method (TMM) optical solver. 'StandingWave' computes the regular optical intensity including wavelength-scale oscillations. 'Envelope' computes a smoothed envelope that filters out oscillations, useful when the simulation grid cannot resolve wavelength-scale variations.

Interface.description: Physical Model Interface (PMI) keyword. Used to activate user-defined C++ PMI models by name in the Physics section. Sentaurus Device loads shared object files (.so) at runtime via a dynamic loader. Supports standard C++ interface (based on double type) and simplified C++ interface (based on pmi_float type). Modifiable models include: generation-recombination (avalanche, SRH, lifetime, tunneling parameters), mobility (doping-dependent, high-field saturation, interface degradation), semiconductor band structure (apparent band edge offset, bandgap, bandgap narrowing, effective mass, electron affinity), phase change and state transition, optics, mechanical stress, traps and fixed charge, degradation (diffusive, eNMP transition rates), ferroelectric/ferromagnetic, resistivity, etc.

InterfaceCharge.description: Interface charge mobility degradation model. Simultaneously enables the NegInterfaceCharge and PosInterfaceCharge models to compute carrier mobility degradation caused by Coulomb scattering from charges at the semiconductor-insulator interface (e.g., oxide trap charges). This is a nonlocal model that uses the charge density N_coulomb at the nearest semiconductor-insulator interface.

parameters.name: SurfaceName
parameters.desc: Optional parameter; specifies using the charge density at the nearest user-defined surface instead of the default semiconductor-insulator interface.

InterfaceNBTICharge.description: NBTI (Negative Bias Temperature Instability) interface charge density scalar data. Specified in the Plot section to output the interface charge density computed by the two-stage NBTI degradation model. Unit: 1/cm^2, located at interface vertices.
InterfaceNBTICharge.example:
Plot {
  InterfaceNBTICharge
  InterfaceNBTIState1
  InterfaceNBTIState2
  InterfaceNBTIState3
  InterfaceNBTIState4
}

InterfaceNBTIState1.description: Density scalar data for state 1 in the NBTI two-stage degradation model. Unit: 1/cm^2, located at interface vertices.

InterfaceNBTIState2.description: Density scalar data for state 2 in the NBTI two-stage degradation model. Unit: 1/cm^2, located at interface vertices.

InterfaceNBTIState3.description: Density scalar data for state 3 in the NBTI two-stage degradation model. Unit: 1/cm^2, located at interface vertices.

InterfaceNBTIState4.description: Density scalar data for state 4 in the NBTI two-stage degradation model. Unit: 1/cm^2, located at interface vertices.

InterfaceOrientation.description: Crystal orientation scalar data used at each interface face vertex by the Auto-Orientation Framework. When auto-orientation is enabled, output in the Plot section to view the orientation used at each interface vertex. Values are dimensionless integers.

InterfaceReflection.description: Accounts for interface reflection in the nonlocal path band-to-band tunneling model. When specified, the tunneling path search considers reflection effects at material interfaces.

InterfaceeNMPCharge.description: Interface charge density scalar data for the extended nonradiative multiphonon (eNMP) model. Output in the Plot section for the average interface trap charge density computed by the eNMP model. Unit: 1/cm^2.
InterfaceeNMPCharge.example:
Plot {
  InterfaceeNMPCharge
  InterfaceeNMPState1
  InterfaceeNMPState2
  InterfaceeNMPState1p
  InterfaceeNMPState2p
}

InterfaceeNMPState1.description: Density scalar data for state 1 (neutral stable state s1) in the eNMP model. Unit: 1/cm^2, located at interface vertices.

InterfaceeNMPState1p.description: Density scalar data for state 1' (neutral metastable state s1') in the eNMP model. Unit: 1/cm^2, located at interface vertices.

InterfaceeNMPState2.description: Density scalar data for state 2 (positive stable state s2) in the eNMP model. Unit: 1/cm^2, located at interface vertices.

InterfaceeNMPState2p.description: Density scalar data for state 2' (positive metastable state s2') in the eNMP model. Unit: 1/cm^2, located at interface vertices.

InterpolatedDiscretization.description: Uses an enhanced path search method for avalanche breakdown probability calculation. When enabled, Sentaurus Device uses an interpolated discretization scheme to improve the numerical accuracy of breakdown probability.

Interpolation.description: Interpolation-related keyword. Appears in multiple contexts: (1) as a time interpolation mode in CurrentPlot/Save options; (2) as an interpolation method for numerical data in PMI; (3) as a zero-field interpolation control for driving forces in the Math section. In the PMI framework, Interpolation is a data type used to define the interpolation method for tabular data in PMI models.

contexts.Math: Controls numerical interpolation method in Math section
contexts.File: Specifies mesh interpolation algorithm in FromFile
contexts.Physics: Interpolation method in PMI data types

Interrupt.description: Controls simulation behavior when an INT signal (e.g., Ctrl+C) is received. In BreakRequest mode, writes a .tdr or .sav file and terminates the current Solve statement. In PlotRequest mode, writes a .tdr or .sav file and continues simulation. Used for the Snapshots feature.

Intervals.description: Keyword specifying the number of output points. Has different meanings in different sections: (1) in the Solve section's CurrentPlot option, specifies the number of equally spaced points within the current output time range; (2) in the GainPlot section, specifies the number of plot points per gain curve.

parameters.name: Intervals
parameters.desc: Number of output points or plot points.

contexts.Solve: Number of output points for CurrentPlot in Solve section
contexts.GainPlot: Number of plot points per gain curve in GainPlot section
contexts.PMI: Complex refractive index integration intervals in PMI Optics

IntrinsicDensity.description: Intrinsic carrier concentration (ni) scalar data. Located at vertices, unit: cm^-3. Corresponds to the ni equation, computed from the bandgap and bandgap narrowing models. Can be used as a dependent variable in nonlocal PMI models.

contexts.Plot: Scalar plot data
contexts.PMI: Input variable available for nonlocal PMI

InvokeAtDivergence.description: Allows considering relaxed convergence for diverged solutions. When specified, if Newton iteration neither converges nor diverges, Sentaurus Device uses relaxed convergence criteria to determine whether to accept the solution. Only used in the AcceptNewtonParameter statement.

IonIntegral.description: Specifies the TDR filename for avalanche breakdown path visualization output. Sentaurus Device writes the breakdown path and interpolated field variables along the path to the specified TDR file. Used for path visualization in Approximate Breakdown Analysis.

parameters.name: IonIntegral
parameters.desc: Base name for the output TDR file; extension defaults to _des_ABA.tdr.

IsVg.description: Gate voltage-related extraction curve identifier in the Extraction statement. In Quasistationary's Extraction option, IsVg indicates extraction of the IdVg (drain current vs. gate voltage) characteristic curve.

IscaleMin.description: Minimum value of the current scaling factor in the Continuation statement. When using the NewArc option, this value is added to the current scaling factor to prevent numerical issues when the current approaches zero. Default value is 1e-10 A.

parameters.name: IscaleMin
parameters.desc: Minimum current scaling factor, unit A. Default 1e-10.

IsotropyType.signature: Physics { LED ( Optics ( RayTrace ( Disable ( OutputLightToolsRays ( IsotropyType=InBuilt | Random | UserRays ) ) ) ) ) }
IsotropyType.description: Isotropy type for starting rays in LED ray tracing. Controls the angular distribution of starting rays emitted from each active emission vertex cluster. InBuilt uses the built-in geodesic distribution, Random uses a random distribution, and UserRays uses a user-imported ray direction set via SourceRaysFromFile.

parameters.name: IsotropyType
parameters.desc: Ray distribution type: InBuilt (built-in geodesic distribution), Random (random distribution), UserRays (user-defined rays).

Isource_pset.description: Declares a SPICE current source instance in the System section for mixed-mode simulations. The current source injects a current from node0 to node1. Supports DC, AC, pulse, piecewise-linear, and sinusoidal waveform specifications. The default parameter set Isource_pset provides standard SPICE current source parameters.

parameters.name: dc
parameters.desc: DC current value (A)

parameters.name: ac
parameters.desc: AC current amplitude for small-signal analysis

parameters.name: pulse
parameters.desc: Pulse waveform: pulse=(i1 i2 td tr tf pw period)

parameters.name: pwl
parameters.desc: Piecewise-linear waveform: pwl=(t1 i1 t2 i2 ...)

parameters.name: sine
parameters.desc: Sinusoidal waveform: sine=(vo va freq td theta phi)

IterationStep.description: Requests current output every n iterations in the Plugin command. When specified, Sentaurus Device writes a current file record every specified number of iterations.

parameters.name: IterationStep
parameters.desc: Output a current record every n iterations.

Iterations.description: Maximum number of Newton iterations. Global Math section setting controlling the maximum allowed iterations per Newton solve step. Default value is 50. Can be locally overridden in sub-statements such as EquilibriumSolution.

parameters.name: Iterations
parameters.desc: Maximum Newton iterations. Default 50.

contexts.Math: Global Newton iteration control
contexts.EquilibriumSolution: Local iteration count for equilibrium solution calculation

IterativeRefinement.description: Iterative refinement option for the ParDiSo linear solver. When enabled, the ParDiSo direct solver performs up to two iterative refinement steps after factorization to improve solution accuracy.

Jacobian.description: Jacobian matrix-related control keyword. Appears in multiple contexts: (1) Math section for controlling numerical/analytical Jacobian computation (Numerically, Derivatives); (2) PMI framework for defining non-zero Jacobian elements (DefineJacobians, Allocate); (3) command-line options for controlling Jacobian generation; (4) statistics reporting Jacobian-related data. By default, analytical derivatives are used for Jacobian computation.

contexts.Math: Numerical/analytical Jacobian control

JainRoulston.description: Jain-Roulston bandgap narrowing model. Used to compute the bandgap narrowing amount in heavily doped semiconductors. The model accounts for carrier-carrier and carrier-impurity scattering corrections to the band edges. Activated in the EffectiveIntrinsicDensity statement.

JouleHeat.description: Plot variable for Joule heat in conductive insulators, computed as |J_CI|^2/sigma (W/cm^3). Related keywords include eJouleHeat (electron Joule heat) and hJouleHeat (hole Joule heat). Used in the Plot section for thermodynamic and hydrodynamic simulations.

KMCDefects.description: Defines defect particles, filament particles, and kinetic Monte Carlo (KMC) events for ReRAM simulations. Supports diffusion events, Frenkel pair generation/recombination, and filament growth/recession. Each particle type has a name, exclusion list, and sharing capacity. Up to three particle types and three filament types can be defined.

Kanda.description: In Tensor()/eTensor()/hTensor() piezoresistance mobility model, activates the calculation of doping and temperature factors P1(N,T) and P2(N,T). When not specified, these factors default to 1. Based on the Kanda piezoresistance model which includes temperature and doping dependency of piezoresistive coefficients.

KeepSpectralData.description: In OpticalGeneration{ComputeFromSpectrum()}, retains spectral data in memory after optical solution. Used when the absorbed photon density remains constant but the quantum yield is expected to vary. Also required for plotting spectral results with SpectralPlot in the File section.

KineticEnergy.description: In BalMob() ballistic mobility model, activates the kinetic energy-based ballistic mobility variant. Uses kinetic energy formulation instead of the default drift-velocity formulation for computing ballistic mobility degradation at short channel lengths.

Klaassen.description: Activates the Philips unified mobility model proposed by Klaassen. This model unifies majority and minority carrier bulk mobility descriptions, accounting for electron-hole scattering, ionized impurity screening, and impurity clustering. Can be specified via DopingDependence(PhuMob) or directly as Mobility(PhuMob).

LED.description: Configures light-emitting diode (LED) simulation physics including quantum-well band structure (zincblende/wurtzite), radiative and stimulated emission coefficients (PMI or built-in), gain broadening models, spontaneous emission integration, QW transport, and raytracing optics. Supports strain effects and spontaneous emission scaling.

LEDRadiation.description: In the File section, specifies the base file name for LED radiation pattern output files and activates the LED radiation plot. Parameters for the radiation plot (observation radius and discretization) are set by LEDRadiationPara in the Physics-Optics-RayTrace section.

parameters.name: <string>
parameters.desc: Base file name for LED radiation pattern output files.

LEDRadiationPara.description: Specifies radiation parameters for LED simulations, controlling the optical emission characteristics including spontaneous and stimulated emission properties within the LED physics model.

LEDSpectrum.description: In LED(Optics(RayTrace())), specifies the starting and ending energy range (eV) and the number of subdivisions for computing the LED emission spectrum. Each energy bin produces rays with the corresponding photon energy for spectral analysis.

LED_TraceSource.description: Plot variable that shows the influence of each active vertex on the total extracted light. Activated by the TraceSource() option in RayTrace(). Results are saved to the TDR plot file as a per-vertex dataset.

LHSCoef.description: Defines particle numbers to be removed (left-hand side coefficients) in hydrogen reaction equations. Specifies how many electrons, holes, and hydrogen species are consumed in each reaction.

LHeatFlux.description: Plot variable for the lattice heat flux vector (kappa * grad(T)), representing heat conduction through the lattice. Available in both scalar (magnitude |S_L|) and vector (S_L) forms. Used in thermodynamic and hydrodynamic simulations.

LLG.description: Activates the Landau-Lifshitz-Gilbert (LLG) equation for magnetization dynamics in ferromagnetic layers. Used in spintronics simulations (MTJ devices) to model precession and damping of magnetization under spin-transfer torque. The LLG equation describes the time evolution of magnetization in the presence of effective magnetic fields.

LNVSD.description: Abbreviation for Local Noise Voltage Spectral Density. A noise analysis plot variable available in the NoisePlot section. LNVSD is the integrand of the noise voltage spectral density S_V for local noise sources K(x,w) = delta(x-x')*K(x,w). Related to LNS (local noise source) and LNISD (local noise current spectral density).

Lackner.description: Selects the Lackner avalanche generation model for impact ionization. One of several available avalanche models (vanOverstraeten, Okuto, Lackner, UniBo, UniBo2, Hatakeyama). Can be combined with BandgapDependence option. Used within Avalanche(), eAvalanche(), or hAvalanche() in the Recombination section of Physics.

Lambda.description: Damping factor in Newton iterations, controlling the step size of the solution update. Selected such that the norm of the gradient decreases (||g_{k+1}|| < ||g_k||) but is as close to 1 as possible. The Newton iteration equation is: j_{k+1} - j_k = Lambda * x. Used internally by the nonlinear solver in the Math section.

LambdaTemp.description: Physics keyword related to temperature-dependent lambda parameters, used in the MLDA (Modified Local Density Approximation) quantum correction model. Controls temperature dependency of the screening length lambda in density-gradient and MLDA quantization models.

Landsberg.description: Selects the Landsberg line-shape broadening model for gain spectrum calculation in LED/laser simulations. Provides a narrower, asymmetric broadening compared to Lorentzian. The energy-dependent linewidth Gamma(E) uses a polynomial expansion in terms of the quasi-Fermi level separation. Used as a sub-option of Broadening in the LED physics section.

Laser.description: Configures laser diode simulation physics, including optical cavity effects, stimulated emission, and gain clamping above threshold. Contains similar electrothermal and quantum-well physics as LED simulations but adds threshold-dependent carrier behavior and optical feedback.

LatticeHeatCapacity.description: Specifies the lattice heat capacity model for thermodynamic simulations. The heat capacity follows cL = cv + cv_b*T + cv_c*T^2 + cv_d*T^3. Supports constant values, temperature-dependent models, PMI models, and MSC-dependent (multistate configuration) models. Coefficients can be mole-fraction dependent for alloy materials.

LatticeParameters.description: Defines the crystal coordinate system by specifying the X and Y basis vectors. The Z axis is computed as the cross product of X and Y. Used for anisotropic material property calculations including stress, piezoelectric effects, and carrier quantization. Can be read from parameter file or TDR file (parameter file takes priority). Keywords Piezo and PiezoParameters are also recognized as synonyms.

LatticeTemperature.description: Configures lattice temperature physics for nonisothermal simulations. Activates thermodynamic equations including heat capacity, thermal conductivity, and Joule heat models. Used in BreakCriteria to set maximum temperature limits. Also used as a PMI data field (LatticeTemperature) for postprocessing.

LatticeTemperatureLimit.description: In the Physics section, specifies an upper bound for the lattice temperature (in K). When exceeded, the simulation terminates. This is an alternative to specifying break criteria in the Math section. Only valid for nonisothermal simulations where the Temperature keyword appears in the Solve section.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Maximum allowed lattice temperature in K (e.g., 1693 for melting point of Si).

LayerStackExtraction.description: Controls how the layer stack is extracted for 1D optical solvers (TMM or OptBeam). Can operate in RegionWise or ElementWise mode with specified starting position and illumination window reference.

LayerStructure.description: Defines a multilayer structure for the transfer matrix method (TMM) optical calculation. Each line specifies layer thickness (in um) and material name. Used in RayTraceBC for optical boundary conditions.

LayerThickness.description: Extracts the quantization length (layer thickness) for quantum correction models such as thin-layer mobility and MLDA. Automatically detects layer boundaries in defined regions. The extracted thickness is used by PMI models (e.g., ReadLayerThickness()) and affects mobility, thermal conductivity, and other layer-dependent properties.

LayerThicknessField.description: Plot variable for the extracted layer thickness field. Related to LayerThickness, this quantity represents the quantization length used by quantum correction models. Can be selected by certain thermal conductivity PMI models (e.g., UseLayerThicknessField parameter) as an alternative to the default LayerThickness.

Lch.description: Specifies the explicit channel length (in um) for ballistic mobility models. Used in BalMob() (simple ballistic mobility) and CNTMob() (carbon nanotube mobility) to set the effective channel length for ballistic degradation calculations. Default value is 107 nm.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Channel length in um. Default: 0.107 um (107 nm).

Left.description: Damping factor (lambda) in Newton iteration equations controlling the step size for solution updates. The left-hand side of the Newton update equation uses lambda to ensure convergence. This is an internal solver concept; users control convergence through Digits, ErrRef, RhsFactor, and UpdateFactor in the Math section.

Length.description: Maximum extension distance (unit: cm) of nonlocal mesh lines from interfaces or contacts. Used in Nonlocal() to control the construction range of nonlocal mesh lines. Also used in NonlocalPath() to define the search length for nonlocal tunneling paths.

parameters.name: Length
parameters.desc: Maximum distance from interface or contact to nonlocal mesh line endpoint, unit cm.

LengthDiscretization.description: Specifies the number of ray discretizations along the long edge of a rectangular starting ray window in RayTrace's RectangularWindow. Total rays = LengthDiscretization x WidthDiscretization. Only used in 3D simulations in conjunction with WidthDiscretization.

parameters.name: LengthDiscretization
parameters.desc: Number of ray discretizations along the long edge of the rectangular window.

Level.description: Specifies the extrapolation algorithm number in Extrapolate(). Algorithm=1 or Algorithm=2 switches between different extrapolation methods. Also used with SolveSpectrum in HB{} for harmonic balance analysis.

parameters.name: Algorithm
parameters.desc: Extrapolation algorithm number: 1 or 2.

LifeTime.description: Specifies the base name of the carrier lifetime profile file (input file, .tdr format). Used to load electron and hole lifetime distribution data for the SRH recombination model from an external file, replacing the default analytical lifetime model. Can also be used as PMI lifetime model output, or to output lifetime-related data in the Plot section.

parameters.name: LifeTime
parameters.desc: Base name of the lifetime profile TDR file.

LightHole.description: In the localized quantum-well model's SchroedingerParameters, specifies the ladder parameters for the Light Hole band. Includes parallel effective mass m^z, perpendicular effective mass m^t, degeneracy, band offset, etc. Used for quantum well band structure calculation in wurtzite crystal structures.

parameters.name: mz
parameters.desc: Effective mass in the parallel direction (quantization direction).

parameters.name: mt
parameters.desc: Effective mass in the perpendicular direction.

parameters.name: degeneracy
parameters.desc: Band degeneracy, typically set to 1 for GaN-based semiconductors.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.desc: Band offset relative to the unstrained valence band edge, unit eV.

LightTools.signature: Physics { LED { Optics { RayTrace { Disable ( OutputLightToolsRays( <params> ) ) } } } }
LightTools.description: Interface with Synopsys LightTools optical design software. Provides two output modes: OutputLightToolsRays exports LED starting rays to LightTools format files (ASCII or Binary), OutputLightToolsFarfieldRays exports far-field rays to LightTools format files. Supports wavelength discretization and isotropic/random/user-defined ray distributions.

parameters.name: Filename
parameters.desc: Base name for the LightTools ray data file.

parameters.name: SaveType
parameters.desc: File format: Ascii or Binary.

parameters.name: WavelengthDiscretization
parameters.desc: Number of spectral discretization points; spectral range is automatically determined.

parameters.name: IsotropyType
parameters.desc: Ray distribution type: InBuilt (built-in geodesic distribution), Random (random distribution), UserRays (user input).

parameters.name: RaysPerCluster
parameters.desc: Number of starting rays per active cluster.

Line.description: In Window() (Excitation section), defines a line-shaped illumination window for optical excitation. In Sensor() (far field), defines a line sensor with two corner points. In ComputeCarrierPath(), defines an end condition for carrier path tracing.

contexts.ComputeCarrierPath: Define endpoint conditions for carrier path tracing

LineSearchDamping.description: Minimum allowed damping coefficient for line search damping in Newton iteration, range (0,1). Default is 1 (damping disabled). Controls the step size of each Newton update. When set to less than 1, enables the Bank-Rose damping strategy. Can be set globally in the Math section or locally overridden in Coupled() and EquilibriumSolution().

parameters.name: LineSearchDamping
parameters.desc: Minimum allowed damping coefficient, range (0,1). Default 1 means damping disabled.

contexts.Math: Global Newton iteration damping coefficient setting
contexts.Coupled: Local override of damping coefficient for coupled solve
contexts.EquilibriumSolution: Local override of damping coefficient for equilibrium solve

Linear.description: Uses linear frequency spacing in AC analysis. Mutually exclusive with Decade (logarithmic spacing). When Linear is specified, frequency points between StartFrequency and EndFrequency are evenly spaced linearly, with the number of points controlled by NumberOfPoints.

parameters.name: Linear
parameters.desc: Enable linear frequency spacing.

Load.description: Specifies the filename to load energy-dependent data from a file in MVMLDAcontrols(). Used for saving/loading energy-dependent data in the multi-valley MLDA model, allowing precomputed data to be used in subsequent simulations. Also related to LoadFromFile in the Piezo section.

parameters.name: Load
parameters.desc: Filename of the energy-dependent data to load.

LoadFromFile.description: Specifies loading strain tensor data from an external file in the Piezo section. Replaces automatic calculation via Hooke's law or manual specification of strain components. Requires specifying the TDR data file via the Piezo keyword in the File section simultaneously.

parameters.name: LoadFromFile
parameters.desc: Load strain tensor from Piezo TDR file.

LoadWithInterpolation.description: Specifies loading energy-dependent data from a file with mesh interpolation in MVMLDAcontrols(). Unlike Load, this option allows the loaded data file to use a different mesh than the current simulation; Sentaurus Device interpolates the data onto the current simulation mesh.

parameters.name: LoadWithInterpolation
parameters.desc: Filename of the energy-dependent data to load and interpolate.

Loadable.description: Used in Solve section's Load(), Plot(), Save() to indicate writing additional information to support loading simulation state from .tdr files. Loadable cannot be used with SpectralPlot. In the Math section, PlotLoadable can be set globally to include this option by default in all Plot statements.

parameters.name: Loadable
parameters.desc: Write additional information required for loading simulation state.

Local.description: Used in Nonlocal() to create a named nonlocal mesh (rather than using a specific tunneling model). Local also appears in the PMI interface, representing a local (vertex-level) computation scope. In multithreaded parallel computing, involves thread-local storage concepts. These are different usage scenarios, but the core meaning is always "local, non-global".

LocalModel.description: Specifies the PMI model name for computing the apparent band edge offset Lambda_PMI in eQuantumPotential or hQuantumPotential. Typical usage is the Schenk bandgap narrowing model (SchenkBGN_elec / SchenkBGN_hole). The LocalModelOnly option automatically sets gamma=0 to suppress density gradient model quantum corrections. Used in scenarios requiring apparent band edge offsets such as Schenk BGN and BandGapNarrowing.

parameters.name: LocalModel
parameters.desc: PMI model name for computing the apparent band edge offset.

parameters.name: LocalModelOnly
parameters.desc: Use only LocalModel for computation, suppressing density gradient quantum correction (automatically sets gamma=0).

Location.description: Specifies the position coordinates (bidirectional track) where an alpha particle enters the device in AlphaParticle(). The same Location parameter is used in HeavyIon() to specify the heavy ion strike position. In the PMI interface, represents the spatial position of the computation point. Unit: um.

parameters.name: Location
parameters.desc: Position coordinate vector where the particle enters the device, unit um.

LogCurrent.description: Enables logarithmic current scaling in the Continuation section. When specified, the current range in Current(Intervals=...) uses logarithmic (asinh(1e100 * current)) spacing instead of linear spacing. Used with LogDifference to control output based on logarithmic current difference.

parameters.name: LogCurrent
parameters.desc: Enable logarithmic current scaling for Continuation current interval division.

LogDensity.description: Enables the logarithm of scaled carrier density as the solve variable in the Harmonic Balance HB section. Helps improve Newton convergence under large-signal variations.

parameters.name: LogDensity
parameters.desc: Use logarithm of scaled density as the solve variable.

LogDifference.description: In Continuation simulation, writes save/plot files when the logarithmic current difference exceeds the specified threshold. Requires LogCurrent to be specified in the Continuation section simultaneously.

parameters.name: LogDifference
parameters.desc: Logarithmic current difference threshold; files are written when the actual logarithmic current difference from the last output exceeds this value.

LogTemperature.description: Enables the logarithm of scaled temperature as the solve variable in the Harmonic Balance HB section. Helps improve Newton convergence under large-signal variations involving temperature.

parameters.name: LogTemperature
parameters.desc: Use logarithm of scaled temperature as the solve variable.

Logarithmic.description: Uses logarithmic (decade) frequency spacing in AC analysis. Enabled via the Decade keyword, mutually exclusive with Linear (linear spacing). Frequency range is defined by StartFrequency and EndFrequency, with NumberOfPoints controlling the number of points per decade.

parameters.name: Decade
parameters.desc: Use logarithmic (decade) frequency spacing.

Lombardi.description: Enhanced Lombardi surface mobility degradation model. Considers three components: phonon scattering, surface roughness scattering, and charged interface state scattering. Can be used in Enormal() (normal electric field dependence) and ThinLayer() (thin layer model). AutoOrientation can automatically select parameter sets based on the nearest interface orientation.

parameters.name: AutoOrientation
parameters.desc: Automatically select parameter sets based on the nearest interface orientation.

parameters.name: ParameterSetName
parameters.desc: Specifies the EnormalDependence parameter set name.

Lombardi_highk.description: High-k dielectric degradation extension for the enhanced Lombardi model. Adds a mobility degradation component caused by high-k gate dielectrics on top of the standard Lombardi model. Used for advanced CMOS device simulations with high-k dielectrics.

Lorentzian.signature: Physics { LED { Optics { GainBroadening ( Type = Lorentzian ) } } }
Lorentzian.description: Selects Lorentzian lineshape broadening function in the LED simulation gain broadening model. Alongside CosHyper (hyperbolic cosine broadening) and Landsberg broadening, used to compute the broadening effect of LED gain spectra. Broadening coefficient controlled by the Gamma parameter.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.desc: Broadening type, set to Lorentzian.

parameters.name: Gamma
parameters.desc: Broadening coefficient Gamma, unit eV.

LowDensityLimit.description: Sets the lower limit of carrier density in Extrapolate(); extrapolation is performed only when the density exceeds this limit. Used to avoid convergence issues caused by extrapolation in regions with extremely low carrier density. Unit: cm^-3.

parameters.name: LowDensityLimit
parameters.desc: Carrier density lower limit, unit cm^-3. Default 1e-100.

MATH.description: Section keyword for specifying mathematical and numerical parameters. Controls solver settings, convergence criteria, discretization options, and other numeric method configurations.

parameters.name: Digits
parameters.desc: Approximate convergence digits, default 5.

parameters.name: Error
parameters.desc: Absolute error parameter for each equation.

parameters.name: ErrRef
parameters.desc: Reference error parameter for each equation.

parameters.name: Iterations
parameters.desc: Maximum Newton iterations, default 50.

parameters.name: Extrapolate
parameters.desc: Extrapolation settings for obtaining the initial guess of the next solution.

parameters.name: Transient
parameters.desc: Transient analysis time integration method: BE (Backward Euler) or TRBDF.

parameters.name: Method
parameters.desc: Linear solver selection.

parameters.name: SubMethod
parameters.desc: Inner linear solver.

parameters.name: LineSearchDamping
parameters.desc: Newton line search damping coefficient.

parameters.name: NotDamped
parameters.desc: Number of iterations before activating Bank-Rose damping.

MDFT.description: Enables Multi-Dimensional Fourier Transform (MDFT) mode in the Harmonic Balance HB section. MDFT mode is applicable to multi-tone and single-tone harmonic balance analysis, using multi-dimensional Fourier transforms to switch between frequency and time domains. Requires a CMI compact model that supports frequency-domain assembly. Mutually exclusive with SDFT mode.

parameters.name: MDFT
parameters.desc: Enable MDFT mode for harmonic balance analysis.

MIMBand.description: Specifies the base name for the metal-insulator-metal (MIM) band diagram output file in the File section. File format is TDR (_mimband_des.tdr). Contains band diagram information with defect positions (distance from reference electrode) and defect energies (within insulator bandgap), viewable in Sentaurus Visual.

parameters.name: MIMBand
parameters.desc: Base name for the MIM band diagram file.

MIMCurrent.description: Specifies the base name for the MIM steady-state current output file in the File section. File format is PLT (_mimcur_des.plt), containing current and capacitance data. Sentaurus Device automatically computes capacitance via numerical differentiation C = dQ/dV in KMC-MIM simulations.

parameters.name: MIMCurrent
parameters.desc: Base name for the MIM current file.

MIMSensitivity.description: Specifies the base name for the MIM sensitivity analysis output file in the File section. File format is PLT (_<volt>_mimsa_des.tdr). Sensitivity analysis places a single defect at different positions X_t and energy levels E_t, computes the leakage current matrix, and identifies the maximum leakage current and its corresponding (X_t, E_t) location.

parameters.name: MIMSensitivity
parameters.desc: Base name for the MIM sensitivity analysis file.

MLDA.description: Modified Local-Density Approximation model for quantum correction of carrier densities near semiconductor-insulator interfaces. Computes carrier density including quantum confinement effects using a local approximation of the Schrodinger equation. Available for electrons (eMLDA), holes (hMLDA), and within the multivalley framework. Can be combined with ThinLayer for geometric quantization in thin films.

contexts.Physics: As eMLDA/hMLDA for electrons/holes, or within MultiValley(MLDA) for multivalley quantization
contexts.Math: MLDAbox and MVMLDAcontrols parameters defined in Math section

MLDAMinDistanceToContact.description: Specifies the minimum distance to a contact (in micrometers) where the MLDA quantum correction model is switched off. This avoids numerical and physical inconsistencies near contacts. Default is 75e-8 um (75 angstroms).

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Minimum distance to contact in um. Default: 75e-8.

MLDAbox.signature: MLDAbox (MinX=<float> MaxX=<float> MinY=<float> MaxY=<float> MinZ=<float> MaxZ=<float>)
MLDAbox.description: Defines a box region for the modified local-density approximation (MLDA) model. Specifies the spatial bounds (in micrometers) within which the MLDA quantum correction is applied for interface scattering calculations.

parameters.name: MinX
parameters.desc: Lower x-coordinate of the box in um.

parameters.name: MaxX
parameters.desc: Upper x-coordinate of the box in um.

parameters.name: MinY
parameters.desc: Lower y-coordinate of the box in um.

parameters.name: MaxY
parameters.desc: Upper y-coordinate of the box in um.

parameters.name: MinZ
parameters.desc: Lower z-coordinate of the box in um.

parameters.name: MaxZ
parameters.desc: Upper z-coordinate of the box in um.

MPBC.description: Mortar Periodic Boundary Conditions approach for nonconforming meshes at periodic boundaries. Both sides of the PBC interface are glued on one side (mortar side), while a weak continuity condition is imposed on the other side (nonmortar side). Required when the device structure forms a heterointerface at the PBC interface. Specified via PeriodicBC in the Math section.

parameters.name: Direction
parameters.desc: Direction of periodicity: 0 for x-axis, 1 for y-axis, 2 for z-axis.

parameters.name: MortarSide
parameters.desc: Side to use as mortar side: XMin, XMax, YMin, YMax, ZMin, or ZMax.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.desc: Must be MPBC to select mortar approach.

MSConfig.description: Defines a multistate configuration (MSC) within the MSConfigs section of Physics. Describes the states (at least two), transitions between them, and optional band-edge shift models. MSCs model phenomena such as NBTI trap states with charge and hydrogen composition. Used for MSC-dependent PMI models in mobility, recombination, and other physical models.

MSConfigs.description: Manipulates multistate configurations (MSCs) during a simulation, used inside Set() in the Solve section. Allows freezing MSCs or setting specific MSC state occupations by device name and state name.

MSDistResist.description: Alternative distributed resistance model at metal-semiconductor interfaces, improving convergence performance compared to DistResist. Supports constant resistance (in ohm*cm^2) or Schottky resistance emulation. Used at material interfaces, not contacts.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Distributed resistance in ohm*cm^2, or the keyword SchottkyResistance.

MSPeltierHeat.description: Activates Peltier heat computation at metal-semiconductor interfaces or semiconductor contacts. Peltier heating/cooling occurs when current flows across an interface between two materials with different thermoelectric powers. Fitting parameters (alpha, beta, gamma, deltaE) can be specified in the MSPeltierHeat parameter set.

parameters.name: alpha
parameters.desc: Peltier heat fitting parameter alpha. Default: 1.0.

parameters.name: beta
parameters.desc: Peltier heat fitting parameter beta. Default: 1.0.

parameters.name: gamma
parameters.desc: Peltier heat fitting parameter gamma. Default: 1.0.

parameters.name: deltaE
parameters.desc: Energy offset parameter in eV. Default: 0.0.

MVMLDAcontrols.description: Controls for the modified local-density approximation (MLDA) model. Specifies parameters such as the minimum distance to contacts where the MLDA quantum correction is switched off. Default minimum distance is 75e-8 um.

parameters.name: AveDistanceFactor
parameters.desc: Factor controlling computation of averaged distance from vertex to interface. Default: 0.05.

parameters.name: Load
parameters.desc: Name of file from which to load energy-dependent data.

parameters.name: LoadWithInterpolation
parameters.desc: Name of file to load energy-dependent data, possibly from different mesh.

parameters.name: MaxDoping4Majority
parameters.desc: Maximum doping concentration (cm^-3) where multivalley MLDA is applied to majority carriers. Default: 1e22.

parameters.name: MaxIntDistance
parameters.desc: Distance (cm) from interface up to which multivalley MLDA is applied. Default: 1e-6.

parameters.name: Save
parameters.desc: Name of file where to save energy-dependent data.

MagneticField.description: Specifies a constant magnetic field vector (in Tesla) for the galvanic transport model (Hall effect). Enables simulation of Hall mobility and magnetoresistance effects. The field vector components correspond to (x, y, z) directions. Cannot be combined with hydrodynamic, impact ionization, aniso, piezo, or quantum models.

parameters.name: vector
parameters.desc: Magnetic field vector (Bx, By, Bz) in Tesla.

Makram-Ebeid.description: Phonon-assisted tunnel emission model for deep-level traps, primarily used for two-band charge transport in silicon nitride. The deep trap acts as an oscillator defined by phonon energy, thermal energy, and optical energy. Specifies trap ionization rates for eNeutral and hNeutral traps with Level distributions. Options control coupling to conduction/valence bands and capture rate computation.
Makram-Ebeid.example:
Traps((eNeutral Level ... Makram-Ebeid))
Traps((eNeutral Level ... Makram-Ebeid(electron)))
Traps((hNeutral Level ... Makram-Ebeid(hole)))
Traps((eNeutral Level ... Makram-Ebeid(simpleCapt)))

Masetti.description: Masetti doping-dependent mobility degradation model. Default model for silicon in Sentaurus Device. Computes mobility degradation due to charged impurity ion scattering using reference mobilities, doping concentrations, and exponents accessible in the DopingDependence parameter set. Can be combined with other mobility models via Matthiessen's rule.

MatGain.description: Plot variable for LED simulations representing the local material gain (in m^-1). Output as the OpticalMaterialGain dataset in the plot file. Available when LED framework is activated.

Material.description: Specifies physics models or region properties by material name. Used as a qualifier in Physics section to apply models materialwise (e.g., Physics(Material="Silicon")). Also used in the Region section of the command file to assign a material to a region.

contexts.Physics: Qualifier to restrict model application to specific material

MaterialInterface.description: Specifies physics models at the interface between two materials. The interface is specified as "Material1/Material2". Controls models such as distributed resistance, Peltier heat, thermionic emission, dipole layers, eNMP, and interface recombination. Material order determines PMI variable set labeling (set 1 = first material, set 2 = second).

contexts.Physics: Qualifier for interface-specific models
contexts.Math: EnormalInterface uses MaterialInterface for normal field computation

Materials.description: Specifies physics models by listing multiple material names. A shorthand for applying the same models to multiple materials without repeating Physics statements. Each material in the list receives the same set of physical model specifications.

Math.description: Specifies numerical methods and solver settings, including equation discretization, linear solver selection, convergence criteria, and error control. Can be restricted to a specific location.

parameters.name: Extrapolate
parameters.desc: Use extrapolation for faster convergence.

parameters.name: Method
parameters.desc: Linear solver method (e.g., Blocked, ILS, Pardiso, Super).

parameters.name: Digits
parameters.desc: Relative error target for Newton solver.

parameters.name: Iterations
parameters.desc: Maximum number of Newton iterations.

parameters.name: Error
parameters.desc: Global error control tolerance.

parameters.name: ErrRef
parameters.desc: Reference error value for convergence check.

Max.description: Maximum value parameter used in various Math section contexts. Commonly used to set upper bounds for numerical quantities such as carrier density limits, temperature ranges, and solver control parameters.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Maximum value.

MaxAngle.description: Maximum angle (in degrees) for nonlocal mesh line construction. Suppresses construction of nonlocal mesh lines that enclose an angle more than the specified value with the vector specified by Direction in the NonLocal statement.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Maximum angle in degrees. Default: 180.

MaxCurrent.description: Maximum current value used as a stopping criterion. In KMC/TDDB simulations (Math section), stops the calculation when current exceeds this value. In Continuation (Solve section), defines the upper current limit of the I-V curve tracing window.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Maximum current in A. Default: 0 (KMC context).

contexts.Math: KMC stopping criterion for TDDB calculation
contexts.Solve: Continuation I-V curve tracing window limit

MaxDoping4Majority.description: Maximum doping concentration (in cm^-3) where the multivalley MLDA model is applied to majority carriers. Used to exclude heavily doped source/drain regions from MLDA computation. If set to zero, source/drain regions are completely excluded.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Maximum doping concentration in cm^-3. Default: 1e22.

MaxElectricField.description: Cutoff value for the electric field (in V/cm) in the QWLocal quantum-well quantization model. Large fields during Newton iterations can cause excessive computation time; this parameter sets a maximum field for the QWLocal calculation. Default is 1e6 V/cm.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Maximum electric field in V/cm. Default: 1e6.

MaxEnergy.description: Option for the DensityTail parameter in the Schrodinger equation. When set to MaxEnergy, the highest computed subband energy is used for the density tail cutoff. Otherwise, an estimate of the lowest uncomputed subband energy is used.

MaxFitWeight.description: Parameter for the LayerThickness command that controls the thickness extraction algorithm. Adjusts the fitting weight used during automatic layer thickness extraction for quantum models such as multivalley MLDA and ThinLayer mobility.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Maximum fit weight value. Default: 0.

MaxIFactor.description: In the Continuation command (Solve section), specifies the maximum factor that the current is allowed to increase in one step along the I-V curve. Limits the projection calculated by Sentaurus Device between successive points. Alias: MaxIfactor.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Maximum current increase factor per step.

MaxIfactor.description: Case-insensitive alias of MaxIFactor. In the Continuation command, specifies the maximum factor that the current is allowed to increase in one step along the I-V curve.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Maximum current increase factor per step.

MaxIntDistance.description: Distance from the interface (in cm) up to which the multivalley MLDA model is applied. Controls the spatial extent of MLDA quantum correction. Default is 1e-6 cm (10 nm).

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Maximum interface distance in cm. Default: 1e-6.

MaxInternalPoints.description: Maximum number of internal points used in random field generation for statistical variations (sIFM). Controls the resolution of the spatial correlation computation for workfunction variations, geometric variations, band edge variations, metal conductivity variations, dielectric constant variations, and doping profile variations.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Maximum number of internal points for random field resolution.

MaxIstep.description: In the Continuation command (Solve section), specifies the maximum current step between successive points along the I-V curve. Limits the projection of current change calculated by Sentaurus Device.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Maximum current step in A.

MaxIterations.description: Maximum number of smoother sweeps in the multi-grid preconditioner for the Bitlis solver. Controls the quality of the approximate solution within the multi-grid preconditioner. More iterations improve convergence but increase computation time per GMRES iteration.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Maximum number of iterations. Default: 3 (MGPreconditioner context).

MaxMismatch.description: Tolerance in matching of slice meshes and device mesh for external quantum solvers (ExternalBoltzmannSolver and ExternalSchroedingerSolver). Controls how closely the external solver's slice mesh must align with the device mesh for quantum correction interpolation. Default is 1e-4 um.

parameters.name: MaxMismatch
parameters.desc: Tolerance in um for matching of slice meshes and device mesh. Default: 1e-4 um.

MaxNumberOfIterations.description: Maximum number of iterations for the iterative scattering solver in the TMM optical solver. Used within Scattering() to determine the termination condition of the iterative rough-interface scattering computation. Default is 10.

parameters.name: MaxNumberOfIterations
parameters.desc: Maximum number of iterations for the scattering solver. Default: 10.

MaxRload.description: Maximum value for the adaptive load resistor in Continuation simulations. Controls the upper bound of the automatically adjusted load resistance used to improve convergence during continuation (voltage/current ramping). Default is 1e14 Ohm (1e8 Ohm with NewArc option).

parameters.name: MaxRload
parameters.desc: Maximum value for adaptive load resistor in Ohm. Default: 1e14 (1e8 with NewArc).

MaxSolutions.description: Maximum number of eigensolutions (energy subbands) computed per ladder in the 1D Schrodinger equation solver. Used within Schroedinger() for quantum well quantization. Default is 5. Can be specified per carrier type (Electron or Hole).

parameters.name: MaxSolutions
parameters.desc: Maximum number of eigensolutions computed per ladder. Default: 5.

parameters.name: (<carrier>)
parameters.desc: Optional carrier specification: Electron or Hole. If omitted, applies to both.

MaxStep.description: Maximum step size for simulation continuation. Used in Continuation(), Quasistationary(), and Transient() to limit the arc length variable or time step increment. Default is 1e35 for Continuation, 1 for Quasistationary, 1 s for Transient.

parameters.name: MaxStep
parameters.desc: Maximum step size. Units depend on context (arc length for Continuation, dimensionless for Quasistationary, seconds for Transient).

contexts.Continuation: Maximum step for the internal arc length variable

MaxVal.description: Maximum-allowed value of extrapolation factor in Cyclic() analysis. Controls the upper bound for the cyclic extrapolation factor to prevent instability during large-signal cyclic steady-state analysis. Default is 25.

parameters.name: MaxVal
parameters.desc: Maximum-allowed value of extrapolation factor. Default: 25.

MaxVoltage.description: Upper voltage limit in Continuation simulations. Specifies the maximum allowed voltage for the biased electrode during voltage ramping. Must be specified for voltage-controlled continuation.

parameters.name: MaxVoltage
parameters.desc: Upper voltage limit in V.

MaxVstep.description: Maximum-allowed voltage step in Continuation simulations. Controls the largest single-step voltage increment during adaptive voltage ramping. Default is 1e35 V.

parameters.name: MaxVstep
parameters.desc: Maximum-allowed voltage step in V. Default: 1e35.

MaxX.description: Maximum x-coordinate for the quantum box in eQPBox() and hQPBox(). Defines the right boundary of the quantum correction region along the x-axis. Used in the density gradient model to confine the quantum potential computation. Default is 1e30 um.

parameters.name: MaxX
parameters.desc: Maximum x-coordinate for quantum box in um. Default: 1e30.

MaxY.description: Maximum y-coordinate for the quantum box in eQPBox() and hQPBox(). Defines the top boundary of the quantum correction region along the y-axis. Default is 1e30 um.

parameters.name: MaxY
parameters.desc: Maximum y-coordinate for quantum box in um. Default: 1e30.

MaxZ.description: Maximum z-coordinate for the quantum box in eQPBox() and hQPBox(). Defines the front boundary of the quantum correction region along the z-axis (3D only). Default is 1e30 um.

parameters.name: MaxZ
parameters.desc: Maximum z-coordinate for quantum box in um. Default: 1e30.

Maximum.description: Specifies maximum value limits for numerical parameters in the Math section, such as maximum electric field, maximum carrier concentration, or maximum iteration counts. Used to constrain solution variables during Newton iterations to improve convergence.

MeanIonIntegral.description: Scalar dataset for the mean ionization integral over all computed breakdown paths. Output at each vertex. Used in approximate breakdown analysis to assess the overall breakdown susceptibility. Unit is dimensionless.
MeanIonIntegral.example:
Plot {
  eDensity
  hDensity
  ElectricField
  MeanIonIntegral
  eIonIntegral
}

Mechanics.description: Activates the mechanical stress solver to compute stress and strain distributions in the device. Enables stress-dependent mobility, piezoresistance, and deformation potential models.

Medium.description: Specifies the properties of surrounding media (top or bottom) for the extracted layer structure in the LayerStackExtraction section of the TMM optical solver. Overrides the default vacuum assumption by specifying a material name or direct refractive index and extinction coefficient values.

Memory.description: Activates the compact memory model for the raytracer to reduce memory consumption during optical raytracing simulations. Used within Physics{Optics{OpticalSolver(RayTrace())}} to switch to a memory-efficient raytracing mode at the cost of reduced information output.

Mesh.description: Global section keyword that declares the mesh file (TDR format) for device simulation. Specifies the input boundary and grid data from Sentaurus Mesh or SStructure. The mesh defines the geometry and discretization of the device structure.

MeshDomain.description: Defines a mesh domain for localized mesh refinement or restricted computation regions. Used to confine certain numerical operations to specific subregions of the device.

MesherInput.description: Specifies the base name of the boundary and command files to be used for calling Sentaurus Mesh from within the optical solver. The mesher generates the dual mesh used for raytracing computations. Specified in the File section.

parameters.name: MesherInput
parameters.desc: Base name of boundary and command file for Sentaurus Mesh.

Meshing.description: Section keyword for mesh refinement and adaptation controls in Sentaurus Device. Specifies parameters for adaptive meshing strategies during simulation, including refinement criteria and maximum element sizes.

Metal.description: Declares a metal region in the global section of the command file. Defines metal contact properties including thermoelectric power, resistivity, and workfunction. Metal regions can be used in thermal and electrical simulations with dedicated transport models.

MetalConductivity.description: Activates the computation of electrical conductivity in metal regions. When specified in the Math section, the thermal conductivity of metals is always simulated, but electrical conductivity requires explicit activation. Enables current flow computation in metal contacts.

MetalResistivity.description: Specifies the metal resistivity model using a PMI (Physical Model Interface) model. The metal resistivity rho = 1/sigma can be defined by a PMI to enable field-dependent, temperature-dependent, or anisotropic resistivity in metal regions. Supports self-consistent anisotropic metal resistivity.

MetalTEPower.description: Thermoelectric power model for metals. Uses a PMI model to compute the Seebeck coefficient and thermoelectric behavior in metal regions. Specified as Physics(Material="Copper") { MetalTEPower(pmi_model) }.

MetalWorkfunction.signature: MetalWorkfunction( [ Randomize( AverageGrainSize=<float> GrainProbability=(<P1>,...) GrainWorkfunction=(<wf1>,...) RandomSeed=<int> ... ) ] )
MetalWorkfunction.description: Specifies the workfunction of metal regions. Supports workfunction randomization to model grain-structure-induced variability using average grain size, grain probability distribution, and grain workfunction values.

Method.description: Specifies the linear solver method for the Coupled() Newton solve statement. Options include direct solvers (Pardiso, TAUCS) specified by Table 223 identifiers, or the Blocked block decomposition solver. Controls the numerical approach for solving the linearized system of equations during Newton iterations.

contexts.Math: Global default linear solver setting

Meyer.description: Reference citation [6]: I. Vurgaftman, J. R. Meyer, and L. R. Ram-Mohan, 'Band parameters for III-V compound semiconductors and their alloys,' Journal of Applied Physics, vol. 89, no. 11, pp. 5815-5875, 2001. This is a bibliography entry, not a command keyword.

Min.description: Specifies minimum value limits for numerical parameters in Solve section commands such as Continuation, Quasistationary, and Coupled. Used to set lower bounds for current, voltage, step sizes, and extrapolation factors.

MinAmplitude.description: Signal amplitude used to define time points t0 and t3 for analytic optical signals with asymptotic tails (e.g., Gaussian pulses). Controls the threshold below which the signal tail is truncated for optical turning point detection. Default is 0.01.

parameters.name: MinAmplitude
parameters.desc: Signal amplitude threshold for defining turning points. Default: 0.01.

MinAngle.description: Specifies minimum angles for width extraction in quantum well structures. Used within WidthExtraction() to define how the quantum-well thickness is computed when the well is not aligned to a major axis. Two values specify the minimum angles for two sides of the well.

parameters.name: MinAngle
parameters.desc: Pair of minimum angles (<float>, <float>) in degrees for QW width extraction.

contexts.WidthExtraction: Minimum angle parameter in quantum well width extraction
contexts.LayerThickness: Minimum angle parameter in thin layer thickness extraction

MinCurrent.description: In the Continuation command, specifies the lower current limit (in A) of the continuation window. The I-V curve tracing stops when the current at the continuation electrode drops below this value. Used together with MaxCurrent, MinVoltage, and MaxVoltage to define the continuation operating range.
MinCurrent.example:
Solve {
  Continuation(
    NewArc
    Name="collector"
    InitialVstep=0.1
    MinCurrent=1e-12
    MaxCurrent=1e-3
    MinVoltage=-5.0
    MaxVoltage=5.0
  )
}
