MinElectricField.description: In ComputeIonizationIntegrals(), specifies the minimum electric field threshold (in V/cm). All semiconductor elements where the electric field exceeds this value are taken into the initial element list for ionization integral path computation. Used for approximate breakdown analysis to restrict the search domain.

MinError.description: Option of NewtonPlot in the Math section. When specified, NewtonPlot output files are written only if the Newton error has decreased compared to the previous step. Helps reduce the number of Newton plot files generated during convergence monitoring.

MinIntensity.description: In RayTracing(), specifies the relative intensity threshold below which a ray is terminated. When a ray's intensity drops below this fraction of its original starting intensity, ray tracing stops for that ray. Synonym: RelativeMinIntensity.

MinRload.description: In the Continuation command, specifies the minimum value (in ohms) for the adaptive load resistor. Default is 1 ohm. Controls the lower bound of the adaptive resistance used in continuation-based I-V curve tracing.

MinSpacing.description: In the Plot command within Quasistationary or Transient, specifies the minimum step spacing between successive save/plot points. Instead of uniformly dividing the range into intervals, plot files are generated only when the ramping variable advances by at least MinSpacing from the last saved point. When MinSpacing is defined, other Plot commands (Intervals) are ignored.

contexts.Quasistationary: Minimum spacing for quasistationary ramp save points
contexts.Transient: Minimum spacing for transient time save points

MinStep.description: Specifies minimum step sizes in various contexts: (1) In Continuation/Quasistationary, the minimum step size for the internal arc length or ramp variable. (2) In Extrapolate(), the minimum step for extrapolation to be applied. (3) In NonlocalPath(), the lower boundary for step-size control in derivative computation. (4) In Math section, controls automatic activation thresholds for CNormPrint and NewtonPlot via AutoCNPMinStepFactor and AutoNPMinStepFactor.

contexts.Math: Extrapolate and auto-activation thresholds
contexts.Solve: Continuation and Quasistationary step control

MinVal.description: Specifies minimum value thresholds in various contexts: (1) In Cyclic() Extrapolate(), the minimum allowed extrapolation factor r_min. (2) In BreakCriteria Current(), the lower current limit. (3) In KMC() CurrentCompliance(), the minimum current value accepted (must be negative). (4) In Mixed-Mode break criteria, lower bounds for power and current values.

MinVoltage.description: In the Continuation command, specifies the lower voltage limit (in V) of the continuation window. The I-V curve tracing stops when the voltage at the continuation electrode drops below this value. Used together with MaxVoltage, MinCurrent, and MaxCurrent to define the continuation operating range.

MinX.description: In eQPBox(), hQPBox(), and MLDAbox(), specifies the minimum x-coordinate (in um) defining the boundary of the quantum correction box or MLDA calculation domain. Used to spatially restrict quantum potential correction or interface distance calculations.

MinY.description: In eQPBox(), hQPBox(), and MLDAbox(), specifies the minimum y-coordinate (in um) defining the boundary of the quantum correction box or MLDA calculation domain.

MinZ.description: In eQPBox(), hQPBox(), and MLDAbox(), specifies the minimum z-coordinate (in um) defining the boundary of the quantum correction box or MLDA calculation domain.

Minimum.description: In the Math section, specifies minimum values for various numerical parameters such as grid element sizes (minimum size of the coarsest grid) or convergence thresholds.

Mirror.description: In ThinLayer(), specifies mirror planes for the simulation system axes. Each axis mirror specification can be Min, Max, None, or Both. Used to define geometric symmetry for thin-layer structures, particularly with the external 2D Schrodinger solver which takes ThinLayer(Mirror) into account for 3D symmetric structure simulations.

MixAverageBoxMethod.description: Enables the mix average box method algorithm for computing box method coefficients and control volumes in 3D. When specified, uses a mixed averaging approach for element intersection box method calculations. Default is off.

Mixed.description: Indicates mixed-mode simulation context. Used in various scenarios: (1) As a device specification prefix for mixed-mode setups. (2) In optical generation contexts for mixed optical simulation modes. (3) In Quasistationary Extrapolate() to control extrapolation behavior in mixed-mode. (4) In CurrentPlot to specify device-specific plotting in mixed-mode simulations.

MixedMode.description: In HBCoupled() Initialize option, sets the harmonic balance initialization mode. When Initialize=MixedMode, the 0-th harmonic is taken from the DC solution and all other harmonics are taken from the previous HB solution. This provides a compromise between DC-only and full HB initialization.

Mobility.description: Configures carrier mobility models including doping dependence (Arora, Masetti, PhuMob), high-field saturation (CaugheyThomas, TransferredElectronEffect), interface degradation (Lombardi, IALMob, Enormal), and strain-dependent models (eSubband, hSixband, piezoresistive factor/tensor). Applied per carrier type with eMobility/hMobility variants.

MobilityAcceptorConcentration.description: Plot variable representing the acceptor doping concentration (in cm^-3) used specifically for mobility calculations. This concentration is read from the MobilityDoping file (if specified) or derived from Add2TotalDoping(ChargedTraps). Can be used in the Plot section to visualize the mobility-specific doping profile.

MobilityDonorConcentration.description: Plot variable representing the donor doping concentration (in cm^-3) used specifically for mobility calculations. Read from the MobilityDoping file (if specified) or derived from Add2TotalDoping(ChargedTraps). For PMI models, accessible via ReadDoping("MobilityDonorConcentration").

MobilityDoping.description: In the File section, specifies a separate TDR file from which donor and acceptor concentrations for mobility calculations are read. The geometry and mesh in this file must match the Grid file. When specified, it deactivates the mobility dependency on IncompleteIonization. The concentrations can be visualized using MobilityDonorConcentration and MobilityAcceptorConcentration plot variables.

Mod_eGradQuasiFermi_ElectricField.description: Plot variable (vertex-based) representing the magnitude of the electron quasi-Fermi potential gradient modified by electric field interpolation. Computes |grad(Phi_n)| with zero-field interpolation, representing the effective driving force for electron high-field mobility. Unit: V/cm.

Mod_eQuasiFermi_ElectricField_Potential.description: Plot variable (vertex-based) representing the electron quasi-Fermi potential modified by electric field interpolation. Computes Phi_n with zero-field interpolation for the quasi-Fermi potential contribution to the driving force. Unit: V.

Mod_hGradQuasiFermi_ElectricField.description: Plot variable (vertex-based) representing the magnitude of the hole quasi-Fermi potential gradient modified by electric field interpolation. Computes |grad(Phi_p)| with zero-field interpolation, representing the effective driving force for hole high-field mobility. Unit: V/cm.

Mod_hQuasiFermi_ElectricField_Potential.description: Plot variable (vertex-based) representing the hole quasi-Fermi potential modified by electric field interpolation. Computes Phi_p with zero-field interpolation for the quasi-Fermi potential contribution to the driving force. Unit: V.

Mode.description: Specifies operating or simulation mode in various contexts: (1) In command-line options, specifies simulation mode. (2) In CurrentPlot, selects a model for parameter printing. (3) In Math HB section, selects MDFT or SDFT mode for harmonic balance. (4) In Coupled(), specifies the linear solver method. (5) In ThinLayer(), used as part of ThinLayer(Mirror=Mode) specification. (6) In PMI magnetostatic calculations, determines the mode of operation.

ModeGain.description: In the File section, specifies the base filename for the LED gain plot output file. The gain file contains modal stimulated and spontaneous emission spectra computed during LED simulations.

ModeOscillator.description: In the Traps() Transition option, selects the oscillator tunneling transition mode for trap-to-trap tunneling. Used as an alternative to InelasticPhonon for the tunneling mechanism between traps in DiscreteTrapT2T models.

Model.description: Specifies a PMI (Physical Model Interface) model for a given species or physical quantity. The model name and the target species are provided as arguments.

ModelParameter.description: In the Goal{} section of Quasistationary, specifies the parameter path or name of a model parameter to be ramped during parameter ramping. In CurrentPlot, selects a model parameter for plotting when using the unified interface for optical generation computation.

MoleFraction.description: Specifies mole fraction values (xMoleFraction, yMoleFraction) for compound semiconductor regions. Supports linear grading from boundaries with configurable grading distances.

parameters.name: xFraction
parameters.desc: Mole fraction of the x-component material.

parameters.name: yFraction
parameters.desc: Mole fraction of the y-component material.

parameters.name: GrDistance
parameters.desc: Grading distance for linear mole fraction transition at boundaries, in um.

contexts.Physics: Used in Physics section to define material composition in compound semiconductors such as SiGe, AlGaAs, InGaN

MonopolarGRNoise.signature: Noise { MonopolarGRNoise( e_alpha=<float> h_alpha=<float> e_tau=<float> h_tau=<float> ) }
MonopolarGRNoise.description: Equivalent monopolar generation-recombination noise model for a two-level GR process. Expresses noise as a tensor depending on fitting parameter alpha and equivalent GR lifetime tau. Can be specified in the Noise section or the IFM section. Note: This model does not use actual GR models from the simulation.

parameters.name: e_alpha
parameters.desc: Fitting parameter for electron monopolar GR noise. Default: 1.

parameters.name: h_alpha
parameters.desc: Fitting parameter for hole monopolar GR noise. Default: 1.

parameters.name: e_tau
parameters.desc: Equivalent GR lifetime for electrons, in s. Default: 1e-7.

parameters.name: h_tau
parameters.desc: Equivalent GR lifetime for holes, in s. Default: 1e-7.

contexts.Physics: Within Noise{} subsection of Physics
contexts.IFM: Also specifiable in IFM section for impedance field method

Monte Carlo.description: Configures the Monte Carlo particle transport simulation. See the Sentaurus Device Monte Carlo User Guide for detailed options.

parameters.name: <options>
parameters.desc: Monte Carlo simulation options as described in the MC User Guide.

contexts.Physics: MonteCarlo within RayTrace{} for LED Monte Carlo raytracing

MonteCarlo.description: Configures the Monte Carlo particle transport simulation. See the Sentaurus Device Monte Carlo User Guide for detailed options.

parameters.name: <options>
parameters.desc: Monte Carlo simulation options as described in the MC User Guide.

contexts.Physics: MonteCarlo within RayTrace{} for LED Monte Carlo raytracing

MortarSide.description: Specifies the mortar side for mortar periodic boundary conditions (MPBC). The side determines which boundary face acts as the mortar side in the periodic constraint, with values XMin/XMax, YMin/YMax, or ZMin/ZMax.

parameters.name: side
parameters.desc: One of XMin, XMax, YMin, YMax, ZMin, or ZMax, specifying the mortar side for MPBC.

MoveBoundaryStartRays.description: Shifts the starting ray position at device boundaries inward by the specified distance. Recommended values are 1 to 5 nm. This helps avoid numerical issues when rays originate exactly at material boundaries in LED raytracing simulations.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Distance to shift starting rays inward at boundaries, in nm. Default: 0.

MultiValley.description: Activates multivalley band structure statistics for accurate modeling of carrier transport in materials with multiple conduction/valence band minima (e.g., Si delta-valleys). Default is off.

contexts.Physics: Activates multivalley statistics in Physics section
contexts.Math: DensityIntegral in Math section controls integration points for multivalley band structure

MultipleRHS.description: Option for linear solvers ILS and ParDiSO to solve linear systems with multiple right-hand sides simultaneously. Only appropriate for AC analysis. May produce a small parallel speedup or slightly more accurate results when selected.

N.description: Generic letter N appearing in various contexts throughout the SDevice command file (e.g., as part of variable names, parameter names, or mathematical expressions). Not a standalone keyword.

NBTI.description: Two-stage NBTI (Negative Bias Temperature Instability) degradation model. Models threshold voltage shift due to trap generation and hydrogen diffusion in oxide under negative bias stress. Includes parameters for trap concentration, number of random samples, and optional hole-energy distribution function.

parameters.name: Conc
parameters.desc: Concentration of NBTI traps in cm^-2. Default: 0.

parameters.name: NumberOfSamples
parameters.desc: Number of random samples for statistical variation. Default: 0.

parameters.name: hSHEDistribution
parameters.desc: If specified, use hole-energy distribution function.

NDopantActiveConcentration.description: Active N-type dopant concentration scalar data available for plotting. Represents electrically active N dopant atoms per unit volume, in cm^-3. Specified at each vertex.

NDopantConcentration.description: Total N-type dopant concentration scalar data available for plotting. Represents all N dopant atoms (active and inactive) per unit volume, in cm^-3.

NDopantPlusConcentration.description: Ionized N-type dopant (NDopant+) concentration scalar data available for plotting. Represents positively charged N dopant ions per unit volume, in cm^-3.

Name.description: Specifies the name of an electrode, contact, or other named entity. In the Electrode section, defines the electrode name or uses a regular expression pattern for matching structure contacts.

parameters.name: string
parameters.desc: Name of the electrode or entity.

contexts.Electrode: Name of electrode in Electrode{} section
contexts.Math: Name in BreakCriteria{}, Surface{}, Volume{}

NaturalBoxMethod.description: Activates the edge-oriented element intersection box method algorithm for computing box method coefficients and control volumes. When disabled (default), the quadrilateral box method algorithm is used. Incompatible with BM_ExtendedPrecision, BoxCoefficientsFromFile, and BoxMeasureFromFile.

Ndivisions.description: Sets the number of subdivisions for the collection ring in a sensor sweep during unified raytracing far-field calculations.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Number of subdivisions for the collection ring.

NearestInterfaceOrientation.description: Scalar data for plotting the nearest interface orientation at each vertex. Available when auto-orientation is enabled for models such as mobility. Represents the Miller indices of the closest interface orientation, useful for verifying auto-orientation calculations.

NegInterfaceCharge.description: Negative interface charge density scalar data available for plotting, in cm^-2. Represents the net negative charge at semiconductor-insulator interfaces, used for mobility degradation modeling due to Coulomb scattering.

Netlist.description: Specifies a SPICE netlist file for mixed-mode simulations. The netlist defines the external circuit connected to the TCAD device(s). Sentaurus Device supports HSPICE and PrimeSim HSPICE netlist formats with .INCLUDE statements for hierarchical designs.

parameters.name: filename
parameters.desc: Path to the SPICE netlist file.

Neumann.description: Specifies homogeneous Neumann boundary condition (zero normal derivative) for various equations. In the Electrode section, applied to Poisson equation at Ohmic contacts. In the HydrogenBoundary section, applied to hydrogen flux. Also used for lattice temperature boundary conditions.

contexts.Electrode: Poisson boundary condition type for contacts
contexts.Math: Density gradient Neumann BC parameters (RefNa_QuantumPotential, RefNd_QuantumPotential)

NewArc.description: Activates a new arc length calculation for the continuation method. When enabled, uses a different algorithm for step size control in continuation (DC sweep) simulations. Changes default Increment/Decrement values to 1.5/2.0 and MaxRload to 1e8. Automatically enables NewGdiff.

NewCurrentPrefix.description: Appends a specified prefix to the default current file name in the Solve section. All subsequent Plot statements write to the new file with the appended prefix. Can be specified multiple times to switch output files during a simulation. Also recognized within a Continuation command.

parameters.name: prefix
parameters.desc: String prefix to append to the current file name.

contexts.Solve: In Solve section to redirect current plot output

NewGdiff.description: Enables a new differential conductance calculation within the continuation method. Automatically enabled when NewArc is active. Changes the conductance computation algorithm used for step size control in DC sweeps.

Newton.description: Newton solver method for coupled equation systems. Specified through Coupled() in the Solve section, which solves all selected equations simultaneously using Newton-Raphson iteration. Supports relaxed Newton via AcceptNewtonParameter and controls convergence through the Math section error criteria.

contexts.Solve: Coupled{} uses Newton iteration by default

NewtonPlot.description: Activates convergence monitoring for Newton iterations. Produces plots of equation residuals and damping factors versus iteration count. Automatically activated when MinStep drops below a threshold controlled by AutoNPMinStepFactor.

parameters.name: Error
parameters.desc: Write the error of all solution variables.

parameters.name: MinError
parameters.desc: Write file only if error decreases.

parameters.name: NewtonPlotStep
parameters.desc: Upper limit for step size for which to write Newton plot files.

parameters.name: Plot
parameters.desc: Write everything specified in the Plot section.

parameters.name: Residual
parameters.desc: Write the residuals (right-hand sides) of all equations.

parameters.name: Update
parameters.desc: Write the updates of all solution variables from the previous step.

contexts.Math: Global Math section for default NewtonPlot behavior
contexts.Solve: NewtonPlotStep parameter also in Quasistationary() for step-controlled output

NewtonPlotStep.description: Specifies the upper limit for step size for which to write Newton plot files. Only applicable within NewtonPlot() options. Also available as a parameter in Quasistationary() in the Solve section.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Maximum step size for Newton plot file output.

contexts.Math: Within NewtonPlot() in Math section
contexts.Solve: Also within Quasistationary() in Solve section

NitrogenActiveConcentration.description: Active nitrogen dopant concentration scalar data available for plotting, in cm^-3. Represents electrically active nitrogen atoms at each vertex.

NitrogenConcentration.description: Total nitrogen dopant concentration scalar data available for plotting, in cm^-3. Represents all nitrogen atoms at each vertex, relevant for doping species specification and multiple lattice site modeling.

NitrogenPlusConcentration.description: Ionized nitrogen dopant (N+) concentration scalar data available for plotting, in cm^-3. Represents positively charged nitrogen dopant ions at each vertex.

NoAutomaticCircuitContact.description: Prevents Sentaurus Device from automatically adding circuit and contact equations when Poisson is solved. When specified in the Math section, the Contact equation must be added explicitly in the Coupled statement to account for conductivity in metals. Default is off.

NoBandgapNarrowing.description: Disables bandgap narrowing in the effective intrinsic density calculation. When specified within EffectiveIntrinsicDensity(), no bandgap narrowing model is applied regardless of other bandgap narrowing settings (Slotboom, Schenk, BennettWilson, etc.).

NoCheckTransientError.description: Deactivates local truncation error (LTE) evaluation and automatic time-step control for transient simulations. When specified, the selection of the next time step is based only on convergence of nonlinear iterations rather than error estimation. This is the inverse of CheckTransientError.

contexts.Math: Global transient error control setting
contexts.Solve: Local override within Transient statement

NoFermi.description: Omits the Fermi statistics correction in the bandgap narrowing calculation even when Fermi statistics is enabled. This flag disables the additional correction term (Equation 172) that accounts for the interaction between bandgap narrowing and Fermi-Dirac statistics.

NoSRHperPotential.description: Omits potential derivatives of the SRH (Shockley-Read-Hall) recombination rate from the Jacobian matrix. This flag excludes derivatives of the field enhancement factor g(F) with respect to the electrostatic potential, which can help resolve convergence problems when using the Schenk or Hurkx trap-assisted tunneling models.

NoSRHperT.description: Omits temperature derivatives of the SRH (Shockley-Read-Hall) recombination rate from the Jacobian matrix. Used to improve convergence in simulations where temperature-dependent SRH recombination causes numerical difficulties.

Node.description: In mixed-mode circuit simulations, references a circuit node by name. Used in BreakCriteria{Current(Node=<ident>)} to define break conditions on specific circuit nodes, and in System{} for node voltage/current specifications.

NodesPerWavelength.description: Specifies the number of nodes per wavelength used for the computation of the optical field in the transfer matrix method (TMM). Controls the discretization resolution of the 1D optical field profile.

parameters.name: NodesPerWavelength
parameters.desc: Number of grid nodes per wavelength for optical field computation.

Noise.description: Activates noise analysis using the impedance field method (IFM). Specifies local noise source (LNS) models such as DiffusionNoise, GenerationRecombination, TrapNoise, and others. The optional string name allows multiple noise specifications in one simulation.

NoisePlot.description: Specifies noise output data to plot, including scalar/vector datasets, local noise sources, local noise voltage spectral densities, and Green's functions.

parameters.name: AllLNS
parameters.desc: Plot all used local noise sources.

parameters.name: AllLNVSD
parameters.desc: Plot all used local noise voltage spectral densities.

parameters.name: AllLNVXVSD
parameters.desc: Plot all used local noise voltage cross-correlation spectral densities.

parameters.name: GreenFunctions
parameters.desc: Plot all used Green's functions and their gradients.

NonActiveAbsorptionOff.description: Excludes optical absorption in nonactive regions from being added as generation rate to the continuity equation. When specified, the plot variable OpticalGeneration still shows values but these are not included in the carrier continuity equations. Used in LED simulations for photon recycling control.

NonLocal.description: Declares a named nonlocal mesh in the Math section. Nonlocal meshes are used for tunneling and nonlocal recombination models that require connectivity information beyond the standard finite element mesh, such as band-to-band tunneling and trap-assisted tunneling.

NonLocalBackDirection.description: Vector plot dataset that visualizes the back direction of nonlocal mesh lines. Available as a vertex-based vector dataset in the NonLocal plot. Used for inspecting the geometry and orientation of nonlocal tunneling paths.

NonLocalDirection.description: Vector plot dataset that visualizes the direction of nonlocal mesh lines. Available as a vertex-based vector dataset in the NonLocal plot, with units of micrometers. Used for inspecting the geometry and orientation of nonlocal tunneling paths.

NonLocalLengthLimit.description: Sets the maximum length limit for nonlocal mesh lines (in cm). Nonlocal mesh segments longer than this limit are not generated. Also used as the radius of influence for the BTCSMob (bulk trap carrier scattering) nonlocal mobility degradation model, beyond which remote traps do not contribute to mobility lowering at a given mesh point.

parameters.name: NonLocalLengthLimit
parameters.desc: Length limit in cm. Default: 1e-4 cm (1 um) for nonlocal mesh; 0 cm for BTCSMob.

contexts.Math: Global nonlocal mesh line length limit
contexts.Physics: Radius of influence for BTCSMob nonlocal mobility model

NonLocalPath.description: Controls handling of nonlocal path derivatives in the Math section, applicable to Quasistationary and Transient statements. Configures derivative computation, frozen tunneling direction, step-size control boundaries, Newton iteration count for Jacobian collection, and postprocessing mode.

NonLocalPlot.description: Plots data defined on nonlocal line meshes, including eigenenergies, wavefunctions, and overlap integrals from 1D Schrodinger equation solutions.

parameters.name: <vector>
parameters.desc: Spatial coordinates defining the nonlocal evaluation path.

parameters.name: EigenEnergy
parameters.desc: Plot eigenenergies from Schrodinger solver.

parameters.name: WaveFunction
parameters.desc: Plot wavefunctions (with optional Electron/Hole and Number restrictions).

parameters.name: OverlapIntegral
parameters.desc: Plot overlap integrals from Schrodinger solver.

NonParabolicity.signature: Physics { eMultiValley ( Nonparabolicity ) | hMultiValley ( Nonparabolicity ) | MultiValley ( Nonparabolicity ) }
NonParabolicity.description: Enables band nonparabolicity in the multivalley model. When activated, the nonparabolic energy dispersion relation is used to compute density-of-states and carrier statistics for multiple conduction/valley bands. Also affects stress-dependent band structure calculations through the kpDOS option. Can be excluded from MLDA quantization using MLDA(-Nonparabolicity).

NonSemiconductor.signature: Physics { Optics { OpticalSolver { RayTracing ( NonSemiconductor Absorption ) } } }
NonSemiconductor.description: Enables optical absorption calculation in nonsemiconductor regions or materials during raytracing. By default, optical generation is only computed in semiconductor regions. When this option is active, the raytracer also computes generation in insulator and other nonsemiconductor materials.

NonSymmetric.description: Refers to nonsymmetric (asymmetric) device structures in the context of LED radiation pattern computation and solver configuration. In LED simulations, the radiation pattern for nonsymmetric 2D structures produces different output files compared to symmetric structures. Also appears in solver configuration contexts for nonsymmetric matrix handling.
NonSymmetric.example:
# Nonsymmetric LED structure - handled automatically by Sentaurus Device
# The radiation pattern files differ for nonsymmetric vs symmetric structures

contexts.Math: Nonsymmetric matrix/solver configuration

NonlocalPlot.description: Controls the output of nonlocal tunneling path and band-to-band tunneling data for visualization and analysis.

NonsymmetricPermutation.description: Computes an initial nonsymmetric matrix permutation and scaling for the ParDiSo (PARDISO) direct linear solver, which places large matrix entries on the diagonal. This can improve solver speed at the cost of some accuracy. The default is +NonsymmetricPermutation.

NormalFieldCorrection.description: Specifies a dimensionless normal electric field correction factor for mobility at interface points. When nonzero, applies a correction to the electric field normal to the interface, improving the accuracy of mobility models near interfaces. The correction is based on the space charge density and dielectric constant. Reasonable values range from 0 to 1, with a default of zero.

parameters.name: NormalFieldCorrection
parameters.desc: Dimensionless correction factor alpha. Range: 0 to 1. Default: 0.

North.description: Cardinal direction specifying the north position of the bounding box of an illumination window. Used with OriginAnchor to define which point of the window bounding box coincides with the local origin. North places the origin at the top-center of the window bounding box.

contexts.LayerStackExtraction: Starting point of TMM layer stack extraction

NorthEast.description: Cardinal direction specifying the north-east corner of the bounding box of an illumination window. Used with OriginAnchor to place the local origin at the top-right corner of the window bounding box.

NorthWest.description: Cardinal direction specifying the north-west corner of the bounding box of an illumination window. Used with OriginAnchor to place the local origin at the top-left corner of the window bounding box.

contexts.LayerStackExtraction: Starting point of TMM layer stack extraction

NotDamped.description: Specifies the number of undamped iterations allowed before activating Bank-Rose damping in each Newton iteration. Default value is 1000. Can be set globally in the Math section or individually in Coupled and EquilibriumSolution statements.

NumberOfAssemblyThreads.description: Specifies the number of threads used for matrix assembly. Default value is 1. Separately controls assembly parallelism from NumberOfSolverThreads for the linear solver, with higher priority than NumberOfThreads.

NumberOfCellsPerLayer.description: Sets the number of wireframe discretization cells per propagation layer in weighted absorbed photon density (APD) integration. Used in 1D optical solvers (TMM, OptBeam, FromFile) to improve integration accuracy. Default value is 100. Denser wireframes provide higher accuracy but increase simulation time.
NumberOfCellsPerLayer.example:
Physics { Optics { OpticalSolver { Raytracing { ... } } } } # via Window Illumination
WeightedAPDintegration(NumberOfCellsPerLayer = 200)

NumberOfClusters.description: Specifies the number of active vertex clusters in LED optical simulation's ClusterActive. Only effective when ClusterQuantity=Nodes or ClusterQuantity=PlaneArea.
NumberOfClusters.example: Physics { LED { Optics { RayTrace { ClusterActive(NumberOfClusters = 50 ClusterQuantity = Nodes) } } } }

NumberOfCrystalFieldSplitHoleSubbands.description: Sets the maximum number of crystal field split hole subbands in the QWLocal quantum well model.

parameters.name: <int>
parameters.description: 晶体场分裂空穴子带数

NumberOfElectronSubbands.description: Sets the maximum number of electron subbands in the QWLocal quantum well model.

NumberOfFailures.description: Specifies the maximum number of consecutive extrapolation failures in the Extrapolate statement. After reaching this count, Sentaurus Device switches to using the current solution without extrapolation as the initial guess. Default value is 0. Can be used with Algorithm and MinStep.

parameters.name: <int>
parameters.description: 连续失败次数上限，默认 0

NumberOfHarmonics.description: Specifies the number of harmonics per fundamental frequency in HBCoupled's Tone clause.

NumberOfHeavyHoleSubbands.description: Sets the maximum number of heavy hole subbands in the QWLocal quantum well model.

NumberOfLightHoleSubbands.description: Sets the maximum number of light hole subbands in the QWLocal quantum well model.

NumberOfPoints.description: Specifies the number of frequency points for AC analysis in the ACCoupled statement. Used with StartFrequency, EndFrequency, Linear, or Decade to define the frequency sweep range.

NumberOfRandomTraps.description: Specifies the number of randomly distributed traps in the Traps DiscreteTrapT2T (discrete trap-to-trap tunneling) entry. Used with Randomize for statistical trap tunneling analysis.

NumberOfRays.description: Sets the number of rays in RayDistribution or UserWindow. Used for Monte Carlo or AutoPopulate distribution modes, or specifies the number of rays in a user-input ray file.
NumberOfRays.example: Physics { Optics { OpticalSolver { Raytracing { RayDistribution(NumberOfRays = 1000 Mode = MonteCarlo) } } } }

contexts.RayDistribution: Number of rays when Mode=MonteCarlo or Mode=AutoPopulate
contexts.UserWindow: Number of rays in user-input ray file

NumberOfSamples.description: Determines the number of samples in a set of statistical variations in RandomizedVariation. Used for sIFM (statistical impedance field method) simulations. Used with Randomize to control the random seed.

NumberOfSlices.description: Specifies the number of 2D slices (cross-sections) on the 3D mesh in the external 2D Schrodinger solver (ExternalSchroedinger) or external Boltzmann solver (ExternalBoltzmannSolver), on which the 2D Schrodinger equation is solved or quantum corrections are applied. At least 2 slices are required.

contexts.ExternalSchroedinger: Number of slices for 2D Schrodinger solver
contexts.ExternalBoltzmannSolver: Number of slices for Boltzmann solver

NumberOfSolverThreads.description: Specifies the number of threads used by the linear solver. Default value is 1. Separately controls solver parallelism from NumberOfAssemblyThreads for assembly, with higher priority than NumberOfThreads.

parameters.name: <int>
parameters.description: 线性求解器线程数，默认 1

NumberOfThreads.description: Specifies the number of threads used jointly by the linear solver and matrix assembly. Default value is 1. Can be set to maximum to use all available processor cores. Can be overridden by NumberOfAssemblyThreads and NumberOfSolverThreads.

parameters.name: <int>
parameters.description: 线程数，默认 1；可设为 maximum

NumberOfTransverseCellsPerDirection.description: Sets the wireframe discretization parameter in the transverse direction of the illumination window plane in weighted absorbed photon density (APD) integration. Default value is 200.

parameters.name: <int>
parameters.description: 每个横向方向的线框单元数，默认 200

NumberOfValenceBands.description: Sets the number of valence bands in the QWLocal quantum well model. Default value is 2. When Formula=2, this value cannot exceed 2.

Numerator.description: Defines the coefficients of the numerator polynomial in formulas for energy-dependent parameters (e.g., energy relaxation time, mobility, Peltier coefficient, Lombardi model). The numerator polynomial has the form (sum a_i * w^p_i)^g_n, where w is the main variable.

contexts.EnergyRelaxationTime: Numerator polynomial for energy relaxation time
contexts.Enhanced Lombardi: Numerator polynomial for enhanced Lombardi model

NumericalIntegration.description: Forces the use of numerical integration to compute hole masses in the strain effective mass and DOS models. By default, thermal simulations use analytical fitting and isothermal simulations use numerical integration. This option overrides the default behavior.

NumericalTable.description: Specifies wavelength-dependent data in tabular form in the parameter file, for complex refractive index models, thermal conductivity models, etc. Supports linear, logarithmic, spline, and other interpolation methods. When Formula=1, data is read from the parameter file; when Formula=2, from an external file.

contexts.ComplexRefractiveIndex: Tabular data for complex refractive index

Numerically.description: Enables numerical derivative computation instead of analytical derivatives. Disabled by default. An optional equation list can restrict numerical computation to specific Jacobian blocks. Note: incompatible with the Blocked method; generally not recommended.

ObservationCenter.description: Sets the fixed center point for radiation observation in LED optical simulation's RayTrace. Defaults to the device center. Used for LED radiation pattern calculation and ray tracing debugging.

parameters.name: <vector>
parameters.description: 观测中心坐标 (x y z)，单位 um

ObservationNode.description: Specifies circuit nodes for which noise spectral densities or deviation analysis is computed during AC-coupled simulations. Must be a subset of the nodes listed in the Node parameter of the ACCoupled statement.

ObservationRadius.description: Sets the radius of the far-field observation sphere in the unified ray tracing interface's Farfield. Default value is 1e6 um (1 meter). Used for far-field radiation pattern and sensor calculations.

Okuto.description: Okuto-Crowell avalanche generation (impact ionization) model. Expresses the ionization coefficient as a function of electric field with temperature-dependent parameters. Specified within Avalanche() in the Physics section.

parameters.name: a
parameters.desc: Pre-exponential coefficient.

parameters.name: b
parameters.desc: Exponential coefficient (V/cm).

parameters.name: c
parameters.desc: Temperature coefficient for the pre-exponential term.

parameters.name: d
parameters.desc: Temperature coefficient for the exponential term.

OldSlotBoom.description: Original Slotboom bandgap narrowing model. An older variant of the Slotboom BGN model with different default parameters. Used within EffectiveIntrinsicDensity() in the Physics section.

OldSlotboom.description: Alias for OldSlotBoom. Original Slotboom bandgap narrowing model with different default parameters from the current Slotboom model. Both spellings are accepted.

OmitReflectedRays.description: Discards all reflected rays from the raytracing process. Available in both unified raytracing (Physics{Optics(OpticalSolver(RayTracing(...)))}) and LED raytracing (Physics{LED{Optics{RayTrace(...)}}}).

OmitWeakerRays.description: Discards the weaker ray at a material interface during raytracing. The decision is based on comparing reflectivity and transmittivity. Available in both unified raytracing and LED raytracing contexts.

Once.description: Generates the tensor mesh once at the beginning of the FDTD simulation. The mesh remains in use for all subsequent FDTD solver calls. Alternative options are ForEachWavelength and a wavelength list.

OneOverDegradationTime.description: Scalar plot quantity representing 1/tau_D, the inverse of the predicted device lifetime for local degradation. For a single trap level, it equals 1/tau_D; for multiple trap levels, it equals the sum of individual 1/tau_D^i. Units: s^-1.

parameters.name: <vertex>
parameters.desc: Optional vertex number to plot at a specific location.

Operation.description: In the CurrentPlot Tcl() section, sets the operation mode that determines how results from each vertex are combined. Controls how per-vertex formula results are aggregated into a single value.

parameters.name: <string>
parameters.desc: Operation mode string specifying the aggregation method.

OptBeam.description: Uses the optical beam absorption method to compute optical generation rates. Supports layer stack extraction with region-wise or element-wise mode and illumination window positioning.

OptGenOffset.description: Specifies the offset parameter alpha (0 to 1, default 0.5) that divides the contribution of the optical generation rate into energy gain/loss terms Hn and Hp in the hydrodynamic model. Used with RecGenHeat in the Physics section.

parameters.name: OptGenOffset
parameters.desc: Offset value between 0 and 1 (default 0.5). Divides optical generation contribution between electron and hole heat terms.

OptGenScaling.description: Sets a multiplication factor to scale the nonactive region optical generation computed in LED simulations. Used within LED RayTrace section to control the contribution of photon recycling from nonactive regions.

parameters.name: OptGenScaling
parameters.desc: Scaling factor applied to nonactive optical generation.

OptGenTransientScaling.description: Specifies a file containing interpolation points for time-dependent optical generation scaling in transient simulations. Used in the File section to define scaling profiles loaded via TimeDependence(FromFile).

parameters.name: <string>
parameters.desc: Filename containing time-dependent scaling interpolation data.

OptGenWavelength.description: Specifies the wavelength in um used for hydrodynamic model optical generation heat calculation when optical generation is loaded from file. Defines the angular frequency in the heat equations.

parameters.name: OptGenWavelength
parameters.desc: Wavelength in um. Only used when optical generation is loaded from file.

Optical.description: Optical physics model within Physics{}. Enables optical generation/recombination effects and couples the optical solver with the electrical simulation. Used in optoelectronic device simulations such as solar cells, LEDs, and photodetectors.

OpticalAbsorptionHeat.description: Scalar plot quantity representing the optical absorption heat (W/cm^3). Computed when optical absorption generates heat in the device. Variants include OpticalAbsorptionHeat_Bandgap and OpticalAbsorptionHeat_Vacuum.

parameters.name: ScalingFactor
parameters.desc: Scaling factor for optical absorption heat (default 1). Specified in Physics{OpticalAbsorptionHeat(ScalingFactor=<float>)}.

OpticalDevice.description: Defines an optical device specification for mixed-mode simulation and Composite Method. Uses the same sub-sections as Device (Table 205).

parameters.name: <string>
parameters.desc: Optical device name.

parameters.name: File
parameters.desc: Input and output file specifications.

parameters.name: Electrode
parameters.desc: Electrode boundary conditions.

parameters.name: Physics
parameters.desc: Physical models.

parameters.name: Plot
parameters.desc: Plot data specifications.

OpticalField.description: Plot quantity for optical field (real and imaginary parts of optical field, W/m^3 V/m). In the Plot section, plots optical intensity and field components. In TensorPlot, plots BPM results on the native tensor grid.

contexts.Plot: Plots optical intensity and real/imaginary parts of optical field
contexts.TensorPlot: Plots BPM results on native tensor grid

OpticalGeneration.description: Specifies the type of optical generation computation and controls interpolation when loading optical generation profiles. Supports monochromatic source, spectrum, constant value, and file-based approaches with time dependence.

OpticalGenerationFile.description: Specifies a file from which to load optical generation data for optical AC analysis. Used in the File section of the command file.

parameters.name: <string>
parameters.desc: Filename containing optical generation data.

OpticalGenerationFromConstant.description: Scalar plot quantity (cm^-3 s^-1) showing the contribution to optical generation from the constant generation source (SetConstant). Plotted under the generic name OpticalGeneration.

OpticalGenerationFromMonochromaticSource.description: Scalar plot quantity (cm^-3 s^-1) showing the contribution to optical generation from a monochromatic light source (ComputeFromMonochromaticSource). Plotted under the generic name OpticalGeneration.

OpticalGenerationFromSpectrum.description: Scalar plot quantity (cm^-3 s^-1) showing the contribution to optical generation from an illumination spectrum (ComputeFromSpectrum). Plotted under the generic name OpticalGeneration.

OpticalGenerationInput.description: Specifies the file from which optical generation rate data is loaded. Used in the File section for loading pre-computed optical generation profiles.

parameters.name: <string>
parameters.desc: Filename containing optical generation rate data.

OpticalGenerationOutput.description: Specifies the file to which optical generation rate data is written. Used in the File section for saving computed optical generation profiles. Supports collected mode to write consecutive plots into one file.

parameters.name: <string>
parameters.desc: Base filename for optical generation output.

OpticalGridElement.description: Clustering method for LED raytracing that groups active vertices within each active optical element. Used within ClusterActive() in the LED RayTrace section.

OpticalIntensity.description: Scalar plot quantity (W/cm^2) for the optical intensity. Plots the total optical intensity in the device. Variants include OpticalIntensityCoherent and OpticalIntensityIncoherent for TMM solver.

OpticalIntensityCoherent.description: Scalar plot quantity (W/cm^2) for the coherent component of optical intensity from the TMM solver. Plotted under the generic name OpticalIntensity.

OpticalIntensityIncoherent.description: Scalar plot quantity (W/cm^2) for the incoherent component of optical intensity from the TMM solver. Plotted under the generic name OpticalIntensity.

OpticalSolver.description: In Physics{Optics(OpticalSolver(...))}, selects the optical solver method. Options include RayTracing for geometric optics, BPMScalar for scalar beam propagation, and TMM for transfer matrix method. Each solver has its own set of configuration parameters.

OpticalSolverInput.description: Specifies the command file for the optical solver, primarily used with the FDTD method. The FDTD-specific parameters such as boundary conditions and extractors are defined in this separate command file.

parameters.name: <string>
parameters.desc: Filename of the optical solver command file (e.g., EMW command file for FDTD).

OpticalTurningPoints.signature: OpticalTurningPoints( Dt=<float> DtRise=<float> DtFall=<float> DtRiseStart=<float> DtRiseEnd=<float> DtFallStart=<float> DtFallEnd=<float> DtPlateau=<float> MinAmplitude=<float> )
OpticalTurningPoints.description: Specifies optical turning points that control the temporal resolution of time-dependent optical generation profiles. Used within TimeDependence() in OpticalGeneration{} to define critical time points (t0, t1, t2, t3) for piecewise-linear or analytic signals with asymptotic tails.

parameters.name: Dt
parameters.desc: Limiting time step for range [t0,t3]. For FromFile time-dependence, default is smallest time interval from OptGenTransientScaling file. For other types, sets DtRise, DtPlateau, and DtFall to same value.

parameters.name: DtRise
parameters.desc: Limiting time step for rise range [t0,t1]. Default 0 s. Not for FromFile.

parameters.name: DtFall
parameters.desc: Limiting time step for fall range [t2,t3]. Default 0 s. Not for FromFile.

parameters.name: DtRiseStart
parameters.desc: Limiting time step at t0. Default (t1-t0)/10 s. Not for FromFile.

parameters.name: DtRiseEnd
parameters.desc: Limiting time step at t1. Default (t1-t0)/10 s. Not for FromFile.

parameters.name: DtFallStart
parameters.desc: Limiting time step at t2. Default (t1-t0)/10 s. Not for FromFile.

parameters.name: DtFallEnd
parameters.desc: Limiting time step at t3. Default (t1-t0)/10 s.

parameters.name: DtPlateau
parameters.desc: Limiting time step for plateau range [t1,t2]. Default 0 s. Not for FromFile.

parameters.name: MinAmplitude
parameters.desc: Signal amplitude used to define time points t0 and t3 for analytic signals with asymptotic tails. Default 0.01.

Optics.description: Specifies the optical solver and related optical physics models. Supports TMM (transfer matrix method), BPMScalar (beam propagation method), and LED-specific raytracing for optical generation computation.

OpticsFromFile.description: Flag to read optical generation data from a file instead of computing it during simulation. Used in OpticalGeneration{} to load pre-computed optical profiles, avoiding repeated raytracing or BPM calculations.

OpticsOutput.description: Specifies the base name for the runtime log file of optical solvers. The output file has extension _des.log. This is a global File section keyword.

parameters.name: <string>
parameters.desc: Base name for the optical solver runtime log file. Default SWB variable: @optlog@.

Options.description: Command-line options for Sentaurus Device that can also be specified within the Math section of the command file. Includes transient simulation controls, mobility stress factor settings, trap options, and PMI-related options. Many Options entries correspond to global settings.

contexts.Math: As a sub-section of Math{}
contexts.Solve: Transient simulation control options
contexts.Physics: Trap-related options within PMI models

Order.description: Specifies the order of extrapolation in Quasistationary or Transient commands within the Math section. Used in Extrapolate() to control how many previous solutions are used for initial guess computation.

parameters.name: <int>
parameters.desc: Order of extrapolation. Default 1.

contexts.Math: Within Extrapolate() in Math section
contexts.Physics: Ferroelectric model polynomial order

Organic_Gaussian.description: Activates the thermionic-like Gaussian emission model at organic heterointerfaces for both electrons and holes. Used within the Physics section at a RegionInterface. Requires GaussianDOS effective mass model to be activated in both adjacent regions. Automatically enables double points at the organic heterointerface.

OriKddX.description: Defines the x-component (Miller indices) of the stress/strain coordinate system relative to the simulation coordinate system. Used within Physics{Piezo()} to specify the orientation in which stress and strain tensors are defined. Default (1,0,0).

parameters.name: <float>*3
parameters.desc: Miller indices defining the x-direction of the stress coordinate system. Default (1,0,0).

OriKddY.description: Defines the y-component (Miller indices) of the stress/strain coordinate system relative to the simulation coordinate system. Used within Physics{Piezo()} to specify the orientation in which stress and strain tensors are defined. Default (0,1,0).

parameters.name: <float>*3
parameters.desc: Miller indices defining the y-direction of the stress coordinate system. Default (0,1,0).

OriKddx.description: Alias for OriKddX. Defines the x-component of the stress/strain coordinate system Miller indices within Physics{Piezo()}.

parameters.name: <float>*3
parameters.desc: Miller indices for x-direction of stress coordinate system.

OriKddy.description: Alias for OriKddY. Defines the y-component of the stress/strain coordinate system Miller indices within Physics{Piezo()}.

parameters.name: <float>*3
parameters.desc: Miller indices for y-direction of stress coordinate system.

Origin.description: Specifies the global location of the illumination window origin in the Excitation section. Used in optical generation setups to position the light source window in 3D device coordinates.

parameters.name: <float>*3
parameters.desc: Global coordinates (x, y, z) of the illumination window origin in um.

OriginAnchor.description: Anchors the illumination window origin to a cardinal direction relative to the device boundary. Values: North, South, East, West, NorthEast, SouthEast, Northwest, SouthWest. Used in Excitation{Window{}}.

parameters.name: <ident>
parameters.desc: Cardinal direction anchor: North, South, East, West, NorthEast, SouthEast, Northwest, SouthWest.

OuterDevicePower.description: Defines a break criterion based on the outer (dissipated) device power. Simulation stops when the absolute value of the outer power exceeds the specified threshold. Synonym of DevicePower.

Output.description: Specifies the base name for the Sentaurus Device runtime log file. The output file has extension _des.log. This is a global File section keyword.

parameters.name: <string>
parameters.desc: Base name for the runtime log file. Default: 'output'. SWB variable: @log@.

OutputLevel.description: Controls output frequency for tensor plots in bidirectional BPM simulations. When set to maximum, tensor plots are generated not only for the final solution, but also after every iteration.

parameters.name: maximum
parameters.desc: Generate tensor plots at every iteration (bidirectional BPM only).

OutputLightToolsFarfieldRays.description: Outputs far-field ray data for interfacing with LightTools optical design software. Controls output file format (ASCII or binary) and wavelength discretization for the exported ray data.

OutputLightToolsRays.description: Outputs LED starting rays from active vertices to a Synopsys LightTools-compatible ray data file. Used inside RayTrace(Disable(...)) to skip internal raytracing. Supports ASCII and binary formats, spectrum discretization, and ray clustering. The ray file base name is derived from the plot file name with _lighttools.txt or _lighttools.ray extension.

OutputT2TPaths.description: Specifies a debug output file for trap-to-trap (T2T) tunneling path information in discrete trap models. Used within DiscreteTrapT2T trap entries to write debugging data about trap-to-trap transition paths. Only needs to be defined once across all trap entries.

Outside.description: Flag used in various Physics contexts to indicate behavior outside a specified region or domain. Common usages include: piezoelectric polarization boundary conditions (assumed zero outside device), Schrodinger equation boundary conditions, spline interpolation, sIFM options, and KMC simulation space definitions.

contexts.Physics: Multiple Physics submodels (Piezo, Schrodinger, sIFM, etc.)

OverlapIntegral.description: Plots overlap integrals between electron and hole wavefunctions from 1D Schrodinger equation solutions. Used in NonLocalPlot{} section to visualize the wavefunction overlap in quantum wells.

Overwrite.description: Flag in Solve{Plot()}, Solve{Save()}, Solve{SpectralPlot()}, and Solve{CurrentPlot()} that rewrites the same file name at each loop iteration. When not specified, each new output file gets a new numbered name. This is the default behavior.

contexts.Solve: Within Plot/Save/SpectralPlot/CurrentPlot options in Solve section
contexts.Physics: pmi_msc_heatcapacity model parameter

PARDISO.description: Specifies the PARDISO direct solver as the linear solver method. PARDISO is a high-performance direct sparse matrix solver. Can be used in Math{Method=}, Solve{HBCoupled{Method=Pardiso}}, and Solve{Coupled{SubMethod=Pardiso}}.

contexts.Math: As Math{Method} or Math{SubMethod}
contexts.Solve: As Solve{HBCoupled{Method}}

PDopantActiveConcentration.description: Scalar plot dataset for the active (electrically active) concentration of p-type dopant species. Plotted at vertices. Related to Specifying Doping Species. Unit: cm^-3.

PDopantConcentration.description: Scalar plot dataset for the total concentration of p-type dopant species (PDopant). Plotted at vertices. Related to Specifying Doping Species. Unit: cm^-3.

PDopantMinusConcentration.description: Scalar plot dataset for the negatively charged p-type dopant species concentration (PDopant-). Plotted at vertices. Related to incomplete ionization. Unit: cm^-3.

PE_Charge.description: Scalar plot dataset for the piezoelectric charge density (q_PE). Computed as the divergence of the piezoelectric polarization vector. Plotted at vertices. Unit: cm^-3.

PE_Polarization.description: Vector plot dataset for the piezoelectric polarization vector. Requires /vector qualifier to plot as vector field. Plotted at vertices. Unit: C/cm^2.

PFMob.description: Poole-Frenkel mobility model for organic semiconductors. Activated as a high-field saturation model within the Mobility section. The mobility follows mu = mu0 * exp(-E0/kT) * exp(sqrt(F)*(beta/T - gamma)), where mu0 is low-field mobility, E0 is activation energy, and beta/gamma are fitting parameters. Parameters are adjusted in the PFMob section of the parameter file.

parameters.name: <driving_force>
parameters.desc: Driving force specification, typically Eparallel.

PMI.description: Physical Model Interface (PMI) framework keyword. PMI allows user-defined physical models through shared library (.so.arch files). Related keywords include PMIPath (search path for PMI models), Output (PMI output function), and model invocation syntax pmi_model_name(parameters). PMI models can be used for recombination, mobility, affinity, polarization, and more.

contexts.Physics: PMI model invocation in Physics section
contexts.Plot: PMI-related plot data (PMIRecombination, PMIUserField)

PMIHeat.description: Scalar plot dataset on vertices showing the PMI heat generation rate in W/cm^3. Plots the Heat value computed by a PMI model specified in HeatingSource(pmi_model) within the Physics section.

PMIModel.description: Specifies a Plug-In Module (PMI) model by name, with optional index and string arguments passed to the model. Used in various Physics contexts including high-field saturation (e.g., HighFieldSaturation, eHighFieldSaturation, hHighFieldSaturation), doping-dependent mobility, thermal conductivity, heat capacity, nonlocal band-to-band tunneling, and MSC-dependent models. Enables user-defined or MSC-dependent physical models.

PMIPath.description: Global File section keyword that defines the search path for PMI shared object files (.so.arch). The path consists of a colon-separated sequence of directories. For each PMI model activated in the Physics section, the specified directories are searched for the corresponding shared object file.

parameters.name: PMIPath
parameters.desc: Colon-separated list of directories to search for PMI shared object files.

PMIRecombination.description: Scalar plot dataset on vertices showing the total PMI recombination rate in cm^-3 s^-1. Plots the spatial distribution of recombination computed by a PMI model (such as intrinsicRichter) or the constant carrier generation model. Also used to visualize the constant carrier generation rate G_const.

PMIUserField0.description: Scalar plot dataset on vertices for user-defined field 0 (dimensionless). PMIUserField0 through PMIUserField299 provide access to mesh-based scalar fields defined in a separate TDR data file, specified by PMIUserFields in the File section. Up to 300 user fields are supported. These fields can be read and written by PMI models, used as initial conditions in the Solve section, and serve as spatial distribution datasets for trap concentration (SFactor).

PMIUserField1.description: Scalar plot dataset on vertices for user-defined field 1 (dimensionless). Part of the PMIUserField0-299 series providing mesh-based scalar field access for PMI models. Commonly used in PMI postprocessing to store computed intermediate results (e.g., temperature change rates).

PMIUserField3.description: User-defined field 3 (dimensionless). Part of the PMIUserField0-299 series. In the context of hydrogen transport, used as the initial density field for hydrogen ions loaded from a TDR file specified by PMIUserFields.

PMIUserField4.description: User-defined field 4 (dimensionless). Part of the PMIUserField0-299 series. In the context of hydrogen transport, used as the initial density field for hydrogen Species A loaded from a TDR file specified by PMIUserFields.

PMIUserField5.description: User-defined field 5 (dimensionless). Part of the PMIUserField0-299 series. In hydrogen transport, used as the initial density field for hydrogen Species B. Also usable as a generic PMI user field in CurrentPlot Tcl formulas.

PMIUserField6.description: User-defined field 6 (dimensionless). Part of the PMIUserField0-299 series. In the context of hydrogen transport, used as the initial density field for hydrogen Species C loaded from a TDR file specified by PMIUserFields.

PMIUserFields.description: Device-specific File section keyword specifying the base name of a TDR data file containing any of the user-defined fields PMIUserField0 through PMIUserField299. The file provides mesh-based scalar fields accessible by PMI models at runtime, and can be used for initial conditions in Solve Set commands, spatial distributions for trap concentration (SFactor), and eNMP precursor concentration.

parameters.name: PMIUserFields
parameters.desc: Base name of the TDR file containing user-defined fields.

PMI_model.description: References a PMI (Physical Model Interface) model by name within various Physics subsections. The specific semantics depend on the context: in HeatSource, defines a custom heat generation model; in MetalTEPower, defines metal thermoelectric power; in TEPower, defines semiconductor thermoelectric power; in Diffusivity, defines custom diffusivity; in MetalResistivity, defines metal resistivity; in Piezo Tensor, defines piezoresistive coefficients; in HotCarrierInjection, defines hot-carrier injection current. In each context, the PMI model name is used directly as a keyword without the PMI_model prefix.

contexts.HeatSource: PMI model for heat generation rate [W/cm^3]
contexts.MetalTEPower: PMI model for metal thermoelectric power [V/K]
contexts.TEPower: PMI model for semiconductor thermoelectric power [V/K]

PMIeNonLocalRecombination.description: Scalar plot dataset on vertices showing the electron nonlocal PMI recombination rate in cm^-3 s^-1. Plots the spatial distribution of electron recombination computed by a nonlocal generation-recombination PMI model.

PMIhNonLocalRecombination.description: Scalar plot dataset on vertices showing the hole nonlocal PMI recombination rate in cm^-3 s^-1. Plots the spatial distribution of hole recombination computed by a nonlocal generation-recombination PMI model.

PML.description: Perfectly Matched Layer absorbing boundary condition for optical solvers (BPM, FDTD). PML boundary conditions absorb the optical field at domain boundaries to minimize spurious reflections. Supports configuration of grid nodes, stretching parameters (imaginary and real parts), polynomial order, and side selection. Also applicable to boundary conditions in electromagnetic wave propagation contexts.
PML.example: Physics { Optics(OpticalSolver(BPM(Boundary(Type="PML" GridNodes=10 StretchingParameter Imag=(1 5) Order=2 Side="X")))) }

ParDiSo.description: Selects the parallel supernodal direct solver (Parallel Direct Solver). Provides high accuracy and parallel performance for small to medium-sized problems. Supports iterative refinement and nonsymmetric permutations.

Parallel.description: Math section keyword related to parallelization configuration. In the context of numerical methods, controls parallel execution of solver algorithms. The behavior of parallel simulations is governed by related keywords including Parallelization, nThreads, and ParallelLicence.

ParallelLicence.description: In the Math section, determines the behavior when insufficient parallel (thread) licenses are available. Controls whether the simulation falls back to fewer threads or aborts when license limits are exceeded.

ParallelToInterfaceInBoundaryLayer.description: Math section keyword that controls field correction close to interfaces. When active, the electric field component parallel to the interface is used instead of the full electric field for vertices in the boundary layer near semiconductor-insulator interfaces or external boundaries. This avoids nonphysical breakdowns along interfaces with coarse meshes. Supports PartialLayer (default, elements connected by edge/face) and FullLayer (elements touching by face, edge, or vertex) options. Can be selectively deactivated on external boundaries or specific planes. Activated by default; use -ParallelToInterfaceInBoundaryLayer to deactivate entirely.

contexts.DrivingForce: Affects avalanche and high-field saturation driving force computation near interfaces
contexts.Enormal: Affects electric field component normal to current near interfaces

ParallelToInterfaceInBoundaryLayerActive.description: Scalar plot dataset on elements (dimensionless). Indicates whether the ParallelToInterfaceInBoundaryLayer field correction is active at each element. Value 1 means the correction is applied, 0 means it is not.

Parallelization.description: Math section keyword that enables or configures parallel computation for the solver. When specified, Sentaurus Device uses multiple threads for numerical computations. The number of threads is controlled by the nThreads keyword. Related to ParallelLicence for license management.

Param1.description: SPICE subcircuit parameter in the System section. Defines a named parameter within a .SUBCKT or .MACRO subcircuit definition that can be referenced in the subcircuit body and overridden when the subcircuit is instantiated.

parameters.name: Param1
parameters.desc: Subcircuit parameter name and value assignment.

Parameter.description: Keyword appearing in the Math section related to parameter handling and solver configuration. In the broader context of Sentaurus Device, parameters for PMI models can be defined in the parameter file (.par) under sections corresponding to the PMI model name. The ParameterPath and Parameters keywords in the File section control parameter file loading.

ParameterInheritance.description: Math section keyword controlling how parameter specifications from different scopes (global, materialwise, regionwise, interface-wise) are combined. When set to Flatten (default), parameter specifications from outer scopes are inherited and can be overridden by inner scopes, contributing to a global default set. When set to None, regionwise parameters that do not have explicit sections are discarded entirely and the material parameters are accessed instead.
ParameterInheritance.example:
Math { ParameterInheritance = Flatten }
Math { ParameterInheritance = None }

ParameterPath.description: Device-specific File section keyword specifying a list of subdirectories in the $STROOT/tcad/$STRELEASE/lib/sdevice/MaterialDB directory to be added to the search path for parameter files. Multiple release-specific directories can be specified to prioritize parameter files from specific releases.

parameters.name: ParameterPath
parameters.desc: Space-separated list of release names or subdirectory paths added to the MaterialDB search path.

ParameterSetName.description: Keyword used within various Physics model subsections to select a named parameter set. Named parameter sets allow alternative parameterizations for models such as high-field saturation mobility, ballistic mobility, mobility stress factor, piezoresistance, and band-to-band tunneling. Parameter sets are defined in the parameter file with a quoted name and can be referenced from the command file.

ParameterVariation.description: Specifies deterministic parameter variations for sensitivity analysis. Each variation modifies a physical model parameter to compute its cumulative impact on device characteristics.

Parameters.description: Device-specific File section keyword specifying the base name of the device parameter file (input, .par). This file contains physical model parameters for materials, regions, and interfaces that override or supplement the built-in default values.

parameters.name: Parameters
parameters.desc: Base name of the parameter file (.par). The @parameter@ SWB variable is the default.

Pardiso.description: Alias for ParDiSo. Selects the parallel supernodal direct solver (PARDISO). Provides high accuracy and parallel performance for small to medium-sized problems. Pardiso and ParDiSo are interchangeable; the solver options and behavior are identical.

PartialLayer.description: Option for the ParallelToInterfaceInBoundaryLayer keyword in the Math section. When specified, parallel fields are only used in boundary layer elements that are connected to the interface by an edge (in 2D) or a face (in 3D). This is the default layer mode. The alternative is FullLayer which uses parallel fields in all elements touching the interface.

Particle.description: Plot dataset name for particle-related data in the Plot section. Refers to MIM (metal-insulator-metal) particle data representing MIM defects, as listed in the particle data table (Table 199). Used for visualizing defect locations in KMC-MIM transport simulations.

Particles.description: Defines particle types and their properties in the KMC (Kinetic Monte Carlo) MIM transport model. Each particle can be assigned a name, excluded particle list, and sharing capacity. Particles defined with Filament prefix are conductive and considered in the conductance equation.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.desc: Name of the particle.

parameters.name: Exclude
parameters.desc: List of excluded particles.

parameters.name: ShareCapacity
parameters.desc: List of particle sharing capacities.

PasCoef.description: Passivation coefficient (gamma_0) in s^-1 for the hydrogen passivation/depassivation model (NBTI/HCS degradation). Specifies the rate at which silicon-hydrogen bonds are passivated at the equilibrium passivation temperature T0. By default, computed automatically as v0*N0hb/(N-N0hb).

parameters.name: PasCoef
parameters.desc: Passivation coefficient gamma_0 in s^-1.

PasTemp.description: Passivation temperature T0 in K for the hydrogen passivation/depassivation model. Defines the equilibrium temperature at which the passivation coefficient is measured. Default is 300 K.

parameters.name: PasTemp
parameters.desc: Passivation temperature T0 in K (default 300).

PasVolume.description: Passivation volume Omega in cm^2 (2D) or cm^3 (3D) for the hydrogen passivation/depassivation model. Defines the effective volume for the passivation reaction. Default is 0.

parameters.name: PasVolume
parameters.desc: Passivation volume Omega in cm^2|cm^3 (default 0).

PathSearchStopField.description: Threshold value of the absolute value of the electric field (in V/cm) where the path search stops in approximate breakdown analysis. Default is 1000 V/cm. Controls the stopping criterion for ionization integral path computation.

parameters.name: PathSearchStopField
parameters.desc: Electric field threshold in V/cm where path search stops (default 1000).

PeakPosition.description: Peak position of the Gaussian intensity profile in the Excitation section, specified in local coordinates of the defined shape function. Used within Gaussian() in IntensityDistribution() to define where the optical intensity peaks.

parameters.name: PeakPosition
parameters.desc: Two floats specifying (x, y) peak position in local coordinates.

PeakWidth.description: Width of the plateau of the modified Gaussian profile in the Excitation section. Used within Gaussian() in IntensityDistribution() to define a flat-topped Gaussian intensity distribution.

parameters.name: PeakWidth
parameters.desc: Two floats specifying (x, y) width of the plateau region.

PeltierHeat.description: Scalar plot dataset for Peltier heat generation rate in W/cm^3. Represents the heat generated or absorbed due to carrier flow across potential gradients (J·∇E/q). Available as a vertex-based quantity for both electrons and holes.

Period.description: Specifies the period for large-signal cyclic analysis in the Solve section. Defines the time period of one full cycle for periodic steady-state simulations. Also appears in optical excitation and ferroelectrics model contexts.

parameters.name: Period
parameters.desc: Period of the cycle in seconds.

contexts.Solve: Large-signal cyclic analysis period
contexts.Physics: Ferroelectrics oscillation period parameter

Periodic.description: Specifies periodic boundary conditions for the device boundary in the Boundary section. Used to simulate periodic device structures. Also appears in optical raytracing (RayTraceBC) for periodic boundary treatment of rays.

contexts.Physics: RayTraceBC periodic option for optical raytracing

PeriodicBC.description: Activates periodic boundary conditions (PBCs) for the device simulation. The Direction parameter specifies the axis of periodicity: 0 for x-axis, 1 for y-axis, and 2 for z-axis. Used for simulating periodic device structures such as finFET arrays.

Permeable.description: Controls whether nonlocal mesh lines can be extended through a region. When set (default), nonlocal lines are allowed to extend across the reference surface into or through the region. When negated (-Permeable), the extension of nonlocal lines is blocked for that region. Can be specified globally in Math or region-specifically.

Permeation.description: Specifies the length (in cm) by which nonlocal mesh lines are extended across the reference surface. Defaults to 0. The extension is limited by NonLocalLengthLimit. Sentaurus Device never extends lines outside the device or into regions flagged with -Permeable.

parameters.name: Permeation
parameters.desc: Extension length in cm (default 0).

Perpendicular.description: Specifies the perpendicular direction for anisotropic models in the Physics section. Used in anisotropic mobility, avalanche, quantum potential, and other tensor-dependent models to define the orientation of anisotropy relative to the crystal or simulation coordinate system.

Phi.description: Phase angle parameter in piezoelectric polarization or optical excitation models. In piezoelectric context, relates to the crystallographic orientation angles. In optical context, specifies the phase of the electromagnetic field or excitation source.

parameters.name: Phi
parameters.desc: Phase angle in degrees or radians depending on context.

PhononCombination.description: Selects how 2D and 3D phonon mobility components are combined in the IALMob (inversion and accumulation layer mobility) model. Values range from 0 to 2, with default 1. Controls the blending between surface phonon scattering (2D) and bulk phonon scattering (3D) contributions.

parameters.name: PhononCombination
parameters.desc: Combination mode (0-2, default 1). Selects how 2D and 3D phonon mobility are combined.

PhononEnergy.description: Phonon energy in eV for inelastic tunneling models. Used in nonlocal trap-to-trap tunneling, trap-assisted tunneling calculations, and KMC-MIM transport simulations. Defines the energy of phonons involved in inelastic tunneling processes.

parameters.name: PhononEnergy
parameters.desc: Phonon energy in eV (>=0).

Phosphorus.description: Selects phosphorus-specific parameters for the Philips unified mobility model (PhuMob). Used within PhuMob() to apply dopant-dependent mobility parameters optimized for phosphorus-doped silicon. Also referenced in thermal conductivity and Coulomb scattering model contexts.

PhosphorusActiveConcentration.description: Scalar plot dataset for the electrically active phosphorus dopant concentration in cm^-3. Represents the ionized phosphorus atoms contributing to conductivity.

PhosphorusConcentration.description: Scalar plot dataset for the total phosphorus dopant concentration (P) in cm^-3. Includes both electrically active and inactive phosphorus atoms.

PhosphorusPlusConcentration.description: Scalar plot dataset for the positively ionized phosphorus donor concentration (P+) in cm^-3. Relevant when incomplete ionization model is enabled.

PhuMob.description: Philips unified mobility model that combines lattice (phonon) scattering and impurity scattering into a single analytical expression. Provides physically consistent behavior across all doping concentrations. Options include dopant-specific parameters (Arsenic/Phosphorus) and can be used within DopingDependence(). Also serves as a submodel for IALMob surface mobility.

parameters.name: Arsenic
parameters.desc: Use arsenic-specific parameters (default).

parameters.name: Phosphorus
parameters.desc: Use phosphorus-specific parameters.

PhuMob2.description: Alternative Philips unified mobility model with modified parameterization. Provides a different analytical form compared to PhuMob for doping-dependent mobility. Must be specified within DopingDependence() in the Mobility section.

Physics.description: Specifies physical models for the device simulation, including mobility, recombination, quantum corrections, thermodynamic models, and more. Can be restricted to a specific location (material, region, interface, or electrode).

parameters.name: Mobility
parameters.desc: Mobility model selection (Table 278).

parameters.name: Recombination
parameters.desc: Generation-recombination models (SRH, Auger, Avalanche, etc.).

parameters.name: Fermi
parameters.desc: Enable Fermi-Dirac statistics.

parameters.name: Temperature
parameters.desc: Device (lattice) temperature in K (default 300).

parameters.name: EffectiveIntrinsicDensity
parameters.desc: Band gap and bandgap narrowing model.

parameters.name: Thermodynamic
parameters.desc: Enable thermodynamic transport model.

PieceWiseConstant.description: Specifies piecewise constant interpolation between loaded optical generation profiles. When loading optical generation from files, the spectral interpolation uses nearest-neighbor (piecewise constant) between discrete spectral data points.

Piezo.description: Configures piezoresistive and stress-dependent models in the Physics section. Specifies stress or strain tensor components, selects mobility tensor models (e.g., Tensor("pmi_model")), and enables stress-dependent PMI models. Constant stress/strain tensors can be specified directly, or read from a TDR file via the File section.

PiezoCharge.description: Scalar plot dataset for the discontinuous piezoelectric charge distribution at heterointerfaces. Unlike PE_Charge which always provides a continuous distribution, PiezoCharge outputs the interface-discontinuous charge values that occur at double points in heterostructure simulations.

PiezoParameters.description: Alternative keyword to LatticeParameters for defining crystal and simulation coordinate system orientations. Recognized within the Physics section, it specifies the X and Y vectors of the crystal coordinate system relative to the simulation coordinate system. If not specified, defaults to X=(1,0,0), Y=(0,1,0).

Piezoelectric_Polarization.description: Enables piezoelectric polarization modeling for III-nitride and other polar semiconductor materials (GaN, AlN, InN).

Piezolectric_Polarization.description: Activates piezoelectric polarization modeling by adding the divergence of the piezoelectric polarization vector as an additional charge term to the Poisson equation. The polarization vector is defined by a PMI model. Relevant for wurtzite crystals (e.g., GaN-based devices) where piezoelectric effects significantly impact carrier distributions. The resulting polarization vector and charge can be plotted as PE_Polarization/vector and PE_Charge.

Piezoresistance.description: Piezoresistive mobility model based on the expansion of the mobility enhancement tensor with respect to stress to model the piezoresistive effect. Supports first-order (linear stress dependence) and second-order (linear + quadratic stress dependence) models. Isotropic factor models can also be used via Factor()/eFactor()/hFactor().

parameters.name: FirstOrder
parameters.desc: Use first-order piezoresistance model (default)

parameters.name: SecondOrder
parameters.desc: Use second-order piezoresistance model, including linear and quadratic stress dependence

parameters.name: Kanda
parameters.desc: off includes temperature and doping dependence

parameters.name: AutoOrientation
parameters.desc: off automatically select parameter set based on nearest interface orientation

parameters.name: ParameterSetName
parameters.desc: Piezoresistance parameter set name

parameters.name: Enormal
parameters.desc: off use piezoresistance coefficients (first-order model only)

parameters.name: ChannelDirection
parameters.desc: 1 channel direction (Factor model only)

parameters.name: SFactor
