parameters.desc: Mobility enhancement factor spatial distribution dataset or PMI model name

parameters.name: EffectiveStressModel
parameters.desc: Use effective stress Factor model

PlaneArea.description: Active vertex clustering method in LED ray tracing that uniformly divides the quantum well plane area and groups active vertices by region. A sub-parameter of LED(Optics(RayTrace(ClusterActive()))).

parameters.name: ClusterQuantity
parameters.desc: =PlaneArea clustering mode, uniformly dividing QW plane area

parameters.name: NumberOfClusters
parameters.desc: Specifies the number of clusters, only for Nodes or PlaneArea clustering

PlaneWave.signature: Physics { Optics( OpticalSolver( BPM( Excitation( PlaneWave( ... ) ) ) ) ) }
PlaneWave.description: Plane wave excitation mode for the BPM optical solver, one of the optical excitation sources (the other being Gaussian). Used to compute optical generation rate.

Plot.description: Specifies which datasets to include in the output TDR plot file. Supports solution variables (e.g., ElectricField, eDensity, Potential), optical quantities, trap distributions, hydrogen species, and derived quantities. The output file is named via the File section's Plot keyword.

PlotBandstructure.description: Plots band structure data in an LED simulation during a Quasistationary or Transient run. Outputs local band structure information including eigenvalues and eigenfunctions at specified vertices. Requires the k.p model or Schrodinger equation to be solved. The base name for output files is set by Bandstructure in the File section. Follows the same parameter syntax as the Plot command (Table 345).

parameters.name: FilePrefix
parameters.desc: Prefix of the output file name

parameters.name: Overwrite
parameters.desc: Rewrite the same file name at each loop (default: on)

parameters.name: noOverwrite
parameters.desc: Give each new plot file a new name by numbering

parameters.name: Time
parameters.desc: Time points for plotting during transient

parameters.name: When
parameters.desc: Generate output when a target voltage or current is crossed

parameters.name: Intervals
parameters.desc: Number of intervals within Range

PlotExplicit.description: Global Math section keyword that prevents Plot statements from outputting datasets not explicitly specified in the Plot section (such as default output items like carrier density). Can be locally overridden using Explicit in Plot statements within Solve.

PlotFarField.description: Plots the optical far-field radiation pattern of an LED device during a Quasistationary or Transient simulation. Outputs angular-dependent intensity data to the plot file. Follows the same parameter syntax as the Plot command (Table 345). Requires LED radiation physics to be enabled in the Physics section.

parameters.name: FilePrefix
parameters.desc: Prefix of the output file name

parameters.name: Overwrite
parameters.desc: Rewrite the same file name at each loop (default: on)

parameters.name: noOverwrite
parameters.desc: Give each new plot file a new name by numbering

parameters.name: Time
parameters.desc: Time points for plotting during transient (range, intervals, decade)

parameters.name: When
parameters.desc: Generate output when a target voltage or current is crossed

parameters.name: Intervals
parameters.desc: Number of intervals within the Range.

PlotGain.description: Plots stimulated and spontaneous emission spectra data in an LED or laser simulation during a Quasistationary or Transient run. Outputs modal gain and spontaneous emission data. The base name for output files is set by Gain in the File section. Follows the same parameter syntax as the Plot command (Table 345).

parameters.name: FilePrefix
parameters.desc: Prefix of the output file name

parameters.name: Overwrite
parameters.desc: Rewrite the same file name at each loop (default: on)

parameters.name: noOverwrite
parameters.desc: Give each new plot file a new name by numbering

parameters.name: Time
parameters.desc: Time points for plotting during transient

parameters.name: When
parameters.desc: Generate output when a target voltage or current is crossed

parameters.name: Intervals
parameters.desc: Number of intervals within the Range.

PlotGainPara.description: Plots gain parameters in an LED or laser simulation, providing modal gain and material gain data for analysis.

PlotInterfaceFlux.description: Enables interface optical flux tracking. Records reflected, transmitted, and absorbed flux at each interface or contact BC. Output variables include RaytraceInterfaceFlux, R(region1/region3), T(region1/region3), etc. Only for the unified ray tracing interface.

PlotLEDRadiation.description: Plots the optical far-field radiation pattern of an LED device during a Quasistationary or Transient simulation. This is a specialized plot command for LED simulations that outputs the angular-dependent far-field intensity. Follows the same parameter syntax as the Plot command (Table 345). Requires LED radiation raytracing to be enabled in the Physics section.

parameters.name: FilePrefix
parameters.desc: Prefix of the output file name

parameters.name: Overwrite
parameters.desc: Rewrite the same file name at each loop (default: on)

parameters.name: noOverwrite
parameters.desc: Give each new plot file a new name by numbering

parameters.name: Time
parameters.desc: Time points for plotting during transient

parameters.name: When
parameters.desc: Generate output when a target voltage or current is crossed

parameters.name: Intervals
parameters.desc: Number of intervals within the Range.

PlotLoadable.description: Global Math section keyword. PlotLoadable causes all Plot statements to write additional information for loading and continuing simulation from .tdr files (enabled by default). -PlotLoadable prohibits writing this extra information. Can be locally overridden with Loadable in Plot statements within Solve.

PlotRequest.description: Used with Interrupt in the Math section. When an INT signal is received, Sentaurus Device writes plot/save files and continues simulation (unlike BreakRequest, which terminates the current solve statement).

Plugin.description: Controls a Gummel-type iterative loop over two or more Coupled statements. Used when a fully coupled Newton method would consume too many resources or diverge. The standard Gummel iteration solves each semiconductor equation (Poisson, Electron, Hole) consecutively. Plugin commands can be nested to create complex iterative solve patterns.

parameters.name: Iterations
parameters.desc: Maximum number of outer Plugin iterations

parameters.name: Digits
parameters.desc: Number of significant digits for convergence check

parameters.name: ErrRef
parameters.desc: Reference error parameters per equation

parameters.name: Error
parameters.desc: Absolute error parameters per equation

parameters.name: RelErrControl
parameters.desc: Use relative error control (default)

parameters.name: Method
parameters.desc: Linear solver method (e.g., Blocked, ILS)

parameters.name: BreakOnFailure
parameters.desc: Stop when the internal Coupled solver fails.

PoECImACGreenFunction.description: Scalar plot data: imaginary part of electron admittance Green's function (Im(Gn)). For AC small-signal and noise analysis. Unit: V*s/C.

PoECReACGreenFunction.description: Scalar plot data: real part of electron admittance Green's function (Re(Gn)). For AC small-signal and noise analysis. Unit: V*s/C.

PoETImACGreenFunction.description: Scalar plot data: imaginary part of electron temperature Green's function (Im(GTn)). For AC small-signal and noise analysis. Unit: A^(-1).

PoETReACGreenFunction.description: Scalar plot data: real part of electron temperature Green's function (Re(GTn)). For AC small-signal and noise analysis. Unit: A^(-1).

PoGeoGreenFunction.description: Scalar plot data: real part of geometry variation Green's function. For AC small-signal and noise analysis. Unit: V/cm^3.

PoHCImACGreenFunction.description: Scalar plot data: imaginary part of hole admittance Green's function (Im(Gp)). For AC small-signal and noise analysis. Unit: V*s/C.

PoHCReACGreenFunction.description: Scalar plot data: real part of hole admittance Green's function (Re(Gp)). For AC small-signal and noise analysis. Unit: V*s/C.

PoHTImACGreenFunction.description: Scalar plot data: imaginary part of hole temperature Green's function (Im(GTp)). For AC small-signal and noise analysis. Unit: A^(-1).

PoHTReACGreenFunction.description: Scalar plot data: real part of hole temperature Green's function (Re(GTp)). For AC small-signal and noise analysis. Unit: A^(-1).

PoLTImACGreenFunction.description: Scalar plot data: imaginary part of lattice temperature Green's function (Im(GT)). For AC small-signal and noise analysis. Unit: A^(-1).

PoLTReACGreenFunction.description: Scalar plot data: real part of lattice temperature Green's function (Re(GT)). For AC small-signal and noise analysis. Unit: A^(-1).

PoPotImACGreenFunction.description: Scalar plot data: imaginary part of electrostatic potential Green's function (Im(Gphi)). For AC small-signal and noise analysis. Unit: V/C.

PoPotReACGreenFunction.description: Scalar plot data: real part of electrostatic potential Green's function (Re(Gphi)). For AC small-signal and noise analysis. Unit: V/C.

Poisson.description: Poisson equation for electrostatic potential, solved as part of the coupled system. In Solve{} section, specifies Poisson as the equation to solve. Default error reference is 0.0258 V (thermal voltage at 300 K).

Polarization.description: Scalar plot data: magnitude of polarization vector |P|, unit C/cm^2. Also available as vector data (vertex/element positions). Related to ferroelectrics and piezoelectric polarization models.

PolarizationAngle.description: In Physics{Optics{Excitation(...)}}, specifies the angle (in degrees) between the H-field and z*k used for vectorial optical solvers. Corresponds to the Psi keyword in EMW for FDTD solver.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Angle between H-field and z*k in degrees.

PolarizationFactor.description: In QWLocal() or Schroedinger(), sets the polarization factor (a in [0,1]) used for computing the optical transition matrix element in mixed polarization LED simulations. The factor interpolates between TE (a=1) and TM (a=0) contributions.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Polarization factor in [0,1]. 1=TE, 0=TM.

contexts.QWLocal: In Physics{QWLocal(...)} for localized QW model

PolarizationVector.description: In RayTracing() or UserWindow(), sets the polarization vector for starting rays. Random automatically chooses a perpendicular polarization; a fixed vector can also be specified. Also appears in RayTrace() within LED context.

parameters.name: Random
parameters.desc: Automatically choose random polarization perpendicular to ray direction.

parameters.name: <vector>
parameters.desc: Fixed polarization vector (x y z).

Polygon.description: Defines a polygon-shaped illumination window in 3D optical simulations. Specified as a series of vertices in the local 2D coordinate system of the window. Used within Window() in the Physics{Optics{Excitation}} section to define the spatial extent of incident optical excitation.

parameters.name: (<float>*2)*n
parameters.desc: Vertices of the polygon in local window coordinates (x y).

parameters.name: ((<float>*2)*n)*m
parameters.desc: Multiple loops of vertices for complex polygon shapes.

PooleFrenkel.description: In Traps() specification, activates the Poole-Frenkel model for field-dependent cross sections for exchange with conduction or valence band. The optional Electron/Hole qualifier forces the model to a specific band irrespective of trap type.

PosInterfaceCharge.description: A scalar plot variable representing positive interface charge density (cm^-2) used for mobility degradation due to Coulomb scattering at semiconductor-insulator interfaces.

Position.description: Specifies a spatial position vector (x y z) in um. Used in various contexts including NonLocal mesh definitions, PMI raytracer dependencies, and trap placement for DiscreteTrapT2T.

parameters.name: <vector>
parameters.desc: Position vector (x y z) in um.

PostProcess.description: In the Math section, specifies a PMI postprocessing routine to be called after each transient time step. The string identifies the PMI postprocess model name.

PostProcessing.description: In NonLocalPath(), switches on and off postprocessing mode for dynamic nonlocal path trap-assisted tunneling. Also appears as a general concept for hot-carrier injection models that compute results after each simulation point.

contexts.NonLocalPath: In Math{NonLocalPath(...)} to enable postprocessing mode
contexts.GateCurrent: General concept for post-computation of hot-carrier injection

PostTemperature.description: Activates uniform self-heating model in the global Physics section. Temperature is obtained by postprocessing rather than self-consistently. Dissipated power can be computed as integral of Joule heat, or as sum of IV over all or user-selected electrodes.

parameters.name: IV_diss
parameters.desc: Compute dissipated power as sum of IV over electrodes. Optionally specify electrode names.

parameters.name: <string>
parameters.desc: Electrode name for selective IV dissipation calculation.

Postprocessing.description: In NonLocalPath(), switches on and off postprocessing mode for dynamic nonlocal path trap-assisted tunneling derivatives. Identical to PostProcessing (uppercase P).

Potential.description: Electrostatic potential variable. In Aniso{}, enables anisotropic dielectric constant models. Also used as a reference in convergence criteria and error computation.

contexts.Aniso: In Physics{Aniso{}} to enable anisotropic model

Power.description: A PMI variable for thermoelectric power. Also appears as a parameter in Electrode{} (via Goal{Power=<float>}), in BreakCriteria{DevicePower}, and in various thermal/heat-related PMI interfaces.

contexts.Goal: In Goal{} for quasistationary ramp target power
contexts.BreakCriteria: In BreakCriteria{DevicePower()} for power-based break condition

PowerEnhan.description: In Traps() Degradation model, specifies parameters (beta0, beta_parallel, beta_perpendicular) in the chemical potential for kinetic equation degradation or the power for power law degradation.

parameters.name: <float>*3
parameters.desc: Three floats: beta0 (1), beta_parallel (V^-1 cm), beta_perpendicular (V^-1 cm).

PowerLaw.description: In Traps() specification, selects the power law form of the trap degradation model instead of the general kinetic equation. The hydrogen diffusion is solved by a 1D diffusion equation in the oxide.

Print.description: Controls printing of simulation information to the log file or current plot. Used in various contexts including raytracing (create raytree in output .tdr file) and general diagnostics.

PrintInfo.description: In Window{WeightedAPDintegration(...)}, prints detailed information about the wireframe integration for accurate absorbed photon density computation.

PrintLinearSolver.description: Global Math section keyword that prints additional information regarding the linear solver being used during the simulation.

PrintORArays.description: Outputs optical ray data for visualization and analysis. Prints ray information from optical simulations (raytracing) to the output file for post-processing or debugging purposes.

PrintRayInfo.description: In LED(Optics(RayTrace())), prints all indices and positions of starting rays into a specified file. Used for debugging LED raytracing simulations by recording the origin of each ray.

PrintSourceVertices.description: In LED(Optics(RayTrace())), prints the index and coordinates of all active emission vertices into the specified file. Used for debugging and verifying ray source vertex placement in LED raytracing simulations.

parameters.name: <string>
parameters.desc: Output file name for source vertex information.

ProfileIndex.description: In OpticalSolver(FromFile(...)), addresses a specific optical profile by its index instead of identifying parameters. Enables ramping through profiles in Quasistationary without knowing exact parameter values.

parameters.name: <int>
parameters.desc: Index of the optical profile (0-based).

Program.description: Appears in continuation command context and MLDA notes. Not a standard user-facing keyword in the command file. Referenced in Sentaurus Device command-line and continuation documentation.

ProgressMarkers.description: In LED(Optics(RayTrace())), sets the completion meter interval for raytracing progress output. Displays progress every N rays processed.

parameters.name: <int>
parameters.desc: Number of rays between progress marker outputs. Default 5.

PropagationDirection.description: In OpticalSolver(TMM(...)), chooses the interpolation mode for mapping the 1D TMM solution to the higher-dimensional device grid. Perpendicular uses simple perpendicular interpolation; Refractive (default) uses Snell's law.

parameters.name: Perpendicular
parameters.desc: Perpendicular interpolation of 1D profile.

parameters.name: Refractive
parameters.desc: Snell's law interpolation (default).

QCEffectiveBandGap.description: A scalar plot variable representing the effective band gap including quantum correction and bandgap narrowing (Eg - Ebgn - q(An + Ap)), in eV.

QCEffectiveIntrinsicDensity.description: A scalar plot variable representing the effective intrinsic density with corrections due to Fermi statistics and quantization effects, in cm^-3.

QFtraps.description: In Cyclic{Extrapolate(...)}, applies extrapolation to trap quasi-Fermi level instead of trap occupation probabilities during cyclic simulation.

QTX.description: Connection keyword for the external Boltzmann solver (Sentaurus Device QTX / Subband-BTE solver) for 3D quantum correction and effective mobility extraction via 2D cross-sections.

parameters.name: <string>
parameters.desc: Name for establishing connection to Subband-BTE solver process.

parameters.name: NumberOfSlices
parameters.desc: Number of 2D cross-section slices (required, at least 2).

parameters.name: Carriers
parameters.desc: Carrier type: Electron or Hole (required).

parameters.name: Mobility
parameters.desc: Optional: extract effective mobility for specified carrier type.

parameters.name: ContactName
parameters.desc: Optional: electrode name for current output to QTX.

parameters.name: SBTECommandFile
parameters.desc: Optional: command file to start Subband-BTE solver automatically.

parameters.name: Volume
parameters.desc: Optional: domain restriction for quantum corrections.

QW.description: Activates the quantum well model (localized QW via QWLocal or nonlocal QW via Schroedinger) in specified regions. Also appears as a prefix in numerous QW-related plot variables (QW_eEigenEnergy, QW_hhEigenEnergy, etc.).

parameters.name: Type
parameters.desc: QuantumWell - activates the QW model.

contexts.Active: In Physics{Active(Type=QuantumWell)} to mark QW regions
contexts.Plot: Prefix for QW plot variables (QW_eEigenEnergy, etc.)

QWExtension.description: Auto-detects quantum well width in LED simulation. The quantum well region must be marked with the Active(Type=QuantumWell) keyword. Used with the QWTransport model for radiative recombination and gain coefficient calculation.

parameters.name: AutoDetect
parameters.desc: Enable auto-detection of quantum well width.

QWLocal.description: Activates the localized quantum-well quantization model for semiconductor regions. Computes quantum correction factors for carrier densities in quantum well structures using a local approximation.

parameters.name: eDensityCorrection
parameters.desc: Enable electron quantization correction model (disabled by default).

parameters.name: hDensityCorrection
parameters.desc: Enable hole quantization correction model (disabled by default).

parameters.name: ElectricFieldDep
parameters.desc: Toggle electric field dependence (enabled by default; use -ElectricFieldDep to disable).

parameters.name: MaxElectricField
parameters.desc: Electric field cutoff value in V/cm (default 1e6).

parameters.name: NumberOfElectronSubbands
parameters.desc: Maximum number of electron subbands.

parameters.name: NumberOfHeavyHoleSubbands
parameters.desc: Maximum number of heavy-hole subbands.

parameters.name: NumberOfLightHoleSubbands
parameters.desc: Maximum number of light-hole subbands.

parameters.name: NumberOfCrystalFieldSplitHoleSubbands
parameters.desc: Maximum number of crystal-field split-off hole subbands.

parameters.name: NumberOfValenceBands
parameters.desc: Number of valence bands (default 2; set to 3 to include crystal-field split-off hole band).

parameters.name: Polarization
parameters.desc: Optical transition matrix element polarization mode: TE | TM | Mixed (default TE).

parameters.name: PolarizationFactor
parameters.desc: Mixed polarization factor, range [0, 1] (default 1).

parameters.name: WidthExtraction
parameters.desc: Quantum well width extraction parameter configuration.

QWTransport.description: Activates the three-point quantum well transport model with thermionic emission. Used with QWExtension=AutoDetect as the default model for simple quantum well subband calculations. For radiative recombination and gain coefficient calculation in LED simulation.

QW_ElectricFieldProjection.description: Scalar plot variable: electric field projection inside the quantum well. Only available with the local quantum well model (not applicable to non-local QW models). Unit: V/cm.

QW_OverlapIntegral.description: Scalar plot variable: overlap integral between electron and hole wave functions. Used to analyze optical transition probability of carriers in the quantum well.

QW_QuantizationDirection.description: Scalar plot variable: quantization direction of the quantum well. Indicates the direction in which carriers are confined.

QW_Width.description: Scalar plot variable: extracted width of the quantum well. Unit: um. Width is extracted using the algorithm configured by the WidthExtraction parameter.

QW_chEigenEnergy.description: Scalar plot variable: eigenenergy of crystal-field split-off hole bound states. Unit: eV.

QW_chNumberOfBoundStates.description: Scalar plot variable: actual number of quantum well bound states for crystal-field split-off holes.

QW_chRelativeEffectiveMass.description: Scalar plot variable: relative effective mass of crystal-field split-off holes.

QW_chStrainBandshift.description: Scalar plot variable: band shift of crystal-field split-off holes due to strain effects. Unit: eV.

QW_eEigenEnergy.description: Scalar plot variable: eigenenergy of electron bound states. Unit: eV.

QW_eNumberOfBoundStates.description: Scalar plot variable: actual number of quantum well bound states for electrons.

QW_eRelativeEffectiveMass.description: Scalar plot variable: relative effective mass of electrons.

QW_eStrainBandShift.description: Scalar plot variable: conduction band shift due to strain effects. Unit: eV.

QW_hhEigenEnergy.description: Scalar plot variable: eigenenergy of heavy-hole bound states. Unit: eV.

QW_hhNumberOfBoundStates.description: Scalar plot variable: actual number of quantum well bound states for heavy holes.

QW_hhRelativeEffectiveMass.description: Scalar plot variable: relative effective mass of heavy holes.

QW_hhStrainBandShift.description: Scalar plot variable: heavy-hole band shift due to strain effects. Unit: eV.

QW_lhEigenEnergy.description: Scalar plot variable: eigenenergy of light-hole bound states. Unit: eV.

QW_lhNumberOfBoundStates.description: Scalar plot variable: actual number of quantum well bound states for light holes.

QW_lhRelativeEffectiveMass.description: Scalar plot variable: relative effective mass of light holes.

QW_lhStrainBandShift.description: Scalar plot variable: light-hole band shift due to strain effects. Unit: eV.

QWeDensity.description: Scalar plot variable: quantum well electron density. Unit: cm^-3. Corresponds to n(x) = Ne^2D * sum_i |zeta_i(x)|^2 * F0(eta_n - Ei).

QWeQuasiFermi.description: Scalar plot variable: quasi-Fermi potential of quantum well electrons. Unit: V.

QWhDensity.description: Scalar plot variable: quantum well hole density. Unit: cm^-3. Corresponds to p(x) = Nhh^2D * sum_j |zeta_j(x)|^2 * F0(eta_p - Ehh^j) + Nlh^2D * sum_m |zeta_m(x)|^2 * F0(eta_p - Elh^m).

QWhQuasiFermi.description: Scalar plot variable: quasi-Fermi potential of quantum well holes. Unit: V.

Quantum.description: Activates quantum mechanical effect models, including the density-gradient (DG) model and modified local density approximation (MLDA) model. Provides quantum corrections to carrier transport equations for simulating quantum size effects and tunneling in devices.

QuantumWell.description: Marks a region as an active quantum well. Used with QWLocal or Schroedinger models to indicate quantum well modeling is required. Quantum well width can be auto-detected via the Active keyword or manually specified.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.desc: Must be set to QuantumWell to mark the quantum well region.

QuantumYield.signature: QuantumYield( Unity | EffectiveAbsorption | StepFunction(...) | pmiModel=<string> | Factor=<float> )
QuantumYield.description: Specifies the quantum yield model for optical generation, describing the fraction of absorbed photons converted to electron-hole pairs. Supports Unity, StepFunction (bandgap-based), EffectiveAbsorption, and PMI custom models. Results can be plotted via the QuantumYield variable in the Plot section.

parameters.name: Unity
parameters.desc: All absorbed photons produce electron-hole pairs (default).

parameters.name: EffectiveAbsorption
parameters.desc: Quantum yield model based on effective absorption.

parameters.name: StepFunction
parameters.desc: Bandgap-based step function model; supports Bandgap/EffectiveBandgap/Energy/Wavelength parameters.

parameters.name: Factor
parameters.desc: Quantum yield scaling factor (default 1).

parameters.name: pmiModel
parameters.desc: Name of PMI custom quantum yield model.

QuasiFermiPotential.description: Scalar data variable. Quasi-Fermi potential Phi (V), specified in the Plot section to output to .plt files. Separate variables eQuasiFermiPotential and hQuasiFermiPotential exist for electron and hole quasi-Fermi potentials respectively.

QuasiStationary.description: Ramps a device from one solution to another by modifying boundary conditions (contact voltages, currents, charges), quasi-Fermi potentials in doping wells, or physical model parameters. Internally ramps a variable t from 0.0 to 1.0, with step control via MaxStep, MinStep, InitialStep, Increment, and Decrement. The inner solve command is executed at each step.

parameters.name: InitialStep
parameters.desc: Size of the first ramping step (default: 0.1)

parameters.name: MaxStep
parameters.desc: Maximum ramping step size (default: 1)

parameters.name: MinStep
parameters.desc: Minimum ramping step size; simulation exits if step falls below this (default: 0.001)

parameters.name: Increment
parameters.desc: Multiplier for step size when the previous step converged (default: 2)

parameters.name: Decrement
parameters.desc: Divisor for step size when the previous step failed (default: 2)

parameters.name: Goal
parameters.desc: Target values for boundary condition ramping, e.g. Goal {Name="drain" Voltage=5}

parameters.name: Extrapolate
parameters.desc: Extrapolation option for accelerating convergence.

Quasistationary.signature: Quasistationary ( InitialStep=<float> MaxStep=<float> MinStep=<float> Increment=<float> Goal { Name=<string> Voltage=<float> } ) { <CoupledStatements> }
Quasistationary.description: Quasistationary sweep solve section (lowercase form). Same command as QuasiStationary. Used to incrementally vary bias conditions for DC characteristic analysis.

parameters.name: InitialStep
parameters.desc: Initial step size.

parameters.name: MaxStep
parameters.desc: Maximum step size.

parameters.name: MinStep
parameters.desc: Minimum step size.

parameters.name: Increment
parameters.desc: Step size growth factor.

parameters.name: Decrement
parameters.desc: Step size reduction factor.

parameters.name: Goal
parameters.desc: Target bias condition.

parameters.name: Extrapolate
parameters.desc: Extrapolation option.

RCS.description: Remote Coulomb Scattering (RCS) model option. Simulates mobility degradation caused by high-k gate dielectrics. Used as a sub-option of Enormal(), combined with other mobility degradation models via Matthiessen's rule. Parameters are defined in the RCSMobility parameter set.

RHSCoef.description: In Physics{HydrogenReaction()}, defines the right-hand side stoichiometric coefficients that specify the number of particles created in a hydrogen transport reaction. Each species coefficient defaults to 0.

parameters.name: Electron
parameters.desc: Number of generated electrons (default 0).

parameters.name: Hole
parameters.desc: Number of generated holes (default 0).

parameters.name: HydrogenAtom
parameters.desc: Number of generated hydrogen atoms (default 0).

parameters.name: HydrogenIon
parameters.desc: Number of generated hydrogen ions (default 0).

parameters.name: HydrogenMolecule
parameters.desc: Number of generated hydrogen molecules (default 0).

parameters.name: HydrogenSpeciesA
parameters.desc: Number of generated hydrogen species A (default 0).

parameters.name: HydrogenSpeciesB
parameters.desc: Number of generated hydrogen species B (default 0).

parameters.name: HydrogenSpeciesC
parameters.desc: Number of generated hydrogen species C (default 0).

RPBC.description: Robin Periodic Boundary Conditions (RPBC) method. Activated via the PeriodicBC() command in the Math section. Supports selecting specific equations and boundaries, using current/flux models to establish continuity across the PBC interface. The Factor parameter is a tuning parameter, default 1.e8.

parameters.name: Direction
parameters.desc: Coordinate axis direction (0=x, 1=y, 2=z).

parameters.name: Coordinates
parameters.desc: Coordinate range of the PBC interface.

parameters.name: Factor
parameters.desc: Tuning parameter alpha controlling interface continuity strength (default 1.e8).

parameters.name: Poisson
parameters.desc: Enable RPBC for the Poisson equation.

parameters.name: Electron
parameters.desc: Enable RPBC for the electron continuity equation.

RPS.description: Remote Phonon Scattering (RPS) model option. Simulates mobility degradation caused by remote phonons in high-k gate dielectrics. Used as a sub-option of Enormal(), combined with other mobility degradation models via Matthiessen's rule. Parameters are defined in the RPSMobility parameter set.

RTA.description: Relaxation Time Approximation (RTA) mode. A simplified mode of the Spherical Harmonics Expansion (SHE) method for hot-carrier injection current calculation. RTA removes energy coupling to reduce simulation time, but carrier flux is not conserved and cannot be used for self-consistent calculations. Requires SHEIterations=1 and SHESOR in the Math section.

Radiation.description: Enables radiation-induced carrier generation models including gamma radiation, alpha particles, and heavy ions. Specifies radiation source parameters and geometry.

parameters.name: Dose
parameters.desc: Total dose (rad), used together with DoseTime.

parameters.name: DoseRate
parameters.desc: Dose rate (rad/s).

parameters.name: DoseTime
parameters.desc: Exposure time range (start, end) in seconds.

parameters.name: DoseTSigma
parameters.desc: Standard deviation of Gaussian exposure rise/fall time (s).

RadiationGeneration.description: Scalar data variable. Carrier generation rate Gr from gamma radiation (cm^-3 s^-1), reference Equation 766. Specified in the Plot section to output the radiation generation rate.

Radiative.description: Radiative recombination model within Recombination(). Models band-to-band radiative recombination using the optical recombination rate. Coefficient can be doping-dependent or constant.

RadiativeRecombination.description: Scalar data variable. Radiative recombination rate R (cm^-3 s^-1), reference Equation 441. Specified in the Plot section to output the radiative recombination rate distribution.

Radius.description: Far-field observation radius parameter. Specifies the far-field observation sphere radius (um) in Farfield() for ray tracing far-field radiation pattern calculation. Default is 1e6 um (1 m). Also used in the Circle excitation shape.

contexts.Farfield: Far-field observation sphere radius in Farfield().
contexts.Circle: Circle radius in the Circle excitation shape.

Random.description: Randomization seed option. Sets the pseudo-random number generator seed value in RandomizedVariation(). Zero (default) auto-selects a different seed; positive values are used directly; negative values disable sIFM.

RandomField.description: Declares a random field for spatially correlated statistical variations within the RandomizedVariation section of the Math section. Specifies correlation function type (Grain, Exponential, Gaussian), correlation length (Lambda or AverageGrainSize), amplitude, and optional volume restriction. When declared, all variations in the same RandomizedVariation section become correlated.

parameters.name: CorrelationFunction
parameters.desc: Correlation function type: Grain | Exponential | Gaussian.

parameters.name: AverageGrainSize
parameters.desc: Average grain size vector (um).

parameters.name: Lambda
parameters.desc: Correlation length vector.

parameters.name: Resolution
parameters.desc: Resolution vector.

parameters.name: MaxInternalPoints
parameters.desc: Maximum number of internal points.

RandomSeed.signature: Math { KMC( RandomSeed=<int> ) } / Math { GrainFieldGenerator( RandomSeed=<int> ) }
RandomSeed.description: Random number seed. Sets the KMC simulation seed in KMC() for reproducible results; sets the grain field generation seed in GrainFieldGenerator().

contexts.GrainFieldGenerator: Random seed for grain field generation.
contexts.RayTracing: Seed for RaysRandomOffset in ray tracing.

Randomize.description: Randomizes trap positions and/or energies. When applied to traps, it enables trap randomization for statistical analysis. An optional integer seed controls the random number generator for reproducibility.

RandomizeEnergy.description: Trap energy randomization option. Enables randomization of trap energy levels in TrapConcentration(). Trap levels are randomly distributed within a specified range, producing a discrete trap level spectrum.

RandomizedDoping.description: Scalar data variable. Doping concentration after sIFM randomization (cm^-3). When specified in the device-specific Plot section, writes all randomized acceptor and donor profiles to the plot file. May consume significant memory when the number of random samples is large.

RandomizedVariation.description: Specifies parameters for the statistical impedance field method (sIFM). Controls random field generation with spatial correlations, number of samples, and random seed for Monte Carlo-style variability analysis.

parameters.name: NumberOfSamples
parameters.desc: Number of samples per variation set.

parameters.name: Randomize
parameters.desc: Random seed. 0=auto, >0=fixed seed, <0=disable sIFM.

parameters.name: ExtrudeTo3D
parameters.desc: Extrude the 2D structure to 3D for randomization.

parameters.name: RandomField
parameters.desc: Declare statistical properties of the random field.

contexts.Math: Declare sIFM settings in the global Math section.
contexts.Physics: Declare specific variation quantities in the Physics section.

Range.description: Range parameter in Solve-Plot. Specifies the step range for Plot statement output during voltage/bias sweeps. Range=<int> specifies the total range (0 to this value), Intervals specifies the number of intervals.

contexts.Solve: Plot output range control.

RayDirection.description: Ray direction parameter. Specifies the direction vector of rays imported from a file in UserWindow().

RayDistribution.description: Creates a ray distribution section to be used in conjunction with an excitation illumination window for raytracing. Defines how excitation parameters translate into raytracing context including starting ray positions and directions.

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.desc: Distribution mode: AutoPopulate | Equidistant | MonteCarlo.

parameters.name: NumberOfRays
parameters.desc: Number of rays for MonteCarlo or AutoPopulate mode.

parameters.name: Dx
parameters.desc: x-direction discretization size for Equidistant mode.

parameters.name: Dy
parameters.desc: y-direction discretization size for Equidistant mode.

parameters.name: Scaling
parameters.desc: Scaling factor for rays in this window.

parameters.name: WindowName
parameters.desc: Name of the ray distribution window. Unnamed means global.

RayTrace.description: Ray tracing related context. RayTrace is not a standalone command but appears in the ray tracing PMI interface (Special Contact PMI for Raytracing) and related code examples. The main ray tracing configuration is done via RayTracing() in the Physics section.

contexts.PMI: Function call context in the ray tracing PMI interface.
contexts.Device: Ray tracing settings in the Device section.

RayTraceBC.description: Section keyword for defining raytracing boundary conditions at special contacts. These contacts are drawn like electrodes and thermodes. Supports Fresnel reflection, TMM multilayer structures, custom PMI models, and configurable reflectivity/transmittivity.

RayTraceIntensity.description: LED simulation scalar data. Ray tracing intensity, outputtable in the Plot section. Used for LED radiation pattern analysis.

RayTracing.description: Unified raytracing interface for optical generation computation in the Physics{Optics{OpticalSolver}} section. Configures ray propagation parameters including depth limits, minimum intensity thresholds, far-field sensors, Monte Carlo mode, external material CRI, polarization, user-defined ray windows, and interface flux plotting.

parameters.name: MonteCarlo
parameters.desc: Enable Monte Carlo ray tracing.

parameters.name: MinIntensity
parameters.desc: Minimum relative intensity threshold; rays below this value are stopped.

parameters.name: DepthLimit
parameters.desc: Maximum number of material boundary crossings (default 5).

parameters.name: RayDistribution
parameters.desc: Ray distribution window specification.

parameters.name: Farfield
parameters.desc: Far-field and sensor configuration.

parameters.name: ExternalMaterialCRIFile
parameters.desc: Path to the external medium CRI file.

parameters.name: CompactMemoryOption
parameters.desc: Enable compact memory mode.

parameters.name: OmitReflectedRays
parameters.desc: Discard all reflected rays.

parameters.name: OmitWeakerRays
parameters.desc: Discard weaker rays at interfaces.

parameters.name: Print
parameters.desc: Create ray tree in the output .tdr file.

parameters.name: WeightedOpticalGeneration
parameters.desc: Enable weighted optical generation distribution.

RayTrees.description: Scalar data variable. Ray tree data (vertex positions) representing the traced paths of rays in the device. Value is 1 (ray tree present). Used for visualization and debugging of the ray tracing process.

RaysFromFile.description: Import initial ray directions from a file. In RayTracing(), uses SourceRaysFromFile() to import a set of initial ray directions from a specified file, combined with RaysPerVertex to specify the number of imported rays.

RaysPerCluster.description: Number of initial rays per active vertex cluster. Set in OutputLightToolsRays() for exporting LED initial rays to a LightTools ray data file.

RaysPerVertex.description: Sets the number of initial rays per active emitting vertex. Controls ray tracing precision in LED optical simulation (RayTrace). In 3D mode, the actual ray count is constrained to the nearest value in the sequence 6, 18, 68, etc., not less than the specified value.

parameters.name: RaysPerVertex
parameters.desc: Number of initial rays per active source vertex (default 10).

RaysRandomOffset.description: Randomizes the angular shift of starting rays in LED raytracing simulations. Without this keyword, a fixed angular shift determined by the active vertex number is used. An optional random seed ensures reproducibility.

parameters.name: RandomSeed
parameters.desc: Seed value for the random number generator. When set, reproduces the same pseudo-random ray distribution.

Raytrace.description: Alternative spelling for RayTrace. Configures the raytracing optical solver within the Optics section. Controls ray emission, clustering, polarization, intensity thresholds, Monte Carlo mode, and output options for LED and optical device simulations.

parameters.name: CompactMemoryOption
parameters.desc: Activate compact memory mode for ray tracing.

parameters.name: DepthLimit
parameters.desc: Maximum number of material boundary crossings; tracing stops beyond this limit.

parameters.name: ExternalMaterialCRIFile
parameters.desc: Specify the complex refractive index (CRI) file for external media.

parameters.name: Farfield
parameters.desc: Activate far-field and sensor functionality.

parameters.name: IntensityAbsCoeff
parameters.desc: Set a non-zero absorption coefficient for intensity calculation.

parameters.name: MinIntensity
parameters.desc: Stop tracing when ray intensity falls below original intensity multiplied by this value.

parameters.name: MonteCarlo
parameters.desc: Activate Monte Carlo ray tracing.

parameters.name: NonSemiconductorAbsorption
parameters.desc: Include optical generation rate calculation in non-semiconductor regions or materials.

parameters.name: OmitReflectedRays
parameters.desc: Discard all reflected rays.

parameters.name: OmitWeakerRays
parameters.desc: Discard weaker reflected or transmitted rays at material interfaces.

parameters.name: PlotInterfaceFlux
parameters.desc: Activate interface flux plotting on all boundary conditions.

parameters.name: PolarizationVector
parameters.desc: Random or <vector>; sets the polarization vector of initial rays.

parameters.name: Print
parameters.desc: Create ray tree in the output .tdr file.

parameters.name: RayDistribution
parameters.desc: Create a ray distribution for use with excitation illumination windows.

parameters.name: RedistributeStoppedRays
parameters.desc: Redistribute the total accumulated power from stopped rays back into the ray tree.

parameters.name: RetraceCRIchange
parameters.desc: Fractional change threshold for complex refractive index; rays are force-retraced when exceeded.

parameters.name: UserWindow
parameters.desc: Input initial ray set from a user-specified file.

parameters.name: VirtualRegions
parameters.desc: Specify virtual regions that the ray tracer will ignore.

parameters.name: WeightedOpticalGeneration
parameters.desc: Enable weighted method to distribute optical generation rate from elements to vertices.

contexts.Physics: Unified ray tracing interface in Physics{Optics(OpticalSolver(RayTrace(...)))}.
contexts.Device: Ray tracing boundary condition definition in Device{RayTraceBC{...}}.

Raytracing.description: Generic term/section name for ray tracing, referring to the ray tracing functionality in Sentaurus Device for computing optical generation rates. The actual keyword to use is RayTrace (capital T). Appears in optics and PMI interface sections.

contexts.Physics: Ray tracing settings in Physics{Optics(OpticalSolver(RayTrace(...)))}.
contexts.Math: Carrier model related in Math{RecomputeQFP}.

ReConductionCurrentResponse.description: Real part of conduction current response in AC small-signal analysis. Vector data. Used in the Plot section for plotting AC analysis results.

ReDisplacementCurrentResponse.description: Real part of displacement current response in AC small-signal analysis. Vector data. Used in the Plot section.

ReElectrostaticPotentialResponse.description: Real part of electrostatic potential response Re(phi) in AC small-signal analysis. Scalar data. Used in the Plot section, unit is dimensionless (1).

ReLNISD.description: Real part of Local Noise Current Spectral Density (LNISD). Noise analysis output keyword, usable in the NoisePlot section or Plot section.

ReLatticeTemperatureResponse.description: Real part of lattice temperature response Re(T) in AC small-signal analysis. Scalar data. Used in the Plot section, unit is K/V.

ReTotalCurrentResponse.description: Real part of total current response in AC small-signal analysis. Vector data. Used in the Plot section.

ReTrapLNISD.description: Real part of local trap noise current spectral density. Noise analysis output keyword, usable in the NoisePlot section or Plot section.

ReTrapLNVSD.description: Real part of local trap noise voltage spectral density. Noise analysis output keyword, usable in the NoisePlot section or Plot section.

ReadExtrapolation.description: Attempts to use extrapolation information from the previous Quasistationary or Transient command as the initial guess for the current solve. Requires the previous command to have compatible extrapolation information (e.g., all goals incrementing at the same rate, unchanged solution variables). Enabled by default.

ReadFromExcitation.description: Read ray polarization vector settings from the excitation definition. Used as one of the PolarizationVector options in UserWindow ray definitions.

ReadFromFile.description: Load optical generation rate or optical solver results from a file. Can be used as shorthand for FromFile(), or as a TimeDependence() option to read time-dependent tables. Also usable in UserWindow as a PolarizationVector option to read the polarization vector from a file.

contexts.Physics: Physics{Optics(OpticalSolver(ReadFromFile))} - load optical solution from file.

RecBoxIntegr.description: Enables integration of beam distribution over a rectangular window for optical generation computation in the transfer matrix method (TMM). Controls how the optical generation profile is computed from the beam intensity distribution across the illumination window.

parameters.name: MaxRelativeDeviation
parameters.desc: Maximum relative deviation of the covering volume (default 1e-2).

parameters.name: MaxLevels
parameters.desc: Maximum recursion depth (default 10).

parameters.name: MaxTotalRectangles
parameters.desc: Maximum total number of rectangles per box (default 1000).

RecGenHeat.description: Activates generation-recombination heat sources in the energy conservation equations. By default, these terms are excluded; this keyword enables the H_n, H_p, and H_L contributions from optical generation, impact ionization, and recombination.

parameters.name: OptGenOffset
parameters.desc: Offset factor for optical generation rate contribution (default 0.5), controlling the distribution of optical generation rate between electron and hole energy gain/loss terms.

parameters.name: OptGenWavelength
parameters.desc: Wavelength in um when optical generation rate is loaded from a file.

Recombination.description: Selects recombination models for carrier generation-recombination processes. Supports SRH, Auger, radiative, band-to-band tunneling, and singlet exciton recombination models.

parameters.name: SRH
parameters.desc: Shockley-Read-Hall recombination model; supports TempDependence, DopingDependence, ElectricField, ExpTempDependence options.

parameters.name: Auger
parameters.desc: Auger recombination model; supports WithGeneration option.

parameters.name: Radiative
parameters.desc: Radiative recombination model (enabled by default).

parameters.name: Avalanche
parameters.desc: Impact ionization model.

parameters.name: Band2Band
parameters.desc: Band-to-band tunneling model.

parameters.name: CDL
parameters.desc: Coupled defect level recombination model.

parameters.name: SurfaceSRH
parameters.desc: Interface SRH recombination model.

parameters.name: TrapAssistedAuger
parameters.desc: Trap-assisted Auger recombination model.

parameters.name: ConstantCarrierGeneration
parameters.desc: Constant carrier generation model.

contexts.Physics: Region-level recombination models in Physics{Recombination(...)}.

RecombinationHeat.description: Recombination heat: heat released or absorbed during recombination. Scalar data, unit W/cm^3. Used in the Plot section for self-heating / thermodynamic model analysis.

RecomputeNonsymmetric.description: Recompute permutation and scaling of nonsymmetric matrices before each factorization. An option for the ParDiSo linear solver to improve numerical stability.

RecomputeQFP.description: Recompute quasi-Fermi potentials while keeping density variables unchanged, when the electrostatic potential changes and carrier equations are not solved. Applicable when only solving the Poisson equation (e.g., initial solution) to ensure quasi-Fermi potentials are consistent with the electrostatic potential.

contexts.Math: Global setting in the Math section.
contexts.Physics: Associated with carrier transport models.

Rectangle.description: In the Math section, defines a rectangular geometric region for localized operations such as spatial constraints, mesh refinement zones, or domain restrictions.

parameters.name: AxisAligned
parameters.desc: Treat Corner1 and Corner2 as opposite corners of the rectangular sensor.

parameters.name: Corner1
parameters.desc: First corner coordinate vector of the rectangle.

parameters.name: Corner2
parameters.desc: Second corner coordinate vector of the rectangle.

parameters.name: Corner3
parameters.desc: Third corner coordinate vector of the rectangle (required for 3D).

parameters.name: UseNormalFlux
parameters.desc: Compute flux projected onto the normal.

contexts.Math: Define rectangular element region in Math{Element(Rectangle(...))}.
contexts.Physics: Rectangular sensor in Physics{Optics(OpticalSolver(RayTrace(Farfield(Sensor(Rectangle(...))))}.

RectangleV1.description: First vertex coordinate of a rectangular source window (unit: um). Used in RayTrace's RectangularWindow to specify one vertex of the rectangle.

parameters.name: RectangleV1
parameters.desc: First vertex coordinate vector of the rectangular window.

RectangleV1.example:
RayTrace (
  RectangularWindow (
    RectangleV1 = (0.0 0.0)
    RectangleV2 = (1.0 0.0)
    LengthDiscretization = 20
    WidthDiscretization = 10
  )
)

RectangleV2.description: Second vertex coordinate of a rectangular source window (unit: um). Used in RayTrace's RectangularWindow.

parameters.name: RectangleV2
parameters.desc: Second vertex coordinate vector of the rectangular window.

RectangleV3.description: Third vertex coordinate of a rectangular source window (unit: um), required only for 3D simulation. Used in RayTrace's RectangularWindow.

parameters.name: RectangleV3
parameters.desc: Third vertex coordinate vector of the rectangular window (3D only).

RectangleV3.example:
RectangularWindow (
  RectangleV1 = (0.0 0.0 0.0)
  RectangleV2 = (1.0 0.0 0.0)
  RectangleV3 = (1.0 1.0 0.0)
  LengthDiscretization = 20
  WidthDiscretization = 20
)

RectangularWindow.description: Defines a rectangular window of starting rays for raytracing. Specifies three vertices defining the rectangle (two required for 2D, three for 3D), the number of rays along each side, and the ray direction vector. Alternative to UserWindow; only one can be used per raytracing setup.

parameters.name: RectangleV1
parameters.desc: First vertex coordinate of the rectangular window.

parameters.name: RectangleV2
parameters.desc: Second vertex coordinate of the rectangular window.

parameters.name: RectangleV3
parameters.desc: Third vertex coordinate of the rectangular window (3D only).

parameters.name: LengthDiscretization
parameters.desc: Discretization count for the long edge.

parameters.name: WidthDiscretization
parameters.desc: Discretization count for the short edge (3D only).

parameters.name: RayDirection
parameters.desc: Ray direction vector.

RedistributeStoppedRays.description: Redistribute the total accumulated power from stopped rays back into the ray tree. Ensures energy from terminated rays is not wasted but is redistributed into the ray tracing energy allocation.

contexts.Physics: Usable in both LED(Optics(RayTrace(RedistributeStoppedRays))) and Optics(OpticalSolver(RayTrace(RedistributeStoppedRays))).

ReeCurrentResponse.description: Real part of electron current response in AC small-signal analysis. Vector data. Used in the Plot section.

ReeDensityResponse.description: Real part of electron density response Re(n) in AC small-signal analysis. Scalar data. Used in the Plot section, unit is cm^-3/V.

ReeEnFluxResponse.description: Real part of electron energy flux response in AC small-signal analysis. Vector data. Used in the Plot section.

ReeTemperatureResponse.description: Scalar plot dataset: real part of electron temperature response to small-signal AC perturbation, Re(Tn). Unit: K/V. Plotted at vertex locations.

ReeeDiffusionLNVXVSD.description: Scalar plot dataset: real part of electron-electron diffusion noise spectral density (LNVXVSD). Unit: V^2*s/cm^3. Plotted at vertex locations. See Table 142 on noise modeling.

ReeeFlickerGRLNVXVSD.description: Scalar plot dataset: real part of electron-electron flicker (1/f) generation-recombination noise spectral density (LNVXVSD). Unit: V^2*s/cm^3. Plotted at vertex locations.

ReeeLNVXVSD.description: Scalar plot dataset: real part of electron-electron total noise spectral density (LNVXVSD). Unit: V^2*s/cm^3. Plotted at vertex locations. See Table 142 on noise modeling.

RefDens_Eparallel_ElectricField.description: Damping parameter (reference carrier density, unit cm^-3) for electron and hole high-field mobility and avalanche driving forces using Eparallel interpolation to the electric field at low carrier concentrations. Default: 0 cm^-3. Applies to both electrons and holes simultaneously.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Reference carrier density in cm^-3. Default 0.

contexts.Math: Damping parameter for both carrier types' Eparallel driving force interpolation.

RefDens_Eparallel_ElectricField_Aval.description: Damping parameter (reference carrier density, unit cm^-3) for electron and hole avalanche driving force using Eparallel interpolation to the electric field at low carrier concentrations. Default: 0 cm^-3. Affects avalanche generation only.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Reference carrier density in cm^-3. Default 0.

contexts.Math: Avalanche-specific Eparallel driving force interpolation parameter.

RefDens_Eparallel_ElectricField_HFS.description: Damping parameter (reference carrier density, unit cm^-3) for electron and hole high-field mobility driving force using Eparallel interpolation to the electric field. Default: 0 cm^-3. Affects high-field saturation mobility only (not avalanche).

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Reference carrier density in cm^-3. Default 0.

contexts.Math: High-field mobility Eparallel driving force interpolation parameter.

RefDens_GradQuasiFermi_ElectricField.description: Damping parameter (reference carrier density, unit cm^-3) for electron and hole high-field mobility and avalanche driving forces using GradQuasiFermi interpolation to the electric field at low carrier concentrations. Default: 0 cm^-3. Applies to both electrons and holes simultaneously, and affects both mobility and avalanche.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Reference carrier density in cm^-3. Default 0.

contexts.Math: Damping parameter for GradQuasiFermi driving force interpolation to electric field.

RefDens_GradQuasiFermi_ElectricField_HFS.description: Damping parameter (reference carrier density, unit cm^-3) for electron and hole high-field mobility driving force using GradQuasiFermi interpolation to the electric field. Default: 0 cm^-3. Affects high-field saturation mobility only.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Reference carrier density in cm^-3. Default 0.

contexts.Math: High-field mobility GradQuasiFermi driving force interpolation parameter.

RefDens_GradQuasiFermi_EparallelToInterf.description: Damping parameter (reference carrier density, unit cm^-3) for electron and hole high-field mobility driving force using GradQuasiFermi interpolation to the electric field parallel to the interface. Default: 0 cm^-3. Affects high-field saturation mobility only.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Reference carrier density in cm^-3. Default 0.

contexts.Math: Interpolation between GradQuasiFermi gradient and Eparallel-to-interface for HFS mobility.

RefDens_GradQuasiFermi_Zero.description: Damping parameter (reference carrier density, unit cm^-3) for electron and hole driving force interpolation to zero field. Default: 0 cm^-3. Alias: DrForceRefDens. Improves convergence in regions with small carrier density and strong generation-recombination. Affects both high-field mobility and avalanche driving forces.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Reference carrier density in cm^-3. Default 0.

contexts.Math: Damping parameter for interpolation of driving forces to zero field.

RefDens_eEparallel_ElectricField_Aval.description: Damping parameter (reference electron density, unit cm^-3) for electron avalanche driving force using Eparallel interpolation to the electric field. Default: 0 cm^-3. Electron-specific variant for avalanche generation.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Reference electron density in cm^-3. Default 0.

contexts.Math: Electron-specific avalanche Eparallel driving force interpolation parameter.

RefDens_eEparallel_ElectricField_HFS.description: Damping parameter (reference electron density, unit cm^-3) for electron high-field mobility driving force using Eparallel interpolation to the electric field. Default: 0 cm^-3. Electron-specific for HFS mobility.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Reference electron density in cm^-3. Default 0.

contexts.Math: Electron-specific HFS Eparallel driving force interpolation parameter.

RefDens_eGradQuasiFermi_ElectricField.description: Damping parameter (reference electron density, unit cm^-3) for electron high-field mobility and avalanche driving forces using GradQuasiFermi interpolation to the electric field. Default: 0 cm^-3. Electron-specific, affects both mobility and avalanche generation.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Reference electron density in cm^-3. Default 0.

contexts.Math: Electron-specific GradQuasiFermi driving force interpolation for both mobility and avalanche.

RefDens_eGradQuasiFermi_ElectricField_HF.description: Damping parameter (reference electron density, unit cm^-3) for electron high-field mobility driving force using GradQuasiFermi interpolation to the electric field. Default: 0 cm^-3. Electron-specific for HFS mobility only.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Reference electron density in cm^-3. Default 0.

contexts.Math: Electron-specific HFS GradQuasiFermi driving force interpolation parameter.

RefDens_eGradQuasiFermi_EparallelToInter.description: Damping parameter (reference electron density, unit cm^-3) for electron high-field mobility driving force using GradQuasiFermi interpolation to the electric field parallel to the interface. Default: 0 cm^-3. Electron-specific for HFS mobility only.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Reference electron density in cm^-3. Default 0.

contexts.Math: Electron-specific interpolation between GradQuasiFermi and Eparallel-to-interface for HFS.

RefDens_eGradQuasiFermi_Zero.description: Damping parameter (reference electron density, unit cm^-3) for electron driving force interpolation to zero field. Default: 0 cm^-3. Alias: eDrForceRefDens. Improves convergence in regions with small electron density and strong generation-recombination.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Reference electron density in cm^-3. Default 0.

contexts.Math: Electron-specific damping for interpolation of driving forces to zero field.

RefDens_hEparallel_ElectricField.description: Damping parameter (reference hole density, unit cm^-3) for hole high-field mobility and avalanche driving forces using Eparallel interpolation to the electric field. Default: 0 cm^-3. Hole-specific.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Reference hole density in cm^-3. Default 0.

contexts.Math: Hole-specific Eparallel driving force interpolation for mobility and avalanche.

RefDens_hEparallel_ElectricField_Aval.description: Damping parameter (reference hole density, unit cm^-3) for hole avalanche driving force using Eparallel interpolation to the electric field. Default: 0 cm^-3. Hole-specific for avalanche generation.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Reference hole density in cm^-3. Default 0.

contexts.Math: Hole-specific avalanche Eparallel driving force interpolation parameter.

RefDens_hEparallel_ElectricField_HFS.description: Damping parameter (reference hole density, unit cm^-3) for hole high-field mobility driving force using Eparallel interpolation to the electric field. Default: 0 cm^-3. Hole-specific for HFS mobility only.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Reference hole density in cm^-3. Default 0.

contexts.Math: Hole-specific HFS Eparallel driving force interpolation parameter.

RefDens_hGradQuasiFermi_ElectricField.description: Damping parameter (reference hole density, unit cm^-3) for hole high-field mobility and avalanche driving forces using GradQuasiFermi interpolation to the electric field. Default: 0 cm^-3. Hole-specific, affects both mobility and avalanche generation.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Reference hole density in cm^-3. Default 0.

contexts.Math: Hole-specific GradQuasiFermi driving force interpolation for both mobility and avalanche.

RefDens_hGradQuasiFermi_ElectricField_HF.description: Damping parameter (reference hole density, unit cm^-3) for hole high-field mobility driving force using GradQuasiFermi interpolation to the electric field. Default: 0 cm^-3. Hole-specific for HFS mobility only.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Reference hole density in cm^-3. Default 0.

contexts.Math: Hole-specific HFS GradQuasiFermi driving force interpolation parameter.

RefDens_hGradQuasiFermi_EparallelToInter.description: Damping parameter (reference hole density, unit cm^-3) for hole high-field mobility driving force using GradQuasiFermi interpolation to the electric field parallel to the interface. Default: 0 cm^-3. Hole-specific for HFS mobility only.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Reference hole density in cm^-3. Default 0.

contexts.Math: Hole-specific interpolation between GradQuasiFermi and Eparallel-to-interface for HFS.

RefDens_hGradQuasiFermi_Zero.description: Damping parameter (reference hole density, unit cm^-3) for hole driving force interpolation to zero field. Default: 0 cm^-3. Alias: hDrForceRefDens. Improves convergence in regions with small hole density and strong generation-recombination.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Reference hole density in cm^-3. Default 0.

contexts.Math: Hole-specific damping for interpolation of driving forces to zero field.

RefLatticeConst.description: Reference lattice constant (unit: m) for strain computation in LED simulations with wurtzite crystal structures. Used within LED(Strain(...)) to specify the substrate lattice constant as a reference for computing the strain tensor in each layer. Required when strain effects are modeled in wurtzite crystals.

parameters.name: RefLatticeConst
parameters.desc: Reference lattice constant in meters (e.g., 3.183e-10 for GaN).

contexts.Physics: Sub-parameter of LED(Strain(...)) for wurtzite strain computation.

Reference.description: Specifies the energy reference for trap energy levels in the Traps section. Reference=BandGap refers trap energies to band edges excluding bandgap narrowing; Reference=EffectiveBandgap refers to effective band edges including BGN. Used within Physics{...Traps{...}} to control how trap energy distributions are positioned relative to band edges.

parameters.name: BandGap
parameters.desc: Refer trap energies to band edges excluding bandgap narrowing.

parameters.name: EffectiveBandgap
parameters.desc: Refer trap energies to effective band edges (including BGN). Default.

contexts.Physics: Sub-keyword of Traps for trap energy reference.

ReferenceMaterial.description: Defines the orientation of a multilayer structure in TMM (Transfer Matrix Method) thin-layer-stack boundary conditions. Specifies the material adjacent to the topmost layer of the LayerStructure. Used within RayTraceBC(TMM(...)) or Physics{RayTraceBC(TMM(...))} to fix the layer ordering relative to the contact.

parameters.name: ReferenceMaterial
parameters.desc: Name of the reference material adjacent to the topmost layer.

contexts.Physics: Sub-keyword of RayTraceBC(TMM(...)) for TMM boundary condition orientation.

ReferencePoint.description: Specifies a coordinate (vector) from where the electric field and lattice temperature are taken for the trap energy shift PMI computation. Used within Traps{...EnergyShift=...} to override the default behavior of taking these quantities at the trap location itself.

parameters.name: ReferencePoint
parameters.desc: Coordinate vector (x y z) in the device from where field and temperature are sampled.

contexts.Physics: Sub-keyword of Traps for EnergyShift reference location.

ReferenceRefractiveIndex.description: Specifies the reference refractive index for the Beam Propagation Method (BPM) optical solver. Options: a constant float value, 'average' (average in propagation plane), 'fieldweighted' (field-weighted average), or 'maximum' (maximum in propagation plane). Greatly influences solution accuracy due to its use in the propagation operator expansion.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Constant float value, or one of: average, fieldweighted, maximum.

ReferenceRefractiveIndex.example: Physics { Optics { OpticalSolver { BPM( ReferenceRefractiveIndex=2.2 GridNodes=(256,256,1600) ... ) } } }

contexts.Physics: Sub-keyword of BPM() in the OpticalSolver section.

ReferenceRefractiveIndexDelta.description: Additive offset added to ReferenceRefractiveIndex in the BPM optical solver calculation. Used for fine-tuning the numerics of the beam propagation method.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Offset value added to the reference refractive index.

ReferenceRefractiveIndexDelta.example: Physics { Optics { OpticalSolver { BPM( ReferenceRefractiveIndex=2.2 ReferenceRefractiveIndexDelta=0.01 ) } } }

ReferenceRegion.description: Scalar plot dataset on vertices indicating the region name index for each vertex. Used for visualization of region assignments in the device structure.

ReferenceStep.description: Specifies a reference step in quasistationary or transient simulations. The simulation state at this step is stored and can be used for subsequent convergence checks or as a reference for extrapolation. Used to control the convergence behavior in the Solve section.

Refined.description: Controls automatic refinement of nonlocal mesh lines inside specified regions. When enabled (+Refined), nonlocal lines passing through the region are automatically subdivided for higher accuracy in tunneling current calculations. Can be applied globally or restricted to specific regions via a region table.

RefinedIntervals.description: Specifies the number of intervals for refined energy grid resolution in tunneling models. Controls the discretization of the energy integral for nonlocal tunneling calculations, affecting accuracy of computed tunneling currents.

Refinement.description: Controls mesh refinement level for numerical accuracy. Used in Math section to specify the degree of mesh refinement applied during simulation, affecting discretization quality for equations such as Poisson, continuity, and tunneling.

Reflective.description: Specifies a homogeneous Neumann boundary condition (zero flux) for hydrogen species at a contact. Applied to HydrogenAtom, HydrogenIon, HydrogenMolecule, and other hydrogen species in the HydrogenBoundary section to indicate a reflective (impermeable) boundary.

Reflectivity.description: Specifies the reflectivity (ratio of reflected to incident optical power) at a material interface or contact for raytracing optical simulations. Value must be in [0,1]. Used in both Physics-based RayTraceBC and standalone RayTraceBC sections. Alternatively, Fresnel boundary condition can be used for physically computed reflectivity.

parameters.name: Reflectivity
parameters.desc: Reflectivity value in range [0,1]. Default is 0.

contexts.RayTraceBC: Boundary condition for raytracing at material interfaces or contacts

RefractiveIndex.description: Specifies the real part of the refractive index for a medium surrounding the extracted layer structure in the TMM LayerStackExtraction section. Used as an alternative to specifying a Material name in the Medium section. The corresponding imaginary part (ExtinctionCoefficient) can also be specified.

RefreshEverytime.signature: Physics { OpticalGeneration { ComputeFromSpectrum( RefreshEverytime ... ) } } | Physics { OpticalGeneration { ReadFromFile( RefreshEverytime ... ) } } | Physics { OpticalGeneration { SetConstant( RefreshEverytime ... ) } }
RefreshEverytime.description: Forces recomputation of the respective optical generation contribution at every occasion, bypassing caching. Used within OpticalGeneration sub-sections ComputeFromSpectrum, ReadFromFile, and SetConstant to ensure fresh computation each time the optical generation is needed.

contexts.ComputeFromSpectrum: Force recomputation of spectrum-based optical generation
contexts.ReadFromFile: Force recomputation of file-based optical generation
contexts.SetConstant: Force recomputation of constant optical generation

Regexp.description: Regular expression pattern matching for electrode and thermode names. Used in Electrode, Thermode, and other boundary condition sections to match multiple structure contacts with a single specification.

Region.description: Top-level section and parameter used to specify region-dependent physical models, material parameters, and boundary conditions. In the Physics section, Region="<name>" restricts model activation to a specific device region. As a standalone command, the Region section defines doping profiles, mesh refinement, and geometry properties for device regions.

contexts.Physics: Region qualifier for physics model specification

RegionInterface.description: Specifies interface-dependent physical models and parameters at the boundary between two device regions. Used to define interface-specific mobility degradation, trap models, recombination velocities, and other properties that differ from bulk region values.

contexts.Physics: RegionInterface qualifier for interface model specification

RegionName.description: Specifies the name of a region to be excluded from cyclic extrapolation in large-signal cyclic analysis. Used within the Cyclic section of Quasistationary to identify regions where extrapolation should not be applied.

RegionWise.description: Qualifier indicating that a physical model parameter or equation setting is applied on a per-region basis rather than globally. Used throughout Physics and Math sections to restrict model activation, parameter values, and solver settings to specific device regions.

RegionWiseAssembly.description: Activates regionwise assembly for trap equations in the Math section. When enabled, trap equations are assembled separately for each region rather than globally, which can improve convergence for devices with region-dependent trap distributions.

Regions.description: Top-level section in the command file defining device regions. Each region specifies material properties, doping profiles, and geometry. The Regions section works together with the Mesh section to define the complete device structure loaded from the boundary/grid file.

RehCurrentResponse.description: Vector plot dataset on vertices. Real part of the hole current density response to AC small-signal excitation, in A/cm^2/V. Used for AC analysis visualization of hole current response.

RehDensityResponse.description: Scalar plot dataset on vertices. Real part of the hole density response to AC small-signal excitation, in cm^-3/V. Used for AC analysis visualization of hole carrier density response.

RehEnFluxResponse.description: Vector plot dataset on vertices. Real part of the hole energy flux response to AC small-signal excitation, in W/cm^2/V. Used for AC analysis visualization of hole heat flux response.

RehTemperatureResponse.description: Scalar plot dataset on vertices. Real part of the hole temperature response to AC small-signal excitation, in K/V. Used for AC analysis visualization of hole carrier temperature response in hydrodynamic simulations.

RehhDiffusionLNVXVSD.description: Scalar plot dataset on vertices. Real part of the hole-hole diffusion noise spectral density (Langevin noise, voltage spectral density), in V^2*s/cm^3. Used for noise analysis visualization.

RehhLNVXVSD.description: Scalar plot dataset on vertices. Real part of the hole-hole generation-recombination noise (Langevin noise, voltage spectral density), in V^2*s/cm^3. Used for noise analysis visualization.

RelEnFluxResponse.description: Vector plot dataset on vertices. Real part of the electron energy flux response to AC small-signal excitation, in W/cm^2/V. Used for AC analysis visualization of electron heat flux response.

RelErrControl.signature: Math { RelErrControl } | Coupled { RelErrControl } | EquilibriumSolution { RelErrControl } | AcceptNewtonParameter { RelErrControl }
RelErrControl.description: Activates relative error control for Newton convergence criteria. When enabled, convergence is determined by the relative update error rather than absolute error, improving accuracy for solutions spanning multiple orders of magnitude. Available in Math, Coupled, EquilibriumSolution, and AcceptNewtonParameter sections.

contexts.Math: Global setting for all solve statements

RelFactor.description: Specifies the relaxation factor for cyclic extrapolation in large-signal cyclic analysis. Controls the damping of the extrapolation factor to improve convergence stability during cyclic simulations.

parameters.name: RelFactor
parameters.desc: Relaxation factor for cyclic extrapolation (dimensionless).

RelTermMinDensity.description: Stabilization parameter for the temperature relaxation term in the hydrodynamic model. Specifies the minimum carrier density threshold below which the relaxation term is modified to prevent numerical instability. Default is 1e3 cm^-3.

parameters.name: RelTermMinDensity
parameters.desc: Minimum carrier density threshold in cm^-3. Default: 1e3.

RelativeToDOSMass.description: Specifies that the effective mass used in mobility or recombination models is defined relative to the density-of-states (DOS) effective mass rather than the conductivity effective mass. Affects carrier transport calculations in models that depend on effective mass values.

Reliability.description: Top-level section for specifying reliability and degradation models for device lifetime simulation. Includes hot-carrier degradation (HCD), negative bias temperature instability (NBTI), and other time-dependent device degradation mechanisms. Used together with the Quasistationary command to simulate device aging over time.

Reservoirs.description: Specifies particle reservoirs for MSC transitions that involve charge state changes. The conduction band (CB) serves as an electron reservoir and the valence band (VB) as a hole reservoir. Each reservoir specifies the number of particles exchanged during the transition. Ensures total charge conservation during MSC state transitions.

Residual.description: In NewtonPlot context, requests that the residuals (right-hand sides) of all equations be written to the NewtonPlot output file for convergence monitoring. As a convergence parameter, specifies the residual threshold for Newton iteration convergence control.

Resist.description: Specifies the contact resistance for an electrode in ohms. Used in mixed-mode simulations to add a series resistance to a device contact. When specified, the contact voltage is interpreted as the external voltage applied through this resistance.

Resistivity.description: Controls the electrical resistivity model for metal contacts. The metal resistivity (rho = 1/sigma) can be defined through a PMI (Physical Model Interface) model specified in the Physics section. The PMI receives lattice temperature and electric field as inputs and returns the resistivity value.

Resistor_pset.description: Declares a SPICE resistor instance in the System section for mixed-mode simulations. Connects between two circuit nodes with a specified resistance. The default parameter set Resistor_pset provides standard SPICE resistor parameters. Widely used as contact resistors, load resistors, and thermal resistors in mixed-mode circuit simulations.

parameters.name: resistance
parameters.desc: Resistance value in ohms (default: 1)

parameters.name: tc1
parameters.desc: First-order temperature coefficient

parameters.name: tc2
parameters.desc: Second-order temperature coefficient

parameters.name: temp
parameters.desc: Instance operating temperature

Resolution.description: Specifies the spatial resolution of exponential and Gaussian random fields for statistical impedance field method (sIFM) variations. Defined as a vector (rx, ry, rz) in micrometers. Controls the grid spacing for Fourier transform-based random field generation.

Resolve.description: Activates a more accurate discretization of non-heterointerfaces for the density gradient quantum potential model. When specified, the quantum correction is computed with higher accuracy at interfaces that are not heterointerfaces (e.g., insulator interfaces).

Restart.description: Restarts a simulation from a previously saved state. Used in the Solve section to continue a simulation from a snapshot (.tdr or .sav file) saved during a prior run. Also appears in the Math section as Bitlis solver parameter for the number of iterations between successive restarts.

contexts.Math: Bitlis solver parameter: Restart=<int> (default 100) - number of iterations between successive restarts

RetraceCRIChange.description: Specifies the fractional change of the complex refractive index (either the real or imaginary part) from its previous state that will force a total recomputation of raytracing. When the CRI changes by more than this fraction between ramping points, all rays are retraced. Setting to a negative number forces retracing at every ramping point.

RetraceCRIchange.description: Alias for RetraceCRIChange. Specifies the fractional change of the complex refractive index that triggers raytracing recomputation. Both spellings (RetraceCRIChange and RetraceCRIchange) are recognized by Sentaurus Device.

ReverseReactionCoef.description: Specifies the reverse reaction coefficient (k_r) for a hydrogen reaction defined in the HydrogenReaction section. Units depend on reaction dimensionality: 1/cm^2/s for interface reactions (d=2) or 1/cm^3/s for bulk reactions (d=3). Default is 0.

ReverseReactionEnergy.description: Specifies the reverse reaction activation energy (E_r) for a hydrogen reaction, in eV. Used in the Arrhenius-type temperature dependence of the reverse reaction rate. Default is 0 eV.

ReverseReactionFieldCoef.description: Specifies the reverse reaction field coefficient (delta_r) for a hydrogen reaction, in cm/V. Controls the electric field dependence of the reverse reaction rate. Default is 0.
