Rfixed.description: Specifies the fixed resistor value (in ohms) used in the continuation method when the adaptive load resistance algorithm is switched off. The adaptive algorithm is off by default from MinVoltage to Vadapt (and from MinCurrent to Iadapt). Default value is 0.001 ohms.

RhsAndUpdateConvergence.description: Requires both the RHS (right-hand side residual) norm and the update error to satisfy convergence criteria before a solution is considered converged. By default, convergence is satisfied if either RHS norm < RhsMin or update error < 1. With this flag, both conditions must be met simultaneously.

RhsFactor.description: Specifies the maximum allowed increase factor of the L2-norm of the RHS (right-hand side residual) between Newton iterations. If the RHS norm increases by more than this factor during a single Newton step, the solution is considered diverged. Default is 1e10.

RhsMax.description: Specifies the maximum allowed L2-norm of the RHS (right-hand side residual) in each Newton iteration for transient simulations. If the RHS norm exceeds this value, the solution is considered diverged. Default is 1e15.

RhsMaxQ.description: Specifies the maximum allowed L2-norm of the RHS (right-hand side residual) in each Newton iteration for non-transient (quasistationary) simulations. If the RHS norm exceeds this value, the solution is considered diverged. Default is 1e100.

RhsMin.description: Specifies the minimum L2-norm of the RHS (right-hand side residual) for Newton convergence. If the RHS norm falls below this value, the solution is considered converged. Default is 1e-5. Also used in AcceptNewtonParameter() for relaxed Newton method convergence threshold.

RhsMinFactor.description: Specifies a factor for RhsMin that affects convergence behavior. If the RHS norm < RhsMinFactor * RhsMin, the solution is accepted as converged even if the update error criterion is not satisfied. Default is 1e-5.

RhsScale.description: Scales the RHS (residual) norm in the Newton convergence criteria for individual equations during Harmonic Balance analysis. Used in MDFT and SDFT modes to weight the convergence importance of different equations. Often necessary for electron and hole continuity equations.

contexts.Math/HB: Used in the HB section of Math for harmonic balance analysis (MDFT and SDFT modes)

Right.description: A common English word appearing in many contexts throughout the SDevice command file syntax, including: Mesh section boundary specifications (Right/Left), interval notation in command syntax descriptions, equation definitions, and code examples. Not a standalone SDevice command or keyword with specific semantics.

RloadAdjust.description: Adjusts the adaptive load resistance at low values of slope during continuation analysis. Only available when used together with the NewArc option. Helps stabilize the continuation method when tracing I-V curves at low bias regions.

RotationAngles.description: Specifies the angles (phi, theta, psi) defining the global orientation of the illumination window coordinate system. Used in the Excitation section for optical generation to rotate the illumination window relative to the device coordinate system.

RoughInterface.description: Flags interfaces as rough, activating rough interface scattering physics in the Transfer Matrix Method (TMM) optical solver. By default, all interfaces are treated as rough when the Scattering section is specified. Can be disabled globally with -RoughInterface or selectively per region interface.

SBTECommandFile.description: Specifies the command file used to automatically start the Subband-BTE solver (Sentaurus Device QTX) for coupled carrier-Poisson simulations. When specified, Sentaurus Device launches the external Boltzmann solver process using the given command file to compute quantum corrections and effective mobility.

SDFT.description: Enables the SDFT (Single-Dimensional Fourier Transform) mode for harmonic balance analysis. SDFT mode supports only one-tone HB analysis but allows mixed-mode circuits containing standard SPICE models and CMI models that do not provide the CMI-HB-MDFT function set. Used by default when MDFT mode is not enabled.

SFactor.description: Specifies a dataset or PMI model for the spatial distribution of various physical quantities including metal workfunction, trap concentration, bond concentration, eNMP precursor concentration, and piezoresistance enhancement factors. Can reference a named dataset from a file or a PMI model name. Supports optional Factor (scale) and Offset parameters for normalization.

contexts.Physics/Traps: Traps(SFactor=<dataset>) - spatial distribution of trap concentration
contexts.Physics/MetalWorkfunction: MetalWorkfunction(SFactor=<dataset>) - spatial distribution of metal workfunction
contexts.Physics/eNMP: eNMP(SFactor=<pmi_model>) - spatial distribution of eNMP precursor concentration
contexts.RandomizedVariation: DopingVariation(SFactor=<dataset>) and Workfunction(SFactor=<dataset>) - variation spatial distribution

SHE.description: Refers to the Spherical Harmonics Expansion (SHE) method for computing carrier energy distribution. The SHE method solves the Boltzmann transport equation by expanding the distribution function in spherical harmonics. Used for hot-carrier transport simulations, providing energy-dependent carrier distribution beyond drift-diffusion or hydrodynamic models.

SHECutoff.description: Specifies the maximum kinetic energy (in eV) to be plotted for the SHE (Spherical Harmonics Expansion) distribution. Controls the energy range of the uniform kinetic energy grid used for plotting the carrier energy distribution. Default is 5.0 eV.

SHEIterations.description: Specifies the number of SOR (Successive Over-Relaxation) iterations in the SHE (Spherical Harmonics Expansion) distribution model. Controls how many times the blockwise SOR method iterates over different total energies when solving the SHE equation. Default is 20. Must be set to 1 when using RTA mode.

SHEMethod.description: Specifies the linear solver used for the SHE (Spherical Harmonics Expansion) distribution model. Controls the block linear solver used in the SOR iteration over total energies. Default is 'super'. References the same solver options as the general Method keyword (Table 223).

SHERefinement.description: Number of energy grid intervals inside the phonon energy spacing in the SHE (Spherical Harmonics Expansion) distribution model. Controls the resolution of the energy grid used for computing the carrier energy distribution. Higher values provide finer energy resolution at increased computational cost. Default is 1.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Number of energy grid intervals per phonon energy (default 1)

SHESOR.description: Enables or disables the blockwise successive over-relaxation (SOR) method in the SHE (Spherical Harmonics Expansion) distribution model. When enabled (default), SOR iteration is performed over different total energies. When disabled with -SHESOR, equations for different energies are solved simultaneously using the ILS iterative linear solver.

SHESORParameter.description: Successive over-relaxation (SOR) parameter in the SHE (Spherical Harmonics Expansion) distribution model. Controls the relaxation factor for the blockwise SOR iteration over different total energies. Value must be in range [1, 2). Default is 1.1.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: SOR relaxation parameter, range [1, 2), default 1.1

SHETopMargin.description: Top energy margin in the SHE (Spherical Harmonics Expansion) distribution model. Defines the energy margin above the maximum conduction band edge for the total energy coordinate. Default is 1.0 eV.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Energy margin in eV, default 1.0

SPICE.description: Keyword denoting that the circuit equations should be solved in mixed-mode simulations. Used within the Solve section to indicate circuit-coupled simulation. SPICE circuit elements are defined in the System section and circuit files (.scf) found via SPICEPath in the File section.

contexts.Solve: Solve section keyword for circuit simulation
contexts.Math: Spice_gmin, Spice_Temperature settings

SPICEPath.description: Search path for SPICE circuit files (extension .scf) used in mixed-mode simulations. All directories in the path are scanned for SPICE circuit files. If the environment variable STROOT.Lib is defined, the directory $STROOT.Lib/sdevice/spice is automatically added. The circuit files found are parsed and added to the System section.

parameters.name: path
parameters.desc: Colon-separated list of directories to search for .scf files

SRDoping.description: Activates the image-force potential lowering model at Schottky contacts. Used in the Electrode section to include image-force lowering of the potential energy barrier. Can also be used with DistResist=SchottkyResistance in Physics region/material interface sections to enable image-force lowering for distributed Schottky interface resistance.

contexts.Electrode: Contact boundary condition for Schottky contacts
contexts.Physics: Interface resistance model option

SRH.description: Shockley-Read-Hall recombination model within Recombination(). Models trap-assisted recombination through defect energy levels in the bandgap. Supports doping dependence (dopant-dependent lifetimes), electric field dependence (Hurkx model), and temperature dependence.

contexts.Physics: Recombination model within Physics { Recombination() }
contexts.Plot: Plot keyword SRHRecombination for visualization

parameters.name: DopingDependence
parameters.desc: Enable doping-dependent lifetimes using Scharfetter relation

parameters.name: TempDependence
parameters.desc: Enable temperature-dependent lifetimes

parameters.name: NonlocalPath
parameters.desc: Dynamic nonlocal path trap-assisted tunneling (Table 251)

parameters.name: <pmi_model_name>
parameters.desc: PMI model name for custom lifetime computation

SRHRecombination.description: Plot keyword for visualizing the Shockley-Read-Hall recombination rate. Outputs the SRH recombination rate as a spatially distributed quantity in the plot file (.tdr).

SUPER.description: Supernodal direct linear solver. Best accuracy for small problems, not parallelized. Default solver for 1D and 2D structures. Can be used as the linear solver in Coupled, ACCoupled, HBCoupled, and SHEMethod settings.

SaturationFactor.description: Saturation factor for stress-dependent saturation velocity in piezoresistance mobility models. Scales the stress effect on saturation velocity for both electrons and holes. Can be specified globally or per carrier type (eSaturationFactor, hSaturationFactor). Default is 1.0.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Scaling factor for saturation velocity stress dependence (default 1.0)

Save.description: Saves the current simulation solution for later retrieval with Load. The output file is _des.sav by default. Supports time-based, current-based, and voltage-based save triggers within Quasistationary, Transient, and Continuation ramps. Options mirror those in Plot (Intervals, Range, IterationStep, Overwrite, noOverwrite, FilePrefix, etc.).

parameters.name: Intervals
parameters.desc: Number of intervals for saving within a range

parameters.name: Range
parameters.desc: Range specification for save points

parameters.name: Overwrite
parameters.desc: Rewrite same file name at each loop

parameters.name: noOverwrite
parameters.desc: Give each new save file a new name by numbering

parameters.name: FilePrefix
parameters.desc: Prefix of output file name

parameters.name: Time
parameters.desc: Time point(s) for saving

parameters.name: Number
parameters.desc: Number of the solution to retrieve by Load

contexts.Solve: Save/Load solution during simulation
contexts.File: File section specifies Save output name

SaveOptField.description: Saves optical field data during an LED simulation within a Quasistationary or Transient ramp. Uses the same syntax as the Plot command (Range, Intervals, MinSpacing) to control at which ramping points the optical field data is written. The output file name is specified by SaveOptField in the File section.

parameters.name: Range
parameters.desc: Range of ramping variable (t) for saving optical field data

parameters.name: Intervals
parameters.desc: Number of intervals within the range for saving data

parameters.name: MinSpacing
parameters.desc: Minimum spacing between consecutive save points

SaveType.signature: LED { Optics ( RayTrace ( ... OutputLightToolsRays ( SaveType = Ascii | Binary ) ) ) }
SaveType.description: Specifies the output format for LightTools ray data files in LED simulations. Available within OutputLightToolsRays (far-field rays) and OutputLightToolsFarfieldRays tables. Options are Ascii for text format or Binary for binary format.

parameters.name: Ascii
parameters.desc: Choose ASCII format for LightTools ray data file

parameters.name: Binary
parameters.desc: Choose binary format for LightTools ray data file

SbandCommandFile.description: Specifies the command file for Sentaurus Band Structure (sband) when using the external 2D Schrodinger solver. When provided, Sentaurus Device automatically starts Sentaurus Band Structure processes with this command file, passing each slice number on the command line.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Path to the Sentaurus Band Structure command file

ScaleThickness.description: PMI (Physical Model Interface) parameter for thin-layer thermal conductivity models. Defines the scaling thickness (in um) used to normalize the layer thickness in the thermal conductivity formula. The normalized thickness h = LayerThickness/ScaleThickness is used in the thickness-dependent thermal conductivity function. Default is 1e-3 um (1 nm).

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Scaling thickness in um, default 1e-3

Scaling.description: Scaling factor for optical generation rates. Used in multiple contexts: ComputeFromSpectrum, ComputeFromMonochromaticSource, ReadFromFile, and RayDistribution. Multiplies the optical generation by the specified factor (default 1.0).

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Scaling factor for optical generation (default 1.0)

contexts.Physics: Within OpticalGeneration and RayDistribution

ScalingFactor.description: Scaling factor for optical absorption heat in LED simulations. Used within the OpticalAbsorptionHeat statement to allow flexible fine-tuning of the heat source contribution from absorbed photons. Default is 1.0.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Scaling factor for optical absorption heat (default 1.0)

Scattering.description: Specifies the scattering rate model used in multivalley mobility calculations (eSubband/hSubband). MLDA uses modified local density approximation DOS; without argument uses simplified bulk scattering rate.

parameters.name: MLDA
parameters.desc: Include interface scattering via MLDA model

Schenk.signature: Band2Band ( Model = Schenk ) / NonlocalPath ( Lifetime = Schenk )
Schenk.description: Schenk model name, used in multiple contexts: (1) Band-to-band tunneling model (Band2Band Model=Schenk), (2) SRH NonlocalPath lifetime enhancement (Lifetime=Schenk), (3) Schenk bandgap narrowing model (SchenkBGN_elec/SchenkBGN_hole as LocalModel), (4) GateCurrent direct tunneling. The Schenk model provides a comprehensive treatment of interband tunneling and bandgap narrowing based on the density matrix formalism.

contexts.Recombination: SRH NonlocalPath lifetime enhancement and Band2Band tunneling model
contexts.Physics: SchenkBGN_elec/SchenkBGN_hole as LocalModel in QuantumPotential

SchenkBGN_elec.description: Schenk bandgap narrowing model for electrons (conduction band correction). Used as the LocalModel in eQuantumPotential within the density gradient framework. Implements the Schenk density matrix formalism for many-body bandgap narrowing in silicon. Parameters (alpha_e, g_e, Ry_ex, a_ex, etc.) can be customized in the parameter file SchenkBGN_elec section.

parameters.name: LocalModelOnly
parameters.desc: Automatically set gamma=0, using only bandgap narrowing without quantization corrections

SchenkBGN_hole.description: Schenk bandgap narrowing model for holes (valence band correction). Used as the LocalModel in hQuantumPotential within the density gradient framework. The complete bandgap narrowing correction is the sum of conduction (SchenkBGN_elec) and valence (SchenkBGN_hole) band corrections.

parameters.name: LocalModelOnly
parameters.desc: Automatically set gamma=0, using only bandgap narrowing without quantization corrections

SchenkBN_elec.description: Synonym for SchenkBGN_elec. Schenk bandgap narrowing model for electrons used as LocalModel in eQuantumPotential. See SchenkBGN_elec for details.

SchenkBN_hole.description: Synonym for SchenkBGN_hole. Schenk bandgap narrowing model for holes used as LocalModel in hQuantumPotential. See SchenkBGN_hole for details.

Schottky.description: Enables Schottky boundary conditions. In the Physics section, activates Schottky contact boundary conditions for metals (electric boundary conditions). In the Electrode section, specifies that the contact is a Schottky contact with thermionic emission boundary conditions. Also relates to Schottky contact resistance models (SchottkyResist, SchottkyResistance) and PMI implementations.

contexts.Physics: Global Schottky boundary conditions for metals

SchottkyResist.description: Activates the built-in Schottky contact resistance model for Ohmic contacts. Used as the value for DistResist in the Electrode section. The model emulates Schottky contact behavior with a doping-dependent resistivity derived from the image-force potential. Parameters (barrier height, tunneling mass, R_inf) are specified in the parameter file.

contexts.Physics: DistResist=SchottkyResist in Physics for interface resistance

SchottkyResistance.description: Built-in Schottky interface resistance model activated by DistResist=SchottkyResistance. Emulates Schottky contact resistivity using a doping-dependent model derived from image-force potential lowering. Parameters (R_inf, tunneling mass m_t, barrier height Phi_B) can be mole-dependent and are specified in parameter file region/material sections.

parameters.name: Rinf
parameters.desc: Schottky resistance for infinite doping concentration (Ohm*cm^2)

parameters.name: mt
parameters.desc: Tunneling effective mass

parameters.name: Phi_B
parameters.desc: Schottky barrier height (eV)

contexts.Physics: DistResist=SchottkyResist for region/material interfaces

Schroedinger.description: Solves the 1D Schrodinger equation for quantum-confined carriers along a nonlocal line. Activated with Active(Type=QuantumWell). Options include Electron/Hole (carrier types), DensityTail, EnergyInterval, MaxSolutions, Smooth, and polarization settings for optical matrix elements.

SecondOrder.signature: Physics { Piezo ( Model ( Mobility ( Tensor ( [FirstOrder | SecondOrder] ... ) ) ) ) }
SecondOrder.description: Selects the second-order piezoresistance mobility model for stress-dependent mobility calculations. Uses a different (and extended) set of piezoresistance coefficients compared to the first-order model, including second-order terms (pi_111, pi_112, pi_122, pi_123). The first-order coefficients also differ from the FirstOrder model, allowing independent calibration.

Seebeck.signature: PMI model: Seebeck coefficient (metal thermoelectric power)
Seebeck.description: The Seebeck effect relates to thermoelectric power in metals. In Sentaurus Device, the Seebeck coefficient is defined through the MetalTEPower PMI model, which depends on the gradient of the Fermi potential and lattice temperature. It is used in connection with thermodynamic transport in metals and activated when the Temperature equation is solved with Thermodynamic specified in the Physics section.

Select.description: Used in the Optics section within OpticalGeneration ComputeFromSpectrum to select active parameters from a multidimensional spectrum file. Also used in CurrentPlot to filter plot data by model or dataset.

parameters.name: Active parameters
parameters.desc: Named parameters to select from the spectrum data file for optical generation computation.

Semiconductor.description: In the CurrentPlot section, specifies that data sampling (Average, Integrate, Maximum, Minimum) should be restricted to all semiconductor regions of the device.

SemiconductorElectricField.description: A PMI data field name representing the electric field on semiconductor regions. Available as both scalar and vector data types at vertex locations. Used within PMI model implementations to read the electric field in semiconductor portions of the device.

Sensor.description: Defines a sensor detector for collecting ray power in the Farfield section of the unified raytracing interface. Supports Rectangle (3D), Line (2D), and Angular sensor types. UseNormalFlux sums normal-projected power from impinging rays. AxisAligned (3D only) requires only two opposing corners. Multiple sensors with unique names can be defined.

SensorSweep.description: Specifies a sensor sweep configuration for far-field raytracing analysis. Visualizes the variation of ray power collected on a latitude or longitude ring band of a sensor surface.

Serial.description: A ParallelLicense option that causes the simulation to continue in serial mode (single-threaded) when insufficient parallel licenses are available. This is the default behavior.

Set.description: In the Math section, used to assign values to mathematical or numerical parameters. Also used as a plot dataset qualifier in the Plot section for setting visualization options.

SetConstant.description: Defines a constant optical generation rate value (in s^-1 cm^-3). Used within OpticalGeneration to specify a spatially uniform, time-independent generation profile.

Sfactor.description: Specifies a space factor PMI model name that computes the spatial distribution of quantities such as metal workfunction, trap concentration, bond concentration, eNMP precursor concentration, or piezoresistance enhancement factors. The PMI model is loaded at runtime.

parameters.name: pmi_model_name
parameters.desc: Name of the PMI model that computes the spatial distribution factor.

ShareCapacity.description: In the KMCDefects section, specifies the sharing capacity for a particle type. Defines how particle capacity is shared among multiple particles in kinetic Monte Carlo defect simulations for metal-insulator-metal transport.

parameters.name: ShareCapacity
parameters.desc: Sharing capacity value for the particle, controlling how capacity is distributed.

ShiftVector.description: In the OpticalGeneration ReadFromFile and FromFile sections, specifies a displacement vector for the source grid. Used when loading optical generation profiles from files where the source and destination grids are offset. Default is (0,0,0).

parameters.name: ShiftVector
parameters.desc: 3D displacement vector in micrometers to shift the source grid relative to the destination grid.

Side.description: In the BPM (Beam Propagation Method) Boundary section, specifies the side of the computational domain where boundary conditions are applied. Also used in RayTraceBC for defining boundary conditions on specific device sides.

parameters.name: Side
parameters.desc: Axis identifier: X, Y, or Z, indicating which side of the domain the boundary condition applies to.

SideMaterial.description: In the WidthExtraction section for LED simulations, specifies the materials adjoining a quantum well. Used to identify the QW side materials for accurate width extraction calculations.

parameters.name: SideMaterial
parameters.desc: List of material names that adjoin the quantum well on its sides.

SideRegion.description: In the WidthExtraction section for LED simulations, specifies the regions adjoining a quantum well. Used together with SideMaterial to identify QW boundaries for width extraction.

parameters.name: SideRegion
parameters.desc: List of region names that adjoin the quantum well on its sides.

Sigma.description: In the Gaussian intensity distribution function (IntensityDistribution Gaussian), specifies the sigma (standard deviation) of the Gaussian decay in two directions. Cannot be used together with the Length parameter. Also appears as a general symbol for capture cross-section in trap and recombination models.

parameters.name: Sigma
parameters.desc: Pair of floating-point values defining the Gaussian sigma in each spatial direction.

SigmaGauss.description: In the BPM Excitation section, specifies the half-width of a Gaussian excitation profile along the propagation direction. In 2D, a single value is used; in 3D, two values specify the half-width in each transverse direction. Units are micrometers.

parameters.name: SigmaGauss
parameters.desc: Gaussian half-width in micrometers. Single value for 2D, two values for 3D.

SimStats.description: Activates writing of simulation statistics to the current plot file (.plt) after each successful solution. Outputs a SimStats group of datasets including iteration counts, convergence residuals, time-step information, and other solver diagnostics for visualization.

Simple.description: In the context of eBarrierTunneling and hBarrierTunneling, specifies that band-to-band tunneling should use a simple (non-physical) parallel momentum integral. Also used as a GridInterpolation option for conservative grid interpolation when loading optical profiles.

SimpleCapt.description: In the Traps section, restricts the field enhancement of trap cross-sections to the emission rates only. The capture rates use the constant (un-enhanced) cross-sections. Applies to all cross-section models including ElectricField (J-model), Tunneling (Hurkx), and PooleFrenkel. Using different cross-sections for capture and emission violates the principle of detailed balance.

SimulationSystem.description: Specifies that the anisotropic direction vector is given in the simulation coordinate system (as opposed to the crystal coordinate system, which is the default). Used in the Aniso section for defining the direction of anisotropy for mobility and other anisotropic models.

parameters.name: direction(SimulationSystem)
parameters.desc: 3D direction vector in simulation coordinates defining the anisotropic axis. The z-component must be zero for 2D simulations.

SingleTrap.description: In the Traps section, simplifies trap specification to mimic the behavior of a single discrete trap. Automatically uses Uniform spatial shape and confined SpaceSig. The trap concentration is computed so that a filled trap corresponds to one electronic charge. Supports optional RTN (random telegraph noise) mode for PMI access functions.

SingletExciton.description: Activates the singlet exciton equation for OLED/organic semiconductor simulations. Configured in the Coupled statement of the Solve section and regionwise in Physics. Supports bimolecular generation, radiative and trap-assisted radiative decay, nonradiative quenching (eQuenching/hQuenching), and interface dissociation.

Skip.description: Omits certain computations or checks during simulation. In KMC-MIM context, SkipKMC omits the kinetic Monte Carlo simulation entirely. In raytracing, Print(Skip) reduces the number of printed ray paths.

SkipKMC.description: In the Math KMC section, omits the kinetic Monte Carlo simulation completely. Used when KMC setup or rate equation analysis is needed without performing the actual stochastic KMC transport simulation.

SkipKMCCurrentCheck.description: In the Math KMC section, omits further KMC simulator calls during a transient ramp when the MIM total current change at two or more electrodes is less than the specified fraction compared to the previous time step. This detects approximate steady state and avoids excessive KMC events.

parameters.name: SkipKMCCurrentCheck
parameters.desc: Fractional threshold (0 to 1) for MIM current change. When current change is below this fraction, KMC simulation is skipped for the remainder of the transient.

SlotBoom.description: An alias for the Slotboom bandgap narrowing model. Selects the Slotboom model for computing bandgap narrowing in the EffectiveIntrinsicDensity statement. The SlotBoom keyword and Slotboom keyword refer to the same model.

Slotboom.description: Selects the Slotboom bandgap narrowing model for computing the effective intrinsic carrier density. The model is based on measurements of mobility times intrinsic density squared in bipolar transistors. The bandgap narrowing depends on the total doping concentration through a logarithmic function with material-specific parameters Eref and Nref.

Smooth.description: In the Math section, keeps mobility and recombination rates from the previous Newton step to obtain better initial conditions for extreme nonlinear iterations. Helps improve convergence stability during difficult simulation steps.

Smoothing.description: Controls the smoothness of transitions between different parameter sets when using the auto-orientation framework. When a nonzero smoothing distance is specified, orientation-dependent parameters (mobility, avalanche, etc.) transition smoothly between parameter sets instead of switching abruptly. Weights are calculated at each vertex based on proximity to different supported surface orientations.

parameters.name: AutoOrientationSmoothingDistance
parameters.desc: Approximate distance in micrometers over which the transition between different parameter sets occurs. Negative values use average interface vertex spacing.

Solve.description: Defines the simulation sequence and what equations to solve. Supports coupled Newton, Gummel (Plugin), quasistationary ramps, transient analysis, small-signal AC, harmonic balance, and continuation methods.

parameters.name: Coupled
parameters.desc: Solve coupled equations consistently by Newton iteration.

parameters.name: Quasistationary
parameters.desc: Quasistationary ramp with step control and goal specification.

parameters.name: Transient
parameters.desc: Transient simulation with time-step control.

parameters.name: ACCoupled
parameters.desc: Small-signal AC analysis with frequency sweep.

parameters.name: Plugin
parameters.desc: Solve equations consistently by Gummel iteration.

parameters.name: Continuation
parameters.desc: Continuation method for tracing IV curves beyond breakdown.

SolveSpectrum.description: References a predefined solve spectrum for use in an HBCoupled (harmonic balance) statement. The spectrum is defined in the HB section of the Math section and allows truncating the full harmonic balance spectrum to only specific (multi-)indices, reducing computational cost. Each multi-index corresponds to an intermodulation frequency combination.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.desc: Name of the solve spectrum as defined in Math { HB { SolveSpectrum (Name=...) {...} } }

parameters.name: (<int>*n)
parameters.desc: List of spectrum multi-indices. Each entry is a tuple of integers specifying harmonic indices for each tone.

contexts.Math: Global HB configuration defining named solve spectra
contexts.Solve: HBCoupled statement references a Math-level SolveSpectrum

Solver.description: Generic conceptual term appearing across many sections. In the context of complex refractive index models, Solver specifies the optical solver type used for computing the complex refractive index. In Math/Physics contexts, it generally refers to the numerical solver methodology. Not a standalone command but a parameter value used in various parent sections.

contexts.Physics: Solver parameter in ComplexRefractiveIndex model selection
contexts.Math: General reference to numerical solver settings

SourceBoxCorner1.description: Sub-parameter of ImportDomain() specifying the lower-left corner of a bounding box in the source grid to restrict the interpolation domain when loading optical generation profiles from files.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: 3D coordinate vector (x, y, z) in micrometers. Default: (-Inf, -Inf, -Inf).

SourceBoxCorner2.description: Sub-parameter of ImportDomain() specifying the upper-right corner of a bounding box in the source grid to restrict the interpolation domain when loading optical generation profiles from files.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: 3D coordinate vector (x, y, z) in micrometers. Default: (Inf, Inf, Inf).

SourcePosition.description: Specifies the position of a radiation or optical source within the device geometry. Used to define the spatial origin of incoming rays or particles.

SourceRaysFromFile.description: Imports a set of starting ray direction vectors from a file for LED raytracing simulations. The number of imported rays per vertex is specified by RaysPerVertex.

parameters.name: filename
parameters.desc: Path to the file containing starting ray direction data.

contexts.Physics: LED raytracing source ray definition

SourceRegions.description: Sub-parameter of ImportDomain() specifying a list of regions in the source grid that are selected for interpolation when loading optical generation profiles from files. Used together with DestinationRegions to control the domain of optical data transfer between grids.

parameters.name: regions
parameters.desc: List of region name strings in the source grid selected for interpolation.

South.description: Cardinal direction identifier used in various optical and geometry contexts. In Window OriginAnchor, specifies the south anchor point of an illumination window. In LayerStackExtraction WindowPosition, defines the center starting point as the south direction. In RayDistribution, used as a direction identifier for ray placement.

contexts.Physics: Optical excitation window anchor and layer stack extraction

SouthEast.description: Cardinal direction identifier used in optical contexts. In Window OriginAnchor, specifies the south-east anchor point of an illumination window. In LayerStackExtraction WindowPosition, defines the center starting point as the south-east direction.

SouthWest.description: Cardinal direction identifier used in optical contexts. In Window OriginAnchor, specifies the south-west anchor point of an illumination window. In LayerStackExtraction WindowPosition, defines the center starting point as the south-west direction.

SpaceCharge.description: Space charge density (rho) at each vertex, available as a plot dataset. Computed as the difference between positive and negative charge carriers including ions and free carriers. Units: cm^-3.

contexts.Plot: Scalar dataset for space charge density visualization
contexts.Math: Referenced in MIM leakage current examples

SpaceMid.description: Specifies the center of a spatial distribution window for noise sources, trap distributions, and sIFM (statistical impedance field method) variations. Restricts the affected region to a volume centered at the given coordinates. Default is (0, 0, 0) micrometers.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: 3D coordinate vector (x, y, z) in micrometers defining the center of the spatial distribution. Default: (0 0 0).

contexts.Physics: Noise source spatial restriction, trap spatial distribution, sIFM variation spatial restriction

SpaceSig.description: Specifies the width (standard deviation or half-width) of a spatial distribution window for noise sources, trap distributions, and sIFM variations. Controls the extent of the region affected by the modifier function. Default is (1e100, 1e100, 1e100) micrometers (effectively infinite).

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: 3D coordinate vector (x, y, z) in micrometers defining the width of the spatial distribution. Default: (1e100 1e100 1e100).

contexts.Physics: Noise source spatial restriction, trap spatial distribution, sIFM variation spatial restriction

SpatialShape.description: Specifies the shape function of a spatial distribution for noise sources, trap distributions, heavy ion generation, and sIFM variations. Gaussian uses a Gaussian bell-curve shape while Uniform uses a flat (box) shape. Default is Uniform.

parameters.name: Gaussian
parameters.desc: Use Gaussian (bell-curve) shape function for the spatial distribution.

parameters.name: Uniform
parameters.desc: Use uniform (flat/box) shape function for the spatial distribution. This is the default.

parameters.name: PMI_model
parameters.desc: In HeavyIon context, specifies a PMI model name for the spatial distribution function.

contexts.Physics: Spatial shape for noise sources, trap distributions, heavy ion spatial distributions, and sIFM variations

SpecialVector.description: Plot modifier suffix appended to a dataset name to plot special vectorial data. Used primarily for SHE (spherical harmonics expansion) distribution data where each column represents the distribution function at a specific kinetic energy. For example, eSHEDistribution/SpecialVector plots the electron-energy distribution function for each kinetic energy grid.

contexts.Plot: Special vector plot modifier for SHE distribution data

SpectralInterpolation.description: Sub-parameter of FromFile() specifying the type of interpolation between loaded optical profiles when multiple profiles are available. Controls how the optical generation rate is interpolated across different spectral conditions. Default is Off.

parameters.name: Linear
parameters.desc: Use linear interpolation between loaded profiles.

parameters.name: Off
parameters.desc: Disable interpolation between profiles. This is the default.

parameters.name: PiecewiseConstant
parameters.desc: Use piecewise constant (nearest-neighbor) interpolation between loaded profiles.

SpectralPlot.description: Plots spectral simulation results for each entry of the illumination spectrum. Outputs spatially distributed results to a TDR file and spectral curves to a PLT file. Consecutive plots are collected in a single file by default. Used in conjunction with the IlluminationSpectrum option in the Physics section for wavelength-dependent optical simulations. Follows the same parameter syntax as the Plot command (Table 345).

parameters.name: FilePrefix
parameters.desc: Prefix of the output file name

parameters.name: Time
parameters.desc: Time points for plotting during transient

parameters.name: When
parameters.desc: Generate output when a target voltage or current is crossed

parameters.name: Overwrite
parameters.desc: Rewrite the same file name at each loop

parameters.name: noOverwrite
parameters.desc: Write to separate enumerated files

parameters.name: ident
parameters.desc: Device names to be plotted.

contexts.File: Output file specification for spectral plot data
contexts.Solve: SpectralPlot statement to trigger spectral data output

Spectrum.description: Generic conceptual term referring to spectral data in optical simulations. In the File section, IlluminationSpectrum specifies the input spectrum file. In Math/Solve contexts, relates to spectral analysis of illumination. In optical generation, ComputeFromSpectrum computes optical generation from an illumination spectrum. Not a standalone command.

contexts.Physics: OpticalGeneration spectrum-based computation
contexts.Solve: SpectralPlot output

Spice_Temperature.description: In the global Math section, initializes the global SPICE circuit temperature at the beginning of a mixed-mode simulation. The value is in kelvin (default: 300.15 K). Note that SPICE temperatures are measured in degrees Celsius, so a -273.15 offset applies internally. This keyword cannot be used to change the SPICE temperature during a simulation; use a SPICE temperature interface instead.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Global SPICE circuit temperature in kelvin. Default: 300.15 K.

Spice_gmin.description: In the global Math section, specifies the minimum conductance (shunt conductance) added in parallel to every SPICE element in mixed-mode simulations. Default is 1e-12 Ohm^-1. Helps improve numerical convergence by preventing singular matrices.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Minimum conductance value in Ohm^-1. Default: 1e-12.

Spline.description: Specifies spline interpolation for energy-dependent parameters in physics models. Used in energy relaxation time (Formula=3), energy-dependent mobility, and energy-dependent Peltier coefficient definitions. Inside the braces, energy-value pairs define the spline control points. For values outside the specified intervals, the parameter is treated as constant equal to the closest boundary value.

parameters.name: param_name
parameters.desc: Name of the parameter to which spline interpolation is applied (e.g., tau_w_ele, tau_w_hol).

parameters.name: energy
parameters.desc: Energy value in eV for the spline control point.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Parameter value at the corresponding energy.

contexts.Physics: Spline interpolation for energy-dependent parameters in various physics models

Split.description: Used inside IncompleteIonization to distribute a single doping species across multiple lattice sites with specified occupation probabilities (weights). The sum of weights must equal one. Sentaurus Device automatically generates the necessary split doping species (e.g., NitrogenConcentration_split1, _split2, etc.) without requiring entries in datexcodes.txt.

contexts.Physics: Multi-context boolean flag with different meanings per parent section

SplitOff.description: In LED() physics model, specifies the spin-orbit split-off energy in eV. Used in quantum well strain effect calculations to account for the influence of the split-off band on optical transition matrix elements.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Spin-orbit split-off energy in eV.

SponEmissionCoeff.description: In LED() physics model, specifies a PMI model to use for spontaneous emission coefficient computation. The PMI model provides the spontaneous emission coefficient as a function of carrier densities and energies, replacing the built-in calculation.

parameters.name: ident
parameters.desc: Name of the PMI model for spontaneous emission.

parameters.name: Table 364
parameters.desc: Optional PMI parameters passed to the model.

SponEmissionIntegration.description: Specifies numeric integration method for spontaneous emission calculations in LED and gain simulations. When used with GaussianQuadrature, replaces default integration with Gaussian quadrature of the specified order, accelerating gain computations.

parameters.name: GaussianQuadrature
parameters.desc: Enable Gaussian quadrature integration method.

parameters.name: Order
parameters.desc: Order of the Legendre polynomial (1-20). Higher orders give better accuracy but may cause convergence issues.

SponIntegration.description: In LED() optics configuration, specifies the energy integration range and number of discretization intervals for numeric integration of the spontaneous emission rate in LED simulations. The first argument is the energy span in eV measured from the bandgap edge, and the second is the number of discretized points within that span.

parameters.name: energy_span
parameters.desc: Energy integration range in eV, measured from the edge of the energy bandgap.

parameters.name: numpoints
parameters.desc: Number of discretized intervals for the Riemann sum integration within the energy span.

SponScaling.description: In LED() physics model, specifies a scaling factor for the matrix element of spontaneous emission. Allows manual adjustment of the spontaneous emission rate to match experimental data or calibrate the simulation.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Scaling factor for the spontaneous emission matrix element.

SpontaneousRecombination.description: Plot variable available in LED simulations representing the sum of spontaneous emission recombination rate. The unit is cm^-3 s^-1. Available as a spatially distributed dataset in TDR plot files.

contexts.Plot: LED simulation plot variable for spontaneous recombination visualization

StackSize.description: In the global Math section, specifies the stack size in bytes per assembly thread. The default stack size is 1 MB. Increasing it is necessary for LED raytracing simulations where the recursive raytracing process can exhaust the default stack space, causing segmentation faults. Also recognized as environment variables SDEVICE_STACKSIZE and SNPS_STACKSIZE.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Stack size in bytes. Default: 1048576 (1 MB).

Staggered3DFarfieldGrid.description: In LED raytracing, activates the staggered 3D far-field collection sphere for LED radiation pattern computation. This replaces the old (theta, phi) scheme with a more uniform grid that reduces area skew to approximately 1.38. Without this keyword, the collection sphere reverts to the old scheme for backward compatibility.

StandingWave.description: Specifies the computation of regular optical intensity using the full interference pattern between forward and backward propagating waves in the transfer matrix method (TMM) solver.

StartFrequency.description: Sets the lower bound of the frequency range for small-signal AC analysis or noise analysis in Hz.

parameters.name: StartFrequency
parameters.desc: Lower frequency bound in Hz. Must be a positive value. Used together with EndFrequency and NumberOfPoints to define the AC frequency sweep range.

StartingPeriod.description: Specifies the cycle number from which the cyclic extrapolation procedure starts in large-signal cyclic (steady-state periodic) analysis.

parameters.name: StartingPeriod
parameters.desc: The period number (>=2) at which extrapolation begins. Default is 2. Before this period, the simulation proceeds as a standard transient without cyclic extrapolation.

State.description: Defines a single state within an MSConfig (multistate configuration) in the Physics section. Each MSC requires at least two states. Specifies the state name, charge, hydrogen composition, and optional TtOM (trap-to-oscillator model) flag. States participate in transitions defined by the Transition keyword.

parameters.name: ident
parameters.desc: Name of the state variable. The value is a string identifier used to reference this state in PMI models and MSC-dependent physics.

Statistical.description: Activates statistical solver settings for noise and fluctuation computations. Controls evaluation strategies for noise sources and sensitivity analysis.

StepFunction.description: Defines a step function cutoff for interband optical absorption in quantum yield or optical absorption heat models. Options: Bandgap uses Eg-Ebgn as cutoff, EffectiveBandgap uses Eg-Ebgn+2*(3/2)kT, Wavelength/Energy specifies an explicit cutoff value. Takes precedence over EffectiveAbsorption if both are specified in QuantumYield.

parameters.name: Bandgap
parameters.desc: Use Eg - Ebgn as the cutoff energy for interband absorption (for OpticalAbsorptionHeat) or Eg (for QuantumYield).

parameters.name: EffectiveBandgap
parameters.desc: Use Eg - Ebgn (QuantumYield) or Eg - Ebgn + 2*(3/2)kT (OpticalAbsorptionHeat) as the cutoff energy.

parameters.name: Energy
parameters.desc: Explicit cutoff energy value in eV for interband absorption.

parameters.name: Wavelength
parameters.desc: Explicit cutoff wavelength value in micrometers, converted to energy for the absorption threshold.

StimEmissionCoeff.description: In LED(), specifies a PMI model for the stimulated emission coefficient. Used to import gain and spontaneous emission data via PMI for accurate LED and laser simulations.

StimScaling.description: Scaling factor for the stimulated emission optical matrix element in LED and quantum well simulations, allowing fine-tuning of the stimulated gain curve magnitude.

parameters.name: StimScaling
parameters.desc: Multiplier applied to the optical matrix element for stimulated emission. Default is 1.0. Values different from 1.0 change the threshold current.

StimulatedRecombination.description: Plot keyword that outputs the total stimulated recombination rate on active vertices in LED simulations, representing carrier depletion due to stimulated photon emission across all lasing modes.

StoreExtrapolation.description: Stores extrapolation information internally at the end of a Quasistationary command so it can be reused by a subsequent Quasistationary command for faster convergence.

Strain.description: Activates strain-dependent physics models. In LED simulations, enables strain modeling for quantum wells with a given reference lattice constant. In the Model section, enables deformation potential, piezoresistive mobility, and multivalley band structure models for stress/strain simulations.

parameters.name: Strain
parameters.desc: Hooke computes strain from stress via elastic compliance tensor. Explicit values specify six components (XX, YY, ZZ, YZ, XZ, XY). LoadFromFile reads strain from the Piezo TDR file (ElasticStrain field).

Strategy.description: Specifies the strategy for filling the system Jacobian matrix during nonlocal path tunneling computations, controlling how and when derivative information is collected.

parameters.name: Strategy
parameters.desc: Strategy selection: 1 = collect all Jacobian entries; 2 = collect last N entries (default); 3 = collect last N entries with step size control. Used inside NonlocalPath() in the Math section.

Stress.description: Mechanical stress model for strain-dependent simulations. Enables piezoresistive mobility effects and deformation potential effects on band structure. Used in Mechanics{} and Physics{} sections.

parameters.name: Stress
parameters.desc: Six components (XX, YY, ZZ, YZ, XZ, XY) of the symmetric stress tensor in Pa. Alternatively, a PMI model identifier can be used to compute stress via the Physical Model Interface.

StressLimit.description: Limits the magnitude of all stress tensor components at each semiconductor vertex to a specified maximum value, preventing nonphysical results from extremely high stress inputs.

parameters.name: StressLimit
parameters.desc: Maximum allowed magnitude for any stress component in Pa. The sign of the original stress value is retained. For example, a value of -8.2e9 with StressLimit=4e9 is clamped to -4e9.

StressMobilityDependence.description: Specifies how the stress tensor factor is applied to low-field mobility, using a diagonal tensor model where each mobility component is independently scaled by the corresponding stress tensor diagonal element.

parameters.name: StressMobilityDependence
parameters.desc: The only documented value is TensorFactor, which applies a diagonal mobility tensor factor to low-field mobility based on stress tensor diagonal elements.

StressSG.description: Selects the StressSG anisotropic discretization approximation for the Poisson, continuity, and thermodynamic equations, providing mesh-orientation-independent accuracy for stress-dependent problems.

StressXX.description: The XX component of the symmetric stress tensor in Pa, used as an individual scalar stress specification or as a rampable parameter in quasistationary simulations.

parameters.name: StressXX
parameters.desc: Normal stress component along the x-axis in Pa (Pascals). Tensile stress is positive by convention.

StressXY.description: The XY shear component of the symmetric stress tensor in Pa.

parameters.name: StressXY
parameters.desc: Shear stress component in the XY plane in Pa.

StressXZ.description: The XZ shear component of the symmetric stress tensor in Pa.

parameters.name: StressXZ
parameters.desc: Shear stress component in the XZ plane in Pa.

StressYY.description: The YY normal component of the symmetric stress tensor in Pa.

parameters.name: StressYY
parameters.desc: Normal stress component along the y-axis in Pa.

StressYZ.description: The YZ shear component of the symmetric stress tensor in Pa.

parameters.name: StressYZ
parameters.desc: Shear stress component in the YZ plane in Pa.

StressZZ.description: The ZZ normal component of the symmetric stress tensor in Pa.

parameters.name: StressZZ
parameters.desc: Normal stress component along the z-axis in Pa.

StretchingParameter.description: Specifies the complex stretching parameter for PML (perfectly matched layer) boundary conditions in the beam propagation method (BPM) optical solver.

parameters.name: StretchingParameterReal
parameters.desc: Minimum and maximum values of the real part of the complex stretching parameter for PML boundaries.

parameters.name: StretchingParameterImag
parameters.desc: Minimum and maximum values of the imaginary part of the complex stretching parameter for PML boundaries.

StretchingParameterImag.description: Specifies the minimum and maximum values of the imaginary part of the complex stretching parameter for PML boundary conditions in the BPM optical solver.

parameters.name: StretchingParameterImag
parameters.desc: Two floats specifying the minimum and maximum imaginary part of the stretching parameter. Controls the absorption strength of the PML layer.

StretchingParameterReal.description: Specifies the minimum and maximum values of the real part of the complex stretching parameter for PML boundary conditions in the BPM optical solver.

parameters.name: StretchingParameterReal
parameters.desc: Two floats specifying the minimum and maximum real part of the stretching parameter. Affects the phase velocity profile inside the PML layer.

String.description: Passes an arbitrary string argument to a PMI (Physical Model Interface) model for model-specific configuration or identification purposes.

parameters.name: String
parameters.desc: A string value passed to and interpreted by the PMI model. Default is empty string. Used for model-specific configuration that does not fit into typed parameters.

Stripe.description: In optical simulations, defines a stripe-shaped excitation geometry or gain region. Typically used in edge-emitting laser and waveguide simulations to define the lateral extent of the optical mode or pumped region.

SubMethod.description: Specifies the inner linear solver method used within a Coupled Newton iteration, allowing per-device solver selection for mixed-mode simulations.

parameters.name: SubMethod
parameters.desc: Optional device identifier in parentheses followed by the solver name. Super is the default for 1D/2D; ILS is the default for 3D. When a device identifier is given, the specified solver is used only for that device.

Subband.description: Defines subband-related parameters for quantum well models, including simple quantum-well subband configurations used in LED and laser simulations to compute emission coefficients and gain spectra.

Summand.description: Specifies a value to be added to the original parameter value when computing deterministic parameter variations for sensitivity analysis.

parameters.name: Summand
parameters.desc: A constant added to the original parameter value. The modified parameter becomes gamma' = gamma + Summand (if Value is not specified) or gamma' = gamma * Factor + Summand. Default is 0.

Super.description: Supernodal direct linear solver. Best accuracy for small problems but not parallelized. Default for 1D and 2D structures. Can be used as the Method in Coupled, ACCoupled, or Math section.

parameters.name: (SubMethod)
parameters.desc: Inner solver specification for a specific device instance. Super (1D and 2D) or ILS (3D).

Supernodal.description: Refers to the parallel supernodal direct solver ParDiSo, which provides high accuracy and parallel performance for small and medium problems. Mentioned as a property of the ParDiSo solver.

parameters.name: IterativeRefinement
parameters.desc: Perform up to two iterative refinement steps to improve the accuracy of the solution.

parameters.name: MultipleRHS
parameters.desc: Solve linear systems with multiple right-hand sides (only for AC analysis).

parameters.name: NonsymmetricPermutation
parameters.desc: Compute an initial nonsymmetric matrix permutation and scaling, placing large entries on the diagonal.

parameters.name: RecomputeNonsymmetricPermutation
parameters.desc: Compute a nonsymmetric matrix permutation and scaling before each factorization.

Surface.description: Specifies the name of a surface for geometric variation in deterministic sensitivity analysis. The named surface is displaced according to the amplitude parameters defined in the GeometricVariation section.

parameters.name: (location)
parameters.desc: Region interface, material interface, or electrode name that composes the surface.

SurfaceConductance.description: Specifies the contact thermal conductance at a thermode in units of W/(cm^2 K). Used together with Temperature to define a thermal resistive boundary condition for self-heating simulations.

parameters.name: SurfaceConductance
parameters.desc: Contact thermal conductance in W/(cm^2 K). The inverse of SurfaceResistance.

SurfaceName.description: Specifies a reference surface by name for determining the polarization direction in ferroelectric simulations or the surface name for interface charge mobility degradation. The polarization vector at any point is taken opposite to the shortest path to the reference surface.

parameters.name: SurfaceName
parameters.desc: Name of a surface defined in the Math section, used as reference for polarization direction or interface mobility.

parameters.name: SurfaceReconstruction
parameters.desc: Activates improved algorithm for directional polarization calculation (ferroelectrics context).

SurfaceRecombination.description: A scalar plot quantity that outputs the surface SRH recombination rate at region-interface vertices. Activated when the SurfaceSRH option is used in the Recombination keyword.

SurfaceResistance.description: Specifies the contact thermal resistivity at a thermode in units of cm^2 K/W. Used together with Temperature to define a thermal resistive boundary condition for self-heating simulations. The inverse of SurfaceConductance.

parameters.name: SurfaceResistance
parameters.desc: Contact thermal resistivity in cm^2 K/W. Default is unspecified.

SurfaceSRH.description: Surface SRH recombination model within Recombination(). Models Shockley-Read-Hall recombination at material interfaces and surfaces with interface trap density and capture cross-section parameters.

parameters.name: S0
parameters.desc: Surface recombination velocity in cm/s. Default 1e3 for both electrons and holes.

parameters.name: Sref
parameters.desc: Doping-dependence coefficient. Set to 0 to suppress doping dependence.

parameters.name: Nref
parameters.desc: Reference doping concentration in cm^-3. Default 1e16.

parameters.name: gamma
parameters.desc: Exponent for doping dependence. Default 1.

parameters.name: Etrap
parameters.desc: Trap energy level in eV. Default 0.

Sweep.description: Defines a parameter sweep for quasistationary or transient simulations. Sweep-specific break criteria can be specified to control when individual sweep steps terminate.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.desc: Name of the sweep, used for organizing and referencing sweep-specific break criteria.

parameters.name: BreakCriteria
parameters.desc: Sweep-specific break criteria to conditionally stop the simulation.

Symmetric.description: A flag or option used in multiple physical model contexts to indicate symmetric treatment. In mobility models, it controls symmetric averaging. In stress models, it relates to tensor symmetry. In ferroelectrics, it relates to device symmetry handling.

Symmetry.signature: (Symmetry option in stress, ferroelectric, and optics contexts)
Symmetry.description: Relates to symmetry properties in various physical models. In ferroelectric simulations, it controls how polarization profiles reflect device geometry symmetry. In stress models, it specifies tensor symmetry properties. In optics, it relates to reflectivity/transmittivity boundary conditions.

System.description: Defines the circuit (mixed-mode) section of the command file. Instantiates device models, connects electrodes to circuit nodes, specifies netlists, and controls plotting of circuit quantities.

parameters.name: Electrical
parameters.desc: List of electrical nodes in the circuit.

parameters.name: Thermal
parameters.desc: List of thermal nodes in the circuit.

parameters.name: Netlist
parameters.desc: PrimeSim HSPICE netlist file path.

parameters.name: Plot
parameters.desc: Plot circuit quantities to file named <string>.

parameters.name: Set
parameters.desc: Set node voltage or compact model parameter.

parameters.name: Hint
parameters.desc: Set node only for the first solve.

parameters.name: Initialize
parameters.desc: Set node until first transient.

parameters.name: Unset
parameters.desc: Unset node.

parameters.name: SystemTopology
parameters.desc: Print description and analysis of the system.

T2TPDErrorScale.description: Error scale parameter for the trap-to-trap (T2T) tunneling explicit equation in DiscreteTrapT2T model. Controls the convergence tolerance of the trap equation. Defined once across all trap entries.

parameters.name: T2TPDErrorScale
parameters.desc: Error scale for the trap PDE equation. Example value: 1e-15.

T2TconcScale.description: Scaling factor that maps the number of discrete traps to a concentration in the DiscreteTrapT2T model. Defined as (Concentration x Volume of region) / (Total number of region T2T traps). Can be combined with Conc or T2TfixedConc for additional scaling.

parameters.name: T2TconcScale
parameters.desc: Concentration scaling factor. Final trap count at each vertex = T2TfixedConc x vertex_volume x T2TconcScale.

T2TfixedConc.description: Fixes the trap concentration at each trap-to-trap vertex in the DiscreteTrapT2T model. The number of traps at each vertex is T2TfixedConc x vertex_volume. Similar to how Conc works in implicit continuous traps but for discrete trap entries.

parameters.name: T2TfixedConc
parameters.desc: Fixed trap concentration at each vertex in cm^-3. Cannot be used together with Conc.

T2Tflux.description: A plot keyword that outputs the trap-to-trap tunneling fluxes between trap vertices in the DiscreteTrapT2T model. Only applies to the InelasticPhonon trap-to-trap transition type. The output contains eT2Tflux and hT2Tflux for electron and hole charge transport respectively.

TATNonlocalPathNC.description: Specifies the room temperature effective density-of-states NC(300K) for Schottky contacts or metal-semiconductor interfaces in the nonlocal path trap-assisted tunneling (TAT) model. Required to consider electron capture and emission from Schottky contacts in TAT calculations.

parameters.name: TATNonlocalPathNC
parameters.desc: Room temperature effective density-of-states for the Schottky contact in cm^-3. Example: 2.0e19.

TCircuit.description: Activates the solution of the thermal circuit equations in mixed-mode simulations. Used in Coupled statements alongside other equations to include thermal circuit nodes in the self-consistent solve.

TContact.description: Activates the solution of the thermal contact equations (thermal interface conditions) in mixed-mode simulations. Used in Coupled statements to include thermal boundary conditions at contacts in the self-consistent solve.

TE.description: Polarization configuration option for LED optical simulations. Specifies transverse electric (TE) mode polarization in OpticalSolver{} or LED{} sections for far-field and near-field radiation pattern calculations.

parameters.name: Polarization
parameters.desc: Polarization mode: TE (transverse electric), TM (transverse magnetic), or Mixed.

parameters.name: PolarizationFactor
parameters.desc: Polarization factor for mixed polarization, between 0 and 1. Default 1.

TEPower.description: Activates the thermoelectric power model for semiconductor regions. Supports an analytic formula or a user-defined PMI model. Used in conjunction with the thermodynamic model for self-heating simulations.

parameters.name: (Table)
parameters.desc: Use table-based model with experimental thermoelectric power values as functions of temperature and carrier concentration.

parameters.name: (Analytic)
parameters.desc: Use analytic formulas for thermoelectric power in nondegenerate semiconductors.

TM.description: TM (Transverse Magnetic) polarization mode for optical simulations. Specifies that the optical magnetic field is perpendicular to the plane of incidence. Used in QWLocal, Excitation, and optical solver configurations.

parameters.name: Polarization
parameters.desc: Polarization mode: TE, TM, or Mixed.

TMM.description: Transfer Matrix Method optical solver. Computes optical field distribution in multilayer structures by solving the wave equation in 1D through the layer stack. Options control intensity pattern type, node density per wavelength, propagation mode, rough interface scattering, and layer stack extraction from the device geometry.

parameters.name: LayerStructure
parameters.desc: Definition of multilayer structure: pairs of (thickness in um, material name).

parameters.name: ReferenceMaterial
parameters.desc: Topmost layer orientation: connected to region with this material.

parameters.name: ReferenceRegion
parameters.desc: Topmost layer orientation: connected to region with this name.

parameters.name: MapOptGenToRegions
parameters.desc: List of regions to map lumped TMM BC optical generation to.

parameters.name: QuantumEfficiency
parameters.desc: Quantum efficiency of the TMM BC optical generation.

TMM1D.description: Enables the one-dimensional transfer matrix method (TMM) for computing optical generation rates. Used as a boundary condition option for raytracing at interfaces.

TRBDF.description: Trapezoidal Rule / Backward Differentiation Formula (TR-BDF2) time integration method for transient simulations. Default transient method in Sentaurus Device. Combines the stability of BDF methods with the accuracy of trapezoidal integration.

Table.description: A generic keyword used across many Physics contexts to specify table-based parameter definitions. In trap definitions, it specifies tabulated trap energy levels. In bandgap narrowing, it activates the TableBGN model. In PMI contexts, it passes PMI parameters. In mobility and recombination, it enables table-file-based parameter input.

parameters.name: (Table 364)
parameters.desc: PMI parameter block with scalar or vector parameter assignments.

TableBGN.description: Table-based bandgap narrowing model. Specifies bandgap narrowing as a function of donor, acceptor, or total doping concentration using tabulated data. Interpolation is piecewise linear in the logarithm of concentration. Values outside the table range are clamped to the nearest entry.

parameters.name: Total
parameters.desc: Total doping concentration (cm^-3) and bandgap narrowing value (eV) pair.

parameters.name: Donor
parameters.desc: Donor concentration and bandgap narrowing pair.

parameters.name: Acceptor
parameters.desc: Acceptor concentration and bandgap narrowing pair.

TempDep.description: Flag that activates temperature-dependent behavior for various physical models. In HeatCapacity, it enables the empirical temperature-dependent lattice heat capacity model. In SRH recombination, it enables temperature-dependent carrier lifetimes. In BalMob, it enables temperature dependency in simple ballistic mobility.

TempDependence.description: Temperature dependence option within SRH(). Enables temperature-dependent carrier lifetimes for the SRH recombination model, accounting for the variation of trap-assisted recombination rates with lattice temperature.

Temperature.description: Specifies a temperature value in Kelvin. In Thermode sections, sets the contact temperature for thermal boundary conditions. In Goal sections, sets the target temperature for a quasistationary ramp. Also used as a general parameter name in many physics models.

parameters.name: Temperature
parameters.desc: Temperature value in K (Kelvin).

contexts.Thermode: Contact temperature for thermal boundary condition, used with SurfaceResistance or SurfaceConductance.
contexts.Goal: Target temperature value for quasistationary ramp.
contexts.Physics: General model parameter in various physics contexts (e.g., Splice_Temperature, Spice_Temperature).

TemperatureDep.description: Enables temperature dependency of the refractive index in the complex refractive index model for optical simulations.

parameters.name: real
parameters.desc: Keyword 'real' enables the temperature dependency for the refractive index part.

Tensor.description: Activates a PMI-based piezoresistive mobility model that applies a tensor (full matrix) stress factor to carrier mobility, as opposed to a simple scalar prefactor.

parameters.name: pmi_model
parameters.desc: Name of the PMI model implementing the piezoresistive tensor. Must be a valid PMI_PiezoresistanceFactor model.

TensorFactor.description: Applies the stress tensor factor as a diagonal mobility tensor to the low-field mobility rather than as a simple scalar prefactor, allowing each diagonal component of mobility to respond independently to stress.

TensorGridAniso.description: Activates the tensor-grid approximation for anisotropic models. This is the default and most robust anisotropic discretization method, using eigenvalues and eigenvectors of the tensor as multiplication factors for projections on mesh edges.

parameters.name: Aniso
parameters.desc: Use tensor-grid approximation for all anisotropic models (mobility, permittivity, thermal conductivity, density gradient).

parameters.name: Piezo
parameters.desc: Use tensor-grid approximation for piezoresistive stress mobility models only.

TensorPlot.description: Plots tensor data on the native tensor grid for the beam propagation method (BPM). Used to visualize complex refractive index, optical field, and optical intensity distributions.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.desc: Name of the tensor plot, appended to the base file name from File section.

parameters.name: Xmin/Xmax
parameters.desc: Minimum/maximum x-coordinate range in um.

parameters.name: Ymin/Ymax
parameters.desc: Minimum/maximum y-coordinate range in um.

parameters.name: Zmin/Zmax
parameters.desc: Minimum/maximum z-coordinate range in um.

parameters.name: OutputLevel
parameters.desc: Set to 'maximum' to generate plots after every iteration for bidirectional BPM.

Thermal.description: In the Math section, activates or configures thermal simulation options. Controls heat equation coupling, thermoelectric effects, and self-heating numerical settings.

ThermalConductivity.description: Specifies the thermal conductivity model for the lattice heat equation. Supports built-in models (ThinLayerKappa), temperature-dependent models, and user-defined PMI models including anisotropic thermal conductivity.

ThermalConductivityAniso.description: A plot variable that outputs the anisotropic thermal conductivity component (kappa_aniso) at each vertex, used when anisotropic thermal conductivity is enabled via the Temperature keyword in the Physics Aniso section.

ThermalizationYield.description: A plot variable that outputs the thermalization yield factors, representing the fraction of absorbed photon energy converted to heat rather than generating electron-hole pairs.

Thermionic.description: Activates the thermionic emission current model at heterointerfaces for carrier transport. The model computes current density based on thermionic emission theory across band edge discontinuities.

parameters.name: Organic_Gaussian
parameters.desc: Use Gaussian transport model for organic heterointerfaces instead of standard thermionic emission.

ThermionicEmission.description: A parameter set in the parameter file that defines coefficients for the thermionic emission model at specific region interfaces, controlling the emission velocity and current computation.

parameters.name: A
parameters.desc: Coefficient A for the thermionic emission model (dimensionless, default 2). Controls the emission velocity prefactor.

parameters.name: B
parameters.desc: Coefficient B for the thermionic emission model (dimensionless, default 4). Controls the energy flux coefficient.

parameters.name: C
parameters.desc: Coefficient C for the thermionic emission model (dimensionless, default 1). Controls the emission velocity formulation.

Thermode.description: Specifies thermal boundary conditions for lattice temperature at a contact. Supports fixed temperature, heat flux, and thermal contact resistance/conductance models.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.desc: Name of the thermode, or Regexp(<pattern>) for regular expression matching.

parameters.name: Temperature
parameters.desc: Contact temperature in K.

parameters.name: Power
parameters.desc: Heat flux boundary condition in W/cm^2.

parameters.name: SurfaceConductance
parameters.desc: Contact thermal conductance in W/(cm^2*K).

parameters.name: SurfaceResistance
parameters.desc: Contact thermal resistivity in (cm^2*K)/W.

parameters.name: AreaFactor
parameters.desc: Multiplier for heat fluxes (default 1).

Thermodynamic.description: Thermodynamic transport model that includes Peltier and Thomson effects in addition to Joule heating. Provides more accurate self-consistent electrothermal simulation by considering carrier energy flux and its coupling to the lattice thermal equation.

Thermoelectric.description: Specifies a PMI model for the semiconductor thermoelectric power, which couples carrier transport with the temperature gradient in the thermodynamic model.

parameters.name: pmi_model
parameters.desc: Name of the PMI model implementing the thermoelectric power computation. Must be a valid PMI_ThermoelectricPower model.

Theta.description: Specifies the polar angle theta (in degrees) of the incident optical excitation relative to the surface normal.

parameters.name: Theta
parameters.desc: Polar angle of incidence in degrees. 0 means normal incidence (perpendicular to surface). Positive values tilt the excitation direction.

Thickness.description: Specifies layer thickness parameters for thin-layer models, used in conjunction with thermal conductivity and mobility models that depend on layer geometry.

parameters.name: parameters
parameters.desc: Layer thickness extraction parameters that define how layer thickness is computed from the mesh geometry. Used by ThinLayerKappa and similar thin-layer dependent models.

ThinLayer.description: Thin-layer mobility model. Computes mobility degradation in thin semiconductor layers (e.g., ultrathin body SOI, FinFETs) by extracting the local layer thickness and applying surface scattering models. Can be combined with IALMob or Lombardi for interface scattering.

ThomsonHeat.description: A plot variable that outputs the Thomson plus Peltier heat generation rate, computed as -Jn*T*grad(Pn) - Jp*T*grad(Pp) where Pn and Pp are the thermoelectric powers for electrons and holes.
ThomsonHeat.example:
Plot {
  ThomsonHeat
  eJouleHeat
  hJouleHeat
  RecombinationHeat
  TotalHeat
}

Threads.description: Specifies the number of threads for parallel simulation, controlling thread usage for both matrix assembly and linear solver operations.

parameters.name: NumberOfThreads
parameters.desc: Number of threads to use. Set to 'maximum' to use all available processors. Also supports NumberOfAssemblyThreads and NumberOfSolverThreads for separate control.

Time.description: Specifies the time parameter for transient analysis or radiation generation timing. In transient simulations, sets the simulation time point. In radiation models (AlphaParticle, HeavyIon), defines the time at which charge generation peaks.

TimeDependence.description: Specifies the type of time dependency for optical generation in transient simulations. Controls how optical excitation varies with time during transient analysis.

To.signature: Transient ( TStart=<float> TStop=<float> ... ) | Quasistationary ( ... InitialStep=<float> MaxStep=<float> MinStep=<float> Goal { ... To=<float> } )
To.description: Specifies the end time for transient simulations or the target value for quasistationary ramp goals.

parameters.name: To
parameters.desc: End time value [s] for transient, or target voltage/current/power value for quasistationary Goal.

ToCurrentEnormal.description: A mobility model parameter set name in the parameter file that specifies mobility degradation parameters as a function of the electric field normal to the semiconductor-insulator interface.

Tolerance.signature: Scattering ( Tolerance=<float> ) | GMRES ( Tolerance=<float> ) | Cyclic ( Accuracy=<float> )
Tolerance.description: A convergence tolerance parameter used in multiple solver contexts: iterative scattering solver for optical TMM, GMRES linear solver, and cyclic steady-state analysis.

parameters.name: Tolerance
parameters.desc: Convergence tolerance value. In Scattering context: ratio of largest remaining intensity to total initial intensity for solver termination. In GMRES context: required residuum reduction. In Cyclic context: cyclic tolerance epsilon_cyc.

Tone.description: In Solve{HBCoupled(Tone(...))} and Math{HB{}}, defines a tone for harmonic balance analysis. Specifies the base frequency and the number of harmonics for multi-tone (MDFT) simulations. Multiple Tone statements define multi-tone excitation.

Top.description: A convenience keyword in the Excitation section that automatically configures illumination parameters for top-side illumination, setting appropriate excitation angles, window positions, and ray directions.

Total.signature: Aniso ( eMobilityFactor (Total) = <float> ) | Aniso ( hMobilityFactor (Total) = <float> ) | Aniso ( MetalResistivityFactor (Total) = <float> )
Total.description: A modifier keyword used with anisotropic mobility/resistivity factor specifications to indicate the total anisotropic factor applied to the mobility or resistivity.

parameters.name: Total
parameters.desc: Total anisotropic factor value applied as a scalar multiplier to mobility or resistivity.

TotalConcentration.description: A plot variable that outputs the total doping concentration (sum of donor and acceptor concentrations) at each vertex, corresponding to the TotalConcentration dataset read from the Grid file.

TotalCurrent.description: Total current density at each vertex, available as a plot dataset. Computed as the sum of electron and hole current densities plus displacement current. Units: A/cm^2.

TotalCurrentDensity.description: Scalar or vector plot dataset representing the magnitude or vector of total current density |Jn+Jp+JD| (A/cm^2). As a scalar dataset, it gives the absolute value at each vertex. As a vector dataset (Table 196), it gives the vector Jn+Jp+JD.

TotalHeat.description: Scalar plot dataset representing the sum of all heat generation terms in W/cm^3. Includes Joule heat, recombination heat, Thomson heat, and lattice heat sources (see Chapter 9 for thermodynamic model).

TotalInterfaceTrapConcentration.description: Scalar plot dataset representing the absolute value of net interface trap concentration (cm^-2). Unlike TotalTrapConcentration/RegionInterface which is stored as an interface dataset, this is stored as a bulk quantity with nonzero values only at interface vertices.

TotalRecombination.description: Scalar plot dataset representing the sum of all generation-recombination terms (cm^-3 s^-1). Positive values indicate net recombination, negative values indicate net generation. Covers SRH, Auger, radiative, band-to-band, avalanche, and trap-assisted mechanisms (see Chapter 16).

TotalTrapConcentration.description: Scalar plot dataset representing the absolute value of net bulk trap concentration (cm^-3). Sum of all trap entries at each vertex. When used with /RegionInterface, plots the absolute value of net interface trap concentration. Does not account for occupancy.
