TrStepRejectionFactor.description: Factor (frej) that controls time-step reduction when a transient step is rejected. If a time step is rejected, the new step size is multiplied by this factor.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Rejection factor for transient time-step reduction. Default is documented in Transient command section.

Trace.description: In the context of LED raytracing (DebugLEDRadiation and Print options), Trace is used as a print option to output ray paths to the TDR file. In the PMI ray-trace interface, it is a C++ class name for boundary condition customization.

contexts.LED RayTrace: Keyword in RayTrace Print option to output all ray paths to the .tdr file
contexts.PMI Optics: C++ class name PMI_RayTraceBoundary used in the physical model interface for customizing ray behavior at boundaries

TraceSource.description: Activates ray source tracing in LED raytracing simulations. Correlates output rays to their originating active vertices by accumulating ray intensity at each active vertex into an LED_TraceSource profile, identifying the best regions for current channeling to improve extraction efficiency.

TransferredElectronEffect.description: Activates the transferred electron model for high-field mobility saturation. This model accounts for the negative differential mobility effect in materials like GaN, GaAs, and InP where carrier velocity decreases at high electric fields.

TransferredElectronEffect2.description: Activates the alternative transferred electron model 2 (unified model combining two proposed models). Uses parameters mul, E0, E1, alpha, beta, gamma from the TransferredElectronEffect2 parameter set. Default parameters revert to the standard transferred electron model for most materials. Predefined parameters available for AlGaN and InGaN alloys.

TransferredElectronEffect2_MinDerivative.description: Sets a lower bound for the velocity derivative dv/dF_hfs (cm^2/V/s) for both electrons and holes in the Transferred Electron Effect 2 model. Prevents convergence problems when the derivative becomes negative.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Minimum allowed value of dv/dF_hfs in cm^2/V/s. Default: -1e100.

TransferredElectronEffect2_eMinDerivativ.description: Sets a lower bound for the electron velocity derivative dv/dF_hfs (cm^2/V/s) in the Transferred Electron Effect 2 model. Electron-only version of the MinDerivativePerField option.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Minimum allowed value of dv/dF_hfs for electrons in cm^2/V/s. Default: -1e100.

TransferredElectronEffect2_hMinDerivativ.description: Sets a lower bound for the hole velocity derivative dv/dF_hfs (cm^2/V/s) in the Transferred Electron Effect 2 model. Hole-only version of the MinDerivativePerField option.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Minimum allowed value of dv/dF_hfs for holes in cm^2/V/s. Default: -1e100.

Transient.description: Performs a transient time-domain simulation. The device must already have an initial solution. The simulation advances time from InitialTime to FinalTime, re-solving the device equations at each time step. Supports TRBDF (default) and backward Euler (BE) integration methods. Can also be used in a Quasistationary-like mode to ramp boundary conditions with Goal statements.

parameters.name: InitialTime
parameters.desc: Start time of the transient simulation [s]

parameters.name: FinalTime
parameters.desc: End time of the transient simulation [s]

parameters.name: InitialStep
parameters.desc: Initial time step size [s]

parameters.name: MaxStep
parameters.desc: Maximum allowed time step [s]

parameters.name: MinStep
parameters.desc: Minimum allowed time step [s]

parameters.name: Increment
parameters.desc: Multiplier for step size when the previous step converged (default: 2)

TransientDigits.description: Sets the relative accuracy for transient time-step control. Controls how precisely the transient integration method tracks the solution by specifying the number of significant digits required.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Number of significant digits for relative accuracy of transient time-step control. Default: 3.

TransientDigits.example: Transient ( InitialTime=0.0 FinalTime=1e-5 TransientDigits=4 ) { Coupled { Poisson Electron Hole } }

TransientErrRef.description: In the Math section, sets the reference value for absolute error control in transient time-step adaptation. When used with Charge, the reference value is the elementary charge (1.602e-19 C). Controls the local truncation error for transient step acceptance.

TransientError.description: Specifies the transient error tolerance for local truncation error (LTE) based time-step control. Activated by CheckTransientError in Math or Transient. Controls time-step adaptation by monitoring charge update error between successive steps.

Transition.description: Defines a transition between two states of a multistate configuration (MSC). Specifies the interacting state (From), the reference state (To), optional capture/emission PMI model (CEModel), particle conservation reservoirs, and field computation options.

Transmission.description: Enables the use of additional interface transmission coefficients in nonlocal tunneling models (eBarrierTunneling and hBarrierTunneling). When activated, transmission coefficients modify the tunneling probability at interfaces.

Transmittivity.description: Specifies the transmittivity (fraction of light transmitted) at a raytracing boundary condition. Used in both RayTraceBC sections (device-level) and Physics material/interface-based definitions.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Transmittivity value in range [0, 1]. Default: 0.

Transparent.description: Flag that controls which regions or materials allow nonlocal mesh lines to pass through. The part of a nonlocal line between a mesh vertex and the reference surface must NOT pass through regions flagged with -Transparent. By default, all regions are Transparent.

Transport.description: In the PMI (Physical Model Interface) context, Transport refers to the standard C++ interface for carrier transport models. It allows users to implement custom mobility, diffusivity, and velocity models via PMI.

TrapAssistedAuger.description: Activates the trap-assisted Auger (TAA) recombination model. TAA replaces the SRH lifetimes with carrier-density-dependent lifetimes, where the TAA coefficients Cn_TAA and Cp_TAA have units of cm^3/s. Default coefficients are 1e-12 cm^3/s for both electrons and holes.

TrapAssistedTunneling.description: Enables trap-assisted tunneling (TAT) within the SRH recombination model. Uses the Schenk model by default, with parameters including trap energy (Etrap=0 eV), coupling constant (S=3.5), and phonon energy (hbar*omega0=0.068 eV). Provides density correction (DenCorRef parameter) for numeric robustness and MinField parameter for smoothing at small electric fields.
TrapAssistedTunneling.example: Physics { Recombination ( SRH ( ElectricField ( Lifetime=Schenk TrapAssistedTunneling ) ) ) }

TrapConcentration.description: Plot quantity showing the separated full bulk trap concentration defined in each trap entry at each trap energy level. Available only in the CurrentPlot statement. TrapConcentrationPerEntry shows the integrated trap concentration per entry.

TrapDLN.description: Sets the number of discrete energy levels used to approximate trap energy distributions internally. Default is 13. A peak sampling method for Gaussian and Exponential distributions can be activated with TrapDLN(PeakSampling) syntax.

parameters.name: <int>
parameters.desc: Number of discrete levels for trap energy distribution approximation. Default: 13.

TrapFilling.description: Controls the explicit trap occupation mode. When set inside a Solve section, it forces traps to be explicitly occupied (fully filled or emptied) rather than computed self-consistently. The Unset(TrapFilling) statement reverts to standard self-consistent trap equations.

parameters.name: <value>
parameters.desc: Trap filling value controlling explicit trap occupation state.

TrapFilling.example: Solve { Set ( TrapFilling = 0 ) Coupled { Poisson Electron Hole } }

TrapVolume.description: Specifies the effective volume for a trap when modeling nonlocal tunneling to/from traps. Controls the spatial extent of the tunneling region around the trap location. Relevant for nonlocal tunneling from traps to conduction/valence bands at interfaces and contacts.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Effective trap volume parameter used in nonlocal tunneling calculations.

TrapVolume.example: Traps ( hNeutral Conc=1e15 fromMidBandGap EnergyMid=0 Level TrapVolume=1e-7 eBarrierTunneling(NonLocal="NLM1") )

TrappedCarDistrPlot.description: Plots the trapped carrier charge distribution at a specified position or over a specified location (material, region, etc.). Used to visualize trap occupancy and charge density.

parameters.name: <vector>
parameters.desc: Spatial coordinates (x y z) at which to evaluate trapped carrier charge.

parameters.name: Location
parameters.desc: Restrict the plot to a location (Table 362): Material, Region, MaterialInterface, RegionInterface, Everywhere, Semiconductor, Insulator, Window.

TrappedCarPlotFile.description: Specifies the base filename for output of trapped carrier charge density, trap occupancy probability, and trap density versus energy plots. Output file has .plt extension.

parameters.name: <string>
parameters.desc: Base name for the trapped carrier plot output file.

TrappedRadiative.description: Switch on trap-assisted radiative decay of singlet excitons associated with light emission in the singlet exciton equation.

Traps.description: Defines trap levels and their properties for recombination, trapping noise, degradation, and trap-assisted tunneling models. Specifies trap concentration, energy level, cross sections, and spatial distributions.

parameters.name: Damping
parameters.desc: Damping factor for traps in the nonlinear Poisson equation. A value of 0 disables damping. Default is 10.

parameters.name: RegionWiseAssembly
parameters.desc: Apply regionwise assembly for traps instead of global assembly.

contexts.Math: Global Math section keyword controlling trap assembly damping and regionwise assembly behavior.
contexts.Physics: Define trap-assisted recombination models and trap energy distributions per region or material.
contexts.Solve-Set: Set explicit trap occupation values via Set(Traps(...)).

Truncated.description: In the BPM optical solver, specify truncation parameters for plane wave excitation. Also used in avalanche generation for truncated element-vertex volumes.

contexts.Physics-Optics-BPM: Truncation parameters for plane wave excitation in beam propagation method (TruncationPositionX, TruncationPositionY, TruncationSlope).
contexts.Math: ElementVolumeAvalanche keyword uses truncated element-vertex volumes for avalanche generation.

TruncationPositionX.description: Specify the left and right truncation positions of a plane wave along the x-axis for BPM excitation.

parameters.name: TruncationPositionX
parameters.desc: Two float values defining the left and right truncation positions in micrometers along the x-axis.

TruncationPositionY.description: Specify the left and right truncation positions of a plane wave along the y-axis for BPM excitation.

parameters.name: TruncationPositionY
parameters.desc: Two float values defining the left and right truncation positions in micrometers along the y-axis.

TruncationSlope.description: Specify the plane-wave truncation slope vector for BPM excitation. The vector size is d-1 where d is the spatial dimension.

parameters.name: TruncationSlope
parameters.desc: Vector defining the truncation slope. Size is d-1 (one less than spatial dimension).

TtOM.description: Activate transient trap occupancy model for a given state in a multistate configuration (MSC). Used for dynamic trap filling behavior during transient simulations.
TtOM.example:
Physics(MSConfigs(MSConfig(
  Name="MSC1"
  State(Name="S1" Charge=0 TtOM)
  State(Name="S2" Charge=1)
)))

Tunneling.description: Activates tunneling-related physics models. Used as a top-level keyword to enable various tunneling mechanisms including Fowler-Nordheim, direct tunneling, and band-to-band tunneling.

contexts.Physics-Mobility: Tunneling correction to carrier mobility model.

TurnOffTreeNodeCount.description: Disable counting the total number of nodes in the raytree during LED raytracing simulation, reducing memory usage and computation time.

TurningPoints.description: Specifies time-step control parameters for optical generation in transient simulations. Defines limiting time steps for rise, plateau, and fall phases of time-dependent optical signals.

parameters.name: time_point
parameters.desc: A time point value in seconds.

parameters.name: dt_limit
parameters.desc: Maximum allowed time step at the corresponding time point, in seconds.

TwoBand.description: Use a two-band dispersion relation for tunneling probability calculation in barrier tunneling models. Also available in NonlocalPath for SRH trap-assisted tunneling.

contexts.Physics-eBarrierTunneling: Enable two-band dispersion for nonlocal tunneling probability.
contexts.Physics-hBarrierTunneling: Enable two-band dispersion for hole barrier tunneling.
contexts.Physics-Recombination-SRH-NonlocalPath: Enable two-band dispersion for trap-assisted tunneling transmission coefficient.

Type.description: A generic parameter name used in multiple sections to specify the type or model variant. Its meaning depends entirely on the parent section context.

contexts.Physics-Optics-BPM-Excitation: Type of optical excitation: Gaussian or PlaneWave.
contexts.Physics-Recombination-SRH-ElectricField-: Lifetime model type: Constant, Hurkx, or Schenk.
contexts.PMI: Data type specification for PMI numeric data.

UCS.description: Unified Coordinate System for anisotropic model computations. Provides a consistent reference frame for evaluating tensor-valued material properties (mobility, permittivity) in anisotropic regions of the device.

UniBo.description: University of Bologna model. Used in three contexts: as an impact ionization (avalanche) generation model, as a doping-dependent mobility model, and as a surface mobility degradation model at interfaces.

contexts.Physics-Recombination-Avalanche: University of Bologna impact ionization model for avalanche generation.
contexts.Physics-Mobility-DopingDependence: University of Bologna doping-dependent mobility degradation model.
contexts.Physics-Mobility-Enormal: University of Bologna surface mobility degradation model at semiconductor-insulator interfaces.

UniBo2.description: New University of Bologna impact ionization model for avalanche generation. An updated version of the UniBo model with refined parameterization.

Uniform.description: A generic keyword appearing in multiple contexts. In parameter randomization, it selects uniform spatial distribution for grain field generation. In Math, it can relate to doping variations. Its meaning depends entirely on the parent section.

contexts.Physics-GrainFieldGenerator: Distribute grain centers randomly with uniform spatial distribution.
contexts.Physics-MetalWorkfunction-Randomize: Sub-part of UniformDistribution for metal workfunction randomization.
contexts.Physics-Doping: Uniform doping profile variation in randomization models.

UniformDistribution.description: In metal workfunction randomization, attempt to use grains with a uniform size distributed uniformly across the metal region. The success depends heavily on the grid used in the metal region.

Unit.description: Specify the physical unit for a numeric parameter in the Math section. Used in parameter randomization and PMI model definitions to annotate quantities with their SI or CGS units.

parameters.name: Unit
parameters.desc: Physical unit string (e.g., cm, eV, V/cm, s).

contexts.Math: Unit annotation for numeric parameters.
contexts.Physics-ParameterRandomization: Unit specification for randomized parameter definitions.
contexts.Physics-PMI: Unit annotation in PMI parameter tables.

Unity.description: Set the quantum yield to unity (1.0), meaning every absorbed photon generates one electron-hole pair. This is one of the quantum yield model options for optical generation.

Unset.description: In Solve{}, unsets circuit nodes, disconnecting them from device electrodes. With TrapFilling option, reverts to standard trap equations (disables explicit trap occupation). Used in mixed-mode simulations for dynamic circuit topology changes.

parameters.name: ident
parameters.desc: Name of circuit node(s) to unset.

contexts.Solve: Unset circuit node voltages or trap filling mode.
contexts.System: Unset circuit nodes in System section for initialization.

Update.description: In Coupled or NewtonPlot contexts, requests that solution variable updates from the previous Newton step be written to diagnostic output files for convergence analysis.

contexts.Math-NewtonPlot: Write the updates of all solution variables from the previous Newton step to diagnostic files.
contexts.Solve-Coupled: Related to update error convergence control in Newton iterations.
contexts.Physics-FloatingGates: Update floating gate charge during simulation.

UpdateFactor.description: In the Incomplete Newton algorithm, specify the maximum allowed change in the update norm to permit reusing the old Jacobian matrix. If the update change exceeds this factor, the Jacobian is recomputed.

parameters.name: UpdateFactor
parameters.desc: Maximum allowed change in update norm for Jacobian reuse. Default is 0.1.

contexts.Math: Control parameter for the Incomplete Newton algorithm in the global Math section.
contexts.Solve-Coupled: Override Incomplete Newton parameters for a specific Coupled solve statement.

UpdateIncrease.description: Set the maximum allowed update error increase factor per Newton step. If the update error increases by more than this factor between consecutive Newton iterations, the step is considered diverging.

parameters.name: UpdateIncrease
parameters.desc: Maximum allowed multiplicative increase of update error per Newton step. Default is 1e100.

contexts.Math: Global convergence control for Newton iterations.
contexts.Solve-Coupled: Override for a specific Coupled solve statement.

UpdateMax.description: Set the maximum allowed update error in the Newton algorithm. If the update error exceeds this value, the Newton iteration is considered to have failed.

parameters.name: UpdateMax
parameters.desc: Maximum allowed update error. Default is 1e100.

contexts.Math: Global convergence control for Newton iterations.
contexts.Solve-Coupled: Override for a specific Coupled solve statement.

UpdateScale.description: Additional multiplicative factor for the update error in Newton convergence control. Used in relaxed Newton method, harmonic balance analysis, and related convergence checks.

parameters.name: UpdateScale
parameters.desc: Scaling factor applied to the update error. Scales the relaxed ANP error in AcceptNewtonParameter, or controls update scaling in HB/HBCoupled.

contexts.Math-AcceptNewtonParameter: Multiplies update error to give a relaxed ANP error in the relaxed Newton method.
contexts.Math-HB: Scaling of update in Newton for harmonic balance (MDFT and SDFT modes).
contexts.Solve-HBCoupled: Scaling of update in Newton for harmonic balance coupled solve.

UpsideDown.description: Include band-to-band tunneling with a nonphysical parallel momentum integral in barrier tunneling models. This option uses a simplified but nonphysical treatment of the parallel momentum.

UseNormalFlux.description: In raytracing sensor definitions, compute the projected-to-normal flux instead of the total flux on the sensor surface. This accounts for the angle of incidence of rays hitting the sensor.

UseQuasiFermi.description: Request that the gradient of the quasi-Fermi potential is used to compute the high-field saturation driving force within mesh elements touching a contact, instead of the default electrostatic potential method.

UseSchurSolver.description: Replace the default Blocked linear solver method with a specialized MPBC (multi-point boundary condition) solver for all Coupled solve statements. This improves robustness for periodic boundary condition simulations using the mortar PBC approach.

UserDefinedParameters.description: Path in the parameter tree where user-defined parameters added to optical profiles are stored. These are additional parameters that do not exist in Sentaurus Device, assigned to profiles for identification purposes.

UserRays.description: Option for the LED raytracing OutputLightToolsRays feature that uses a user-defined set of starting rays imported from a file via SourceRaysFromFile, instead of the internally generated geodesic or random distribution.

parameters.name: IsotropyType
parameters.desc: Selects the distribution type for starting rays: InBuilt (internal geodesic), Random, or UserRays (user-supplied file).

contexts.Physics: Sub-option of OutputLightToolsRays in LED raytracing context

UserWindow.description: Defines a user-defined window of rays for optical raytracing simulations. Allows importing custom ray directions from a file, setting the number of rays, and configuring polarization vectors. Used within Physics{Optics{OpticalSolver{Raytracing(...)}}} or Physics{Optics{OpticalSolver{FDTD{...}}}}.

parameters.name: Filename
parameters.desc: Path to the file containing the user-defined ray window data.

parameters.name: WindowType
parameters.desc: Type of ray window specification.

contexts.Physics: Sub-option of RayTracing for custom illumination window

VB.description: Valence band particle reservoir identifier used in multistate configuration (MSC) transitions. When a transition involves the VB reservoir, holes are exchanged with the valence band, contributing a charge of +1 per particle to the space charge.

parameters.name: Particles
parameters.desc: Number of holes exchanged per transition event (positive integer). A positive value means holes are consumed; the sign convention follows the reservoir charge.

contexts.Physics: MSC transition reservoir specifying valence band hole exchange
contexts.Analysis: Abbreviation for VB (valence band) in analysis mode curve names (e.g., noise_Vi_Vj)

VBBarrierLowering.description: Scalar plot dataset representing the barrier lowering value of the valence band edge computed by the nonlocal tunneling model. Visualized on the nonlocal mesh, this quantity shows the position-dependent inward shift of the valence band edge due to the BarrierLowering option.

VBEnergyWithLowering.description: Scalar plot dataset representing the valence band energy modified by the barrier lowering correction in the nonlocal tunneling model. Shows the valence band edge EV shifted inward toward midgap by the BarrierLowering model.

VBRate.description: Specifies a PMI model to compute hole capture and emission rates for the valence band in the trap capture and emission model. The optional integer argument is a model index passed to the PMI constructor for selecting parameter variants.

parameters.name: VBRate
parameters.desc: Name of the PMI model for valence band hole capture/emission rates, optionally with a constructor argument integer.

contexts.Physics: Sub-keyword of Traps for specifying valence band capture/emission PMI model

VCCS_pset.description: Declares a voltage-controlled current source (VCCS) instance in the System section for mixed-mode simulations. The output current from out+ to out- is equal to the transconductance times the voltage difference between in+ and in-. The default parameter set VCCS_pset provides standard SPICE VCCS parameters.

parameters.name: gain
parameters.desc: Transconductance gain in siemens (default: 1)

parameters.name: dc
parameters.desc: DC transfer value

parameters.name: ac
parameters.desc: AC transfer value for small-signal analysis

VCSEL.description: Enables vertical-cavity surface-emitting laser (VCSEL) simulation models. Configures optical cavity, mirror reflectivity, and stimulated emission parameters for VCSEL device analysis.

VCSELNearFieldPlot.description: Generates near-field optical intensity plots for VCSEL (Vertical-Cavity Surface-Emitting Laser) device simulations.

VCVS_pset.description: Declares a voltage-controlled voltage source (VCVS) instance in the System section for mixed-mode simulations. The output voltage between out+ and out- is equal to the gain times the voltage difference between in+ and in-. The default parameter set VCVS_pset provides standard SPICE VCVS parameters.

parameters.name: gain
parameters.desc: Voltage gain (default: 1)

parameters.name: dc
parameters.desc: DC transfer gain value

parameters.name: ac
parameters.desc: AC transfer gain for small-signal analysis

VacuumGridNodes.description: Specifies the number of vacuum grid nodes to be inserted between the physical simulation domain and the PML boundary on each side of the beam propagation method computational window.

parameters.name: VacuumGridNodes
parameters.desc: Number of vacuum grid nodes for the left and right sides of the boundary, respectively.

Vadapt.description: Sets the minimum voltage threshold that must be reached before the adaptive algorithm is activated in continuation (I-V curve tracing) simulations. Below Vadapt, the simulation proceeds as a simple voltage ramp through a fixed-value resistor.

parameters.name: Vadapt
parameters.desc: Lower voltage limit for adaptive algorithm in volts. Default is 0.0 V.

ValBand.description: Specifies that the valence band-edge difference should be used as the barrier type for singlet exciton flux injection across organic heterointerfaces, instead of the default bandgap difference.

contexts.Physics: Barrier type option in SingletExciton at region interfaces

ValenceBandEnergy.description: Scalar plot dataset representing the valence band energy EV at each vertex or element-vertex. This is a fundamental band structure quantity used in many physical models and available as both vertex and element-vertex located data.

Value.description: Target value for a parameter being ramped in a Quasistationary simulation. Used together with Parameter or ModelParameter in the Goal section to specify the endpoint of a parameter sweep.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.desc: The target value for the ramped parameter.

contexts.Physics: Modified parameter value in DeterministicVariation ParameterVariation
contexts.Solve: Target value in Goal section for quasistationary ramping

ValueMin.description: Specifies the allowed minimum value for positive solution variables in the time domain during harmonic balance analysis. Grid points violating the specified bounds are counted as corrections.

parameters.name: ValueMin
parameters.desc: Minimum allowed value for positive solution variables in the time domain.

contexts.Math: Harmonic balance convergence parameter in global Math section

ValueVariation.description: Specifies the allowed ratio of maximum to minimum values for positive solution variables in the time domain during harmonic balance analysis. Used together with ValueMin to enforce physical bounds on carrier densities and temperatures.

parameters.name: ValueVariation
parameters.desc: Maximum allowed ratio of maximum to minimum values for positive solution variables.

contexts.Math: Harmonic balance convergence parameter in global Math section

Var.description: Auxiliary floating-point parameter in the optical generation Select section that can be ramped in a Quasistationary statement. It provides additional flexibility for creating dynamic conditions in multidimensional spectrum selection without affecting other optical generation contributions.

parameters.name: Var
parameters.desc: Auxiliary parameter value (default 0). Can be referenced in the Condition expression and ramped via modelParameter.

VaryPhi.description: Sets the sensor sweep as a latitude ring (constant elevation, varying azimuthal angle phi) in the far-field raytracing analysis. The phi range must be specified with the Phi parameter.

parameters.name: Phi
parameters.desc: Range of azimuthal angle phi in degrees for the latitude ring.

VaryTheta.description: Sets the sensor sweep as a longitudinal ring (constant azimuth, varying polar angle theta) in the far-field raytracing analysis. The theta range must be specified with the Theta parameter.

parameters.name: Theta
parameters.desc: Range of polar angle theta in degrees for the longitudinal ring.

Vectorial.description: Requests vectorial data to be written to the current plot file. Refers to vector datasets defined in Table 196 such as current densities, electric fields, and carrier velocities.

parameters.name: Vectorial
parameters.desc: List of vector dataset names from Table 196 to be plotted to the current file.

Verbosity.description: Controls the verbosity level of optical solver output. Setting Verbosity=0 suppresses all optical solver logging except for warning and error messages.

parameters.name: Verbosity
parameters.desc: Verbosity level for optical solver output. 0 suppresses all output except warnings and errors. Default is 1.

Vertex.description: Mesh location identifier used in Physical Model Interface (PMI) to specify that data or computations are defined at mesh vertices. Vertex-based PMI models operate on vertex-centered data such as carrier densities, quasi-Fermi potentials, and band energies.

contexts.Physics: PMI data location specifier for vertex-based models

VertexIndex.description: Scalar plot dataset that outputs the vertex numbers (indices) of the device mesh. Useful for identifying specific mesh locations and correlating simulation results with mesh structure.

Voltage.signature: Voltage(absval=<float>, maxval=<float>, minval=<float>, contact=<string>, Node=<ident>, DevName=<ident>)
Voltage.description: In BreakCriteria{}, defines a voltage break criterion. The simulation stops when the voltage at a contact or circuit node exceeds the specified limits. Uses the same syntax as Current for specifying contact, node, and device identifiers.

VoltageGreenFunctions.description: Activates the computation of voltage Green's functions and their related responses at frequency zero in small-signal AC analysis. By default, voltage responses are deactivated at frequency zero; this keyword enables them when all parts of the structure are conductively coupled.

Volume.description: Specifies a named volume for physics model restriction. Used in BandEdge() within RandomizedVariation() to confine band-edge variations, in Conductivity() for metal conductivity variations, and in ExternalBoltzmannSolver() to restrict quantum corrections to a specific domain.

Vsource_pset.description: Defines a voltage source compact model instance in the System section for mixed-mode circuit simulations. Supports DC, piecewise-linear (PWL), pulse, sine, and exponential waveforms. The voltage source is connected between the positive node and the negative (ground) node.

parameters.name: dc
parameters.desc: DC voltage value [V]

parameters.name: sine
parameters.desc: Sine waveform parameters: (V_offset V_amplitude freq t_delay phase)

parameters.name: pulse
parameters.desc: Pulse waveform parameters: (V1 V2 t_delay t_rise t_fall t_on t_period)

parameters.name: pwl
parameters.desc: Piecewise-linear waveform: (t1 V1 t2 V2 ...)

parameters.name: ac
parameters.desc: AC magnitude for small-signal AC analysis [V]

parameters.name: temp
parameters.desc: Instance operating temperature [C]

WKB.description: Specifies the use of the WKB (Wentzel-Kramers-Brillouin) approximation for computing tunneling probabilities in the nonlocal tunneling model. This is the default method for barrier tunneling calculations.

Wait.description: Specifies that the simulation should wait until enough parallel licenses become available if there are insufficient parallel licenses at startup, rather than aborting or falling back to serial mode.

Wallclock.description: When specified in the Math section, Sentaurus Device reports wallclock times (elapsed real time) instead of CPU times after each simulation step. The sdevicestat post-processing tool recognizes this keyword and displays wallclock-based statistics accordingly.

WaveFunction.description: Requests visualization of wavefunctions computed by the 1D Schrodinger equation solver. Wavefunctions are defined on nonlocal meshes and can be restricted to a specified number of lowest electron or hole eigensolutions.

parameters.name: Electron
parameters.desc: Restrict output to electron eigensolutions. Optionally specify Number=<int> to limit to the lowest N solutions.

parameters.name: Hole
parameters.desc: Restrict output to hole eigensolutions. Optionally specify Number=<int> to limit to the lowest N solutions.

contexts.NonLocalPlot: Visualizes wavefunctions from the 1D Schrodinger solver on nonlocal meshes
contexts.Density Gradient Quantization Correction: Referenced in the context of quantum potential interpretation for confinement perpendicular to tunneling direction
contexts.Magnetic Direct Tunneling Model: Spinor wavefunctions used in the magnetic tunnel junction (MTJ) model instead of scalar wavefunctions

WavePeriods.description: Specifies the number of periods of a periodic optical signal. When used with WaveTPeriod, it defines how many times the signal repeats. If omitted, the signal repeats infinitely.

parameters.name: WavePeriods
parameters.desc: Number of periods of the periodic signal. Used together with WaveTPeriod to define a finite-duration periodic optical pulse.

WavePeriods.example:
OpticalGeneration (
  TimeDependence (
    WaveTime = (t1, t2)
    WaveTSlope = m
    WaveTPeriod = Tperiod
    WavePeriods = Nperiod
  )
)

WaveTCos.description: Defines the cosine rise and decay time constant for optical generation time dependency. When specified, a cosine-shaped rise and decay is applied outside the WaveTime interval.

parameters.name: WaveTCos
parameters.desc: Time constant t_cos in seconds defining the cosine-shaped rise and decay of the optical generation rate outside the WaveTime interval.

WaveTExp.description: Defines the exponential rise and decay time constant for optical generation time dependency. When specified, an exponential-shaped rise and decay is applied outside the WaveTime interval.

parameters.name: WaveTExp
parameters.desc: Time constant t_exp in seconds defining the exponential-shaped rise and decay of the optical generation rate outside the WaveTime interval.

WaveTLin.description: Defines the linear rise time for optical generation time dependency. It is the inverse of WaveTSlope, corresponding to the rise time t_lin = t1 - t0 in seconds.

parameters.name: WaveTLin
parameters.desc: Rise time in seconds for the linear time dependency. This is the inverse of the slope parameter WaveTSlope.

WaveTPeriod.description: Transforms the specified analytic time dependency into a periodic signal by defining its period in seconds. The signal repeats according to the specified period.

parameters.name: WaveTPeriod
parameters.desc: Period of the periodic signal in seconds. The specified time dependency is repeated at this interval.

WaveTPeriod.example:
OpticalGeneration (
  TimeDependence (
    WaveTime = (t1, t2)
    WaveTSlope = m
    WaveTPeriod = Tperiod
    WavePeriods = Nperiod
  )
)

WaveTPeriodOffset.description: Defines a temporal offset for periodic optical signals. Controls the relative location of the signal within the period. Only applies to linear and Gaussian time dependencies.

parameters.name: WaveTPeriodOffset
parameters.desc: Temporal offset in seconds that shifts the periodic signal within its period. Only effective for linear and Gaussian time dependency types.

WaveTPeriodOffset.example:
OpticalGeneration (
  TimeDependence (
    WaveTime = (t1, t2)
    WaveTSlope = m
    WaveTPeriod = Tperiod
    WaveTPeriodOffset = toffset
  )
)

WaveTSigma.description: Defines the Gaussian rise and decay time constant for optical generation time dependency. The standard deviation sigma controls the Gaussian-shaped rise and decay outside the WaveTime interval.

parameters.name: WaveTSigma
parameters.desc: Standard deviation sigma in seconds defining the Gaussian-shaped rise and decay of the optical generation rate outside the WaveTime interval.

WaveTSlope.description: Defines the linear rise and decay slope for optical generation time dependency. The slope characterizes the linear decay rate of the optical generation rate outside the WaveTime interval.

parameters.name: WaveTSlope
parameters.desc: Slope m in s^-1 characterizing the linear rise and decay of the optical generation rate outside the WaveTime interval. The inverse of WaveTLin.

WaveTime.description: Specifies the time interval (t_min, t_max) during which the optical generation rate is constant at its maximum value. Outside this interval, the generation rate rises from zero before t_min and decays after t_max according to the selected analytic time dependency.

parameters.name: WaveTime
parameters.desc: Time interval (t_min, t_max) in seconds. The optical generation rate is constant between these two times. If not specified, t1=t2=0 is assumed.

contexts.Physics/OpticalGeneration/TimeDependence: Defines the constant-generation plateau interval for transient optical pulse simulations
contexts.Solve: Referenced as a timing parameter in solve command contexts

Wavefunction.description: Alias for WaveFunction. Requests visualization of wavefunctions computed by the 1D Schrodinger equation solver on nonlocal meshes.

parameters.name: Electron
parameters.desc: Restrict output to electron eigensolutions.

parameters.name: Hole
parameters.desc: Restrict output to hole eigensolutions.

Waveguide.description: In the context of raytracing boundary conditions, a waveguide structure can be created inside the device by setting Reflectivity=1 on selected boundaries. This enables using symmetry to reduce the simulation domain for LED or optical device simulations.

Wavelength.description: Specifies a wavelength value in micrometers. Used in multiple contexts including StepFunction cutoff energy specification for optical absorption, monochromatic optical source definition, and as a parameter in optical generation computation.

parameters.name: Wavelength
parameters.desc: Wavelength value in micrometers (um).

contexts.Physics/OpticalGeneration/StepFunction: Used as cutoff energy for interband absorption in optical absorption heat or quantum yield models
contexts.Physics/OpticalGeneration/RecGenHeat: Specifies the wavelength if optical generation is loaded from file, for hydrodynamic heat generation

WavelengthDep.description: Activates wavelength dependency for the complex refractive index model. When specified with 'real', the refractive index depends on wavelength; with 'imag', the extinction coefficient depends on wavelength.

parameters.name: real
parameters.desc: Enable wavelength dependency of the refractive index (real part of the complex refractive index).

parameters.name: imag
parameters.desc: Enable wavelength dependency of the extinction coefficient (imaginary part of the complex refractive index).

contexts.ComplexRefractiveIndex: Selects wavelength dependency of refractive index and/or extinction coefficient
contexts.LED Optics: Used in LED raytracing with complex refractive index model

WavelengthDiscretization.description: Specifies the number of discrete wavelength bins used when outputting LED starting rays to LightTools format. The emission spectrum at each active cluster is divided into this many wavelength intervals.

parameters.name: WavelengthDiscretization
parameters.desc: Number of wavelength bins for spectrum discretization. The total number of starting rays equals WavelengthDiscretization * RaysPerCluster * NumberOfActiveClusters.

contexts.OutputLightToolsRays: Controls spectrum discretization for LightTools ray file output

WeightDielectric.description: Interpolation coefficient between two approximations of the dielectric term that arises from geometric variations. Used to calibrate the accuracy of geometric variation results, particularly for insulator position variations.

parameters.name: WeightDielectric
parameters.desc: Interpolation coefficient (default 0) between two approximations of the dielectric term. Values between 0 and 1 interpolate between the two available approximations.

contexts.RandomizedVariation/Geometric: Controls dielectric term approximation for random geometric variations
contexts.Noise/GeometricFluctuations: Controls dielectric term approximation for geometric noise fluctuations
contexts.DeterministicVariation/GeometricVariatio: Controls dielectric term approximation for deterministic geometric variations

WeightQuantumPotential.description: Interpolation coefficient between two approximations for the density-gradient quantum-correction term that arises from geometric variations. Used to calibrate accuracy when computing insulator position variations.

parameters.name: WeightQuantumPotential
parameters.desc: Interpolation coefficient (default 0.5) between two approximations for the density-gradient quantum-correction term. Values between 0 and 1 interpolate between the two available approximations.

contexts.RandomizedVariation/Geometric: Controls quantum potential term approximation for random geometric variations
contexts.Noise/GeometricFluctuations: Controls quantum potential term approximation for geometric noise fluctuations
contexts.DeterministicVariation/GeometricVariatio: Controls quantum potential term approximation for deterministic geometric variations

WeightedAPDIntegration.description: Activates weighted wireframe integration of absorption power density (APD) for improved accuracy of optical generation profiles. When enabled, a wireframe mesh is used to integrate the APD across layers and redistribute it to vertices using weighting factors.

parameters.name: PrintInfo
parameters.desc: Print diagnostic information about the wireframe integration.

parameters.name: NumberOfCellsPerLayer
parameters.desc: Number of cells per layer for wireframe discretization in the propagation direction (default 10000).

parameters.name: NumberOfTransverseCellsPerDirection
parameters.desc: Number of transverse cells per direction for discretization of the illumination window (default 200).

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.desc: Integration mode. Auto performs quick assessment; Full computes full transverse numeric integration everywhere.

WeightedAPDIntegrationSetBoundaries.description: Specifies explicit layer boundaries for weighted APD integration when using the FromFile optical solver. Boundaries are measured from the illumination window in the propagation direction and must be positive values.

parameters.name: WeightedAPDIntegrationSetBoundaries
parameters.desc: Comma-separated list of boundary positions in micrometers, measured from the illumination window in the propagation direction. Must be positive values.

WeightedAPDintegrationLayers.description: Specifies the number of layers for weighted APD integration when using the FromFile optical solver. Divides the total propagation length into equal layer thicknesses.

parameters.name: WeightedAPDIntegrationLayers
parameters.desc: Number of layers dividing the total propagation length into equal thicknesses. Default is the number of significant regions (regions with volume at least 10% of total device volume), limited to 20.

WeightedOpticalGeneration.description: Switches on weighted interpolation for distributing raytrace optical generation from elements to vertices. Instead of distributing optical absorption evenly onto all vertices of an element, a weighted approach assigns more generation to vertices closer to the ray center (midpoint of the ray with respect to optical absorption).

WeightedVoronoiBox.description: When specified in the Math section, Sentaurus Device reads squared radii of weighted vertices from a TDR file (computed by Sentaurus Process) and uses them to compute weighted box method coefficients and control volumes. This eliminates overlap control volumes for non-Delaunay meshes.

Weights.description: Specifies occupation probabilities (weights) for multiple lattice sites when using the Split option in IncompleteIonization. Each weight corresponds to a dopant redistribution fraction across available lattice sites.

parameters.name: Weights
parameters.desc: Space-separated list of occupation probabilities for each lattice site. The sum of all weights must equal 1. Each weight corresponds to a doping species split.

contexts.IncompleteIonization/Split: Defines occupation probabilities for dopant species distributed across multiple lattice sites

WellContactName.description: Specifies the name of a contact that defines a semiconductor well where the quasi-Fermi potential will be ramped during a Quasistationary simulation. Used as an alternative to DopingWell for wells connected to a contact.

parameters.name: WellContactName
parameters.desc: Name of the contact associated with the semiconductor well. The contact defines which well's quasi-Fermi potential is ramped.

West.description: Cardinal direction anchor for the illumination window origin. When used as OriginAnchor, it places the window origin at the west (left) edge of the excitation shape. Also used in LayerStackExtraction and TMM as WindowPosition for specifying the starting point of layer extraction.

parameters.name: West
parameters.desc: Cardinal direction specifying the west (left) position for window origin anchoring or layer stack extraction starting point.

contexts.Excitation/Window: OriginAnchor direction for illumination window positioning
contexts.OptBeam/LayerStackExtraction: WindowPosition cardinal direction for layer extraction line starting point
contexts.RayTraceBC/TMM/LayerStackExtraction: WindowPosition cardinal direction for TMM layer stack extraction

When.description: In Solve{}, defines conditional execution of solve commands. The enclosed statements are executed only when the associated condition (typically defined in BreakCriteria) is met. Used for adaptive simulation strategies and mode switching.

parameters.name: Contact
parameters.desc: Instance and contact name for the target. Instance name defaults to empty string for single-device simulation.

parameters.name: Current
parameters.desc: Target current value in Amperes.

parameters.name: Node
parameters.desc: Circuit node name for the target.

parameters.name: Voltage
parameters.desc: Target voltage value in Volts.

WidthDiscretization.description: Specifies the number of ray discretization points along the shorter side of a rectangular window for raytracing. Together with LengthDiscretization, determines the total number of starting rays.

parameters.name: WidthDiscretization
parameters.desc: Number of discretization points along the shorter side of the rectangular window. Total rays = LengthDiscretization x WidthDiscretization.

WidthDiscretization.example:
RayTracing(
  RectangularWindow(
    RectangleV1 = (0.0, 0.0)
    RectangleV2 = (2.0, 0.0)
    RectangleV3 = (2.0, 1.0)
    LengthDiscretization = 50
    WidthDiscretization = 25
    RayDirection = (0, 0, -1)
  )
)

WidthExtraction.description: Controls quantum well width extraction parameters for gain calculations and LED simulations. Specifies chord weight, minimum angles, and the materials or regions adjoining the quantum well.

parameters.name: SideRegion
parameters.desc: List of region names that define the sides of the quantum well, used when well sides do not coincide with domain boundaries.

parameters.name: SideMaterial
parameters.desc: List of material names that define the sides of the quantum well, used when well sides do not coincide with domain boundaries.

parameters.name: MinAngle
parameters.desc: Minimum angle constraints for thickness extraction algorithm.

parameters.name: ChordWeight
parameters.desc: Weight of chord length in the thickness extraction computation.

Window.description: In the Math section, defines a spatial window or region for localized numerical operations such as mesh refinement, plotting, or solver constraints.

parameters.name: Cuboid
parameters.desc: Defines a cuboid window by two diagonally opposite corners.

parameters.name: Cylinder
parameters.desc: Defines a cylindrical window by two center points of the circular bases and a radius.

parameters.name: Sphere
parameters.desc: Defines a spherical window by center point and radius.

WindowName.description: Name of an illumination window or ray distribution window, used to reference specific optical excitation regions when multiple windows exist.

contexts.OptBeam-LayerStackExtraction: References the illumination window in the Excitation section for TMM layer stack extraction
contexts.OptBeam-TMM-LayerStackExtraction: Same as OptBeam, used within TMM optical solver configuration
contexts.RayDistribution: Names the ray distribution window; no name sets the section as global

WindowPosition.description: Specifies the starting point of a layer stack extraction line in terms of a cardinal direction (North, South, East, West, Center, etc.) or a 2D point within the illumination window bounding box.

parameters.name: <ident>
parameters.desc: Cardinal direction such as North, South, East, West, NorthEast, SouthEast, Northwest, SouthWest, or Center.

parameters.name: (<float>, <float>)
parameters.desc: 2D point in the local coordinate system of the illumination window.

WithGeneration.description: Allows the Auger recombination model to produce negative net recombination rates (net Auger generation of electron-hole pairs) instead of clamping to zero.

WithJoyceDixon.description: Activates Fermi statistics using the old Wuensche approximation for Fermi integrals, instead of the default Joyce-Dixon approximation.

Wmax.description: Defines the upper energy boundary (in eV) of an interval for piecewise specification of energy-dependent parameters such as energy relaxation time, mobility, or Peltier coefficient in the parameter file.

parameters.name: <interval>
parameters.desc: Zero-based interval index. If Wmax(interval-1) < Wc < Wmax(interval), the corresponding formula is applied.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Maximum carrier energy in eV for this interval.

Workfunction.description: Specifies the metal workfunction value in eV for a contact. Can be a single value or position-dependent quadruplets (value, x, y, z).

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Metal workfunction in eV.

parameters.name: (<float> <float>*3)...
parameters.desc: Workfunction-position quadruplets: value in eV followed by x, y, z coordinates in um.

contexts.MetalWorkfunction: Sets the metal workfunction for contact boundary conditions
contexts.RandomizedVariation: Used within Workfunction() to define random workfunction variations for statistical analysis

WorkfunctionFluctuations.description: Activates random workfunction fluctuation noise analysis at contacts on insulators and metal-insulator interfaces for a specified surface.

parameters.name: <surface_name>
parameters.desc: Name of a surface defined in the Math section, consisting exclusively of contacts on insulators and metal-insulator interfaces.

WriteAll.description: Outputs information for all computed ionization integral paths to the log file, instead of only the path with the largest value.

WriteDOE.description: Writes cumulative simulation statistics to the output log file as design-of-experiments (DOE) variables, including Restarts, Iterations, AssemblyTime, SolveTime, and TotalTime.

parameters.name: DOE_prefix
parameters.desc: Optional prefix for the DOE variable names written to the log file.

Wurtzite.description: Specifies wurtzite crystal structure for the active region in LED simulations, enabling wurtzite-specific band structure, optical matrix element, and polarization calculations for materials like InGaN.

XConst.description: Fixes the x-coordinate to a constant value (in um) for extracting a 2D plane or 1D line from a 3D tensor plot on the native tensor grid.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: X-coordinate in um at which to slice the tensor plot.

XDirection.description: Specifies the direction vector of the x-axis for an illumination window's local coordinate system. Defaults to (1, 0, 0).

parameters.name: (<float>*3)
parameters.desc: 3D direction vector for the x-axis of the illumination window.

Xconst.description: Fixes the x-coordinate to a constant value (in um) for extracting a 2D plane or 1D line from a 3D tensor plot on the native tensor grid. Alias of XConst.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: X-coordinate in um at which to slice the tensor plot.

Xmax.description: Specifies the maximum x-coordinate (in um) of the domain for tensor plot visualization on the native tensor grid.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Maximum x-coordinate in um.

contexts.TensorPlot: Defines the maximum x boundary for tensor plot extraction
contexts.Math: Used in PeriodicBC Coordinates to define PBC application range

Xmin.description: Specifies the minimum x-coordinate (in um) of the domain for tensor plot visualization on the native tensor grid.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Minimum x-coordinate in um.

contexts.TensorPlot: Defines the minimum x boundary for tensor plot extraction
contexts.Math: Used in PeriodicBC Coordinates and illumination extent definitions

YConst.description: Fixes the y-coordinate to a constant value (in um) for extracting a 2D plane or 1D line from a 3D tensor plot on the native tensor grid.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Y-coordinate in um at which to slice the tensor plot.

YDirection.description: Specifies the direction vector of the y-axis for an illumination window's local coordinate system. Defaults to (0, 1, 0).

parameters.name: (<float>*3)
parameters.desc: 3D direction vector for the y-axis of the illumination window.

Yconst.description: Fixes the y-coordinate to a constant value (in um) for extracting a 2D plane or 1D line from a 3D tensor plot on the native tensor grid. Alias of YConst.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Y-coordinate in um at which to slice the tensor plot.

Ymax.description: Specifies the maximum y-coordinate (in um) of the domain for tensor plot visualization on the native tensor grid.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Maximum y-coordinate in um.

contexts.TensorPlot: Defines the maximum y boundary for tensor plot extraction
contexts.Math: Used in PeriodicBC and illumination window extent definitions

Ymin.description: Specifies the minimum y-coordinate (in um) of the domain for tensor plot visualization on the native tensor grid.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Minimum y-coordinate in um.

contexts.TensorPlot: Defines the minimum y boundary for tensor plot extraction
contexts.Math: Used in PeriodicBC and illumination window extent definitions

ZConst.description: Fixes the z-coordinate to a constant value (in um) for extracting a 2D plane or 1D line from a 3D tensor plot on the native tensor grid.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Z-coordinate in um at which to slice the tensor plot.

Zconst.description: Fixes the z-coordinate to a constant value (in um) for extracting a 2D plane or 1D line from a 3D tensor plot on the native tensor grid. Alias of ZConst.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Z-coordinate in um at which to slice the tensor plot.

ZincBlende.description: Specifies zincblende crystal structure for the active region in LED simulations. This is the default crystal type.

Zmax.description: Specifies the maximum z-coordinate (in um) of the domain for tensor plot visualization on the native tensor grid.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Maximum z-coordinate in um.

contexts.TensorPlot: Defines the maximum z boundary for tensor plot extraction
contexts.Math: Used in PeriodicBC and illumination window extent definitions

Zmin.description: Specifies the minimum z-coordinate (in um) of the domain for tensor plot visualization on the native tensor grid.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Minimum z-coordinate in um.

contexts.TensorPlot: Defines the minimum z boundary for tensor plot extraction
contexts.Math: Used in PeriodicBC and illumination window extent definitions

absorption.description: Optical absorption quantity (A) computed by the transfer matrix method (TMM) and added to the current plot file under the LayerStack dataset group for TE, TM, and mean polarizations.

ac.description: Performs small-signal AC analysis to compute frequency-dependent responses of the device. Results include current densities, voltages, and Green's functions at specified frequency points.

parameters.name: StartFrequency
parameters.desc: Lower frequency in Hz.

parameters.name: EndFrequency
parameters.desc: Upper frequency in Hz.

parameters.name: NumberOfPoints
parameters.desc: Number of frequency points for the analysis.

parameters.name: Decade
parameters.desc: Use logarithmic (decade) frequency intervals.

parameters.name: Linear
parameters.desc: Use linear frequency intervals.

parameters.name: Node
parameters.desc: Nodes for which to perform AC analysis.

activation.description: Activation energy (in eV) for thermally activated processes such as trap-assisted tunneling, hydrogen diffusion, and trap degradation models. Controls the Arrhenius-type temperature dependence of the process rate.

parameters.name: Ea
parameters.desc: Activation energy in eV.

adaptive.description: Controls adaptive nonlocal mesh generation for improved resolution of rapidly varying quantities such as barrier lowering potentials in tunneling models.

parameters.name: RefinedIntervals
parameters.desc: Characteristic length scale for adaptive mesh refinement in um.

af.description: Solution-dependent gamma factor parameter used in ferroelectric polarization and ferromagnetic PMI models to scale the coupling or polarization response based on the simulation solution state.

parameters.name: af
parameters.desc: Solution-dependent gamma factor scaling coefficient.

af.example:
# Used within PMI model parameter file
af = 1.0

anisotropic.description: Enable anisotropic Scharfetter-Gummel discretization for anisotropic physical models (e.g., piezoelectric mobility, stress-dependent thermal conductivity).

ballistic.signature: Mobility( BalMob ) / Mobility( eSubband(BalMob) ) / Mobility( hSubband(BalMob) )
ballistic.description: Enable ballistic mobility model. Options include simple ballistic, Fermi-Dirac correction, Frensley rule, kinetic energy formulation, and stress-induced ballistic mobility.

parameters.name: Fermi
parameters.description: Use Fermi-Dirac correction in ballistic mobility.

parameters.name: Frensley
parameters.description: Use Frensley rule for final mobility.

parameters.name: Lch=<float>
parameters.description: Explicit channel length in simple ballistic mobility (default 107 nm).

parameters.name: TempDep
parameters.description: Use temperature dependency in simple ballistic mobility.

band-to-band.description: Enable band-to-band tunneling models. Models: E1, E1_5, E2, Hurkx, modified Hurkx, Schenk, NonlocalPath. Options include density correction, Franz dispersion, and interface reflection.

parameters.name: FranzDispersion
parameters.description: Use Franz dispersion in the direct nonlocal path model.

parameters.name: InterfaceReflection
parameters.description: Consider interface reflection in the nonlocal path model.

parameters.name: ParameterSetName=(<string>...)
parameters.description: Named parameter sets to be used.

binary.description: In Mechanics(): specifies the Sentaurus Interconnect binary name. In LED raytracing: selects binary format for LightTools ray data output.

parameters.name: binary=<string>
parameters.description: (Mechanics) Name of the Sentaurus Interconnect binary.

parameters.name: SaveType=Binary
parameters.description: (LED) Choose binary format for LightTools ray data file.

break.description: Break criteria allow conditionally stopping a simulation when specified conditions are met. Available in Math, Quasistationary, Transient, and Continuation sections.

parameters.name: BreakAtIonIntegral( <int> <float> )
parameters.description: Terminate when the <int> largest ionization integrals exceed <float>.

break criterion.description: Defines conditions to conditionally stop a simulation. Available in Math (global), Quasistationary, Transient, and Continuation sections. Conditions include current, voltage, charge, and temperature limits.

parameters.name: Current=<float>
parameters.description: Stop when contact current exceeds this value.

parameters.name: Voltage=<float>
parameters.description: Stop when contact voltage exceeds this value.

parameters.name: Name=<string>
parameters.description: Contact or node name for the criterion.

cT_Range.description: Specifies the lower and upper limits for carrier temperature in hydrodynamic simulations. Default is 10 K to 80000 K.

parameters.name: cT_Range = (<float> <float>)
parameters.description: Lower and upper limit for carrier temperature in K. Default: (10 80000).

circuit.description: In mixed-mode simulation: (1) Load option to load circuit state, (2) Circuit equation in error control (Table 342), (3) CircuitNoise in ACCoupled, (4) node type in System section.

parameters.name: Load( Circuit )
parameters.description: Load circuit state from a Save file.

parameters.name: Circuit (error control)
parameters.description: Circuit equation with ErrRef=0.0258 V, Error=1e-3.

parameters.name: CircuitNoise
parameters.description: (ACCoupled) Compute noise from SPICE circuit elements.

circuit.example: Solve { Load ( Circuit ) Coupled { Poisson Electron Hole Circuit } }

collected.description: The collected modifier for File output keywords causes consecutive plot data to be written into a single file instead of several enumerated files. Applies to Plot, OpticalGenerationOutput, and SpectralPlot.

parameters.name: Plot (collected)=<string>
parameters.description: Write consecutive spatial plots into one .tdr file.

parameters.name: OpticalGenerationOutput (collected)=<string>
parameters.description: Write consecutive optical generation into one file.

parameters.name: SpectralPlot (collected)=<string>
parameters.description: Write spectral plots into one file (default).

command.description: Within Mechanics() physics section, specifies Sentaurus Interconnect Tcl commands to be executed by the external mechanics solver.

compound semiconductor.description: Compound semiconductors are specified using the MoleFraction section, which defines mole fractions (xFraction, yFraction) of ternary and quaternary compounds. Default model parameters are loaded from the parameter database.

parameters.name: RegionName=<string>
parameters.description: Regions where the mole-fraction specification takes effect.

conductivity.description: Thermal conductivity models for lattice heat transport, metal conductivity, and conductivity fluctuation noise. Models include constant, temperature-dependent, and PMI.

constant.signature: Recombination( ConstantCarrierGeneration( value=<float> ) ) / ThermalConductivity( Constant ) / HeatCapacity( Constant )
constant.description: The Constant keyword selects constant-value models: constant carrier generation, constant thermal conductivity, and constant heat capacity.

contact.description: Electrode specification for electrical boundary conditions. Supports voltage, current, charge, floating BCs. Options include Schottky, resistive contacts, workfunction, and recombination velocities. Contact is also an equation name in error control.

continue.description: Relates to continuation of quasistationary or transient simulations. The Continuation() solve command provides advanced continuation methods. Extrapolate helps continue from previous solutions.

parameters.name: Extrapolate( ... )
parameters.description: Use extrapolation to continue from previous solutions.

parameters.name: ReadExtrapolation
parameters.description: Try to use extrapolation information from a previous run.

continue.example: Solve { Quasistationary ( InitialStep=0.01 MaxStep=0.1 Extrapolate ) { Coupled { Poisson Electron Hole } } }

convergence.description: Controls the convergence criteria for Newton iterations in the Math section. The Digits parameter sets the approximate number of significant digits (default 5). Error sets absolute error tolerances per equation. ErrRef sets reference values for relative error control.

parameters.name: Digits
parameters.desc: Approximate number of digits to which equations must be solved. Default is 5. Sets epsilon_R = 10^(-Digits).

parameters.name: Error
parameters.desc: Absolute error parameter epsilon_A per equation (Poisson, Electron, Hole, Temperature, etc.).

parameters.name: ErrRef
parameters.desc: Reference value x_ref per equation for relative error control. Ensures numeric stability when solution variables are near zero.

parameters.name: Iterations
parameters.desc: Maximum number of Newton iterations. Default is 50.

parameters.name: NotDamped
parameters.desc: Number of iterations before Bank-Rose damping is activated. Default is 1000.

parameters.name: RhsAndUpdateConvergence
parameters.desc: Require both RHS norm and update error to converge before stopping Newton iterations.

corner1.description: Specifies the first corner point for defining geometric shapes such as cuboids, rectangles, and line sensors. Used in ComputeCarrierPath, Window, Sensor, and other geometry-related commands.

parameters.name: corner1
parameters.desc: First corner coordinates (x, y, z) or (x, y) defining one corner of the geometric shape.

corner2.description: Specifies the second corner point (diagonally opposite to corner1) for defining geometric shapes such as cuboids, rectangles, and sensor regions.

parameters.name: corner2
parameters.desc: Second corner coordinates (x, y, z) or (x, y) defining the diagonally opposite corner of the geometric shape.

corner3.description: Specifies a third corner point for defining rectangle sensors in raytracing, enabling arbitrarily oriented rectangular sensor planes in 3D.

parameters.name: corner3
parameters.desc: Third corner coordinates defining the orientation of the rectangle sensor plane.

correlation.description: Specifies the spatial correlation properties for random fields used in statistical impedance field method (sIFM) simulations. Controls how random variations are correlated across space.

parameters.name: CorrelationFunction
parameters.desc: Type of spatial correlation: Exponential, Gaussian, or Grain (grain-based).

parameters.name: Lambda
parameters.desc: Correlation length along main axes in micrometers.

parameters.name: AverageGrainSize
parameters.desc: Average grain size along main axes for grain-based correlations.

parameters.name: Resolution
parameters.desc: Spatial resolution for exponential and Gaussian randomization. Default is (0.25, 0.25, 0.25).

parameters.name: MaxInternalPoints
parameters.desc: Maximum number of points for Fourier transform. Default is 2147483647.

curLTImACGreenFunction.description: Scalar plot dataset for the imaginary part of the lattice temperature AC Green function current. Used in small-signal AC analysis noise simulations.

curLTReACGreenFunction.description: Scalar plot dataset for the real part of the lattice temperature AC Green function current. Used in small-signal AC analysis noise simulations.

d0.description: A normalization parameter used in various physical model interfaces (PMI), typically representing a reference doping concentration or a characteristic length/field value. The meaning is model-dependent.

parameters.name: d0
parameters.desc: Reference parameter whose meaning depends on the specific model context. In thermal conductivity PMI, it normalizes the doping-dependent factor.

dataname.description: Specifies the name of a dataset in solution variable definitions or ray data export files. Used to identify and reference specific data quantities within the simulation output.

parameters.name: dataname
parameters.desc: Identifier name for a dataset in output files or solution variable definitions.

dc.description: Specifies a DC voltage or current value for circuit sources in mixed-mode simulations. Used within Vsource or Isource definitions to set the DC operating point bias.

parameters.name: dc
parameters.desc: DC bias value in volts (for voltage sources) or amperes (for current sources).

debugging.description: Enables or controls debugging output for various simulation components including physical model interfaces (PMI), raytracing, and clustering features. Primarily a documentation/concept keyword rather than a direct command.

defect.description: Refers to defect-related quantities and parameters in Sentaurus Device. Defects include bulk traps, interface traps, grain-boundary traps, and KMC-MIM defects. They affect recombination, trapping, degradation, and transport properties.

degradation.description: Activates the trap degradation model based on kinetic equations (or power law) within the Traps specification. Models the time-dependent degradation of Si-H bonds under electrical and thermal stress, used for NBTI and HCI reliability simulations.

parameters.name: Degradation
parameters.desc: Enable degradation model based on kinetic equation.

parameters.name: Degradation(PowerLaw)
parameters.desc: Enable degradation model based on power law.

parameters.name: ActEnergy
parameters.desc: Equilibrium activation energy of hydrogen on Si-H bonds (epsilon_A0) in eV.

parameters.name: DePasCoef
parameters.desc: Depassivation coefficient nu_0 at passivation conditions in s^-1.

parameters.name: PasCoef
parameters.desc: Passivation coefficient gamma_0 in s^-1. Computed automatically by default.

parameters.name: BondConc
parameters.desc: Total silicon dangling bond concentration N_tot in cm^-d.

parameters.name: PasTemp
parameters.desc: Passivation temperature T_0 in K. Default is 300 K.

delAlamo.description: Selects the del Alamo bandgap narrowing model for computing the effective intrinsic carrier density. The del Alamo model provides a doping-dependent bandgap narrowing correction based on empirical fitting.

delta.description: A general-purpose parameter used across multiple physical model interfaces. Its meaning is context-dependent: in ComputeIonizationIntegrals it sets the electric field threshold below which elements are included in the breakdown analysis.

parameters.name: delta
parameters.desc: Context-dependent parameter. In breakdown analysis: electric field threshold (V/cm) relative to maximum field. In PMI models: model-specific parameter.

contexts.ComputeIonizationIntegrals: Electric field threshold in V/cm. All elements with field higher than Emax - delta are included in the initial element list.
contexts.PMI: Model-specific parameter used in various physical model interface implementations.

density of states.description: Refers to the effective density of states (DOS) for conduction and valence bands, which is a fundamental semiconductor parameter affecting carrier concentrations, mobilities, and recombination rates. Related plot datasets include eEffectiveStateDensity and hEffectiveStateDensity.

deterministic.description: Specifies deterministic (non-random) variations for device parameter sensitivity analysis. Unlike randomized variations, deterministic variations apply systematic changes such as doping gradients or geometric displacements to study their effects on device characteristics.

parameters.name: DopingVariation
parameters.desc: Name of doping variation with deterministic gradient or SFactor dataset.

parameters.name: GeometricVariation
parameters.desc: Name of geometric variation for deterministic interface displacement.

parameters.name: ParameterVariation
parameters.desc: Name of parameter variation for deterministic model parameter changes.

device instance.description: Refers to a specific device instance in mixed-mode or multi-device simulations. Device instances are named in the System section and referenced in Solve commands to specify which device to operate on.

parameters.name: Device
parameters.desc: Name of the device instance as defined in the System section.

diffusion.description: Specifies diffusion-related parameters in multiple contexts: hydrogen transport diffusion, KMC particle diffusion events, and PMI diffusivity models. Controls the transport of species through the device.

parameters.name: Diffusion
parameters.desc: In KMC: defines a diffusion event for a specified particle with activation energy and frequency.

parameters.name: Ea
parameters.desc: Activation energy for diffusion in eV.

parameters.name: Frequency
parameters.desc: Maximum rate (frequency) of diffusion attempts.

parameters.name: Diffusivity
parameters.desc: PMI model name for computing species diffusivity.

contexts.KMCDefects: Defines diffusion events for KMC-MIM particles with activation energy and attempt frequency.
contexts.HydrogenTransport: Specifies hydrogen species diffusivity through PMI models.
contexts.PMI: PMI model for computing position-dependent diffusivity.

direction.description: Specifies a directional vector for anisotropic models, optical excitation, ferroelectric polarization, stress analysis, and other direction-dependent physical models. The direction can be given as an explicit vector or predefined axis names.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.desc: Direction vector (x, y, z) or predefined axis name (xAxis, yAxis, zAxis). Can optionally use System_Coord for crystal coordinates.

contexts.Aniso: Direction for anisotropic avalanche generation and mobility.
contexts.FEPolarization: Direction of the ferroelectric polarization component being solved.
contexts.eQuantumPotential: Anisotropic direction for density gradient quantum correction.
contexts.EparallelToInterface: Direction of current for parallel electric field computation.

discretization.description: Refers to the numerical discretization scheme used for solving semiconductor equations. Sentaurus Device uses the finite element / box method for spatial discretization with options for different integration schemes and accuracy levels.

displacement.description: Specifies the displacement amplitude for geometric variations in deterministic or randomized variation simulations. Defines how much a surface or interface is displaced from its nominal position.

parameters.name: Amplitude
parameters.desc: Vectorial displacement amplitude in micrometers along each axis.

parameters.name: Amplitude_Iso
parameters.desc: Isotropic displacement amplitude in micrometers, applied equally in all directions.

parameters.name: Surface
parameters.desc: Name of the surface to which the displacement is applied.

dopant.description: Refers to dopant species and doping-related parameters in Sentaurus Device. Individual dopant concentrations (Boron, Phosphorus, Arsenic, Antimony, NDopant, PDopant) can be specified and are available as plot datasets. Doping variations are supported through noise and variation models.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.desc: Type of dopant for variation: Acceptor, Donor, or Doping (all).

parameters.name: BandgapNarrowing
parameters.desc: Include effect of doping variation on bandgap narrowing.

parameters.name: Mobility
parameters.desc: Include effect of doping variation on mobility.

doping variation.description: Specifies a deterministic or randomized variation of the doping profile. Can apply gradient-based, isotropic, or dataset-driven (SFactor) doping changes to study sensitivity to doping fluctuations.

parameters.name: Amplitude
parameters.desc: Vectorial amplitude for gradient specification in micrometers.

parameters.name: Amplitude_Iso
parameters.desc: Isotropic amplitude for uniform doping shift.

parameters.name: Conc
parameters.desc: Normalization concentration in cm^-3.

parameters.name: Factor
parameters.desc: Multiplier for variation. Default is 1.

parameters.name: SFactor
parameters.desc: Dataset name for spatial distribution of doping variation.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.desc: Apply to Acceptor, Donor, or Doping (auto-select by sign).

parameters.name: BandgapNarrowing
parameters.desc: Account for doping dependency of bandgap narrowing.

parameters.name: Mobility
parameters.desc: Account for doping dependency of mobility.

dx.description: Specifies the width or extent along the x-axis for illumination window shapes (Line and Rectangle). Defines the geometric extent of optical excitation regions.

parameters.name: Dx
parameters.desc: Width/extent along the x-direction of the illumination window in micrometers.

dy.description: Specifies the height or extent along the y-axis for rectangular illumination windows. Defines the vertical geometric extent of optical excitation regions.

parameters.name: Dy
parameters.desc: Height/extent along the y-direction of the illumination window in micrometers.

dz.description: Specifies the extent along the z-axis for illumination windows or other geometric definitions in 3D simulations.

parameters.name: dz
parameters.desc: Extent along the z-direction in micrometers.

eAlphaAvalanche.description: Scalar plot dataset for the electron impact ionization coefficient (alpha_n), representing the number of electron-hole pairs generated per unit distance by electrons. Used for visualizing avalanche breakdown behavior.

eAugerRecombination.description: Scalar plot dataset for the electron Auger recombination rate. Represents the recombination rate due to the Auger process for electrons, following the equation R_A = C * (n*p*(n*p - ni^2)).

eAvalanche.description: Electron impact ionization (avalanche) model within Avalanche() or Recombination(). Models carrier generation due to high-field impact ionization. Uses van Overstraeten-de Man, Chynoweth, or Selberherr models with user-definable coefficients.

eB2BGenWithinSelectedRegions.description: Restricts nonlocal path band-to-band tunneling electron generation to only those regions where the Band2Band model is active. When enabled, the electron generation rate from the dynamic nonlocal path model is computed only in selected regions, preventing unphysical generation in regions where the tunneling model should not apply.

eBand2BandGeneration.description: Scalar plot variable representing the electron band-to-band tunneling generation rate. This is the dynamic nonlocal path band-to-band tunneling generation rate for electrons, in units of cm^-3 s^-1. It can also be visualized through NonLocalPlot for data defined on nonlocal meshes.

eBandEdgeShift.description: Computes band-edge shift for the conduction band using a PMI (Physical Model Interface) model with an optional constructor argument. Used for apparent band-edge shift calculations in multistate configurations.

eBandTailDOS.signature: Physics { eBandTailDOS( Exponent | Gaussian | GaussianFromOrganic | TwoPopulation ) }
eBandTailDOS.description: Activates the electron band tail density-of-states (DOS) model. Band tails model localized states extending into the band gap from the conduction band. Available distribution shapes include Exponent (exponential tail), Gaussian, GaussianFromOrganic (organic material variant), and TwoPopulation (two-population model). Parameters such as Nt, sigmaDOS, and E0 are set in the BandTailDOS section of the parameter file.

parameters.name: GaussianFromOrganic
parameters.description: Gaussian shape as implemented for organic materials

parameters.name: TwoPopulation
