parameters.description: Two-population model combining two DOS distributions

eBarrierTunneling.description: Activates nonlocal tunneling for electrons at interfaces and contacts. Options include Band2Band tunneling (Simple/Full/UpsideDown), TwoBand dispersion, Schroedinger-based tunneling probability, Peltier heat, and interface transmission coefficients.

eBreakdownProbability.description: Scalar plot variable for the electron avalanche breakdown probability. This represents the probability that a carrier generated at a given position will trigger an avalanche multiplication event. The breakdown probability computation is controlled by the BreakdownProbability statement in the Physics section. For meaningful results, impact ionization-generated carriers should be excluded using AvalPostProcessing in the Math section.

eCDL1Lifetime.description: Scalar plot variable representing the electron coupled defect level 1 (CDL1) lifetime, denoted as tau_n1. This is the lifetime associated with the first coupled defect level in the CDL recombination model, in units of seconds (s). CDL recombination models traps with two energy levels that are coupled to each other.

eCDL2Lifetime.description: Scalar plot variable representing the electron coupled defect level 2 (CDL2) lifetime, denoted as tau_n2. This is the lifetime associated with the second coupled defect level in the CDL recombination model, in units of seconds (s). Together with eCDL1Lifetime, these describe the two-level coupled defect recombination dynamics.

eConstEmissionRate.description: Sets the constant emission rate term for electron emission from trap levels to the conduction band. This parameter, specified in the Traps section of the command file (or equivalently in the Traps parameter set), represents e_const^n with units of s^-1. The default value is 0 s^-1, meaning no additional constant emission contribution. Used in the trap-assisted recombination model to add a constant emission rate independent of carrier concentrations.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Constant electron emission rate in s^-1 (default: 0)

eCurrent.description: Electron current density at each vertex, available as a plot dataset. Represents the drift-diffusion current of electrons. Units: A/cm^2.

eCurrentDensity.description: Electron current density at each vertex, available as a plot dataset in Plot{} and CurrentPlot{} sections. Represents the electron component of the total drift-diffusion current. Units: A/cm^2.

eDeformationPotential.description: Scalar plot variable representing the change to the conduction band edge due to the deformation potential model. When strain is applied to the device, the deformation potential model computes shifts in the conduction and valence band edges. This variable shows the electron-specific band edge shift in units of eV. It is activated by specifying the DeformationPotential option in the Physics section.

eDensity.description: Electron concentration (density) at each vertex, available as a plot dataset. Represents the free electron carrier density computed from the solution of the electron continuity equation. Units: cm^-3.

eDensityCorrection.description: Activates the electron quantization model in the quantum well for the localized QW model. When enabled, the electron density in the quantum well is corrected to account for quantization effects, replacing the classical density with a quantum-mechanically computed distribution. This is used in LED and laser simulations where quantum wells are present.

eDifferentialGain.description: Scalar plot variable representing the electron differential gain coefficient, in units of cm^2. This quantity is related to the stimulated and spontaneous emission coefficients used in LED and laser diode simulations. The differential gain describes how the optical gain changes with carrier density.

eDiffusivity.description: Specifies the diffusivity mobility model for electrons, used in non-Einstein diffusivity calculations. When the Einstein relation does not hold (e.g., in short-channel devices with steep doping gradients), Sentaurus Device allows independent specification of diffusivities from mobilities. The diffusivity is expressed as D_n = kT * mu_n,diff, where mu_n,diff can use different parameters or models than the transport mobility. Options are the same as for HighFieldSaturation models.

parameters.name: ParameterSetName
parameters.description: Named parameter set for diffusivity mobility (allows different parameterization from transport mobility)

eDiffusivityMobility.description: Scalar plot variable representing the electron diffusivity mobility, mu_n,diff, in units of cm^2 V^-1 s^-1. This is the mobility used to compute the diffusivity when non-Einstein diffusivity is enabled (D_n = kT * mu_n,diff). When the Einstein relation holds, eDiffusivityMobility equals the standard electron mobility eMobility.

eDrForceRefDens.description: Sets the damping parameter for the electron high-field mobility driving force interpolation to zero field. This is an alias for RefDens_eGradQuasiFermi_Zero. It represents n_0 in the driving force formula F_hfs,n = n/(n+n_0) * |grad(Phi_n)|, which interpolates between the gradient of the quasi-Fermi potential and zero as the carrier density decreases. Default is 0 cm^-3.

eDriftVelocity.description: Scalar and vector plot variable representing the electron drift velocity in cm/s. As a scalar, it gives the magnitude of the electron drift velocity. As a vector (with /Vector suffix), it provides the direction and magnitude. The drift velocity is computed from the electron current density and carrier concentration.

eEffectiveField.description: Scalar plot variable representing the effective electric field for electrons, E_eff,n, in units of V/cm. The effective field is used in mobility models and accounts for the charge in the inversion/accumulation layer. It is computed as E_avg,n + (eta_e - 1/2) * q * n_sheet / epsilon, where E_avg,n is the average perpendicular field and n_sheet is the sheet carrier density.

eEffectiveStateDensity.description: Scalar plot variable representing the electron effective density-of-states, N_C, in units of cm^-3. This is the conduction band effective density-of-states, which depends on the effective mass and temperature. It can be modified by strain effects when the strained effective mass model is active.

eEffectiveStress.description: Scalar plot variable representing the effective stress for electrons in units of MPa. This quantity is used in piezoresistance mobility models to compute mobility degradation due to mechanical stress. The effective stress is derived from the stress tensor components applied to the device structure.

eEnormal.description: Scalar plot variable representing the electric field component perpendicular to the interface for electrons, F_perp or F_n_perp, in units of V/cm. This is the normal component of the electric field used in mobility degradation models (such as Lombardi surface mobility) that depend on the field perpendicular to the semiconductor-insulator interface.

eEparallel.description: Scalar plot variable representing the electric field parallel to the interface for electrons, F_n, in units of V/cm. This is the component of the electric field parallel to the nearest semiconductor-insulator interface, used as a driving force in high-field saturation mobility and avalanche generation models. It is also available as a PMI variable in the Physical Model Interface.

eEquilibriumDensity.description: Scalar plot variable representing the electron density at equilibrium (zero applied voltages, zero currents), denoted as n at equilibrium, in units of cm^-3. This shows the electron concentration that would exist in thermal equilibrium without any external bias, which is determined by the doping profile and intrinsic carrier concentration.

eFactor.description: Specifies the piezoresistance mobility factor model for electrons. The eFactor (and hFactor for holes) is used in piezoresistance mobility models to compute mobility degradation or enhancement due to mechanical stress. It supports named parameter sets and the auto-orientation framework, where parameter sets are automatically selected based on the surface orientation of the nearest interface.

parameters.name: ParameterSetName
parameters.description: Named parameter set for piezoresistance factor model

eFiegna.description: Fiegna hot-carrier injection model for electrons at interfaces. This model computes the hot-electron injection current density at contacts using the effective field approach. It is specified as an option within the GateCurrent section of the Physics statement. Options include CarrierTempDrive (use field from carrier temperature), CarrierTempPost (use postprocessed temperature), and Eparallel (use interface-parallel field, default).

parameters.name: CarrierTempDrive
parameters.description: Use field derived from carrier temperature

parameters.name: CarrierTempPost
parameters.description: Use field derived from postprocessed temperature

eGapStatesRecombination.description: Scalar plot variable representing the electron gap states recombination rate in units of cm^-3 s^-1. This is the recombination rate for electrons through gap states (traps) in the band gap, computed from the trap-assisted recombination model. It shows the contribution of trap levels to the overall recombination at each point in the device.

eGfactor.description: Sets the electron degeneracy factor for a trap level. The g-factor (g_e) accounts for the degeneracy of the trap energy level and affects the capture and emission rates in the trap-assisted recombination model. This parameter can be ramped in quasistationary simulations. The corresponding parameter for holes is hGfactor.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Electron degeneracy factor for the trap (dimensionless)

eGradQuasiFermi.description: Scalar and vector plot variable representing the magnitude of the gradient of the electron quasi-Fermi potential, |grad(Phi_n)|, in units of V/cm. As a scalar, it gives the magnitude; as a vector, it provides -grad(Phi_n). This is a key driving force used in high-field saturation mobility and avalanche generation models. It can also be used as a driving force option in the Avalanche section.

eHCSDegradation.description: Electron hot-carrier stress degradation model. Generates interface traps due to hot-carrier damage at semiconductor/insulator interfaces. Used within Physics{Traps()} at a MaterialInterface. Options include SHE distribution, bond dispersion, and energy interval discretization.

parameters.name: SHE
parameters.desc: Use the carrier distribution function from the spherical harmonics expansion (SHE) method instead of the approximate analytic option.

parameters.name: BondDispersion
parameters.desc: Enable bond dispersion integral to account for a distribution of activation energies (default: on). Use -BondDispersion to disable.

parameters.name: BDEnergyIntervals
parameters.desc: Number of energy intervals used in the bond dispersion integral (default: 20).

eHIDensity.description: Plot keyword for electron heterostructure interface density. Displays the density of electrons at heterointerfaces. Requires the /RegionInterface modifier in the Plot section.

eHIDensityPerDensity.description: Plot keyword for electron heterostructure interface density normalized by carrier density. Displays the ratio of electron interface density to total electron density at heterointerfaces. Requires the /RegionInterface modifier.

eHIDensityPerTemperature.description: Plot keyword for electron heterostructure interface density normalized by lattice temperature. Displays the ratio of electron interface density to temperature at heterointerfaces. Requires the /RegionInterface modifier.

eHIDensityPer_eTemperature.description: Plot keyword for electron heterostructure interface density normalized by electron temperature. Displays the ratio of electron interface density to electron carrier temperature at heterointerfaces. Requires the /RegionInterface modifier.

eHeatFlux.description: Plot keyword for the electron heat flux vector, Sn, from the hydrodynamic energy balance equation. Units: W/cm^2. Available as both scalar (magnitude) and vector data.

eHighFieldSaturation.description: Electron high-field saturation velocity model within Mobility() or eMobility(). Configures the saturation velocity and high-field dependence of electron mobility using Caughey-Thomas or TransferredElectronEffect models.

eInterfaceTrappedCharge.description: Plot keyword for the amount of electron trapped charge at interfaces in cm^-2. Has nonzero values only at interface vertices. Includes contributions from acceptor-type traps (eNeutral) and negative fixed charges.

eIonIntegral.description: Plot keyword for the electron ionization integral from approximate breakdown analysis. The best breakdown path is saved as a volume dataset in the existing TDR file. Used together with hIonIntegral and MeanIonIntegral.

eJfactor.description: Electron J-model factor (g_n^J) for trap capture and emission rates. Controls the mixing between the V-model (g=0, default) and the J-model (g=1) for electron capture rates. Values between 0 and 1 interpolate between the two models. Used within Traps() specification.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: The J-model factor for electrons, ranging from 0 (V-model, default) to 1 (J-model, popular for radiation problems).

eJfactor.example: Physics { Traps( (eNeutral eJfactor=0.5 eXsection=1e-15 Level EnergyMid=0.3 fromCondBand) ) }

eJouleHeat.description: Plot keyword for electron Joule heat, computed as |Jn|^2 / (qn*mu_n). Units: W/cm^3. Represents the ohmic heating due to electron current flow. Part of the thermodynamic model heat generation terms.

eLifetime.description: Plot keyword for the electron Shockley-Read-Hall lifetime (tau_n). Units: seconds. Can also be loaded from a file via the LifeTime keyword in the File section for spatially varying lifetime profiles.

eLucky.description: Classical lucky electron hot-carrier injection model for gate leakage. Computes the probability of electrons gaining sufficient energy to overcome the barrier at a semiconductor/insulator interface based on a Maxwellian energy distribution. Used within GateCurrent() at an interface.

parameters.name: CarrierTempDrive
parameters.desc: Use effective field derived from the hydrodynamic carrier temperature (requires Hydro and carrier temperature calculation).

parameters.name: CarrierTempPost
parameters.desc: Use effective field derived from a postprocessed carrier temperature estimate (drift-diffusion model with simplified energy balance).

parameters.name: Eparallel
parameters.desc: Use the electric field parallel to the interface as the effective field (default for hot carrier currents).

eMLDA.description: Activates the simple modified local-density approximation (MLDA) quantum correction model for electrons only. Uses a single thermal wavelength parameter (lambda) to compute effective quantum potential corrections. Can be specified regionwise or materialwise.

parameters.name: LambdaTemp
parameters.desc: Disable temperature dependence of the thermal wavelength. When disabled, lambda is held constant at the 300 K value.

eMLDAQuantumPotential.description: Plot keyword for the electron effective quantum correction potential from the simple MLDA model. Units: eV. Represents the quantum mechanical correction to the conduction band edge.

eMass.description: Enables strain-dependent effective mass and density-of-states (DOS) calculations for electrons. Activated as an argument to DOS() within Piezo(Model()). Uses deformation potentials and inverse six-band k.p method to compute strain-induced changes in electron effective mass and DOS. Calibrated for silicon, germanium, SiGe, and III-V materials.

eMinorityFactor.description: Minority carrier stress mobility factor for electrons. Provides an additional factor beta to scale the stress effect on minority carrier mobility differently from majority carrier mobility. Specified within Piezo(Model(Mobility())) in the Physics section.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: The minority carrier factor beta (default: 1, meaning same stress effect as majority carriers).

parameters.name: DopingThreshold
parameters.desc: Net doping threshold (cm^-3). When specified, the factor is applied based on whether the carrier is minority or majority at each point. Also switches off doping dependency of stress models where the factor is active.

eMobility.description: Electron mobility model within Mobility(). Configures all mobility sub-models (doping dependence, high-field saturation, etc.) specifically for electrons. Overrides generic Mobility() settings for the electron carrier type.

eMobilityAniso.description: Plot keyword for electron mobility along the anisotropic axis. Units: cm^2/(V*s). Visualizes the mobility component in the direction of anisotropy when the self-consistent anisotropic mobility model is used.

eMobilityAnisoFactor.description: Plot keyword for the electron anisotropy factor r_e, defined as the ratio of isotropic mobility to anisotropic mobility. Dimensionless. This factor is solved self-consistently at each vertex.

eMobilityAveraging.description: Controls the averaging scheme for electron mobility values across mesh elements. Element averaging (default) uses one value per element; ElementEdge averaging provides smaller discretization error, especially with the enhanced Lombardi model.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Averaging method: Element for element averaging (default), or ElementEdge for element-edge averaging.

eMobilityFactor.description: Plot keyword for the total anisotropic electron mobility enhancement factor. Dimensionless. Represents the ratio of the total mobility tensor magnitude to the unstressed isotropic mobility.

eMobilityStressFactorXX.description: Plot keyword for the XX component of the electron mobility stress enhancement tensor (mu/mu_0). Dimensionless. Part of the symmetric 3x3 mobility multiplication tensor that can be plotted at mesh nodes.

eMobilityStressFactorXY.description: Plot keyword for the XY component of the electron mobility stress enhancement tensor (mu/mu_0). Dimensionless. Shear component of the symmetric 3x3 mobility multiplication tensor.

eMobilityStressFactorXZ.description: Plot keyword for the XZ component of the electron mobility stress enhancement tensor (mu/mu_0). Dimensionless. Shear component of the symmetric 3x3 mobility multiplication tensor.

eMobilityStressFactorYY.description: Plot keyword for the YY component of the electron mobility stress enhancement tensor (mu/mu_0). Dimensionless. Diagonal component of the symmetric 3x3 mobility multiplication tensor.

eMobilityStressFactorYZ.description: Plot keyword for the YZ component of the electron mobility stress enhancement tensor (mu/mu_0). Dimensionless. Shear component of the symmetric 3x3 mobility multiplication tensor.

eMobilityStressFactorZZ.description: Plot keyword for the ZZ component of the electron mobility stress enhancement tensor (mu/mu_0). Dimensionless. Diagonal component of the symmetric 3x3 mobility multiplication tensor.

eMultiValley.description: Activates the multivalley electron transport model. Computes mobility using multiple conduction band valleys with valley-dependent populations and effective masses. The MLDA option combines with modified local-density approximation for quantum correction. Used for stress-dependent mobility simulations.

eMultivalley.description: Alias for eMultiValley. Activates the multivalley electron transport model for computing stress-dependent mobility using multiple conduction band valleys. This keyword is listed alongside eMultiValley with identical firstLocs and headings, indicating it is an alternative spelling recognized by Sentaurus Device.

eNLLTunnelingPeltierHeat.description: Scalar plot variable representing the Peltier heat generated in the conduction band due to nonlocal tunneling. Units are W/cm^3. This quantity is available regardless of whether it is accounted for in the temperature equation. The Peltier heat arises from energy exchange during electron tunneling processes at interfaces, contacts, and junctions.

contexts.Plot: Visualization of nonlocal tunneling Peltier heat at vertices in W/cm^3

eNMP.description: Extended nonradiative multiphonon (eNMP) model for trap-assisted tunneling and degradation. Specifies precursor concentration, spatial distribution via SFactor (PMI model or dataset), and number of random samples for statistical analysis.

parameters.name: eNMP_options
parameters.desc: Configuration options for the eNMP model including transition rate definitions, defect parameter randomization, and field-dependent hydrogen diffusivity.

contexts.Physics: Activate the eNMP degradation model for a region or interface
contexts.Plot: Plot eNMP-related quantities such as capture/emission times and interface charge states

eNMPCaptureTime.description: Scalar plot variable representing the electron capture time for the eNMP model. Computed as the first passage time from the eNMP transition rate equations. The capture time characterizes how quickly a defect traps an electron from the conduction band, which is essential for BTI and RTN analysis.

contexts.Plot: Visualization of eNMP carrier capture and emission time constants at vertices

eNMPEmissionTime.description: Scalar plot variable representing the electron emission time for the eNMP model. Computed as the first passage time from the eNMP transition rate equations. The emission time characterizes how quickly a defect releases a trapped electron back to the conduction band, relevant for charge recovery and noise analysis.

contexts.Plot: Visualization of eNMP carrier emission time constant at vertices

eNeutral.description: Specifies an electron-type neutral trap in the Traps section. An eNeutral trap is uncharged when unoccupied and carries the charge of one electron when fully occupied. This trap type is one of the five trap charge states (FixedCharge, Acceptor, eNeutral, Donor, hNeutral) used to model defect levels within the semiconductor band gap for SRH recombination and trapping analysis.

contexts.Physics/Traps: Define electron-neutral trap type for SRH recombination and trap-assisted tunneling models

eQCvanDort.description: Activates the van Dort quantum correction model for electrons. This is a simplified quantum correction that modifies the carrier density near semiconductor-insulator interfaces to account for quantum confinement effects. The model uses a smooth error-function-like correction based on a reference length parameter lambda_ref, which determines the distance from the interface up which the correction is relevant.

contexts.Physics: Activate the van Dort quantum correction for electrons or holes near interfaces

eQuantumPotential.description: Density gradient quantum correction model for electrons. Computes quantum-confined potential shift for electrons near semiconductor-insulator interfaces using the density gradient formalism. Supports anisotropic directions, boundary conditions, Gamma factor specification (EffectiveMass or PMI), local model coupling, and named parameter sets.

parameters.name: quantum_options
parameters.desc: Options for the density gradient model, including LocalModel specification for bandgap narrowing with incomplete ionization (e.g., IncompleteBGN_elec), and QuantumPotentialParameters for gamma factor control.

contexts.Physics: Activate electron quantum correction via density gradient model
contexts.Plot: Visualize quantum potential (Lambda_n) and related quantities such as eQDDGamma, eQMDensity, eMLDAQuantumPotential
contexts.Solve: Solve the quantum potential equation by including eQuantumPotential in the Coupled statement

eQuasiFermi.description: Electron quasi-Fermi potential at each vertex, available as a plot dataset. Represents the electrochemical potential of electrons, used as a reference in DopingWell{} ramping and convergence monitoring. Units: eV.

contexts.Physics: Set initial electron quasi-Fermi potential for a region
contexts.Quasistationary/Goal: Ramp electron quasi-Fermi potential in doping wells using DopingWell or WellContactName specification

parameters.name: float
parameters.desc: Initial quasi-Fermi potential value in volts for electrons.

eQuasiFermiEnergy.description: Scalar plot variable representing the electron quasi-Fermi energy level (EF,n) in eV. This is the energy level at which the probability of finding an electron state occupied is 0.5 under Fermi-Dirac statistics. It is related to but distinct from the quasi-Fermi potential: E_F,n = -q * Phi_n (with appropriate reference). Units are eV.

contexts.Plot: Visualization of electron and hole quasi-Fermi energy levels at vertices

eQuasiFermiPotential.description: Scalar plot variable representing the electron quasi-Fermi potential (Phi_n) in volts. This is the electrochemical potential for electrons, which measures the driving force for electron transport. In equilibrium, it is constant throughout the device. Under bias, its gradient determines the electron current density. It is also available as a solution-dependent data field for nonlocal PMI models.

contexts.Plot: Visualization of electron quasi-Fermi potential at vertices
contexts.PMI: Available as a solution-dependent scalar field for nonlocal PMI models via ReadScalar(vertex, "eQuasiFermiPotential")

eQuenching.description: Switches on quenching of singlet excitons due to free electrons in the singlet exciton equation. This is a nonradiative exciton destruction mechanism where free electrons interact with singlet excitons and cause their dissociation. Activated as an option of Recombination within the SingletExciton section of the Physics block.

contexts.Physics/SingletExciton: Activate electron-mediated singlet exciton quenching in organic/OLED simulations

eRecGenHeat.description: Scalar plot variable representing the electron generation-recombination heat source term (Hn) in W/cm^3. This quantity appears in the electron energy conservation equation (Equation 90) of the hydrodynamic model and represents the energy exchange between electrons and the lattice due to generation and recombination processes.

contexts.Plot: Visualization of electron generation-recombination heat at vertices
contexts.Physics: Must activate RecGenHeat in the Physics section to include these terms in the energy equations

eRecVelocity.description: Specifies the electron recombination velocity at an electrode contact. When set, Sentaurus Device applies a current boundary condition Jn_n = q*vn*(n - n0) at the contact instead of the default charge neutrality condition. Setting the velocity to zero (vn=0) makes the electrode define only the electrostatic potential, which is useful for SOI device simulations. Units are cm/s.

parameters.name: float
parameters.desc: Electron recombination velocity in cm/s. Default is 2.573e6 cm/s. Set to 0 for electrostatic-potential-only contacts.

contexts.Electrode: Set electron recombination velocity at an Ohmic or Schottky contact
contexts.Physics: Also appears as eRecVelocity in the Physics section with same semantics for interface boundary conditions

eRelativeEffectiveMass.description: Scalar plot variable representing the relative electron effective density-of-states mass (mn). This is a dimensionless quantity that characterizes the curvature of the conduction band and affects the electron effective density of states NC. It can be used in nonlocal PMI models as a solution-dependent field.

contexts.Plot: Visualization of electron effective mass at vertices
contexts.PMI: Available as a solution-dependent scalar field for nonlocal PMI models via ReadScalar(vertex, "eRelativeEffectiveMass")

eSHEAvalancheGeneration.description: Scalar plot variable representing the electron avalanche generation rate computed by the spherical harmonics expansion (SHE) method. This is the impact ionization rate Gn,SHE for electrons obtained from the SHE energy distribution function, in units of cm^-3 s^-1.

contexts.Plot: Visualization of electron avalanche generation rate from SHE method at vertices

eSHECurrentDensity.description: Vector plot variable representing the electron current density (Jn,SHE) computed by the spherical harmonics expansion (SHE) method. This is the macroscopic electron current density obtained from the SHE distribution function, in units of A/cm^2. Appending /Vector displays the vector field.

contexts.Plot: Visualization of electron current density from SHE method as a vector field at vertices

eSHEDensity.description: Scalar plot variable representing the electron density (nSHE) computed by the spherical harmonics expansion (SHE) method. This is the total electron density obtained by integrating the energy distribution function over all energies, in units of cm^-3.

contexts.Plot: Visualization of electron density from SHE method at vertices

eSHEDistribution.description: In Traps(), specifies the six parameters of the electron energy-dependent terms of the depassivation constant for the trap degradation model: delta_SHE, epsilon_th, epsilon_a, delta_perp, rho_perp, and rho_SHE. Used for HCS degradation modeling.

parameters.name: SHE_options
parameters.desc: Configuration options for the SHE computation including energy grid parameters and convergence criteria.

contexts.Physics: Activate SHE energy distribution computation for electrons in a specified region
contexts.Plot: Plot SHE-related quantities: eSHEDensity, eSHEEnergy, eSHECurrentDensity, eSHEVelocity, eSHEAvalancheGeneration
contexts.File: Define output filename for energy distribution plot via eSHEDistribution = "filename"

eSHEDistributionPlot.description: Plots the electron-energy distribution at specified spatial positions for the Spherical Harmonics Expansion Method (SHE). Used to visualize hot-carrier distribution.

parameters.name: <vector>
parameters.desc: Spatial coordinates (x y z) at which to evaluate the electron distribution function.

contexts.Plot: Visualization of position-dependent electron-energy distribution function as a special vector
contexts.File: Specify output filename for the distribution plot file

eSHEEnergy.description: Scalar plot variable representing the average electron energy (Tn,SHE) computed by the spherical harmonics expansion (SHE) method. This is the mean energy of the electron distribution function obtained from the SHE solution, expressed in Kelvin as an equivalent temperature. It characterizes how hot the electron population is relative to the lattice.

contexts.Plot: Visualization of average electron energy from SHE method at vertices

eSHEVelocity.description: Vector plot variable representing the average electron velocity (vn,SHE) computed by the spherical harmonics expansion (SHE) method. This is the macroscopic drift velocity obtained from the SHE distribution function, in units of cm/s. Appending /Vector displays the vector field.

contexts.Plot: Visualization of average electron velocity from SHE method as a vector field at vertices

eSRHRecombination.description: Scalar plot variable representing the electron recombination rate from the Shockley-Read-Hall (SRH) mechanism, including the dynamic nonlocal path trap-assisted tunneling enhancement. This is the electron component of the SRH recombination rate in units of cm^-3 s^-1. It captures both the standard SRH process and the nonlocal tunneling-assisted SRH recombination.

contexts.Plot: Visualization of electron and hole SRH recombination rates at vertices

eSaturationFactor.description: Specifies the saturation factor for electrons that controls the dependency of electron saturation velocity on mechanical stress. A value of 1.0 (default) means no modification. This factor scales how stress affects the saturation velocity in the piezoresistance mobility model, impacting current drive under strain conditions.

parameters.name: float
parameters.desc: Saturation factor for electrons. Default is 1.0 (no stress effect on saturation velocity).

contexts.Physics/Model/Mobility: Set electron saturation velocity stress dependence factor within the stress-dependent mobility model

eSaturationVelocity.description: Scalar plot variable representing the electron saturation velocity (vSat,n) in cm/s. This is the maximum drift velocity that electrons can achieve under high electric fields, as determined by the velocity saturation model. Units are cm/s.

contexts.Plot: Visualization of electron saturation velocity at vertices

eSaturationVelocityAniso.description: Scalar plot variable representing the anisotropic electron saturation velocity (aniso_vSat,n) in cm/s. This is the saturation velocity component that accounts for anisotropic mobility effects in the velocity saturation model, as defined in the Anisotropic Mobility framework.

contexts.Plot: Visualization of anisotropic electron saturation velocity at vertices

eSchenkBGN.description: Scalar plot variable representing the Schenk bandgap narrowing correction for electrons (-Lambda_n) in eV. The Schenk model provides a more physical bandgap narrowing calculation based on many-body theory, primarily calibrated for silicon. The negative quantum potential from the density gradient model is equivalent to the bandgap narrowing in this context. Units are eV.

contexts.Plot: Visualization of Schenk bandgap narrowing correction for electrons and holes at vertices

eSchenkTunnel.description: Scalar plot variable representing the electron direct tunneling current density from the Schenk tunneling model in A/cm^2. This model covers both direct tunneling and Fowler-Nordheim tunneling regimes for thin oxides (thinner than 3 nm), using a fully quantum-mechanical approach restricted to trapezoidal tunneling barriers. Optionally includes image force barrier reduction.

contexts.Plot: Visualization of electron direct tunneling current density at vertices
contexts.Physics: Activate via Physics(MaterialInterface="Silicon/Oxide") { GateCurrent(DirectTunneling) }

eSubband.description: Multivalley electron subband mobility model for stress simulations. Computes stress-induced electron mobility changes using subband structure calculations. Options control distribution function, effective mass, scattering models, and stress effects on drift-diffusion and ballistic mobility.

parameters.name: Doping
parameters.desc: Strain-induced subband model with doping dependency

parameters.name: Fermi
parameters.desc: Strain-induced subband model with carrier concentration (Fermi statistics) dependency

parameters.name: EffectiveMass
parameters.desc: Stress-induced change of electron effective mass

parameters.name: EffectiveMass(-Transport)
parameters.desc: Exclude 2D inverse transport mass tensor transformation

parameters.name: EffectiveMass(Transport-vector)
parameters.desc: Activate 1D inverse transport mass tensor transformation

parameters.name: Scattering
parameters.desc: Strain-induced subband model with bulk intervalley scattering

parameters.name: Scattering(MLDA)
parameters.desc: Strain-induced subband model with interface scattering (MLDA)

parameters.name: BalMob
parameters.desc: Stress-induced change of electron ballistic mobility

parameters.name: NoStressInDDmob
parameters.desc: Account for only ballistic mobility, ignoring stress in drift-diffusion mobility

parameters.name: EqRefMob
parameters.desc: Activate equilibrium reference mobility computation for transferred carrier model

parameters.name: -RelChDir110
parameters.desc: Use <100> channel direction in reference mobility

parameters.name: -AutoOrientation
parameters.desc: Use full tensor reference mobility

contexts.Physics/Model/Mobility: Activate electron strain-induced subband mobility model within stress-dependent framework

eTemperature.description: Electron temperature at each vertex, available as a plot dataset in hydrodynamic or thermodynamic transport simulations. Used as a convergence criterion parameter and in DiffusionNoise{} temperature options. Units: K.

contexts.Physics: Activate electron temperature equation within the hydrodynamic model
contexts.Solve/Coupled: Include eTemperature in the Coupled statement to actually solve the electron temperature equation
contexts.Plot: Visualize electron temperature, heat flux, and related quantities

eTemperatureRelaxationTime.description: Scalar plot variable representing the electron energy relaxation time (tau_en) in seconds. This is the characteristic time scale for energy exchange between electrons and the lattice in the hydrodynamic model, governing how quickly the electron temperature approaches the lattice temperature. Defined in Equation 87 of the Sentaurus Device manual.

contexts.Plot: Visualization of electron energy relaxation time at vertices

eTensor.description: Piezoresistive tensor for electrons, used to compute stress-dependent mobility enhancement within the Piezo section under Model(Mobility(...)). Specifies how the electron mobility tensor responds to mechanical stress through piezoresistive coefficients.

parameters.name: ident
parameters.desc: Name of PMI model to compute piezoresistive prefactors. If specified, the first-order piezoresistance tensor model is used.

parameters.name: AutoOrientation
parameters.desc: Use parameter set based on orientation of nearest interface instead of fixed orientation.

parameters.name: Enormal
parameters.desc: Use piezoresistive prefactors with dependency on electric field normal to interface and mole fraction.

parameters.name: FirstOrder
parameters.desc: Use the first-order piezoresistance model (default).

parameters.name: Kanda
parameters.desc: Include temperature and doping dependency in piezoresistance coefficients.

parameters.name: ParameterSetName
parameters.desc: Name of a predefined piezoresistance parameter set from the parameter file.

parameters.name: SecondOrder
parameters.desc: Use the second-order piezoresistance model for higher accuracy under large stress.

parameters.name: SFactor
parameters.desc: Dataset or PMI model name for the spatial distribution of the mobility enhancement factor.

eTensorMobilityFactorXX.description: Scalar plot data representing the XX component of the electron mobility stress factor tensor. This factor describes how electron mobility is modified by mechanical stress along the x-direction.

eTensorMobilityFactorYY.description: Scalar plot data representing the YY component of the electron mobility stress factor tensor, showing how electron mobility is modified by stress along the y-direction.

eTensorMobilityFactorZZ.description: Scalar plot data representing the ZZ component of the electron mobility stress factor tensor, showing how electron mobility is modified by stress along the z-direction.

eTensorMobilityXX.description: Scalar plot data representing the XX component of the electron piezoresistive mobility tensor (unit: cm^2/(Vs)). Shows the actual stressed electron mobility value along the x-direction.

eTensorMobilityYY.description: Scalar plot data representing the YY component of the electron piezoresistive mobility tensor (unit: cm^2/(Vs)). Shows the actual stressed electron mobility value along the y-direction.

eTensorMobilityZZ.description: Scalar plot data representing the ZZ component of the electron piezoresistive mobility tensor (unit: cm^2/(Vs)). Shows the actual stressed electron mobility value along the z-direction.

eThermionic.description: Activates thermionic emission model for electrons at interfaces and contacts. Enables current transport across barriers based on thermionic emission theory, applicable to conductive insulators, Schottky contacts, and organic heterointerfaces.

parameters.name: (HCI)
parameters.desc: Inject hot electrons locally at the interface using the hot-carrier injection model. The injected current acts as a source term for the electron continuity equation.

parameters.name: (Organic_Gaussian)
parameters.desc: Use a thermionic-like Gaussian emission model specifically designed for organic heterointerfaces, replacing the standard thermionic emission with a Gaussian transport model.

eThermoElectricPower.description: Scalar plot data for the electron thermoelectric power (Seebeck coefficient) Pn at each vertex. Describes the voltage generated per unit temperature difference for electrons (unit: V/K).

eTrappedCharge.description: Scalar plot data for the electron trapped charge density at each vertex (unit: cm^-3). Represents the net charge contribution from electron-occupied traps. Also available as an interface plot via /RegionInterface modifier.

eVelocity.description: Electron velocity vector at each vertex, available as a plot dataset. Represents the drift velocity of electrons under the applied electric field. Units: cm/s.

eXSection.description: Electron capture cross section for traps (unit: cm^2). Specifies the probability per unit area that an electron will be captured by a trap. Used within the Traps() section to define local capture and emission rates.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Electron capture cross section in cm^2. Default varies by material from parameter file.

e_h_Temperature.description: Specifies that noise temperatures for diffusion noise are taken as the respective carrier temperatures for electrons and holes, rather than the lattice temperature. Used within the Noise/DiffusionNoise() section.

eeDiffusionLNS.description: Scalar plot data for the electron-electron diffusion local noise source spectral density (unit: C^2 s^-1 cm^-1). Represents the noise contribution from electron diffusion processes.

eeFlickerGRLNS.description: Scalar plot data for the electron-electron flicker generation-recombination local noise source spectral density (unit: C^2 s^-1 cm^-1). Represents the noise contribution from flicker (1/f) noise in the electron GR process.

eeMonopolarGRLNS.description: Scalar plot data for the electron-electron monopolar generation-recombination local noise source spectral density (unit: C^2 s^-1 cm^-1). Represents the noise from the equivalent monopolar approximation of the GR process for electrons.

effective mass.description: Specifies the effective mass model used in the simulation. Can use a simplified Gaussian density-of-states model for organic semiconductors, or a PMI model for custom effective mass computation. Effective mass determines density of states, carrier concentrations, and transport properties.

parameters.name: (GaussianDOS)
parameters.desc: Use simplified Gaussian density-of-states model for organic semiconductors, which combines effective mass with band-tail effects.

parameters.name: ident
parameters.desc: Name of PMI model for effective mass. The model computes effective mass as a function of material parameters and conditions.

eg.description: Band gap energy Eg at each vertex (unit: eV). A fundamental semiconductor parameter that defines the energy difference between conduction and valence band edges. Available as a scalar plot dataset and used extensively in physical model equations.

ekp.description: Option for the Deformation Potential model to use the k*p (kappa-dot-p) method for electron bands. Accounts for shear strain components in the band structure deformation calculation, providing more accurate conduction band edge shifts under stress.

electrical.description: General term referring to electrical properties and boundary conditions in device simulation. In the SDevice command file context, it appears as part of various sections (Electrode, Solve, Device, Math) related to electrical simulation setup. Not a standalone keyword but a qualifier used in composite keywords.

electrode.description: Defines electrical boundary conditions for device contacts including voltage, current, charge, workfunction, resistance, and Schottky parameters. Each electrode specification can be restricted to a specific named contact.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.desc: Name of the electrode/contact. Can use Regexp for pattern matching.

parameters.name: Voltage
parameters.desc: Contact voltage in V. Can specify transient behavior with At clauses.

parameters.name: Current
parameters.desc: Current boundary condition in A/um^(d-3). Voltage is used as initial guess only.

parameters.name: Charge
parameters.desc: Charge for floating electrode in C. Voltage must not be specified.

parameters.name: Workfunction
parameters.desc: Electrode workfunction in eV.

parameters.name: Schottky
parameters.desc: Set contact as Schottky contact.

parameters.name: Resist
parameters.desc: Contact resistance in Ohm*um^(3-d).

parameters.name: DistResist
parameters.desc: Distributed resistance in Ohm*cm^2.

parameters.name: Material
parameters.desc: Electrode material name, optionally with doping specification.

parameters.name: Poisson
parameters.desc: Boundary condition type: Dirichlet (default) or Neumann for Poisson equation.

parameters.name: Barrier
parameters.desc: Barrier voltage in V.

parameters.name: eRecVelocity
parameters.desc: Electron recombination velocity in cm/s.

parameters.name: hRecVelocity
parameters.desc: Hole recombination velocity in cm/s.

parameters.name: AreaFactor
parameters.desc: Multiplier for electrode current.

parameters.name: Extraction
parameters.desc: Specify electrode role for extraction: bulk, drain, gate, or source.

electron.description: General term referring to electron-related properties in device simulation. In SDevice context, appears as a carrier type qualifier in physics models, noise analysis, recombination, and transport equations. Not a standalone keyword.

element.description: Mesh element in the finite element discretization. Elements are the basic geometric units (triangles in 2D, tetrahedra in 3D) used for spatial discretization of the device. Certain plot datasets can be defined on elements rather than vertices.

else.description: Tcl conditional keyword used in SDevice command files for control flow. Not a device physics keyword but a standard Tcl language construct used in if-else-elseif conditional statements within the command file.

emission.description: General term for carrier emission processes in trap models and interface transport. Refers to the release of carriers (electrons or holes) from trap states into the conduction or valence band. Not a standalone keyword but a concept used in trap capture/emission rate calculations.

energy distribution.description: Specifies the region where the carrier energy distribution function is calculated using the Spherical Harmonics Expansion (SHE) method. Enables hot-carrier effects modeling by solving the Boltzmann transport equation in energy space.

parameters.name: Table 300
parameters.desc: Options for SHE distribution computation including energy grid parameters and solver settings.

energy level.description: General term referring to the energy levels of trap states within the semiconductor band gap. In the SDevice context, trap energy levels are specified through the EnergyMid parameter in Traps() specifications and can be visualized through trapped charge plots.

equilibrium.description: Specifies parameters for the equilibrium solution used as the initial guess for subsequent simulations. The equilibrium solution is computed at zero applied voltages and zero currents, providing a self-consistent starting point for bias-dependent simulations.

parameters.name: Table 217
parameters.desc: Options controlling the equilibrium solution computation including convergence criteria and method selection.

error.description: Specifies the error tolerance value for equation convergence in the Newton solver. Controls the accuracy to which each equation must be solved. This is a key parameter in both the Math section and Coupled() statements.

parameters.name: Table 342
parameters.desc: Equation name (e.g., Poisson, Electron, Hole, Temperature) to set error tolerance for.

parameters.name: float
parameters.desc: Value of the absolute error parameter epsilon_A for the specified equation.

error control.description: General concept referring to the convergence error control mechanism in the Newton solver. Includes absolute error (Error), reference error (ErrRef), RHS error, and update error control. Managed through multiple Math section keywords.

exciton.description: Activates the singlet exciton equation for organic device simulation, modeling diffusion, generation from bimolecular recombination, decay, and optical emission of singlet excitons (Frenkel excitons). Only relevant for organic materials.

parameters.name: BarrierType
parameters.desc: Sets the barrier type at organic heterointerfaces: Bandgap, CondBand, or ValBand.

parameters.name: FluxBC
parameters.desc: Imposes a zero flux boundary condition on specified interface.

parameters.name: Recombination
parameters.desc: Switches on generation and recombination terms in the singlet exciton equation (Bimolecular, Dissociation, eQuenching, hQuenching, radiative, trappedradiative).

exp.description: Exponential function available in PMI (Physical Model Interface) expressions. Computes e raised to the power of x. Also used in Tcl math expressions within SDevice command files.

parameters.name: x
parameters.desc: The exponent argument.

export.description: Exports ray data in LightTools-compatible format for far-field or LED starting rays. Used in LED(Optics(RayTrace())) sections to interface simulation rays with external optical design tools.

parameters.name: Filename
parameters.desc: Base file name of the LightTools ray data file to be output.

parameters.name: SaveType
parameters.desc: File format: ASCII (.txt) or binary (.ray).

parameters.name: WavelengthDiscretization
parameters.desc: Number of discretization points for the spectrum.

contexts.LED(Optics(RayTrace())): Export far-field rays to LightTools format
contexts.LED(Optics(RayTrace(Disable()))): Export LED starting rays to LightTools format

expr.description: Tcl expression used in CurrentPlot Tcl blocks to compute custom derived quantities from simulation fields. Uses Tcl's expr command with access to simulation data via tcl_lp_ReadScalar and similar functions.

parameters.name: Formula
parameters.desc: Tcl code executed on each mesh vertex to compute a custom quantity.

extraction.description: Defines process parameters for extraction file output. Available in Device, Electrode, and Solve/ACCoupled sections. Specifies which quantities to extract during simulation for post-processing.

parameters.name: <ident>
parameters.desc: Name of the extraction quantity to track.

contexts.Device: Device-level extraction parameters written to extraction file
contexts.Electrode: Specify electrode role for extraction (bulk, drain, gate, source)
contexts.Solve/ACCoupled: Frequency-dependent extraction curves for AC analysis

f1BandOccupancy001.description: Scalar plot data representing the occupancy of the first ellipsoid in the (001) crystallographic plane for the hSixBand multivalley model. Used with the Intel mobility model.

f1BandOccupancy010.description: Scalar plot data representing the occupancy of the first ellipsoid in the (010) crystallographic plane for the hSixBand multivalley model.

f1BandOccupancy100.description: Scalar plot data representing the occupancy of the first ellipsoid in the (100) crystallographic plane for the hSixBand multivalley model.

f2BandOccupancy001.description: Scalar plot data representing the occupancy of the second ellipsoid in the (001) crystallographic plane for the hSixBand multivalley model.

f2BandOccupancy010.description: Scalar plot data representing the occupancy of the second ellipsoid in the (010) crystallographic plane for the hSixBand multivalley model.

f2BandOccupancy100.description: Scalar plot data representing the occupancy of the second ellipsoid in the (100) crystallographic plane for the hSixBand multivalley model.

far field.description: Activates the far-field and sensor feature for raytracing in optical simulations. Computes the angular radiation pattern and power distribution emitted from the device.

parameters.name: Farfield
parameters.desc: Contains sensor definitions (Angular, Line, Rectangle) and sensor sweep parameters for far-field computation.

parameters.name: Sensor
parameters.desc: Defines sensor geometry: Angular (phi/theta ranges), Line (two corners), or Rectangle (up to three corners).

parameters.name: SensorSweep
parameters.desc: Defines sensor sweep as latitude ring (VaryPhi) or longitudinal ring (VaryTheta) with Ndivisions.

fc.signature: FEPolarization { alpha=<float> beta=<float> gamma=<float> g=<float> rho=<float> }
fc.description: One of the Ginzburg-Landau-Khalatnikov (GLK) equation parameters for ferroelectric polarization simulation. The parameter fc does not appear as a standalone keyword; it likely refers to the coupling coefficient or other GLK sub-parameters.

file.description: Specifies input and output file names for the simulation, including grid files, parameter files, plot files, current files, extraction files, and various specialized output files. Supports both global and device-specific sections.

parameters.name: Grid
parameters.desc: Device geometry and mesh input file (.tdr).

parameters.name: Parameters
parameters.desc: Device parameters input file (.par).

parameters.name: Plot
parameters.desc: Spatially distributed simulation results output file (.tdr).

parameters.name: Current
parameters.desc: Device currents and voltages output file (.plt).

parameters.name: Output
parameters.desc: Runtime log file (.log).

parameters.name: Extraction
parameters.desc: Extraction file output (.xtr).

parameters.name: Gain
parameters.desc: Modal gain spectra output file for LED simulation.

parameters.name: PMIPath
parameters.desc: Search path for PMI shared object files.

parameters.name: IlluminationSpectrum
parameters.desc: Illumination spectrum input file for optical generation.

file.example:
File {
  Grid = "nmos_msh.tdr"
  Parameters = "nmos.par"
  Plot = "nmos_des.tdr"
  Current = "nmos_des.plt"
  Output = "nmos_des.log"
}

fixedCharge.description: Specifies a fixed charge trap type within the Traps section. FixedCharge traps contribute a constant charge density (sign determined by Conc sign) but do not participate in trapping/detrapping dynamics. Used for interface fixed charges and polarization sheet charges.

parameters.name: Conc
parameters.desc: Concentration of fixed charges in cm^-3 (region) or cm^-2 (interface). Negative sign denotes negative fixed charges.

parameters.name: Acceptor
parameters.desc: Specifies acceptor-type fixed charge (negative charge).

parameters.name: Donor
parameters.desc: Specifies donor-type fixed charge (positive charge).

contexts.Traps: Fixed charge specification within trap definitions
contexts.Degradation: Used with eHCSDegradation/hHCSDegradation models for degradation-generated fixed charges

for.description: Standard Tcl loop control command. Not an SDevice-specific keyword, but a Tcl language construct used in SDevice command files for repetitive operations and parameter sweeps.

parameters.name: start
parameters.desc: Initialization expression executed once before the loop.

parameters.name: test
parameters.desc: Boolean test expression evaluated before each iteration.

parameters.name: increment
parameters.desc: Increment expression executed after each iteration.

format.description: Specifies the output file format for various data export operations, such as LightTools ray files (ASCII or Binary) or illumination spectrum file format.

parameters.name: SaveType
parameters.desc: Format for LightTools ray export files.

formula.description: Specifies a Tcl expression to be executed on each mesh vertex for computing custom derived quantities in current plot output. Has access to simulation data via tcl_lp_ReadScalar and related functions.

parameters.name: Formula
parameters.desc: Tcl code string executed per vertex. Can use expr, tcl_lp_ReadScalar, tcl_lp_ReadVector, and standard Tcl commands.

contexts.CurrentPlot/Tcl: Compute custom quantities from simulation fields

freq.description: Specifies the frequency parameter for harmonic balance (HB) and AC analysis. Defines the fundamental excitation frequency for periodic or quasi-periodic simulations, used in HBCoupled statements and circuit source definitions (e.g., sine = (vo va freq ...)).

freq2.description: Specifies the second tone frequency for two-tone harmonic balance analysis. Used in voltage source definitions within the System section for multi-tone HB simulations.

parameters.name: freq2
parameters.desc: Frequency of the second tone in Hz.

parameters.name: mag2
parameters.desc: Magnitude of the second tone signal.

parameters.name: phase2
parameters.desc: Phase of the second tone signal in degrees.

fromBottom.description: Convenience keyword that sets default excitation parameters for illumination from the bottom of the device structure. Automatically configures Theta, Origin, XDirection, and Window type based on the coordinate system (DF-ISE or UCS).

fromCondBand.description: Specifies that trap energy levels are referenced from the conduction band edge. The trap energy center is computed as E_trap = EC - E0 - E_shift.

parameters.name: fromCondBand
parameters.desc: Reference trap energy from conduction band edge.

fromCondBand.example:
Traps (
  (SingleTrap eNeutral Level EnergyMid=0.3 fromCondBand
    SpaceMid=(0.0,0.0,0.1))
)

contexts.Traps: Energy reference for trap level definitions

fromMidBandGap.description: Specifies that trap energy levels are referenced from the intrinsic energy level (mid-bandgap). The trap energy center is computed using the average of conduction and valence band edges adjusted by the density-of-states ratio.

parameters.name: fromMidBandGap
parameters.desc: Reference trap energy from the intrinsic (mid-bandgap) energy level.

contexts.Traps: Energy reference for trap level definitions
contexts.Degradation: Used in HCS degradation model for generated trap energy reference

fromTop.description: Convenience keyword that sets default excitation parameters for illumination from the top of the device structure. Automatically configures Theta, Origin, XDirection, and Window type based on the coordinate system (DF-ISE or UCS).

fromValBand.description: Specifies that trap energy levels are referenced from the valence band edge. The trap energy center is computed as E_trap = EV + E0 + E_shift.

parameters.name: fromValBand
parameters.desc: Reference trap energy from valence band edge.

contexts.Traps: Energy reference for trap level definitions

gain.signature: GainPlot { Intervals=<int> Range=(<float>*2)|Auto } or File { Gain=<string> }
gain.description: Modal stimulated and spontaneous emission spectra output for LED simulations. Configured in GainPlot section (plot parameters) and File section (output file name).

parameters.name: Intervals
parameters.desc: Number of points to plot for each gain curve.

parameters.name: Range
parameters.desc: Energy range of the gain plot in eV, or Auto to automatically determine.

contexts.File: Output file name for gain spectra

generation.description: Carrier generation processes in semiconductor device simulation. Includes optical generation, avalanche generation (impact ionization), band-to-band tunneling, constant carrier generation, and Auger generation. Generation is typically specified as part of the Recombination section in Physics.

generation rate.description: Scalar plot quantities representing the carrier generation rate at each mesh vertex. Includes optical generation, avalanche generation, band-to-band tunneling generation, radiation generation, and alpha particle generation.

geometric.description: Geometric variations for statistical impedance field method (sIFM) simulations. Activates random geometric fluctuations at interfaces to model line edge roughness and other geometric variability effects.

parameters.name: GeometricFluctuations
parameters.desc: Activates geometric fluctuations for surface roughness modeling with lambda, Amplitude, and Amplitude_Iso parameters.

geometric variation.description: Deterministic geometric variations applied to specific surfaces for parametric study of device geometry effects. Allows controlled variation of interface positions for sensitivity analysis.

parameters.name: GeometricVariation
parameters.desc: Named deterministic geometric variation specification with surface definition and variation parameters.

contexts.DeterministicVariation: Deterministic geometric variations as opposed to randomized

global.description: Global section in the SDevice command file that contains top-level settings such as file references, coordinate system, and simulation-level parameters. Not a physics keyword per se, but a structural section of the command file.

grading.signature: MoleFraction { Grading( GrDistance=<float> RegionInterface=(<string>*2) xFraction=<float> yFraction=<float> ) }
grading.description: Sub-keyword of MoleFraction that specifies an alternative way to define grading of alloy composition at interfaces. It allows a nonzero mole fraction and different grading distances from different parts of the boundaries.

parameters.name: GrDistance
parameters.desc: Distance in um in the direction normal to the specified interface, where linear interpolation of mole fractions from the constant value to the specified boundary mole fractions occurs.

parameters.name: RegionInterface
parameters.desc: Restrict this grading to the given interface (applied to all interfaces by default).

parameters.name: xFraction
parameters.desc: Boundary xMoleFraction value at the interface.

parameters.name: yFraction
parameters.desc: Boundary yMoleFraction value at the interface.

h.description: Horizontal polarization identifier for optical/raytracing simulations. Used to specify the polarization state of incident light or rays.

hAlphaAvalanche.description: Scalar plot variable representing the hole (p) impact ionization coefficient (alpha_p) from the avalanche generation model. Units: cm^-1.

hAugerRecombination.description: Scalar plot variable representing the hole Auger recombination rate (RAp, Equation 441). Units: cm^-3 s^-1.

hAvalanche.description: Hole impact ionization (avalanche) model within Avalanche() or Recombination(). Models hole-initiated carrier generation due to high-field impact ionization. Parallel to eAvalanche with separate coefficients.

hBand2BandGeneration.description: Scalar plot variable representing the hole contribution to band-to-band tunneling generation rate from the dynamic nonlocal path model. Units: cm^-3 s^-1.

hBandEdgeShift.description: Computes band-edge shift for the valence band using a PMI (Physical Model Interface) model with an optional constructor argument. Used for apparent band-edge shift calculations.

parameters.name: <ident>
parameters.desc: Name of the PMI model to compute the band-edge shift.

parameters.name: <int>
parameters.desc: Optional constructor argument for the PMI model, allowing multiple variants within the same model.

hBandTailDOS.signature: Physics { hBandTailDOS( Exponent | Gaussian | GaussianFromOrganic | TwoPopulation ) }
hBandTailDOS.description: Activates band tail density-of-states models for holes. Supports exponential, Gaussian, organic Gaussian, and two-population distributions.

parameters.name: Exponent
parameters.desc: Use exponential shape band tails.

parameters.name: Gaussian
parameters.desc: Use Gaussian shape band tails.

parameters.name: GaussianFromOrganic
parameters.desc: Use Gaussian shape band tails as implemented for organic materials.

parameters.name: TwoPopulation
parameters.desc: Use two-population model for band tails.

hBarrierTunneling.description: In Traps(), activates hole barrier tunneling (trap-assisted tunneling from the valence band). NonLocal option enables tunneling from named nonlocal meshes. TwoBand option uses two-band dispersion for the WKB tunneling probability.

parameters.name: <string>
parameters.desc: Name identifying the nonlocal mesh for tunneling computation.

parameters.name: Band2Band
parameters.desc: Controls band-to-band tunneling contributions: None (no B2B), Simple, Full (consistent with nonlocal path B2B), or UpsideDown.

parameters.name: BarrierLowering
parameters.desc: Apply a position-dependent barrier lowering for the tunneling barrier.

parameters.name: Multivalley
parameters.desc: Use multivalley band structure for band-to-band contributions.

parameters.name: PeltierHeat
parameters.desc: Include Peltier heating terms for tunneling carriers.

parameters.name: Schroedinger
parameters.desc: Use Schrodinger equation-based model instead of WKB for tunneling probabilities. Not compatible with TwoBand or Band2Band.

parameters.name: Transmission
parameters.desc: Use additional interface transmission coefficients.

hBreakdownProbability.description: Plot variable related to the hole contribution to avalanche breakdown probability analysis.

hCDL1Lifetime.description: Scalar plot variable representing the hole (p) coupled defect level 1 lifetime (tau_p1) from the CDL recombination model. Units: s.

hCDL2Lifetime.description: Scalar plot variable representing the hole (p) coupled defect level 2 lifetime (tau_p2) from the CDL recombination model. Units: s.

hConstEmissionRate.description: Sets a constant hole emission rate to the valence band (ep_const) for a trap entry in the Traps section. Units: s^-1.

parameters.name: hConstEmissionRate
parameters.desc: Constant hole emission rate to the valence band in s^-1.

hCurrent.description: Hole current density at each vertex, available as a plot dataset. Represents the drift-diffusion current of holes. Units: A/cm^2.

hCurrentDensity.description: Hole current density at each vertex, available as a plot dataset in Plot{} and CurrentPlot{} sections. Represents the hole component of the total drift-diffusion current. Units: A/cm^2.

contexts.Plot (Scalar): Magnitude of hole current density |Jp| at vertices, units A/cm^2.
contexts.Plot (Vector): Full hole current density vector Jp at vertices, units A/cm^2.

hDeformationPotential.description: Scalar plot variable representing the hole deformation potential from the deformation potential model for stress-dependent band structure. Units: eV.

hDensity.description: Hole concentration (density) at each vertex, available as a plot dataset. Represents the free hole carrier density computed from the solution of the hole continuity equation. Units: cm^-3.

hDensityCorrection.description: Activates hole quantization effects in quantum well models or enables density correction in band-to-band tunneling models.

contexts.QWLocal: Activate hole quantization model in the quantum well for computing density correction effects on carrier concentrations.
contexts.Band2Band: Use Schenk density correction for hole band-to-band tunneling rate.

hDifferentialGain.description: Scalar plot variable representing the hole differential gain from stimulated and spontaneous emission coefficients in LED simulations. Units: cm^2.

hDiffusivity.description: Plot variable related to hole diffusivity. In the context of non-Einstein diffusivity model, this represents the hole diffusivity derived from mobility.

hDiffusivityMobility.description: Scalar plot variable representing the hole non-Einstein diffusivity mobility (mu_p,diff) when non-Einstein diffusivity is activated. Units: cm^2/(V s).

hDrForceRefDens.description: Damping parameter for the hole high-field mobility driving force at zero field. Alias for RefDens_hGradQuasiFermi_Zero. Controls interpolation of driving forces to zero field for hole carriers. Units: cm^-3.

parameters.name: hDrForceRefDens
parameters.desc: Reference density threshold in cm^-3 for damping the hole driving force interpolation to zero field. Default is 0.

hDriftVelocity.description: Plot variable representing the hole drift velocity. Available as both scalar (magnitude) and vector (direction) data. Units: cm/s.

contexts.Plot (Scalar): Magnitude of hole drift velocity at vertices, units cm/s.
contexts.Plot (Vector): Full hole drift velocity vector at vertices, units cm/s.

hEffectiveField.description: Scalar plot variable representing the effective electric field for holes (Eff_ep, Equation 483), used as the driving force in mobility models. Units: V/cm.

hEffectiveStateDensity.description: Scalar plot variable representing the valence band effective density-of-states NV for holes. Units: cm^-3.

hEffectiveStress.description: Scalar plot variable representing the effective stress for holes. Units: MPa.

hEnormal.description: Scalar plot variable representing the electric field component perpendicular to the interface (F_p_perp, Equation 336 or Equation 337) or the normal field for holes. Units: V/cm.

hEparallel.description: Scalar plot variable representing the electric field parallel to the hole current (F_p, Equation 363). Units: V/cm.

hEquilibriumDensity.description: Scalar plot variable representing the hole density p at zero applied voltages (zero currents) as computed from the equilibrium solution. Units: cm^-3.

hFactor.description: Isotropic factor model for computing hole mobility enhancement under mechanical stress. Uses a diagonal component of the piezoresistance mobility enhancement tensor as an isotropic factor gamma applied to hole mobility. Part of the Piezo mobility stress framework.

parameters.name: FirstOrder
parameters.desc: Use first-order piezoresistance model (default).

parameters.name: SecondOrder
parameters.desc: Use second-order piezoresistance model.

parameters.name: EffectiveStressModel
parameters.desc: Use effective stress factor model.

parameters.name: <pmi_model>
parameters.desc: Name of PMI model to compute mobility enhancement stress factor.

parameters.name: SFactor
parameters.desc: Dataset name or PMI model for spatial distribution of mobility enhancement factor.

parameters.name: ChannelDirection
parameters.desc: Channel direction (1-3), determines which tensor component to use. Default is 1.

parameters.name: Kanda
parameters.desc: Include temperature and doping dependency.

parameters.name: AutoOrientation
parameters.desc: Automatically select parameter set based on nearest interface orientation.

parameters.name: ParameterSetName
parameters.desc: Name of parameter set to use.

parameters.name: ApplyToMobilityComponents
parameters.desc: Apply the factor enhancement to individual mobility components.

hFiegna.description: Fiegna hot-carrier injection model for holes. Computes gate leakage current due to hot-hole injection across a semiconductor-insulator interface using a non-Maxwellian energy distribution. Specified as a sub-option of GateCurrent in an interface-specific Physics section.

parameters.name: CarrierTempDrive
parameters.desc: Use field derived from carrier temperature.

parameters.name: CarrierTempPost
parameters.desc: Use field derived from postprocessed carrier temperature.

parameters.name: Eparallel
parameters.desc: Use electric field parallel to interface (default).

hGapStatesRecombination.description: Plot dataset for the hole component of recombination rates due to gap states (traps) in the bandgap. Available at vertices, unit is cm-3s-1. Relates to Chapter 17 trap recombination models.

hGfactor.description: Hole degeneracy factor gp for trap occupation dynamics. Used in the principle of detailed balance relating capture and emission rates for traps coupled to the valence band. Defaults to 1.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Hole degeneracy factor gp. Default is 1.

hGradQuasiFermi.description: Plot dataset for the magnitude of the hole quasi-Fermi potential gradient, |nabla(Phi_p)| (Equation 365). Available at vertices, unit is V/cm. Can also be used as a direction field for ComputeIonizationIntegrals and as a driving force for avalanche models.

contexts.Plot: Scalar plot dataset for hole quasi-Fermi potential gradient magnitude.
contexts.Math: Direction field for ComputeIonizationIntegrals path search.
contexts.Physics: Driving force option for Recombination(Avalanche(GradQuasiFermi)).

hHCSDegradation.description: Hole version of the hot-carrier stress degradation (HCS) model for MOS-based devices. Includes single-particle, multiple-particle, and field-enhanced thermal degradation mechanisms that contribute to bond breakage and interface trap formation. Specified as part of a Traps definition at the material interface. The complementary electron version is eHCSDegradation.

parameters.name: BDEnergyIntervals
parameters.desc: Number of energy intervals for bond dispersion integral. Default is 20.

parameters.name: BondDispersion
parameters.desc: Enable bond dispersion in the HCS degradation model.

parameters.name: SHE
parameters.desc: Use carrier distribution function from SHE (spherical harmonics expansion).

hHIDensity.description: Interface plot dataset for hole hydrogen-induced defect density. Available at region-interface vertices using the /RegionInterface modifier.

hHIDensityPerDensity.description: Interface plot dataset for hole hydrogen-induced defect density per carrier density. Available at region-interface vertices using the /RegionInterface modifier.

hHIDensityPerTemperature.description: Interface plot dataset for hole hydrogen-induced defect density per lattice temperature. Available at region-interface vertices using the /RegionInterface modifier.

hHIDensityPer_hTemperature.description: Interface plot dataset for hole hydrogen-induced defect density per hole temperature. Available at region-interface vertices using the /RegionInterface modifier.

hHeatFlux.description: Plot dataset for the hole heat flux vector Sp (Equation 80), representing the energy flux associated with hole transport in the hydrodynamic model. Available as both scalar (magnitude) and vector data at vertices, unit is W/cm2.

hHighFieldSaturation.description: Hole high-field saturation velocity model within Mobility() or hMobility(). Configures the saturation velocity and high-field dependence of hole mobility using Caughey-Thomas or similar models.

parameters.name: ParameterSetName
parameters.desc: Name of the HighFieldDependence parameter set to use.

parameters.name: AutoOrientation
parameters.desc: Automatically select parameter set based on nearest interface orientation.

hInterfaceTrappedCharge.description: Plot dataset for the amount of hole trapped charge at interfaces. Available at interface vertices only, unit is cm-2. Has nonzero values only at region-interface vertices.

hIonIntegral.description: Plot dataset for the hole ionization integral along the breakdown path. Used for approximate breakdown analysis visualization. The best path is saved as a volume dataset in the TDR file. Unit is dimensionless (integral value).

hJfactor.description: Hole J-model factor gp^J for trap capture rates. Controls the mixing between the V-model (g=0, thermal velocity only) and the J-model (g=1, current density driven) for hole capture from the valence band. Values between 0 and 1 are allowed.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Hole J-model factor gp^J, range [0, 1]. Default is 0 (V-model).

hJouleHeat.description: Plot dataset for the hole Joule heat, computed as |Jp|^2 / (q * mu_p). Represents resistive heating due to hole current. Available at vertices, unit is W/cm3.

hLadder.description: Explicit ladder specification for holes in the Schrodinger equation solver. Defines the quantization mass, in-plane mass, ladder degeneracy, and band edge offset for each hole subband. Extended syntax for wurtzite crystals supports hole type classification (HH, LH, CH).

parameters.name: <mz>
parameters.desc: Quantization mass parallel to the confinement direction.

parameters.name: <mxy>
parameters.desc: Mass perpendicular to the quantization direction.

parameters.name: <degeneracy>
parameters.desc: Ladder degeneracy (number of equivalent subbands).

parameters.name: <offset>
parameters.desc: Band edge offset with respect to the valence band edge without strain, in eV.

parameters.name: HeavyHole
parameters.desc: Classify as heavy-hole band (wurtzite only).

parameters.name: LightHole
parameters.desc: Classify as light-hole band (wurtzite only).

parameters.name: CrystalFieldSplitHole
parameters.desc: Classify as crystal-field split-off hole band (wurtzite only).

hLifetime.description: Plot dataset for the hole Shockley-Read-Hall lifetime tau_p (Equation 397). Available at vertices, unit is seconds. Can also be loaded from a file via the LifeTime keyword in the File section.

hLucky.description: Classical lucky electron (hole) injection model for holes. Computes gate leakage current due to hot-hole injection assuming a Maxwellian energy distribution. Specified as a sub-option of GateCurrent in an interface-specific Physics section.

parameters.name: CarrierTempDrive
parameters.desc: Use field derived from carrier temperature.

parameters.name: CarrierTempPost
parameters.desc: Use field derived from postprocessed carrier temperature.

parameters.name: Eparallel
parameters.desc: Use electric field parallel to interface (default).

hMLDA.description: Modified Local-Density Approximation (MLDA) quantum correction model for holes only. Provides a simple one-parameter quantum correction to hole carrier density with thermal wavelength lambda_300. Can be specified regionwise or materialwise.

parameters.name: -LambdaTemp
parameters.desc: Switch off temperature dependency of the thermal wavelength. By default, lambda depends on carrier temperature.

hMLDAQuantumPotential.description: Plot dataset for the hole effective quantum correction of the simple MLDA model (Equation 240). Available at vertices, unit is eV.

hMass.description: Strained hole effective mass and density-of-states model. Computes stress-dependent valence-band effective mass and DOS using inverse six-band k*p method. Calibrated for strained silicon, germanium, SiGe, and III-V materials.

parameters.name: AnalyticLTFit
parameters.desc: Use analytic lattice temperature fit for the strain-affected hole mass (default for thermal simulations).

parameters.name: NumericalIntegration
parameters.desc: Use numeric integrations for the strain-affected hole mass (default for isothermal simulations).

hMinorityFactor.description: Calibration factor beta for stress effect on minority hole mobility. Allows the stress dependency of minority carrier mobility to differ from majority carrier mobility. Can optionally disable doping dependency when a DopingThreshold is specified.

parameters.name: <float>
parameters.desc: Minority carrier stress factor beta. Default is 1.0.

parameters.name: DopingThreshold
parameters.desc: Net doping threshold in cm-3. When specified, stress model doping dependency is switched off and the factor is applied based on the threshold condition.

hMobility.description: Hole mobility model within Mobility(). Configures all mobility sub-models (doping dependence, high-field saturation, etc.) specifically for holes. Overrides generic Mobility() settings for the hole carrier type.

parameters.name: ConstantMobility
parameters.desc: Constant mobility model (phonon scattering only).

parameters.name: DopingDependence
parameters.desc: Doping-dependent mobility degradation.

parameters.name: EnormalDependence
parameters.desc: Normal electric field dependent mobility (surface degradation).

parameters.name: HighFieldSaturation
parameters.desc: High-field velocity saturation model.

parameters.name: <pmi_model>
parameters.desc: PMI model name for mobility computation.
