hMobilityAniso.description: Plot dataset for hole mobility along the anisotropic axis (aniso_mu_p). Available at vertices and elements, unit is cm2/(V*s). Requires Aniso(hMobility) to be activated in the Physics section.

hMobilityAnisoFactor.description: Plot dataset for the anisotropic factor for holes (r_h, Equation 945), defined as the ratio of hole mobility along the main axis to mobility along the anisotropic axis. Dimensionless quantity available at vertices.

hMobilityAveraging.description: Controls the averaging method for hole mobility values on the mesh. Element averaging uses the element-centered value, while ElementEdge averaging uses the edge-centered value.

parameters.name: Element
parameters.desc: Use element averaged hole mobility.

parameters.name: ElementEdge
parameters.desc: Use element-edge averaged hole mobility (default).

hMobilityFactor.description: Total anisotropic mobility factor for holes. Defines the ratio r_h between isotropic mobility and anisotropic mobility along the specified anisotropic direction. Sentaurus Device computes mobility along the main crystallographic axis as usual and scales it by this factor.

parameters.name: Total
parameters.desc: Anisotropic mobility factor r_h (ratio of main axis mobility to anisotropic axis mobility).

hMobilityStressFactorXX.description: Plot dataset for the XX component of the hole mobility stress factor tensor. One of six independent components of the symmetric 3x3 mobility enhancement tensor (mu_bar / mu_0) for holes. Dimensionless quantity available at vertices.

hMobilityStressFactorXY.description: Plot dataset for the XY component of the hole mobility stress factor tensor. One of six independent components of the symmetric 3x3 mobility enhancement tensor (mu_bar / mu_0) for holes. Dimensionless quantity available at vertices.

hMobilityStressFactorXZ.description: Scalar plot data for the XZ component of the hole mobility stress enhancement factor tensor. This is the ratio of stressed to unstressed hole mobility in the XZ direction, computed when a piezoresistance or deformation-potential mobility model is active.

hMobilityStressFactorYY.description: Scalar plot data for the YY component of the hole mobility stress enhancement factor tensor. This is the ratio of stressed to unstressed hole mobility in the YY direction, computed when a piezoresistance or deformation-potential mobility model is active.

hMobilityStressFactorYZ.description: Scalar plot data for the YZ component of the hole mobility stress enhancement factor tensor. This is the ratio of stressed to unstressed hole mobility in the YZ direction, computed when a piezoresistance or deformation-potential mobility model is active.

hMobilityStressFactorZZ.description: Scalar plot data for the ZZ component of the hole mobility stress enhancement factor tensor. This is the ratio of stressed to unstressed hole mobility in the ZZ direction, computed when a piezoresistance or deformation-potential mobility model is active.

hMultiValley.description: Activates the multivalley band structure model for holes only. Accounts for multiple valence band valleys in band structure computations, affecting density of states, carrier concentrations, and strain-induced band edge shifts. The DensityIntegral option enables numeric Gauss-Laguerre integration for Fermi-Dirac integrals.

hMultivalley.description: Likely a case-variant alias of hMultiValley. SDevice keywords are case-sensitive; this exact form is not documented as a valid Physics keyword.

hNLLTunnelingPeltierHeat.description: Scalar plot data for the Peltier heat generated in the valence band due to nonlocal line tunneling. Available regardless of whether the Peltier heat is accounted for in the temperature equation. Plotted in units of W/cm^3.

hNeutral.description: Trap type keyword specifying a hole (donor-like) neutral trap. Donor and hNeutral traps are uncharged when unoccupied and carry the charge of one hole when fully occupied. Used within the Traps subsection of Physics to define trap charge characteristics.

hQCvanDort.description: Activates the van Dort quantum correction model for holes. This model computes a quantum correction to the hole density based on the electric field normal to the semiconductor-insulator interface, using fitting parameters k_fit, E_crit, and a reference distance lambda_ref.

hQuantumPotential.description: Density gradient quantum correction model for holes. Computes quantum-confined potential shift for holes near semiconductor-insulator interfaces using the density gradient formalism. Supports anisotropic directions, boundary conditions, Gamma factor specification (EffectiveMass or PMI), local model coupling, and named parameter sets.

hQuasiFermi.description: Hole quasi-Fermi potential at each vertex, available as a plot dataset. Represents the electrochemical potential of holes, used as a reference in DopingWell{} ramping and convergence monitoring. Units: eV.

hQuasiFermiEnergy.description: Scalar plot data for the hole quasi-Fermi energy (E_F,p), which represents the energy level at which the probability of finding a hole state is 50%. Related to the quasi-Fermi potential by E_F,p = -q * Phi_p + E_V (under Boltzmann statistics).

hQuasiFermiPotential.description: Scalar plot data for the hole quasi-Fermi potential (Phi_p), the quantity solved by the hole continuity equation. It drives hole transport and is defined such that the hole concentration p = NV * exp(-(E_V - E_F,p) / kT) under Boltzmann statistics.

hQuenching.description: Activates nonradiative quenching of singlet excitons due to free holes. This is a recombination option within the SingletExciton framework, used for organic semiconductor simulations where exciton-polaron interactions cause exciton destruction.

hRecGenHeat.description: Scalar plot data for the hole generation-recombination heat source term (H_p). This term accounts for the energy exchange between the hole system and the lattice due to generation and recombination processes in the hydrodynamic model.

hRecVelocity.description: Specifies the hole recombination velocity at an electrode or metal-semiconductor interface. When nonzero, it imposes a current boundary condition J_p.n = -q*v_p*(p - p0) instead of the default charge neutrality condition. A value of zero means the electrode only defines the electrostatic potential.

parameters.name: (value)
parameters.desc: Hole recombination velocity in cm/s. Default is 1.93e6 cm/s (thermal velocity). Set to 0 for pure electrostatic potential definition.

contexts.Electrode: Used in the Electrode section to set the hole recombination velocity at Ohmic or Schottky contacts.
contexts.Physics: Used in Physics(MaterialInterface=...) for Schottky metal-semiconductor interfaces to override default recombination velocity.

hRelativeEffectiveMass.description: Scalar plot data for the relative hole effective mass (m_p). Used in carrier density calculations and can be strain-dependent when the Piezo model is active.

hSHEAvalancheGeneration.description: Scalar plot data for the hole avalanche generation rate computed by the spherical harmonics expansion (SHE) method. Represents the impact ionization generation rate for holes (G_p,i) when the SHE transport model is active.

hSHECurrentDensity.description: Scalar plot data for the magnitude of the hole current density computed by the spherical harmonics expansion (SHE) method (|J_p,SHE|). Also available as vector data in the Vector Data table.

hSHEDensity.description: Scalar plot data for the hole density computed by the spherical harmonics expansion (SHE) method (p_SHE). Represents the hole carrier density when the SHE transport model is used instead of the drift-diffusion approximation.

hSHEDistribution.description: Specifies vertex positions at which to plot the hole energy distribution from the spherical harmonics expansion (SHE) method. The distribution is output to the .plt file.

hSHEDistributionPlot.description: Plots the hole-energy distribution at specified spatial positions for the Spherical Harmonics Expansion Method (SHE). Used to visualize hot-carrier distribution for holes.

parameters.name: <vector>
parameters.desc: Spatial coordinates (x y z) at which to evaluate the hole distribution function.

hSHEEnergy.description: Scalar plot data for the hole temperature (T_p,SHE) computed by the spherical harmonics expansion (SHE) method. Represents the average kinetic energy of holes when the SHE transport model is active.

hSHEVelocity.description: Scalar plot data for the magnitude of the hole velocity computed by the spherical harmonics expansion (SHE) method (|v_p,SHE|). Also available as vector data in the Vector Data table.

hSRHRecombination.description: Scalar plot data for the hole recombination rate from the Shockley-Read-Hall (SRH) model. When trap-assisted tunneling (NonlocalPath) is active, this includes the nonlocal tunneling enhancement contribution to the hole SRH recombination rate.

hSaturationFactor.description: Calibration parameter that modifies the dependency of hole saturation velocity on mechanical stress. The saturation velocity formula becomes v_sat,i = v_sat,0 * (1 + alpha * t_i) / (1 + t_i), where alpha is this factor and t_i is the stress enhancement factor. A value of 0 applies the stress enhancement only to low-field mobility; a value of 1 applies it to total mobility.

parameters.name: (value)
parameters.desc: Scalar calibration factor alpha. Default is 1. Range 0 to 1 typical. 0 = stress affects only low-field mobility; 1 = stress affects total mobility.

hSaturationVelocity.description: Scalar plot data for the hole saturation velocity (v_sat,p) from the velocity saturation model. This is the maximum drift velocity that holes can reach under high electric fields.

hSaturationVelocityAniso.description: Scalar plot data for the anisotropic hole saturation velocity, relevant when stress-dependent mobility models are active. Represents the stress-modified saturation velocity used in tensor mobility calculations.

hSchenkBGN.description: Scalar plot data for the Schenk bandgap narrowing contribution for holes (-Lambda_p). This is the quantum correction from the density gradient model that also serves as the Schenk bandgap narrowing when the SchenkBGN_hole parameter set is active in the parameter file.

hSchenkTunnel.description: Scalar plot data for the hole direct tunneling current density computed by the Schenk model. This fully quantum-mechanical tunneling model covers both direct tunneling and the Fowler-Nordheim regime through trapezoidal barriers, typically used for gate leakage simulation.

hSixband.description: Intel stress-induced hole mobility model using six-band k.p method for hole bands. Computes carrier occupancies f1 and f2 in the crystallographic coordinate system for each band. Assumes Boltzmann statistics by default.

hSubband.signature: hSubband( Fermi | Doping | EffectiveMass | Scattering | BalMob )
hSubband.description: Activates the multivalley hole mobility model with subband quantization effects. Options include Fermi statistics, stress-dependent effective mass, MLDA scattering rate, and ballistic mobility correction.

hTemperature.description: Hole temperature at each vertex, available as a plot dataset in hydrodynamic or thermodynamic transport simulations. Used as a convergence criterion parameter and in DiffusionNoise{} temperature options. Units: K.

hTemperatureRelaxationTime.description: Scalar plot variable representing the hole energy relaxation time (tau_ep). Units: seconds. This is the characteristic time for energy exchange between holes and the lattice in the hydrodynamic model.

hTensor.description: Selects the anisotropic piezoresistance tensor model for hole mobility only. Computes mobility enhancement based on a full tensor expansion in terms of stress. Alternatively, the Tensor keyword applies this model to both electrons and holes.

hTensorMobilityFactorXX.description: Scalar plot variable representing the XX component of the hole mobility stress factor tensor. This factor describes how stress modifies hole mobility along the x-direction. Dimensionless (unit: 1).

hTensorMobilityFactorYY.description: Scalar plot variable representing the YY component of the hole mobility stress factor tensor. Describes how stress modifies hole mobility along the y-direction. Dimensionless (unit: 1).

hTensorMobilityFactorZZ.description: Scalar plot variable representing the ZZ component of the hole mobility stress factor tensor. Describes how stress modifies hole mobility along the z-direction. Dimensionless (unit: 1).

hTensorMobilityXX.description: Scalar plot variable representing the XX component of the hole piezoresistance mobility tensor. The actual mobility value after stress modification along the x-direction. Units: cm^2/(V*s).

hTensorMobilityYY.description: Scalar plot variable representing the YY component of the hole piezoresistance mobility tensor. The actual mobility value after stress modification along the y-direction. Units: cm^2/(V*s).

hTensorMobilityZZ.description: Scalar plot variable representing the ZZ component of the hole piezoresistance mobility tensor. The actual mobility value after stress modification along the z-direction. Units: cm^2/(V*s).

hThermionic.description: Activates the thermionic emission model for holes at a heterointerface. Used to define hole current and energy flux at abrupt semiconductor-semiconductor interfaces where conventional drift-diffusion transport equations are invalid. The Thermionic keyword activates this for both carriers.

hThermoElectricPower.description: Scalar plot variable representing the hole thermoelectric power (Pp), computed from Equation 1128. Units: V/K. Describes the voltage generated per unit temperature difference for holes.

hTrappedCharge.description: Scalar plot variable representing the hole trapped charge density in trap states. Units: cm^-3 (bulk) or cm^-2 (interface). Also available as an interface plot dataset (RegionInterface location).

hVelocity.description: Hole velocity vector at each vertex, available as a plot dataset. Represents the drift velocity of holes under the applied electric field. Units: cm/s.

hXSection.description: Hole capture cross section (sigma_p^0) for a trap definition. Specifies the effective area for hole capture by trap states. Used in local capture and emission rate calculations. Can be ramped as a model parameter via ModelParameter.

parameters.name: hXsection
parameters.desc: Hole capture cross section in cm^2. Determines the probability of hole capture by the trap state.

hXSection.example:
Traps (
  Donor Level
  EnergyMid=0 fromMidBandGap
  Conc=1e15
  eXsection=1e-14
  hXsection=1e-14
)

heat.description: Refers to thermal properties and heat-related physics models in Sentaurus Device. Encompasses heat capacity, thermal conductivity, heat generation rate, and thermoelectric power. These are configured as sub-sections within Physics for self-heating and thermodynamic simulations.

heiTemperature.description: Scalar plot variable representing the hot-electron-injection temperature (Thei), computed as a postprocessing approach using the CarrierTempPost model. Units: K. This is the effective temperature of hot electrons injected at an interface.

hhDiffusionLNS.description: Scalar plot variable representing the hole-hole diffusion local noise source spectral density. Units: C^2*s^-1*cm^-1. Used in noise analysis for diffusion noise contributions between holes.

hhFlickerGRLNS.description: Scalar plot variable representing the hole-hole flicker generation-recombination local noise source spectral density. Units: C^2*s^-1*cm^-1. Used in noise analysis for flicker noise contributions between holes.

hhMonopolarGRLNS.description: Scalar plot variable representing the hole-hole monopolar generation-recombination local noise source spectral density. Units: C^2*s^-1*cm^-1. Used in noise analysis for equivalent monopolar GR noise contributions between holes.

hkp.description: Six-by-six k.p method for computing hole band structure deformation under stress. Used within the DeformationPotential model to calculate strain-induced band edge shifts and effective mass changes for holes.

hole.description: Hole continuity equation solved in Sentaurus Device. When specified in a Coupled or Plugin block, the solver computes the hole carrier density and quasi-Fermi potential. Default error reference is 1e10 cm^-3 with error target 1e-5.

hot-carrier.description: Hot-carrier injection models for computing gate currents in MOSFET devices. Sentaurus Device provides both built-in lucky-electron/hole models and a PMI interface for custom hot-carrier injection implementations. The model computes injection current densities at semiconductor-insulator interfaces.

hydrogen.description: Hydrogen transport model in Sentaurus Device. Simulates the diffusion and reaction of hydrogen species (atoms, ions, molecules, and user-defined species A/B/C) in semiconductor devices. Used for modeling NBTI and HCI degradation mechanisms where hydrogen release and transport play a key role.

i.description: Single-letter qualifier designating the imaginary part of a complex quantity in AC or harmonic balance analysis. Used in System{} and Plot{} sections to reference the imaginary component of voltages, currents, or other complex variables.

ibv.description: SPICE diode model parameter specifying the current at reverse breakdown voltage. Used in compact model parameter sets for mixed-mode circuit simulations. Not a Sentaurus Device physics keyword.

parameters.name: ibv
parameters.desc: Current at reverse breakdown voltage in amperes.

ibv.example:
PSET D1n4148
DEVICE Diode
PARAMETERS
  is = 0.1p
  rs = 16
  cjo = 2p
  tt = 12n
  bv = 100
  ibv = 0.1p

ic.description: Not a Sentaurus Device keyword. The hit in the manual refers to IC-CAP (Integrated Circuit Characterization and Analysis Program), a measurement and modeling tool. The 'ic' text appears in context of the iccap_Write Tcl command available for IC-CAP model parameter extraction.

if.description: Tcl control flow keyword (if/elseif/else). Not a Sentaurus Device physics keyword. Appears extensively in example command files as part of Tcl scripting constructs for conditional logic.

illumination.description: Optical illumination configuration in Sentaurus Device. Defines the light source parameters (wavelength, intensity, angle, polarization) and spectral data for optical generation computations. Illumination spectra can be loaded from files and support multidimensional parameter sweeps.

imag.description: Imaginary part of the refractive index for region 1 or region 2 at a raytracing boundary. Used within the PMI raytrace boundary model to describe optical absorption characteristics at material interfaces.

parameters.name: n1_imag
parameters.desc: Imaginary part of refractive index for region 1.

parameters.name: n2_imag
parameters.desc: Imaginary part of refractive index for region 2.

imag.example:
# PMI raytrace boundary model variable
# n1_imag and n2_imag are read-only dependency variables

incomplete ionization.description: Enables incomplete ionization of dopants, which affects the concentration of free carriers available for conduction. At high doping levels or low temperatures, not all dopants are ionized. This keyword also supports PMI models and multiple lattice sites.

parameters.name: Dopants
parameters.desc: Restrict incomplete ionization to dopants named in the string. Dopant names are separated by spaces.

parameters.name: Model
parameters.desc: Use a PMI model for the named species.

parameters.name: Split
parameters.desc: Defines redistribution of a dopant into multiple lattice sites with occupation probabilities.

parameters.name: Doping
parameters.desc: Dopant that is redistributed into multiple lattice sites (inside Split).

parameters.name: Weights
parameters.desc: Occupation probabilities of the various lattice sites (inside Split).

incr.description: Standard Tcl command that increments the value of a variable by the specified amount (default 1). Appears in SDevice Tcl current plot scripts for counter manipulation.

parameters.name: varName
parameters.desc: Name of the variable to increment.

parameters.name: increment
parameters.desc: Amount to add to the variable (default is 1).

inductance.description: Declares a SPICE inductance instance in the System section. Creates an internal node branch representing the current through the inductor, accessible via v(l.branch). Used in mixed-mode electrothermal simulations.

parameters.name: name
parameters.desc: Instance name of the inductor.

parameters.name: nodes
parameters.desc: Terminal nodes of the inductor.

parameters.name: inductance
parameters.desc: Inductance value in henries.

info.description: A C++ interface class between Sentaurus Device and PMI_NewtonStep that provides information about the Newton iteration process. Accessed via getNewtonInfo() and contains methods such as IsTrans(), IsQstat(), InitialStep(), MaxStep(), UsedIters(), RHS(), NewtonStep(), and Error().

parameters.name: IsTrans
parameters.desc: Returns true if Coupled is within a Transient section.

parameters.name: IsQstat
parameters.desc: Returns true if Coupled is within a Quasistationary section.

parameters.name: IsContin
parameters.desc: Returns true if Coupled is within a Continuation section.

parameters.name: UsedIters
parameters.desc: Returns number of used Newton iterations.

parameters.name: NewtonStep
parameters.desc: Returns current Newton step number.

parameters.name: Error
parameters.desc: Returns current error value.

parameters.name: ChangeTimeTo
parameters.desc: Performs PMI time-step control.

initial solution.description: A command-line option (-i) that prints the initial solution in the save file and print files specified in the File section, then exits without performing further computations. Useful for verifying the initial guess before running a full simulation.

initial_structure.description: Specifies the initial structure file for the first call to Sentaurus Interconnect in the Mechanics stress solver. Used in the Physics section to configure the mechanical stress simulation.

parameters.name: initial_structure
parameters.desc: Path to the initial structure file (TDR format) for the first Sentaurus Interconnect call.

interface.description: Base class for Physical Model Interface (PMI) models in Sentaurus Device. PMI models are user-implemented C++ classes derived from this interface that allow custom physical models to be integrated into the simulation. Standard, simplified, and nonlocal interfaces are available.
interface.example:
# C++ PMI class declaration
class MyModel : public PMI_Device_Interface { ... };

intrinsicRichter.description: Extended recombination model for silicon implemented as an installed PMI model, covering both Auger and radiative recombination with temperature-dependent coefficients. Activated in the Recombination section of Physics.

parameters.name: Blow
parameters.desc: Parameter for band-to-band recombination prefactor (cm3/s). Default 4.73e-15.

parameters.name: bmax
parameters.desc: Maximum value for b coefficient. Default 1.

parameters.name: b2
parameters.desc: Shape parameter for b. Default 0.54.

parameters.name: Cn0
parameters.desc: Electron Auger coefficient (cm6/s). Default 2.5e-31.

parameters.name: Cp0
parameters.desc: Hole Auger coefficient (cm6/s). Default 8.5e-32.

irrational.description: Specifies the use of the ratio of two irrational polynomials for energy relaxation times or energy-dependent mobility. When used with EnergyRelaxationTimes, it sets formula=2. When used with HighFieldSaturation via CarrierTempDrivePolynomial, it provides energy-dependent mobility.

is.description: Extremely generic English word that appears pervasively throughout the SDevice manual. It is not an SDevice-specific keyword or command. Its high hit count is due to natural language usage in descriptions.

jBreakdownProbability.description: Plot variable for the joint (combined electron and hole) avalanche breakdown probability. Used together with eBreakdownProbability and hBreakdownProbability to visualize avalanche breakdown analysis results.

join.description: Keyword appearing in LED modeling context, related to coupling electronics and optics in light-emitting diode simulations. The exact SDevice syntax is not clearly documented as a standalone keyword.

kpDOS.description: Activates the k.p band-structure model within the multivalley framework. For electrons (eMultivalley), it uses a two-band k.p model for Delta2 valleys. For holes (hMultivalley), it uses a six-band k.p model creating light, heavy, and spin-orbit hole bands. Parameters include deformation potentials, Luttinger parameters, and effective masses.

parameters.name: Nonparabolicity
parameters.desc: Include band nonparabolicity in the k.p model (electron only).

parameters.name: parfile
parameters.desc: With kpDOS, adds the parameter file valleys for k.p parameters.

krec.description: Parameter specifying the recombination rate coefficient (r_i) for a hydrogen species (atom, molecule, or ion) in the MSC-Hydrogen transport degradation model. Defined in the HydrogenDiffusion parameter set in the parameter file.

parameters.name: krec
parameters.desc: Recombination rate coefficient in cm^-3*s^-1 (bulk) or cm^-2*s^-1 (interface). Default is 0.

l.description: Single letter 'l' with 50+ hits. This is a generic token matching many words and identifiers in the manual (e.g., 'model', 'local', 'parallel'). It is not an SDevice-specific keyword.

lRecGenHeat.description: Plot variable for the lattice generation-recombination heat term HL (see Equation 92). Represents the heat source or sink due to generation-recombination processes contributing to the lattice temperature equation.

lT_Range.description: Specifies the lower and upper limits for the lattice temperature in Kelvin. Default values are 50 K and 5000 K. These ranges apply both during Newton iterations and to the final results.

parameters.name: lT_Range
parameters.desc: Lower and upper lattice temperature limits in K. Default (50, 5000).

lappend.description: Standard Tcl command that appends elements to a list variable. Appears in SDevice Tcl current plot scripts for building result lists.

parameters.name: varName
parameters.desc: Name of the list variable.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: Value(s) to append to the list.

lattice site.description: In certain semiconductors, dopants can occupy different lattice sites with different activation energies. For example, nitrogen in 6H-SiC can occupy hexagonal or cubic sites. The Split sub-keyword within IncompleteIonization models this behavior.

parameters.name: Doping
parameters.desc: Name of the dopant redistributed across multiple lattice sites.

parameters.name: Weights
parameters.desc: Occupation probabilities of each lattice site.

lattice temperature.description: The temperature of the crystal lattice, which serves as the thermal reference for the device simulation. Set globally via Temperature in Physics (default 300 K) and solved via the Temperature equation in the Coupled statement. Affects all temperature-dependent physical models.

parameters.name: Temperature
parameters.desc: Device (lattice) temperature in K. Default is 300 K.

parameters.name: LatticeTemperatureLimit
parameters.desc: Maximum lattice temperature break criterion in K.

layer stack.description: The sequence of material layers in the device structure used by optical solvers (TMM, OptBeam). The layer stack is automatically extracted from the device geometry along the propagation direction and defines the refractive index profile for optical calculations.

lin.signature: Mobility( DopingDependence( PhuMob BalMob( Lch=<float> vdslin=<float> ) ) )
lin.description: A parameter in the injection velocity-dependent ballistic mobility model. vdslin (Vds,lin) is the linear drain-source voltage used in the ballistic mobility expression. Not a standalone keyword.

parameters.name: vdslin
parameters.desc: Linear drain-source voltage in V. Default 0.05 V.

lindex.description: Standard Tcl command that returns the element at a specified index from a list. Appears in SDevice Tcl current plot scripts.

parameters.name: list
parameters.desc: The list variable.

parameters.name: index
parameters.desc: Zero-based index of the element to retrieve.

list.description: Extremely generic English word and Tcl command that appears pervasively throughout the SDevice manual. As a Tcl command, it creates a list from its arguments. The high hit count is from natural language usage.

log.description: The runtime log file of Sentaurus Device, specified by the Output keyword in the File section. The log file contains Newton iteration information, convergence data, and diagnostic messages. PMI models can write to the log file via PMI_Newton::GetLogFile().

parameters.name: Output
parameters.desc: Base name for the runtime log file (output, extension _des.log).

m.description: Single letter 'm' with 50+ hits. Matches many substrings throughout the manual (e.g., 'model', 'mobility', 'mesh'). Not an SDevice-specific keyword.

mag.description: The magnitude (amplitude) of a tone in harmonic balance analysis. Specified within the Tone sub-statement of HBCoupled.

parameters.name: mag
parameters.desc: Magnitude (amplitude) of the harmonic tone.

mag2.description: The magnitude of the second tone in a two-tone harmonic balance analysis. Used with the freq2 parameter for multi-tone HB simulations.

parameters.name: mag2
parameters.desc: Magnitude of the second tone.

matching.signature: Electrode { matching } | Solve { matching } | Thermode { matching }
matching.description: A concept used in SDevice for specifying how contacts and boundary conditions match between the device structure and the command file. Electrode and Thermode sections use name matching (including regular expressions) to associate boundary conditions with structure contacts.

materialInterface.description: Specifies that the enclosed physics model applies at a material interface. Used to define models such as distributed thermal resistance, eNMP, and hot-carrier injection that operate at the boundary between two materials.

maxval.description: Specifies an upper limit for a break criterion variable such as current, voltage, power, or electric field. When the value exceeds this limit, the simulation stops.

contexts.Math: Used in BreakCriteria to set upper limits for global break conditions.
contexts.Solve: Used in Continuation and Quasistationary for sweep-specific break criteria.
contexts.KMC: Used in CurrentCompliance to set maximum current for TDDB simulations.

mcDOS.description: Enables Monte Carlo density-of-states calculation within the multivalley band structure model. Used with eMultiValley or hMultiValley to compute densities numerically.

mesh.description: In the PMI (Physical Model Interface) C++ context, Mesh() returns a pointer to the device mesh object, providing access to vertices, elements, regions, and interfaces. Not a command file keyword.
mesh.example:
const des_mesh* mesh = Mesh();
des_vertex* v = mesh->vertex(0);

mesh generation.description: A descriptive phrase referring to the mesh generation process, not a specific SDevice command keyword.

minval.description: Specifies a lower limit for a break criterion variable such as current, voltage, or power. When the value falls below this limit, the simulation stops.

contexts.Math: Used in BreakCriteria to set lower limits for global break conditions.
contexts.Solve: Used in Continuation and Quasistationary for sweep-specific break criteria.
contexts.KMC: Used in CurrentCompliance to set minimum current for TDDB simulations.

mixed mode.description: Refers to the mixed-mode simulation capability of Sentaurus Device, where physical device simulations are coupled with SPICE-like circuit simulations. The System section defines the circuit netlist connecting devices.

mobility.description: Specifies the carrier mobility model. Supports doping-dependent, high-field saturation, and driving force models for both electrons and holes. Individual carrier mobility can be set with eMobility() and hMobility().

parameters.name: Table 278
parameters.desc: Mobility model options including DopingDependence, Enormal, HighFieldSaturation, and PMI model names.

modelParameter.description: Selects a model parameter for plotting in the current file, used particularly with the unified optical generation computation interface. Also used in Goal for parameter ramping.

contexts.CurrentPlot: Select model parameter for printing to current file.
contexts.Goal: Used in Quasistationary Goal for parameter ramping (ModelParameter=<string>).

modifiedHurkx.description: Selects the modified Hurkx band-to-band tunneling model, which is an improved version of the Hurkx model for direct band-to-band tunneling.

mole fraction.description: Specifies mole fractions for alloy materials such as AlGaAs, SiGe, and InGaN. Defines xMoleFraction and yMoleFraction constants or grading profiles across regions and interfaces.

parameters.name: xFraction
parameters.desc: Constant value of xMoleFraction.

parameters.name: yFraction
parameters.desc: Constant value of yMoleFraction.

parameters.name: RegionName
parameters.desc: Regions where the mole-fraction specification applies.

parameters.name: Grading
parameters.desc: Grading specification for gradual mole fraction transition at interfaces.

multilayer.description: Defines a multilayer thin-film stack for transfer matrix method (TMM) optical calculations at raytracing boundary conditions. Each layer specifies its thickness and material.

parameters.name: LayerStructure
parameters.desc: Multilayer definition with thickness in um and material name for each layer.

parameters.name: ReferenceMaterial
parameters.desc: Material defining the orientation of the LayerStructure.

parameters.name: ReferenceRegion
parameters.desc: Region defining the orientation of the LayerStructure.

parameters.name: QuantumEfficiency
parameters.desc: Quantum efficiency of the TMM BC optical generation.

parameters.name: MapOptGenToRegions
parameters.desc: Regions to which lumped TMM BC optical generation is mapped.

contexts.RayTraceBC: Used within RayTraceBC to define TMM multilayer boundary conditions.
contexts.Plot: Also used in Plot section for multilayer boundary condition visualization.

multistate.description: Defines multistate configurations (MSC) for modeling materials with multiple structural or chemical states, such as ferroelectric phase transitions or resistive switching. Each MSConfig defines states and transitions between them.

parameters.name: MSConfig
parameters.desc: Configuration definition with states, transitions, and band-edge shifts.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.desc: Identifier of the multistate configuration.

parameters.name: State
parameters.desc: Defines a state with Charge, Hydrogen, Name, and TTOM subkeywords.

parameters.name: Transition
parameters.desc: Defines a transition between states.

parameters.name: Conc
parameters.desc: Concentration of states in cm^-2.

multivalley.description: Activates multivalley band structure statistics for more accurate carrier density computation in direct-bandgap semiconductors. Supports k*p DOS, Monte Carlo DOS, MLDA quantization, and geometric quantization models.

parameters.name: DensityIntegral
parameters.desc: Number of Gauss-Laguerre quadrature points for numeric density computation.

parameters.name: kpDOS
parameters.desc: Use k*p band structure model for density of states.

parameters.name: mcDOS
parameters.desc: Use Monte Carlo density of states.

parameters.name: MLDA
parameters.desc: Use multivalley modified local-density approximation quantization model.

parameters.name: Nonparabolicity
parameters.desc: Include band nonparabolicity.

parameters.name: ThinLayer
parameters.desc: Use geometric quantization model.

parameters.name: RelativeToDOSMass
parameters.desc: Use multivalley effective density of states.

parameters.name: parfile
parameters.desc: Add parameter file valleys when using kpDOS.

contexts.Physics: Used in Physics section to enable multivalley band structure.
contexts.eBarrierTunneling: Multivalley keyword in eBarrierTunneling/hBarrierTunneling to include band-to-band contributions.

n.description: The letter 'n' is overwhelmingly used as a generic variable name (electron density, iteration count, etc.) throughout the Sentaurus Device manual. It is not a specific SDevice command keyword.

n0.description: Refers to the equilibrium electron density (n0), commonly used as a reference value in physical models such as MSC hydrogen transport degradation, eNMP model, and mixed-mode boundary conditions.

netlist.description: Specifies a PrimeSim HSPICE netlist file to be included in the mixed-mode circuit simulation. The netlist file defines parameter sets and instances of SPICE and HSPICE models.

parameters.name: Netlist
parameters.desc: Path to the PrimeSim HSPICE netlist file.

noOverwrite.description: When specified in Save or Plot statements, each new output file is given a unique numbered name instead of overwriting the previous file. This is the default behavior for Save and opposite of Overwrite.

noise.description: Specifies noise source models including diffusion noise, bulk flicker noise, trapping noise, random dopant fluctuations, band edge fluctuations, and equivalent monopolar GR noise. Used for small-signal noise analysis.

parameters.name: DiffusionNoise
parameters.desc: Diffusion noise, spatially restricted.

parameters.name: FlickerGRNoise
parameters.desc: Bulk flicker noise.

parameters.name: Traps
parameters.desc: Trapping noise.

parameters.name: Doping
parameters.desc: Random dopant fluctuations.

parameters.name: BandEdgeFluctuations
parameters.desc: Random band edge fluctuations.

parameters.name: GeometricFluctuations
parameters.desc: Random geometric fluctuations for surface.

parameters.name: MonopolarGRNoise
parameters.desc: Equivalent monopolar generation-recombination noise.

contexts.Physics: Noise source specification.
contexts.Math: RandomizedVariation for statistical IFM noise analysis.
contexts.IFM: IFM section for impedance field method noise.
contexts.Device: NoisePlot section for noise output data.
contexts.Solve: ACCoupled with NoisePlot and ObservationNode for noise analysis.

noise_Id_Vg.description: An extraction curve identifier that extracts drain current noise as a function of gate voltage during a quasistationary sweep.

noise_Ig_Vg.description: An extraction curve identifier that extracts gate current noise as a function of gate voltage during a quasistationary sweep.

noise_Is_F.description: An extraction curve identifier that extracts source current noise as a function of frequency during AC-coupled small-signal analysis.

noise_Vd_F.description: An extraction curve identifier that extracts drain voltage noise as a function of frequency during AC-coupled small-signal analysis.

noise_Vg_F.description: An extraction curve identifier that extracts gate voltage noise as a function of frequency during AC-coupled small-signal analysis.

noisy.description: In the KMC (Kinetic Monte Carlo) context, refers to the noisy regime of the simulation where stochastic KMC events are active. The term appears in the context of omitting KMC simulations near steady state.

nonlocal.description: Defines a nonlocal mesh for tunneling calculations. Nonlocal meshes extend from interfaces or contacts into the device to compute nonlocal tunneling currents using WKB or Schrodinger-based models.

parameters.name: Table 361
parameters.desc: Interface that is part of the reference surface.

parameters.name: Barrier
parameters.desc: Regions forming the tunneling barrier.

parameters.name: Electrode
parameters.desc: Electrode that is part of the reference surface.

parameters.name: Length
parameters.desc: Distance from interface up to which nonlocal mesh lines are constructed (cm).

parameters.name: Discretization
parameters.desc: Maximum distance between nonlocal mesh points on a line (cm).

parameters.name: Digits
parameters.desc: Accuracy for nonlocal tunneling currents.

parameters.name: EnergyResolution
parameters.desc: Minimum energy resolution for tunneling integrals (eV).

parameters.name: Endpoint
parameters.desc: Regions where nonlocal lines can end.

parameters.name: Transparent
parameters.desc: Regions that nonlocal lines can cross.

parameters.name: Refined
parameters.desc: Regions in which nonlocal lines are autorefined.

contexts.Math: Nonlocal mesh definition for tunneling calculations.
contexts.Physics: Nonlocal generation-recombination models, Schrodinger solver on named nonlocal mesh.

occupation probability.description: A physics concept referring to the probability of a state being occupied by a carrier. Used in descriptions of trap models, SHE method, and mode oscillator models. Not a command keyword.

open.description: A common English word that appears in the manual in contexts such as 'open source', 'open circuit', and 'open boundary'. Not a specific SDevice command keyword.

optical.description: Specifies optical generation computation settings including excitation parameters, optical solver type (TMM, RayTracing, BPM, FDTD, OptBeam, FromFile), and optical generation models.

parameters.name: Excitation
parameters.desc: Excitation parameters: intensity, wavelength, polarization, angles.

parameters.name: OpticalSolver
parameters.desc: Optical solver method: TMM, RayTracing, BPM, FDTD, Composite, OptBeam, FromFile.

parameters.name: OpticalGeneration
parameters.desc: Optical generation models and computation type.

parameters.name: ComplexRefractiveIndex
parameters.desc: Complex refractive index models.

parameters.name: Verbosity
parameters.desc: Verbosity level for optical solver output.

contexts.Physics: Main optical simulation configuration.
contexts.Device: OpticalDevice section for mixed-mode optical simulations.

optical beam.description: Specifies the optical beam absorption method for computing optical generation. This solver uses a simplified optical beam model to calculate absorption through multilayer structures.

parameters.name: ProfileIndex
parameters.desc: Index for ramping optical beam profiles.

parameters.name: Table 252
parameters.desc: OptBeam solver-specific options.

optical generation.description: Specifies the optical generation rate computation method within the Optics section. Supports monochromatic source, illumination spectrum, constant generation, and file-based loading. The total optical generation rate is the sum of all specified contributions.

parameters.name: ComputeFromMonochromaticSource
parameters.desc: Activate optical generation from a single wavelength source.

parameters.name: ComputeFromSpectrum
parameters.desc: Compute optical generation from an input illumination spectrum.

parameters.name: ReadFromFile
parameters.desc: Import optical generation profile from file.

parameters.name: SetConstant
parameters.desc: Set a background constant optical generation rate.

parameters.name: Scaling
parameters.desc: Scaling factor for optical generation (quasistationary only).

parameters.name: TimeDependence
parameters.desc: Time-dependent scaling for transient simulations (pulse modeling).

optical solver.description: Specifies the optical solver method used to compute the absorbed photon density. Supported methods include FDTD (finite-difference time-domain), TransferMatrix, OptBeam (beam absorption), RayTracing, FromFile, and SetConstant.

parameters.name: FDTD
parameters.desc: Finite-difference time-domain optical solver.

parameters.name: TransferMatrix
parameters.desc: Transfer matrix method for thin-layer stacks.

parameters.name: OptBeam
parameters.desc: Optical beam absorption method.

parameters.name: RayTracing
parameters.desc: Raytracing method for optical generation.

parameters.name: FromFile
parameters.desc: Load optical solution from file.

parameters.name: SetConstant
parameters.desc: Set constant absorbed photon density.

order.description: Specifies the stepping order of the quasistationary or transient simulation. Controls initial, minimum, and maximum step sizes. In the context of the Extrapolate command, sets the order of extrapolation polynomial.

parameters.name: InitialStep
parameters.desc: Initial step size for the ramp.

parameters.name: MaxStep
parameters.desc: Maximum allowed step size.

parameters.name: MinStep
parameters.desc: Minimum allowed step size.

parameters.name: Increment
parameters.desc: Step size increment factor (quasistationary).

parameters.name: Decrement
parameters.desc: Step size decrement factor (quasistationary).

output.description: Specifies the base name for the runtime log output file. Sentaurus Device appends _des.log to the user-supplied name. The default value is 'output' and the SWB variable is @log@.

parameters.name: Output
parameters.desc: Base name for the runtime log file (output, _des.log, @log@).

p.description: Single-letter qualifier in the Math section, typically used to designate hole-related quantities in error control and convergence criteria (e.g., ErrRef(p) for hole error reference).

parameter.description: Specifies the base name for the device parameter file. Sentaurus Device appends .par to the user-supplied name. The SWB variable is @parameter@. Physical model parameters are loaded from this file.

parameters.name: Parameters
parameters.desc: Base name for device parameters file (input, .par, @parameter@).

parameters.name: ParameterPath
parameters.desc: Search path for parameter files in MaterialDB directory.

parameter variation.description: Specifies deterministic variations of physical model parameters for impedance field method (IFM) analysis. Each named variation defines how a parameter fluctuates, including spatial shape and amplitude.

parameters.name: <name>
parameters.desc: Name identifying the variation and its output in the ACExtract file.

parameters.name: SFactor
parameters.desc: Scalar dataset for spatial variation (doping, PMIUserFields).

parameters.name: Amplitude
parameters.desc: Vectorial amplitude for the variation.

parameters.name: Amplitude_Iso
parameters.desc: Isotropic amplitude (um).

parameters.name: Conc
parameters.desc: Normalization concentration in cm^-3.

parameters.name: Factor
parameters.desc: Dimensionless multiplier (default 1).

parameters.name: Type
parameters.desc: Type of parameter: Doping, Acceptor, Donor, BandgapNarrowing, etc.

parameters.name: SpatialShape
parameters.desc: Spatial shape function: Gaussian or Uniform.

parameters.name: SpaceMid
parameters.desc: Center of the spatial shape.

parameters.name: SpaceSig
parameters.desc: Width (sigma) of the spatial shape.

parfile.description: Enables reading multivalley band structure parameters from an external parameter file. Used within eMultiValley or hMultiValley options to add valley parameters from the valleys parameter file when kpDOS is enabled.

perpendicular.description: Specifies the perpendicular polarization component for optical calculations in the context of transfer matrix method and thin-layer-stack boundary conditions. Refers to the electric field component perpendicular to the plane of incidence.

phase.description: In the context of harmonic balance analysis, refers to the phase component of a complex signal. Also used in ferroelectric modeling for the phase of polarization, and in optical raytracing for the phase of optical waves. Not a standalone SDevice command keyword.

phase2.description: Specifies the phase of the second frequency component in a two-tone harmonic balance analysis. Used within the Tone option of the HBCoupled command in the System section.

parameters.name: phase2
parameters.desc: Phase of the second tone in radians.

pmiModel.description: Specifies the name of a Physical Model Interface (PMI) model to use for a given physics calculation. PMI models are user-defined shared library models that override or extend built-in physical models. Used in QuantumYield, FEPolarization, Recombination, and other physics sub-sections.

parameters.name: pmiModel
parameters.desc: Name of the PMI model (must match the shared object filename).

contexts.QuantumYield: PMI model for optical quantum yield factors
contexts.FEPolarization: PMI model for ferroelectric Landau expansion

power.description: In the context of SDevice, power primarily appears in break criteria (DevicePower, InnerDevicePower, OuterDevicePower) to set limits on device power dissipation. Also appears in thermoelectric power models and thermal analysis.

parameters.name: absval
parameters.desc: Upper limit for absolute power value (W/um^d).

parameters.name: maxval
parameters.desc: Upper limit for power value (W/um^d).

parameters.name: minval
parameters.desc: Lower limit for power value (W/um^d).

parameters.name: DevName
parameters.desc: Circuit device name (mixed mode only).

parameters.name: Node
parameters.desc: Circuit node name (mixed mode only).

proc.description: Specifies a Tcl procedure to be called for custom current plot dataset computation within the CurrentPlot section. The proc option allows user-defined Tcl procedures for computing derived quantities from simulation results.

parameters.name: proc
parameters.desc: Name of the Tcl procedure to call for dataset computation.

parameters.name: Formula
parameters.desc: Formula expression for current plot computation.

parameters.name: Operation
parameters.desc: Evaluation operation: Node, Coordinate, Minimum, Maximum, Average, Integrate, Plot.

pulse.description: In the context of optical generation, refers to modeling a light pulse incident on a device. The TimeDependence section within OpticalGeneration allows specification of pulse characteristics including time interval (WaveTime), period (WaveTPeriod), and temporal shape (linear, Gaussian, exponential, cosine).

parameters.name: WaveTime
parameters.desc: Time interval (tmin, tmax) in seconds when optical generation is constant.

parameters.name: WaveTPeriod
parameters.desc: Period of the periodic signal in seconds.

parameters.name: WaveTPeriodOffset
parameters.desc: Offset of periodic signal in seconds.

parameters.name: WaveTSigma
parameters.desc: Standard deviation of temporal Gaussian decay in seconds.

parameters.name: WaveTSlope
parameters.desc: Slope of linear decay outside WaveTime interval in 1/s.

parameters.name: WavePeriods
parameters.desc: Number of periods of the periodic signal.

puts.description: The Tcl puts command, used within CurrentPlot Finish option to print messages to the log during custom current plot computation. This is a standard Tcl command, not an SDevice-specific keyword.

pwl.description: Piecewise-linear (PWL) function specification used in PMI material science (MSC) models for temperature-dependent properties such as heat capacity, thermal conductivity, and ABES parameters. Defines a function as a series of (x,y) data points with linear interpolation between them.

parameters.name: pwl
parameters.desc: Piecewise-linear function defined as pwl(x1,y1, x2,y2, ...).

quantum.description: Activates quantum correction models for carrier transport. The density gradient model (eQuantumPotential/hQuantumPotential) adds a quantum potential term to the carrier transport equations to account for quantum mechanical confinement effects.

parameters.name: eQuantumPotential
parameters.desc: Activate electron density gradient quantum correction.

parameters.name: hQuantumPotential
parameters.desc: Activate hole density gradient quantum correction.

parameters.name: Gamma
parameters.desc: Gamma parameter for density gradient model (via Table 299).

quantum well.description: Activates quantum well physics models for LED and optoelectronic device simulations. Includes both a localized QW model (QWLocal) for efficient simulation and a nonlocal QW model using the Schrodinger solver. The Active keyword designates regions as quantum well active regions.

parameters.name: Active
parameters.desc: Activate QW model with Type=QuantumWell.

parameters.name: QWLocal
parameters.desc: Localized quantum well model parameters.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.desc: Type of active region: QuantumWell.

parameters.name: eDensityCorrection
parameters.desc: Activate electron quantization in QW.

parameters.name: hDensityCorrection
parameters.desc: Activate hole quantization in QW.

parameters.name: Polarization
parameters.desc: Polarization type: TE, TM, or Mixed.

quantum yield.description: Specifies the quantum yield model that converts absorbed photon density to electron-hole pair generation rate. The quantum yield factors (eta_G, eta_T_Eg, eta_T_0) can be constant, band-gap-dependent, or defined through a PMI model. Located within the OpticalGeneration section of Physics.

parameters.name: StepFunction
parameters.desc: Step function quantum yield model with cutoff energy options (Bandgap, EffectiveBandgap, Energy, Wavelength).

parameters.name: pmiModel
parameters.desc: Name of PMI model for spatially varying quantum yield.

parameters.name: Bandgap
parameters.desc: Use Eg as cutoff energy for interband absorption.

parameters.name: EffectiveBandgap
parameters.desc: Use Eg-Ebgn as cutoff energy.

parameters.name: Energy
parameters.desc: Specified cutoff energy in eV.

parameters.name: Wavelength
parameters.desc: Specified cutoff wavelength in um.

quasistationary.description: Performs a quasistationary (DC sweep) simulation by gradually varying a contact voltage or other goal parameter from an initial to a final value. Controls stepping behavior through InitialStep, MaxStep, MinStep, Increment, and Decrement parameters. The Goal specifies the target variable to ramp.

parameters.name: InitialStep
parameters.desc: Initial step size.

parameters.name: MaxStep
parameters.desc: Maximum step size.

parameters.name: MinStep
parameters.desc: Minimum step size.

parameters.name: Increment
parameters.desc: Step size increase factor.

parameters.name: Decrement
parameters.desc: Step size decrease factor.

parameters.name: Goal
parameters.desc: Target parameter to ramp (name and value).

parameters.name: Extrapolate
parameters.desc: Extrapolation options for initial guess.

radiation.description: Activates radiation-induced carrier generation models including gamma radiation, heavy ion strike, and alpha particle generation. Used to simulate total ionizing dose (TID) and single-event effects in semiconductor devices.

parameters.name: AlphaParticle
parameters.desc: Generation by alpha particles.

parameters.name: HeavyIon
parameters.desc: Generation by heavy ions.

parameters.name: GammaRay
parameters.desc: Carrier generation by gamma radiation.

radiative.description: Enables radiative (band-to-band) recombination in the Recombination section of Physics. Models the direct recombination of electrons and holes with emission of photons. The recombination rate is proportional to the product of electron and hole densities.

contexts.SingletExciton: In SingletExciton context, radiative refers to direct radiative decay of singlet exciton (light emission).

radius.description: Specifies the radius parameter for geometric shape definitions used in approximate breakdown analysis (Window section) and far-field sensor configuration (ObservationRadius). Defines the size of spherical or cylindrical regions.

parameters.name: radius
parameters.desc: Radius of sphere or cylinder in micrometers.

parameters.name: ObservationRadius
parameters.desc: Radius in micrometers for far-field observation (default 1e6 um).

parameters.name: ConductiveRadius
parameters.desc: Influence radius of defects for KMC-MIM simulations.

random field.description: Declares a spatially correlated random field for use with the statistical impedance field method (sIFM). The random field provides spatial correlations for randomized variations such as band edge fluctuations and doping profile variations. Supports Grain, Exponential, and Gaussian correlation functions.

parameters.name: AverageGrainSize
parameters.desc: Average grain size along main axes in um.

parameters.name: CorrelationFunction
parameters.desc: Type of spatial correlation: Grain (default), Exponential, or Gaussian.

parameters.name: Lambda
parameters.desc: Correlation length for main axes in um.

parameters.name: Resolution
parameters.desc: Spatial resolution of exponential and Gaussian randomization (default 0.25 0.25 0.25).

parameters.name: MaxInternalPoints
parameters.desc: Maximum number of points for Fourier transform (default 2147483647).

ray data.description: External ray data file input for the raytracing optical solver. Specifies a file containing user-defined starting rays with position, direction, intensity, and polarization information. The ray data is imported via the UserWindow option of the RayTracing solver.

parameters.name: File
parameters.desc: Path to the file containing ray data.

parameters.name: UserWindow
parameters.desc: Input user-defined starting rays from file.

raytracing.description: Selects the raytracing method as the optical solver. Traces individual light rays through the device structure, computing optical generation from absorption at each ray segment. Supports features such as far-field computation, Monte Carlo distribution, sensor detectors, and thin-layer-stack boundary conditions.

parameters.name: MonteCarlo
parameters.desc: Activate Monte Carlo raytracing.

parameters.name: DepthLimit
parameters.desc: Stop tracing after passing through more than N material boundaries.

parameters.name: MinIntensity
parameters.desc: Stop tracing when intensity drops below this fraction of original.

parameters.name: Farfield
parameters.desc: Activate far-field and sensor feature.

parameters.name: RayDistribution
parameters.desc: Ray distribution window parameters.

parameters.name: UserWindow
parameters.desc: User-defined starting rays from file.

parameters.name: WeightedOpticalGeneration
parameters.desc: Weighted interpolation for raytrace optical generation.

parameters.name: Print
parameters.desc: Create raytree in output .tdr file.

parameters.name: NonSemiconductorAbsorption
parameters.desc: Include generation in nonsemiconductor regions.

parameters.name: PolarizationVector
parameters.desc: Fixed or random polarization vector for starting ray.

reaction.description: Specifies reaction processes in the MSC hydrogen transport degradation model. Reactions define the interaction between mobile hydrogen species and defects (traps). The reaction model includes forward and backward reaction rates and can be applied to multiple hydrogen species and trap types.

parameters.name: Reaction
parameters.desc: Define reaction between hydrogen species and trap defects.

parameters.name: eNMP
parameters.desc: Extended nonradiative multiphonon reaction model.

parameters.name: PowerLaw
parameters.desc: Power law and kinetic equation for reaction rates.

contexts.HydrogenDiffusion: Reaction between hydrogen species and defects

read.description: In SDevice context, read refers to loading data from files. The primary keyword is Load in the File section which reads previous simulation results from a .sav file. Also used in optical generation (ReadFromFile), parameter files, and PMI model data loading.

parameters.name: Load
parameters.desc: Base name for loading old simulation results (input, .sav).

parameters.name: OpticalGenerationInput
parameters.desc: File to load optical generation rate from.

parameters.name: LifeTime
parameters.desc: Lifetime profiles file (input, .tdr).

real.description: In SDevice context, real refers to the real part of complex numbers in AC analysis results and optical field calculations. It is not a standalone SDevice command keyword but appears in PMI interface variable types (pmi_float for real numbers) and scalar/vector data names (Re prefix for AC response quantities).

recombination.description: Specifies generation-recombination models within the Physics section. Supports Auger, SRH, radiative, avalanche, band-to-band tunneling, trap-assisted Auger, and coupled defect level recombination. A PMI model name can be used for custom implementations.

parameters.name: Auger
parameters.desc: Enable Auger recombination.

parameters.name: Auger(WithGeneration)
parameters.desc: Enable Auger recombination and generation.

parameters.name: SRH
parameters.desc: Shockley-Read-Hall recombination with options for field enhancement and temperature dependence.

parameters.name: Radiative
parameters.desc: Enable radiative recombination.

parameters.name: Avalanche
parameters.desc: Impact ionization (avalanche generation) with model selection.

parameters.name: Band2Band
parameters.desc: Band-to-band tunneling models including Hurkx, Schenk, and nonlocal path.

parameters.name: CDL
parameters.desc: Coupled defect level recombination.

parameters.name: TrapAssistedAuger
parameters.desc: Enable trap-assisted Auger recombination.

parameters.name: SurfaceSRH
parameters.desc: Enable interface Shockley-Read-Hall recombination.

parameters.name: ConstantCarrierGeneration(value=<float>)
parameters.desc: Constant carrier generation rate.

parameters.name: <ident>
parameters.desc: Use PMI model for generation-recombination.

refinement.description: Refers to mesh refinement capabilities in Sentaurus Device, including adaptive mesh refinement during optical solver execution and nonlocal mesh construction. Not a standalone command keyword but appears in descriptions of optical solver mesh generation.

refractive.description: Complex refractive index model for optical simulations. Controls wavelength, carrier, temperature, and gain dependencies of the refractive index and extinction coefficient. Supports user-defined PMI models via the Complex Refractive Index Model Interface (CRIMI).

parameters.name: CarrierDep(imag|real)
parameters.desc: Use carrier dependency of extinction coefficient or refractive index.

parameters.name: GainDep(real (lin|log))
parameters.desc: Use linear or logarithmic gain dependency of refractive index.

parameters.name: TemperatureDep(real)
parameters.desc: Use temperature dependency of refractive index.

parameters.name: WavelengthDep(imag|real)
parameters.desc: Use wavelength dependency of extinction coefficient or refractive index.

parameters.name: CRIModel(Name=<string>)
parameters.desc: Activate user-defined complex refractive index PMI model.

regexp.description: Regular expression matching for contact/electrode names in the Electrode, Thermode, and Solve/Goal sections. Allows pattern-based electrode selection instead of specifying individual names.

parameters.name: <string>
parameters.desc: Regular expression pattern for matching contact names.

contexts.Thermode: Regular expression matching for thermode names.
contexts.Goal: Regular expression matching for ramping target contacts in Quasistationary goals.

region.description: Specifies a named region of the device mesh for location-restricted physical model assignments. Regions are defined in the device structure and used to restrict Physics, Math, and Recombination models to specific parts of the device.

parameters.name: <string>
parameters.desc: Name of a region that exists in the device.

regionInterface.description: Specifies a named region interface (boundary between two bulk regions) for location-restricted model assignments. Used to apply interface-specific physical models such as distributed resistance, dipole layers, and interface recombination.

parameters.name: <string>
parameters.desc: Name of a region interface in the form 'Region1/Region2'.

regular expression.description: Full form of the regular expression syntax for matching contact/electrode names. Functionally identical to the 'regexp' keyword; this is the expanded descriptive term found in manual text.

parameters.name: <string>
parameters.desc: Regular expression pattern for matching contact names.

resistance.description: Distributed resistance at metal-semiconductor, metal-metal, or semiconductor-semiconductor interfaces. Can be specified as a constant value or using the Schottky contact resistance model. Also appears as Resist in Electrode for contact resistance.

parameters.name: DistResist
parameters.desc: Distributed resistance in Ohm*cm^2.

parameters.name: SchottkyResist
parameters.desc: Use Schottky contact resistance model for distributed resistance.

parameters.name: Resist
parameters.desc: Contact resistance in Ohm*um^(3-d) (in Electrode section).

restriction.description: Refers to spatial and geometric restrictions applied to physical models, noise analysis, and solver configurations. Appears in manual descriptions of volume/surface definitions and trap-to-trap tunneling constraints.

return.description: Tcl built-in command to return a value from a procedure. Not specific to Sentaurus Device but used extensively in Tcl scripts, PMI model implementations, and current plot formulas within SDevice command files.

parameters.name: <string>
parameters.desc: Optional return value.

rs.description: Ohmic series resistance parameter in compact model parameter sets (PSET). Appears within SPICE compact model definitions used in mixed-mode simulations.

parameters.name: rs
parameters.desc: Ohmic series resistance in Ohms.

rs.example:
PSET D1n4148
DEVICE Diode
PARAMETERS
  rs = 16
  is = 0.1p
END PSET

sIFM.description: Statistical Impedance Field Method section for noise, fluctuation, and sensitivity analysis. Extends small-signal AC analysis to compute noise voltage/current spectral densities and responses to deterministic or randomized parameter variations (doping, band edge, conductivity, workfunction).

parameters.name: DeterministicVariation
parameters.desc: Deterministic variations for doping, geometry, and parameter sweeping.

parameters.name: Noise
parameters.desc: Local noise sources such as doping fluctuations, generation-recombination noise, and mobility fluctuations.

parameters.name: RandomizedVariation
parameters.desc: Randomized variations with correlation functions for statistical analysis.

scattering rate.description: Refers to carrier scattering rate computations in the Spherical Harmonics Expansion (SHE) method and intervalley scattering models. Used in the context of high-field transport and energy-dependent transport simulations.

sdft_update_norm_R.description: Convergence parameter in the HB section of the global Math section for harmonic balance analysis. Controls the update error computation variant: the old version (compatible with R-2020.09) or an improved novel variant recommended for logarithmic variables (LogDensity, LogTemperature).

sensitivity.description: Specifies sensitivity analysis for metal-insulator-metal (MIM) tunneling simulations within the KMC_MIM_Transport section of Physics. Computes leakage sensitivity with respect to defect energy levels and spatial positions.

parameters.name: EtRange
parameters.desc: Start, end, and step size for defect energy levels (eV).

parameters.name: PlotType
parameters.desc: Plot type: 1=absolute energy vs Xt (flat band), 2=absolute energy vs Xt (bias), 3=Et vs Xt.

parameters.name: Point
parameters.desc: Path for defect locations (um), default (0,0,0).

parameters.name: PrintAll
parameters.desc: Print leakage values for all (Xt, Et) pairs to log.

parameters.name: XtRange
parameters.desc: Start, end, and step size for defect locations (um).

sensor.description: Defines detector sensors for raytracing far-field analysis within the Farfield subsection of RayTracing. Supports angular, line, and rectangle sensor geometries to collect optical flux data.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.desc: Name of the sensor.

parameters.name: Angular(Phi=<float>*2, Theta=<float>*2)
parameters.desc: Define angular range for the sensor.

parameters.name: Line(Corner1=<vector>, Corner2=<vector>, UseNormalFlux)
parameters.desc: Define line sensor geometry.

parameters.name: Rectangle(AxisAligned|Corner1=<vector>, Corner2=<vector>, Corner3=<vector>, UseNormalFlux)
parameters.desc: Define rectangle sensor geometry.

set.description: Sets simulation state during solve, including node voltages, circuit parameters, trap filling, MSC configurations, boundary condition modes, and hydrogen concentrations. Multiple Set entries must be in separate Set() definitions since each takes only one option.

parameters.name: <ident> = <float>
parameters.desc: Set circuit node voltage.

parameters.name: <ident>.<string> = <float>
parameters.desc: Set compact model parameter.

parameters.name: <ident> mode Charge|Current|Voltage
parameters.desc: Change boundary condition type for a contact.

parameters.name: MSConfigs(<options>)
parameters.desc: Manipulate multistate configuration occupations.

parameters.name: TrapFilling = <value>
parameters.desc: Set trap filling (0, Empty, Full, Frozen, -Degradation).

parameters.name: Traps(<options>)
parameters.desc: Set explicit trap occupations.

parameters.name: HydrogenAtom|Ion|Molecule = <string>
parameters.desc: Set hydrogen species concentrations.

simpleCapt.description: Option within the Traps definition that restricts field enhancement of trapping cross sections to emission rates only. When used with ElectricField, Hurkx, Makram-Ebeid, or Poole-Frenkel cross-section models, the capture rates use constant cross sections while emission rates are field-enhanced.

sin.description: SPICE voltage source sine wave specification in compact model PSET definitions for mixed-mode simulations. Defines a sinusoidal voltage source with parameters: amplitude, frequency, delay, damping factor, phase, and DC offset.

parameters.name: sine
parameters.desc: Parameters: V0 (amplitude), freq (Hz), td (delay), theta (damping), phi (phase), vc (DC offset).

sin.example:
INSTANCE v
  PSET Vsource_pset
  ELECTRODES in 0
  PARAMETERS sine = [0 5 1meg 0 0]
END INSTANCE

sine.description: Full spelling of the SPICE sine wave source parameter. Functionally identical to 'sin'. Also appears in descriptions of sinusoidal steady-state analysis and excitation source definitions.

parameters.name: sine
parameters.desc: Parameters: V0 (amplitude), freq (Hz), td (delay), theta (damping), phi (phase), vc (DC offset).

singlet.description: Activates the singlet exciton equation for organic device simulations. Models diffusion, generation from bimolecular recombination, decay, and optical emission of singlet excitons (Frenkel excitons). Only Frenkel excitons participate in optical processes in organic materials.

parameters.name: BarrierType(Bandgap|CondBand|ValBand)
parameters.desc: Set barrier type at organic heterointerfaces.

parameters.name: FluxBC
parameters.desc: Impose zero flux boundary condition on specified interface.

parameters.name: Recombination(Bimolecular|Dissociation|eQuenching|hQuenching|radiative|trappedradiative)
parameters.desc: Recombination and generation terms in singlet exciton equation.

small-signal.description: Small-signal AC analysis performed as an extension of the coupled Newton solve. Computes frequency-domain responses of solution variables to AC signals. Used for extracting capacitances, conductances, S-parameters, and performing noise analysis.

parameters.name: StartFrequency
parameters.desc: Lower frequency in Hz.

parameters.name: EndFrequency
parameters.desc: Upper frequency in Hz.

parameters.name: NumberOfPoints
parameters.desc: Number of frequency points.

parameters.name: Decade
parameters.desc: Use logarithmic frequency intervals.

parameters.name: Node
parameters.desc: Nodes for AC analysis.

parameters.name: Optical
parameters.desc: Perform optical AC analysis.

parameters.name: CircuitNoise
parameters.desc: Compute noise from circuit elements.

solver.description: Specifies the linear solver used in Coupled and ACCoupled solve statements. Options include iterative (ILS), supernodal direct (ParDiSo), single-threaded direct (Super), and block decomposition (Blocked) solvers.

parameters.name: ILS
parameters.desc: Parallel iterative linear solver. Default for 3D structures.

parameters.name: Super
parameters.desc: Supernodal direct solver. Best accuracy for small problems, not parallelized. Default for 1D and 2D.

parameters.name: ParDiSo
parameters.desc: Parallel supernodal direct solver.

parameters.name: Blocked
parameters.desc: Block decomposition solver.

source.description: Tcl command to source (execute) an external Tcl script file. Used in the File section and System section of SDevice command files to load external definitions, circuit descriptions, or PMI shared objects.

parameters.name: <filename>
parameters.desc: Path to the Tcl script file to execute.

spatial.description: Refers to spatial distribution, correlation, and resolution of physical quantities in SDevice. Appears in contexts such as spatial correlations for random fields, spatial distribution of traps, spatial resolution of optical generation, and spatial restriction of variations.

spectrum.description: Illumination spectrum file for optical generation computation. Contains wavelength-dependent photon flux data used with ComputeFromSpectrum to calculate optical generation rates across the device. Also refers to spectral plot output files.

parameters.name: IlluminationSpectrum
parameters.desc: Base name of the illumination spectrum file.

parameters.name: SpectralPlot
parameters.desc: Base name for spectral plot output files.

parameters.name: Gain
parameters.desc: Base name for modal stimulated and spontaneous emission spectra output.

split.description: Refers to splitting operations in SDevice such as rough surface scattering of rays, splitting of tensor grids for anisotropic models, and strain-related band structure deformation. Not a standalone command keyword.

spontaneous.description: Refers to spontaneous emission in LED simulations. The spontaneous emission rate depletes the carrier population in the active region and produces photons. Controlled through the LED framework activation and optical solver configuration.

statistical.description: Statistical impedance field method (sIFM) for analyzing the impact of random parameter variations (doping, geometry, trap concentration, band edge, conductivity, workfunction) on device characteristics. Uses correlation functions (exponential, Gaussian, grain) for spatial correlations of variations.

parameters.name: RandomizedVariation
parameters.desc: Specifies randomized variation with correlation and spatial restriction.

parameters.name: NumberOfSamples
parameters.desc: Number of statistical samples.

parameters.name: Randomize
parameters.desc: Seed for random number generator.

parameters.name: RandomField(<options>)
parameters.desc: Declare random field with correlation function.

stimulated.description: Refers to stimulated emission in LED and laser simulations. In LEDs below the lasing threshold, stimulated emission is typically negligible compared to spontaneous emission. In laser simulations, the resonant cavity enhances coherent stimulated emission. Controlled through the LED framework.

strain.signature: Piezo { Strain = (xx yy zz yz xz xy) | Hooke | LoadFromFile }
strain.description: Specifies the strain tensor components for piezoelectric and stress-dependent models. Used within the Piezo section of Physics to define mechanical deformation.

parameters.name: Strain
parameters.desc: Components xx, yy, zz, yz, xz, xy of the strain tensor.

parameters.name: Hooke
parameters.desc: Compute strain tensor from stress tensor using Hooke's law.

parameters.name: LoadFromFile
parameters.desc: Load strain from Piezo file specified in File section.

stress.signature: Piezo { Stress = (xx yy zz yz xz xy) | <PMI_ident> }
stress.description: Specifies the stress tensor components for piezoelectric and stress-dependent mobility models. Can be given as explicit values or computed via a PMI model.

parameters.name: Stress
parameters.desc: Components xx, yy, zz, yz, xz, xy of the stress tensor in Pa, or a PMI model name to compute stress.

string.description: String type parameter used in PMI model parameter specifications, System section node names, and various command file contexts. Not a dedicated SDevice keyword but a fundamental data type.

subband.signature: Piezo { Model { Mobility ( eSubband( Scattering | Doping | Fermi | EffectiveMass | BalMob | NoStressInDDmob | ... ) ) } }
subband.description: Stress-induced subband models that account for the modification of carrier mobility due to strain-induced subband splitting. Available for both electrons (eSubband) and holes (hSubband) with various options for scattering, doping dependence, Fermi statistics, and effective mass.

parameters.name: eSubband
parameters.desc: Strain-induced subband model for electrons.

parameters.name: hSubband
parameters.desc: Strain-induced subband model for holes.

parameters.name: Scattering
parameters.desc: Include scattering in the subband mobility model.

parameters.name: Scattering(MLDA)
parameters.desc: Subband model with interface scattering via MLDA.

parameters.name: Doping
parameters.desc: Subband model with doping dependency.

parameters.name: Fermi
parameters.desc: Subband model with carrier concentration (Fermi statistics) dependency.

parameters.name: EffectiveMass
parameters.desc: Stress-induced change of effective mass.

parameters.name: BalMob
parameters.desc: Stress-induced change of ballistic mobility.

parameters.name: NoStressInDDmob
parameters.desc: Account for only ballistic mobility component.

surface.description: Defines a surface in the Math section for mobility degradation calculations due to Coulomb scattering, geometric fluctuations, and other interface-dependent effects. Surfaces are used by Enormal, InterfaceCharge, NegInterfaceCharge, PosInterfaceCharge, and RandomizedVariation models.

parameters.name: <name>
parameters.desc: Name identifier for the surface definition.

parameters.name: Table 361
parameters.desc: Interface location specification (MaterialInterface or RegionInterface).

surfaceSRH.description: Enables interface Shockley-Read-Hall recombination at semiconductor-insulator or semiconductor-semiconductor interfaces. Computes surface recombination rate based on SRH statistics with interface trap parameters.

switch.description: Changes the boundary condition type of a contact during simulation. Allows dynamic switching between voltage, current, and charge-controlled modes for electrodes within the Solve section.

parameters.name: <ident>
parameters.desc: Name of the contact.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.desc: Boundary condition type to switch to.

t.description: Single-letter qualifier in the Math section, typically used to designate time-related quantities in transient simulation error control and convergence criteria.

tSRHRecombination.description: Scalar dataset name for total (electron + hole) SRH recombination rate including trap-assisted tunneling effects. Plotted at vertices with unit cm-3s-1.

tau_w_ele.description: Parameter file keyword specifying the base electron energy relaxation time in picoseconds. Used in energy-dependent energy relaxation time models for hydrodynamic simulations.

parameters.name: tau_w_ele
parameters.desc: Base electron energy relaxation time in picoseconds [ps].

parameters.name: Formula(tau_w_ele)
parameters.desc: Formula type: 0 (constant), 2 (irrational polynomial), 3 (spline interpolation).

tau_w_hol.description: Parameter file keyword specifying the base hole energy relaxation time in picoseconds. Used in energy-dependent energy relaxation time models for hydrodynamic simulations.

parameters.name: tau_w_hol
parameters.desc: Base hole energy relaxation time in picoseconds [ps].
