parameters.name: Formula(tau_w_hol)
parameters.desc: Formula type: 0 (constant), 2 (irrational polynomial), 3 (spline interpolation).

tc1.description: SPICE parameter set keyword for the first-order temperature coefficient of a resistor model (degree Celsius). Used in external SPICE circuit files for mixed-mode simulations.

parameters.name: tc1
parameters.desc: First-order temperature coefficient [1/C].

tc1.example:
PSET cres_pset
  DEVICE Resistor
  PARAMETERS
  rsh = 1
  tc1 = 0.01
  tnom = 27
END PSET

temp.description: SPICE parameter for device temperature in compact model parameter sets. Also used as a parameter name accessible through parameter interfaces in mixed-mode simulations.

parameters.name: temp
parameters.desc: Device temperature parameter.

tensor mesh.description: Enables the use of tensor-grid approximation for anisotropic models, including piezoelectric mobility models. When active, the mobility tensor is resolved on the local tensor grid for improved accuracy.

parameters.name: TensorGridAniso
parameters.desc: Enable tensor-grid approximation for anisotropic models (default on for piezo).

parameters.name: Aniso
parameters.desc: Apply tensor-grid approximation to general anisotropic models.

parameters.name: Piezo
parameters.desc: Apply tensor-grid approximation to piezoelectric mobility models.

testing.description: A debugging option that appears in the context of Solve section. Limited documentation available; likely related to testing/debugging simulation controls.

thermal.description: Relates to thermal simulation capabilities in SDevice, including thermal conductivity models, thermoelectric power, self-heating effects, and lattice temperature-dependent transport. Thermal conductivity can be specified as constant, formula-based, temperature-dependent, or via PMI models.

parameters.name: ThermalConductivity
parameters.desc: Thermal conductivity model specification.

parameters.name: Constant
parameters.desc: Use constant conductivity or resistivity.

parameters.name: Formula
parameters.desc: Use built-in formula strategy.

parameters.name: TempDep
parameters.desc: Temperature-dependent conductivity/resistivity.

parameters.name: PMIModel
parameters.desc: Multistate configuration-dependent model.

thermode.signature: Device { Thermode { Name = <string> | Temperature = <float> | Power = <float> | SurfaceResistance = <float> | SurfaceConductance = <float> | AreaFactor = <float> } }
thermode.description: Specifies thermal boundary conditions for a device contact. A thermode is analogous to an electrode but for heat flow, defining the temperature or heat flux at a thermal contact.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.desc: Name of the thermode.

parameters.name: Temperature
parameters.desc: Contact temperature in K.

parameters.name: Power
parameters.desc: Heat flux boundary condition in W/cm^2.

parameters.name: SurfaceConductance
parameters.desc: Contact thermal conductance in cm^-2 K^-1 W.

parameters.name: SurfaceResistance
parameters.desc: Contact thermal resistivity in cm^2 K W^-1.

parameters.name: AreaFactor
parameters.desc: Multiplier for heat fluxes (default 1).

time.description: In the Plot section, specifies the time variable as a dataset for plotting. The time dataset is automatically available in transient simulations and can be plotted alongside other quantities in Sentaurus Visual.

time dependence.description: Refers to time-dependent degradation models for traps in SDevice, including kinetic equation and power law models for hydrogen-related defect creation/passivation (NBTI, HCS degradation). These models describe how trap concentrations evolve over time under electrical stress.

tnom.description: SPICE parameter set keyword for the nominal temperature of a compact model in degree Celsius. Used in external SPICE circuit files for mixed-mode simulations to define the reference temperature for temperature-dependent parameters.

parameters.name: tnom
parameters.desc: Nominal temperature in degree Celsius.

transfer matrix.signature: Optics { OpticalSolver ( TMM ( Table 268 ) ) } | Optics { TMM ( Table 240 ) }
transfer matrix.description: The Transfer Matrix Method (TMM) optical solver computes optical generation rates in planar layer structures by solving Maxwell's equations using the transfer matrix approach. It accounts for coherent and incoherent optical interference.

parameters.name: TMM
parameters.desc: Activate the Transfer Matrix Method optical solver.

parameters.name: Table 268
parameters.desc: TMM-specific options including layer stack extraction and coherence settings.

transient.description: Performs a time-dependent (transient) simulation. Controls time stepping, initial and final times, step size limits, and integration method (Backward Euler or TRBDF). Supports cyclic analysis and extrapolation.

parameters.name: FinalTime
parameters.desc: Final simulation time in seconds (default: unspecified).

parameters.name: InitialTime
parameters.desc: Start time in seconds (default: 0).

parameters.name: InitialStep
parameters.desc: Initial time step in seconds (default: 0.1).

parameters.name: MaxStep
parameters.desc: Maximum time step in seconds (default: 1).

parameters.name: MinStep
parameters.desc: Minimum time step in seconds (default: 0.001).

parameters.name: Increment
parameters.desc: Multiplier for step size on success (default: 2).

parameters.name: Decrement
parameters.desc: Divisor for step size on failure (default: 2).

parameters.name: Transient
parameters.desc: Integration method: Backward Euler or TRBDF composite (default: TRBDF).

parameters.name: Cyclic
parameters.desc: Enable large-signal cyclic analysis.

parameters.name: TurningPoints
parameters.desc: Time-dependent advancing time-step limits.

parameters.name: Extrapolate
parameters.desc: Enable extrapolation for initial guess.

parameters.name: BreakCriteria
parameters.desc: Break criteria specification.

transition.signature: MSConfig { Transition ( Name=<string> To=<string> From=<string> CEModel(<ident>) [Table 330] ) }
transition.description: Defines a transition between two states of a multistate configuration (MSC). Transitions describe the kinetics of defect state changes and are used in degradation and reliability simulations. Each transition connects a source state (From) to a target state (To) and can use a PMI model for capture/emission rates.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.desc: Identifier for the transition.

parameters.name: To
parameters.desc: Reference (target) state name.

parameters.name: From
parameters.desc: Interacting (source) state name.

parameters.name: CEModel
parameters.desc: PMI_TrapCaptureEmission model for transition rates.

parameters.name: FieldFromInsulator
parameters.desc: Use insulator electric field for interface MSCs.

parameters.name: Reservoirs
parameters.desc: List of reservoirs for particle conservation.

transport.description: Refers to carrier transport models in SDevice including drift-diffusion, thermodynamic, and hydrodynamic transport. Transport equations are solved for electron and hole current densities. Also a PMI interface category for diffusivity models.

trap.description: Defines trap properties for Shockley-Read-Hall recombination, trap-assisted tunneling, and degradation models. Traps are characterized by their energy level, concentration, capture cross-sections, and spatial/energetic distributions. Supports bulk and interface traps, single and multiple energy levels, and KMC-MIM defect specifications.

parameters.name: Conc
parameters.desc: Trap concentration or peak density in cm^-d.

parameters.name: EnergyMid
parameters.desc: Central energy of trap distribution in eV.

parameters.name: EnergySig
parameters.desc: Width of trap energy distribution in eV.

parameters.name: eXsection
parameters.desc: Electron capture cross section in cm^2.

parameters.name: hXsection
parameters.desc: Hole capture cross section in cm^2.

parameters.name: eGfactor
parameters.desc: Electron degeneracy factor.

parameters.name: hGfactor
parameters.desc: Hole degeneracy factor.

parameters.name: Level
parameters.desc: Single energy level trap.

parameters.name: Gaussian
parameters.desc: Gaussian energy distribution.

parameters.name: Exponential
parameters.desc: Exponential energy distribution.

parameters.name: Uniform
parameters.desc: Uniform energy distribution.

parameters.name: SingleTrap
parameters.desc: Use single trap per vertex.

parameters.name: eNeutral
parameters.desc: Electron trap type (neutral when empty).

parameters.name: hNeutral
parameters.desc: Hole trap type (neutral when empty).

parameters.name: Donor
parameters.desc: Donor trap type.

parameters.name: Acceptor
parameters.desc: Acceptor trap type.

parameters.name: FixedCharge
parameters.desc: Fixed charge (no carrier exchange).

parameters.name: Degradation
parameters.desc: Kinetic equation degradation model.

parameters.name: Tunneling
parameters.desc: Hurkx trap-assisted tunneling.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.desc: Trap identifier.

parameters.name: Region
parameters.desc: Region where trap is active.

parameters.name: Material
parameters.desc: Material where trap is active.

parameters.name: SFactor
parameters.desc: Spatial distribution of trap concentration.

parameters.name: Coupled
parameters.desc: Enable trap-to-trap tunneling coupling.

parameters.name: eBarrierTunneling
parameters.desc: Nonlocal tunneling from conduction band.

parameters.name: hBarrierTunneling
parameters.desc: Nonlocal tunneling from valence band.

parameters.name: CBRate
parameters.desc: PMI for electron capture/emission rate.

parameters.name: VBRate
parameters.desc: PMI for hole capture/emission rate.

try.description: Refers to extrapolation attempts and convergence attempts in the Newton solver. The Extrapolate option tries to use information from previous solutions to obtain better initial guesses. Also appears in the context of 'ReadExtrapolation' which attempts to read extrapolation data from a previous run.

tt.description: Appears in multiple contexts: as a variable name in parameter files and command files, as an abbreviation in various model specifications, and in mixed-mode simulation file naming conventions. Not a specific SDevice command.

tunneling.signature: Physics { Recombination { Band2Band ( Tunneling ) } } | Physics { Traps ( Tunneling ( Hurkx ) ) } | eBarrierTunneling / hBarrierTunneling
tunneling.description: Encompasses various tunneling models in SDevice including band-to-band tunneling, trap-assisted tunneling (Hurkx model), nonlocal tunneling at interfaces/contacts/junctions (eBarrierTunneling/hBarrierTunneling), and Fowler-Nordheim tunneling. Also a PMI interface category for tunneling parameters.

parameters.name: Tunneling
parameters.desc: Hurkx trap-assisted tunneling model.

parameters.name: eBarrierTunneling
parameters.desc: Nonlocal tunneling from/to conduction band.

parameters.name: hBarrierTunneling
parameters.desc: Nonlocal tunneling from/to valence band.

parameters.name: Band2Band
parameters.desc: Band-to-band tunneling models (Hurkx, Schenk, NonlocalPath, etc.).

parameters.name: GateCurrent
parameters.desc: Gate current tunneling models.

parameters.name: Schroedinger
parameters.desc: Use Schrodinger equation-based tunneling probability.

parameters.name: TwoBand
parameters.desc: Use two-band dispersion relation.

parameters.name: BandGap
parameters.desc: Allow tunneling into band gap.

parameters.name: BarrierLowering
parameters.desc: Position-dependent barrier lowering.

parameters.name: Multivalley
parameters.desc: Use multivalley band structure.

parameters.name: PeltierHeat
parameters.desc: Include Peltier heating for tunneling carriers.

um.description: Unit of length (micrometers) used in SDevice for spatial coordinates, layer thicknesses, and geometric parameters. Appears as a unit suffix in dataset descriptions and parameter specifications.

unset.description: Frees a circuit node that was previously fixed by a Set statement, returning it to its equation-based behavior. For electrical nodes, the current balance equation is solved; for thermal nodes, the heat balance equation is solved. The TrapFilling option restores standard trap equations.

parameters.name: <ident>
parameters.desc: Name of the node to unset.

parameters.name: TrapFilling
parameters.desc: Restore standard trap equations after explicit trap occupation control.

v.description: Vertical polarization identifier for optical/raytracing simulations. Used to specify the polarization state of incident light or rays.

vanOverstraeten.description: Selects the van Overstraeten-de Man model for impact ionization (avalanche generation). This is the default avalanche model. It can be specified inside Avalanche, eAvalanche, or hAvalanche to set the model for both carriers, electrons only, or holes only.

x1.signature: Physics { Optics ( Excitation ( Window ( Line ( X1=<float> ) ) ) ) }
x1.description: Specifies the starting x-coordinate of a Line illumination window shape. Used together with X2 to define the extent of the line in the local x-direction.

parameters.name: X1
parameters.desc: Starting x-coordinate of the line window.

contexts.Math: Used in LatticeParameters X vector definition for crystal coordinate system.

x2.signature: Physics { Optics ( Excitation ( Window ( Line ( X2=<float> ) ) ) ) }
x2.description: Specifies the ending x-coordinate of a Line illumination window shape. Used together with X1 to define the extent of the line in the local x-direction.

parameters.name: X2
parameters.desc: Ending x-coordinate of the line window.

xAxis.description: Shorthand for anisotropic direction=(1 0 0), specifying the x-axis as the anisotropic direction. Used in Aniso(), eQuantumPotential(), hQuantumPotential(), and related statements for crystal or simulation system coordinate alignment.

contexts.Math: Used in Cylindrical() for specifying the rotation axis: Cylindrical(xAxis=<float>)

xDirection.description: Defines the direction of the local x-axis for an illumination window. Used in the Excitation section to orient optical excitation windows. The default is (1, 0, 0). In 2D, only XDirection needs to be specified.

parameters.name: XDirection
parameters.desc: Direction vector for the local x-axis of the illumination window.

xFraction.description: Specifies the constant value of the x-mole fraction for binary, ternary, or quaternary compound semiconductors. Mandatory for binary and ternary compounds in the MoleFraction statement. Also used in Grading sub-statements to specify boundary mole fraction values.

parameters.name: xFraction
parameters.desc: Constant value of xMoleFraction in the range [0, 1].

xMoleFraction.description: Scalar plot quantity representing the x-mole fraction of a compound semiconductor material. Available as vertex data for plotting and as a dataset for SFactor spatial distribution specifications.

contexts.Traps: Available as SFactor dataset name for spatial trap distribution: SFactor="xMoleFraction"

yAxis.description: Shorthand for anisotropic direction=(0 1 0), specifying the y-axis as the anisotropic direction. Used in Aniso(), eQuantumPotential(), hQuantumPotential(), and related statements.

contexts.Math: Used in Cylindrical() for specifying the rotation axis: Cylindrical(yAxis=<float>)

yFraction.description: Specifies the constant value of the y-mole fraction. Mandatory for quaternary compound semiconductors in the MoleFraction statement. Also used in Grading sub-statements to specify boundary mole fraction values.

parameters.name: yFraction
parameters.desc: Constant value of yMoleFraction in the range [0, 1].

yMoleFraction.description: Scalar plot quantity representing the y-mole fraction of a compound semiconductor material. Available as vertex data for plotting and as a dataset for SFactor spatial distribution specifications.

contexts.Traps: Available as SFactor dataset name for spatial trap distribution: SFactor="yMoleFraction"

zAxis.description: Shorthand for anisotropic direction=(0 0 1), specifying the z-axis as the anisotropic direction. Used in Aniso() and related statements for 3D simulations.
